title: From Few-Shot to Zero-Shot: Towards Generalist Graph Anomaly Detection_supp1-3691902.pdf
authors: Shirui Pan
publish: N/A
abstract: Graph anomaly detection (GAD) is critical for identifying abnormal nodes in graph-structured data from diverse domains, including cybersecurity and social networks. The existing GAD methods often focus on the learning paradigms of “one-model-for-one-dataset”, requiring dataset-specific training for each dataset to achieve optimal performance. However, this paradigm suffers from significant limitations, such as high computational and data costs, limited generalization and transferability to new datasets, and challenges in privacy-sensitive scenarios where access to full datasets or sufficient labels is restricted. To address these limitations, we propose a novel generalist GAD paradigm that aims to develop a unified model capable of detecting anomalies on multiple unseen datasets without extensive retraining/fine-tuning or dataset-specific customization. To this end, we propose a few-shot generalist GAD method with three key designs, namely feature <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>lignment, a <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>esidual encoder, and in-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>ontext learning, abbreviated as ARC. As a generalist approach, ARC only requires a few labeled normal samples during prediction on any unseen graphs. Specifically, ARC consists of three core modules: a feature <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">A</u>lignment module to unify and align features across datasets, a <underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">R</u>esidual graph encoder to capture dataset-agnostic anomaly representations, and a cross-attentive in-<underline xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</u>ontext learning module to score anomalies using few-shot normal context. Building on ARC, we further introduce ARC<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">zero</sub> for the zero-shot generalist GAD setting, which selects representative pseudo-normal nodes via a pseudo-context mechanism and thus enables fully label-free inference on unseen datasets. Extensive experiments on 17 real-world graph datasets demonstrate that both ARC and ARC<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">zero</sub> effectively detect anomalies, exhibit strong generalization ability, and perform efficiently under few-shot and zero-shot settings.

title: Enabling Frictionless and Continuous Authentication for Edge Computing via Privacy-Preserving Behavioral Modeling_supp1-3688247.pdf
authors: Cheng Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Edge computing has emerged as an important paradigm capable of executing complex tasks at the edge of networks. User authentication, as the common gate of any edge service that attaches importance to security, has become increasingly critical. However, the existing authentication methods encounter challenges in meeting the demands of low-latency services within edge computing due to their intrusive access control. Additionally, the vulnerabilities in edge network security necessitate continuous protection. In this paper, we propose a novel edge behavioral authentication (EBA) scheme, which facilitates a frictionless and continuous authentication process. During user enrollment, diverse smart devices collaboratively establish identity extractors, generating measurable behavioral embedding vectors while prioritizing privacy preservation and cost efficiency. For user verification, a two-step method incorporating individual-level verification and swarm-level verification with a collaborative certification committee (C3) mechanism is devised to enhance authentication performance. Extensive experiments have been conducted, focusing on identity theft issues using two representative behavioral modalities: online transaction behavior and user driving behavior. The experimental results validate the efficiency and security of our proposed scheme.

title: Nano-Bio Systems for Intra-body Sensing, Actuation, and Communication in the Terahertz Band and Beyond
authors: Krzysztof Skos, Hadeel Elayan, Samar Elmaadawy, Pawel Kulakowski, Josep Miquel Jornet
publish: IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials
abstract: The terahertz (THz) band and the optical frequency window are emerging as powerful enablers of next generation biomedical technologies, supporting high resolution sensing of biological processes, targeted intra-body actuation, and intra-body communication for coordinated functional operation. Although each modality has demonstrated significant promise individually, research efforts have largely progressed in isolation, limiting the exploration of their potential for integrated deployment. This comprehensive survey bridges these domains by presenting a unified analysis of THz and optical modalities for intra-body sensing, actuation, and communication. Building on this foundation, we articulate a vision for intra-body systems in which multiple functions are seamlessly coordinated at cellular and subcellular scales. Applications such as continuous health monitoring and closed loop therapeutic interventions illustrate the transformative potential of these integrated approaches. We outline a three-stage roadmap covering foundational modeling, in-vitro integration, and in-vivo translation, identifying critical open challenges including compact source generation, power delivery, biosensing miniaturization, and biocompatibility.

title: A Wideband Directional Coupler With Stable Coupling Factor Based on Spoof Surface Plasmon Polaritons
authors: Jiawei Zheng, Yuhui Ren, Cheng Xu, Yiqing Li, Yingxi Liu, Handong Wu, Ke Li, Fuwei Wang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a broadband miniaturized directional coupler based on two parallel spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) transmission lines (TLs). The design leverages the intrinsic dispersion and slow wave characteristics of SSPPs to significantly compress the guided wavelength, thereby achieving substantial miniaturization of the device. To balance the phase velocities of the odd and even modes in the coupled microstrip lines, the capacitive parasitic patches are introduced into the coupling region, resulting in a flatter coupling response. Furthermore, the lumped resistors are incorporated between the parasitic patches and the TL to enable flexible control of the coupling factor while simultaneously enhancing the directivity across a wide operating bandwidth. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed coupler achieves a directivity of at least 14dB over a 64% fractional bandwidth (2.64–5.1 GHz), with a coupling coefficient maintained at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10~\pm ~0.9$</tex-math> </inline-formula> dB. Owing to its wide bandwidth, high directivity, compact size, and stable coupling performance, the coupler meets the stringent requirements of modern wireless systems for high-performance components.

title: Improved Method for Electromagnetic Analysis of Axial Flux Permanent Magnet Motor with 3D Leakage Flux in Overhang Structures
authors: Jun-Won Yang, Tae-Seong Kim, Kyung-Hun Shin, Chang-Woo Kim, Cheol Han, Yong-Joo Kim, Jang-Young Choi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: In this study, an improved analytical technique for axial flux permanent magnet motors is proposed, considering overhang and end leakage flux effects. To account for leakage flux at permanent magnet ends and overhang effects, flux coefficients are determined through the magnetic equivalent circuit method and incorporated into the subdomain method. The accuracy of this improved analytical technique is verified by comparing the results with those obtained from 3D finite element analysis. The proposed method achieves a balance between computational efficiency and accuracy, providing an effective method for axial flux permanent magnet motor design and analysis.

title: Design of N-S-Iron Interleaved Asymmetric Pole Arc Coefficient 24-slot/20-pole CPPM Machine with Rotor Inner Slots
authors: Qiyun Zhang, Jiyao Wang, Youkang Hu, Wei Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: This paper presents two novel 24-slot/20-pole consequent-pole permanent magnet (CPPM) machines incorporating an N-S-iron interleaved rotor topology with asymmetric pole arc coefficients and rotor inner slots. The proposed N-S-iron interleaved configuration effectively eliminates unipolar leakage flux observed in N(S)-iron CPPM machines, while simultaneously reducing the amount of permanent magnets (PMs) used. Furthermore, radial slots are strategically incorporated along the inner rotor surface corresponding to the N-S sequence, leading to enhanced average torque and suppressed torque ripple. The asymmetric pole arc coefficients of both machine designs are optimized to further elevate torque performance. Following elaboration of the topology and operational principles, the electromagnetic characteristics are evaluated using finite element analysis (FEA). Simulation results confirm that both proposed CPPM achieve superior torque along with significant torque ripple reduction.

title: Robust Multi-Stream Massive MIMO Satellite Systems Based on Statistical CSI
authors: Hangsong Yan, Alexei Ashikhmin, Hong Yang, Bin Song, Shu Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Communications
abstract: This paper investigates multi-stream downlink precoding for massive multiple-input multiple-output low-Earth-orbit satellite (SAT) communication systems. We adopt a delay and Doppler precompensation approach to achieve coherent transmission. Under this setting, we formulate a signal transmission model that incorporates the near-independent properties of inter-SAT interference and compensation errors. We then demonstrate that moving beyond single-stream transmission requires both multi-SAT cooperation and multi-antenna user terminals. Based on this configuration and the established signal transmission model, we derive the first- and second-order statistical channel characteristics and utilize them to design locally optimal precoding algorithms for both total power constraint (TPC) and per-antenna power constraint (PAPC) conditions, which rely only on statistical channel state information (sCSI). In particular, the designed PAPC algorithm achieves linear complexity with respect to the number of antennas on the cooperative SATs. To reduce the computational complexity of the locally optimal precoder under TPC, we propose a low-complexity and robust precoding scheme targeting both the minimum mean squared error and sum-rate maximization objectives. Using majorization theory, we also provide a rigorous theoretical analysis of the optimal precoding structure under TPC. Moreover, the Lanczos algorithm is adopted to further reduce the complexity of the proposed robust designs. Simulation results show that when each SAT is equipped with a sufficiently large number of antennas, the proposed sCSI-based designs achieve performance comparable to that of instantaneous CSI-based designs.

title: Sensing Performance Optimization for ISAC with ITS and IRS
authors: Wannian Du, Zheng Chu, Pei Xiao, Meng Hua, Zhengyu Zhu, Yi Wang, Qun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In this paper, a novel integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) system architecture is introduced that incorpo rates an intelligent transmission surface (ITS) in the transmitter and an intelligent reflecting surface (IRS) in the transmission channel. The ITS introduces a novel signal modulation architecture for coordinated operation with the base station (BS), while the IRS facilitates efficient signal transmission for the communication users and the sensing target. The main goal is to maximize the beam response gain from the IRS to the sensing target while satisfying the demand of the communication performance for the users. This is realized by collaboratively designing hybrid beamforming strategies at the BS for the simultaneous transmission of communication and sensing signals, along with the ITS and IRS phase shifts. Then, an effective algorithm is proposed that utilizes penalty factors to simplify the optimization problem. Furthermore, an alternating optimization (AO) method is employed to iteratively optimize the relevant variables. Subproblems in this framework are further solved using techniques such as the Lagrange multiplier method, bi section search, the successive convex approximation (SCA), and the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme yields a significant enhancement in beam response gain and energy efficiency compared to benchmark schemes.

title: Block-MariAuth: Blockchain and PUF Based Authentication Protocol for NTN Assisted Telemedicine Drones in Medical Healthcare 5.0
authors: Aatif Suleman, Shabeer Ahmad, Umar Ajaib Khan, Muhammad Asghar Khan, Mubashir Ahmad, Shahbaz Khan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Use of drones has become essential in environments that are designed to respond to emergency areas. Non-terrestrial networks (NTNs) are able to support seamless mobility without incurring extra energy consumption. Telemedicine drones connected with NTNs are highly suitable for the healthcare environment to serve in disaster areas. To secure communication in NTN-assisted telemedicine in Healthcare 5.0, we enhance and analyze the performance of the designed authentication protocol MariAuth, which is termed as Block-MariAuth owing to the integration of a blockchain-based distributed storage to store cryptographic primitives and other sensitive information. The proposed protocol employed Physical Unclonable Function (PUF) to ensure secure communication and provide physical tamper-proof features in NTN-assisted telemedicine drone networks. Comprehensive security and performance analyses confirm that Block-MariAuth is much more secure and efficient compared with other schemes. Both informal and formal security analyses are conducted by applying the Random or Real (ROR) model and the simulation tool Automated Validation of Internet Security Protocols and Applications (AVISPA), which validate the security features of Block-MariAuth. The proposed protocol securely defends against known attacks (i.e., man-in-the-middle, node tampering, replay, impersonation, cloning). Furthermore, the computational and communication costs of Block-MariAuth achieve reductions of approximately 72% and 63%, respectively. Finally, a state-of-the-art comparison analysis is provided, including the limitations of the proposed study and future planning.

title: CAC-Net: Convolutional Additive Cross-Modal Fusion for Real-Time RGB-T Object Detection
authors: Ziwen Zhu, Xiaoou Song, Guangming Zhou, Mingmei Chen, Xiaobing Deng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Real-time RGB-T object detection faces significant challenges from illumination variations, background thermal clutter, and strict computational constraints. To tackle these, we propose CAC-Net, an efficient convolutional additive cross-modal fusion network that leverages complementary visible and thermal infrared modalities while maintaining linear computational complexity in the fusion pipeline. Our key contributions are threefold: (1) integrating Pinwheel-shaped convolution in the early dual-stream backbone to enhance discriminative representation of faint thermal cues and fine-grained RGB textures; (2) enabling global dependency modeling via a cross-modal additive attention fusion module with explicit spatial and channel alignment; (3) introducing a consensus-driven shared straight-through Top-K sparsification mechanism that adaptively suppresses modality-specific noise while remaining differentiable. Extensive experiments on challenging RGB-T benchmarks demonstrate that CAC-Net significantly outperforms current publicly available state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy and efficiency, establishing a new paradigm for robust, real-time, and resource-efficient multimodal perception. The code will be available at https://github.com/ZZWHLL/CAC-Net.

title: Design and Performance Assessment of a Virtualized IED for Digital Substations
authors: Alailton J. Alves Junior, Denis V. Coury, Ricardo A. S. Fernandes
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Digital substations have significantly enhanced power grid protection by replacing traditional copper wiring with fiber-optic communication and integrating IEC 61850-compliant Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs), resulting in greater efficiency, reliability, and interoperability. While these advancements provide improved interoperability, challenges such as high costs, complex networks, and limited upgrade-ability persist. To mitigate these issues, the virtualization of IEDs has emerged as a cost-effective solution, offering scalability, simplified maintenance, and reduced hardware costs by replacing traditional hardware-based IEDs with software-based counterparts. However, the performance and reliability of virtual IEDs (vIED) must be rigorously evaluated to ensure their robustness in real-time applications. This paper develops, implements, and evaluates a vIED designed to match the performance of its hardware-based counterparts. The vIED was deployed on a server using virtual machines, with its core logic implemented in low-level programming languages to ensure high-speed, deterministic behavior. The performance was evaluated using real-time simulations, focusing on the response times of the protection functions. The results demonstrated that the vIEDs achieved acceptable response times, validating their suitability for deployment in critical time-sensitive environments within digital substations.

title: Structured Time-Frequency Feature Driven Meta Learning for Fall Detection with mmWave Radar
authors: Kaiyu Chen, Shaoxi Wang, Yongxin Guo, Hao Zhang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Millimeter-wave radar has emerged as a preferred modality for privacy-preserving human activity recognition. However, the effectiveness of current deep learning approaches is often severely compromised by data scarcity and incomplete feature representation in complex, real-world environments. To address these challenges, this paper presents a novel meta-learning framework tailored for few-shot radar recognition. Structured time-frequency feature strategy is proposed, representing the systematic extraction of multi-spectral joint temporal-spectral features from raw IQ data. Unlike traditional passive mapping, this strategy utilizes structured temporal alignment to construct distinctive two-dimensional maps that actively compensate for information loss. Synergizing with this, we propose the SAMSCNN architecture, which departs from conventional layer stacking to employ a customized hierarchical multi-scale topology. This architecture innovatively integrates spatially-aware self-attention with meta-learning optimization, enabling the network to rapidly adapt to new tasks while preserving fine-grained signal structures. Experimental results demonstrate state-of-the-art performance, achieving 99.21% accuracy specifically in fall detection. Notably, under severe few-shot conditions with only five training samples, the method sustains an average accuracy of 90.24% across six distinct human activities, significantly outperforming existing methods. Extensive comparative analyses further confirm the framework’s superior robustness across varying scene configurations and environmental interference.

title: Multi-Tier Hierarchical Intelligence for Joint RIS–NOMA Optimization in O-RAN-Enabled 6G Networks
authors: Ahmed Abdelreheem, Aya Allah A. Elserwe, Ahmed H. Zahran, Mohamed Abdel-Nasser
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The evolution toward sixth-generation (6G) networks demands architectures that integrate intelligence, flexibility, and spectral efficiency beyond the limits of conventional fifth-generation (5G) systems. This paper presents a multi-tier hierarchical intelligence framework for joint optimization of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) and Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) within an Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) architecture. The proposed framework takes advantage of the intrinsic control hierarchy—comprising of O-RAN: encompassing Service Management and Orchestration (SMO), RAN Intelligent Controller (RIC in Non-Real time (non-RT) and Near-Real-Time (near-RT) RIC, to decompose the joint optimization of RIS–NOMA into distributed subproblems on multiple timescales. This approach ensures scalable, interference-resilient and fairness-aware operation under realistic control, latency, and quantization constraints. The analytical results establish polynomial-time convergence under strong convexity assumptions, with approximation bounds expressed in terms of explicitly characterized error terms (decomposition, delay, quantization, and CSI errors). Extensive simulations confirm that the proposed framework significantly improves spectral efficiency, fairness, and outage probability compared to conventional centralized approaches. By unifying O-RAN’s architectural intelligence with RIS adaptability and NOMA’s spectral efficiency, this work provides a practical and theoretically grounded foundation for intelligent software-defined 6G heterogeneous networks (HetNets).

title: Reduced graphene oxide-based electrochemical sensors to detect Rhodamine B molecules: A Review
authors: Xikun Lu, Wenqiang Guan, Monalisa Ghosh, Aniket Chakraborthy, Granthana Maulik, Md. Eshrat E Alahi, Anindya Nag
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: The paper presents a substantial review of the use of reduced graphene oxide (rGO)-based electrochemical sensors. rGO, a physicochemical derivative of graphene, is a pivotal carbon nanostructure for multimodal sensing applications. The high electrical conductivity and excellent charge-transfer properties enabled rGO to be functionalized and integrated to form efficient electrochemical sensors. It has been integrated with other nanomaterials to increase the overall selectivity and specificity of the resultant nanocomposites towards the target molecule. Rhodamine B is one of the toxic dyes used as a colouring agent in various food products. Detecting this chemical in food products is necessary because its accumulation has detrimental effects on human health. The paper highlights the capability of rGO-based composite sensors to detect Rhodamine B molecules. The research examples presented here have been divided into two groups based on the conjugation of rGO with metal oxides and non-metal oxides. It also shows the ability of the graphene-based sensors to remove Rhodamine B from contaminated real-time samples. Finally, the paper highlights the limitations of current sensors, potential remedies, and future trajectories for these sensors.

title: 3D-Printed Folded Transmitarray with Gain-Filtering Response and Low Cross-Polarization_supp1-3692666.pdf
authors: Sen Yan
publish: N/A
abstract: An air-filled cavity filter-based folded transmitarray antenna (FTA) is proposed in this communication. The transmitarray (TA) of FTA is realized by rectangular waveguide filters. The varied size of the coupling inductive iris between the resonators enables the filters to realize nearly the same filtering response and the 3-bit phase delay. The bottom plate of the FTA is periodically arranged with 45°-tilted parallel plate waveguides (PPWs), which can out-of-phase reflect the ±45° polarized incident waves, then achieve the polarization conversion. The main contribution of the proposed design is the utilization of transmission and reflection characteristics of rectangular waveguide-based TAs. The concept enables polarization selection in the frequency domain, achieving extremely high reflection coefficients. Moreover, the cutoff characteristic of the TA can effectively suppress the transmission of cross-polarized electromagnetic waves, resulting in low cross-polarization level. To verify the feasibility of the design, an FTA prototype is fabricated using 3D printing technology at the center frequency of 26.0 GHz. The measured results show that the antenna can realize the gain filtering beam with the rejection level around -20.0 dB, and the maximum aperture efficiency of 50.3%. In addition, several defocus sources are used to excite the FTA, demonstrating its multi-beam capability.

title: Hyperchaotic Privacy-Preserving Cooperative Adaptive Control With Application to Multimotor Systems
authors: Hui Ma, Qi Zhou, Hongru Ren, Hongjing Liang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: This article investigates the privacy-preserving cooperative adaptive control problem for nonlinear multiagent systems with unknown disturbances. First, by leveraging the hyperchaotic phenomenon of the Lorenz–Stenflo system, a masking function is proposed, which endows it with uncertainty and unpredictability and thus greatly improves the encryption performance. Meanwhile, a time-assist function is designed to achieve privacy protection within a preassigned period, which enhances the flexibility of the mechanism. In addition, according to the gradient descent method, a dynamically self-adjustable gain parameter is designed in the disturbance observer. The utilization of this parameter enables the proposed observer not only to handle unknown disturbances, but also to better improve the performance of the system. Afterward, the Lyapunov stability theorem is utilized to prove that all signals of the closed-loop system are semiglobally uniformly ultimately bounded. Finally, experimental results validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, which successfully accomplishes speed synchronization across multiple permanent magnet synchronous motors, thereby ensuring secure data transmission.

title: Transformer-Based Modeling of Global Microwave Land Surface Emissivity Using GPM
authors: Fraser King, Sarah Ringerud
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Global land surface microwave emissivity estimates are critical for data assimilation, numerical weather prediction, and quantitative precipitation estimation, yet explicit calculations at multiple frequencies remains problematic. We introduce a machine learning model that converts 10.65 GHz H-polarization emissivity retrieved under clear-sky conditions into emissivity for the 12 higher-frequency channels of the GPM Microwave Imager (GMI). The model is a feature-tokenizer transformer that ingests ancillary surface variables such as skin temperature, leaf area index, soil texture, and soil moisture. Treating each variable as an individual token lets the transformer direct attention between features and learn nonlinear, spatiotemporally consistent relationships across five years of data [2015-2019]. The network reproduces channel-specific emissivities with R2 > 0.80 and mean absolute percentage error < 1%, and it simulates top-of-atmosphere brightness temperatures with R2 = 0.77 − 0.95 and mean biases < 4 K for every channel up to 89 GHz when compared to collocated GMI observations. Using independent TELSEM2 10.65 GHz H-pol inputs produces similar skill, suggesting that the learned mapping is robust to the input source, and captures transferable physical structures. The model is lightweight and can be embedded in satellite retrieval systems, reanalysis pipelines, and Earth-system models to accelerate microwave forward modeling and improve global precipitation monitoring.

title: Toward Automatic GI Tract Intervention With a Novel Shape-Aware Robotic Endoscope_supp1-3684235.mp4
authors: Siyang Zuo
publish: N/A
abstract: Endoscopic procedures often subject endoscopists to heavy workloads and significant radiation exposure, while inappropriate maneuvers can potentially cause patient discomfort or clinical complications. This work presents a novel robotic endoscope system that integrates shape perception and depth estimation for autonomous navigation in complex gastrointestinal (GI) environments. The system features a custom-designed endoscope integrated with the fiber Bragg gratings (FBGs) sensor for shape tracking and a monocular camera for visual input. The FBGs enable real-time endoscopic shape perception through precisely reconstructing the endoscopic pose during dynamic interventions. A unified control framework is proposed to enhance the system’s generality by integrating depth estimation and shape perception. The direction and velocity of endoscopic intervention are adaptively synchronized. Highly realistic GI phantoms and in vivo animal experiments demonstrate that the system successfully performs autonomous interventions in complex GI environments, with a bending angle error of 2.75$\boldsymbol{^{\circ}}$ and a mean pixel tracking error of 74.2 pixels. This work advances robotic endoscopes by enhancing perceptual accuracy and autonomy, demonstrating significant potential for clinical applications in endoscopic examinations and procedures.

title: DrlGoFPGA 2.0: FPGA Global Placement Based on Multi-Agent Graph Transformers and Nonlinear Placement Co-Optimization
authors: Kang Yang, Jianwang Zhai, Liuyu Xiang, Kang Zhao, Wei Li, Ming Lei, Zhaofeng He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: The speed and quality of global placement (GP) are crucial for FPGA performance. The existing nonlinear placers provide higher computational efficiency than heuristic algorithms, but their insufficient attention to the initialization of GP distributions limits rapid convergence towards high-quality global optimal solutions. To resolve this, we propose a GP framework, DrlGoFPGA 2.0, combining LUT, FF, DSP, and RAM (LFDR) instance placement generation based on multi-agent graph transformer network (MAGTN) and LFDR initial placement fine-tuning based on nonlinear placement. A MAGTN model for LFDR initial placement is designed, which utilizes distributed training to circumvent the issues of sub-optimal placement models and prolonged training times due to the non-uniform distribution of large-scale LFDR sites. A lightweight LFDR line-network relationships (LNR) graph supporting MAGTN co-decision is created by separating, renumbering, and deduplicating the LNR graph containing all instances, and inputting the LFDR LNR graph into MAGTN to obtain the connections between LFDRs. The experimental results on ISPD’2016/2017 benchmarks show that compared with the state-of-the-art (SOTA) nonlinear placer, DrlGoFPGA 2.0 can improve GP speed by 27.3% ∼ 82.1%, reduce half-perimeter wirelength by 0.6% ∼ 4.6%, and routed wirelength by 0.4% ∼ 2.8%.

title: RGS: A Unified Representation-Generation-Selection Framework for Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering_supp1-3689433.pdf
authors: Yuke Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Knowledge-based visual question answering (VQA) is a task that answers questions with additional knowledge beyond the image itself. Existing methods have either retrieved external knowledge bases to obtain explicit knowledge or utilized large language models (LLMs) to get implicit knowledge. However, it is a complicated pipeline to construct and retrieve these knowledge bases, which can introduce additional information unrelated to the question. In addition, LLM-based methods leverage image captions related to the question as contextual information to prompt LLMs, which suffer from insufficient contextual information and lack the key details necessary to answer the question. To address the issues, we propose a unified representation-generation-selection framework, named RGS, which first obtains multi-source captioning-fused image context representation based on multiple different question-aware image captioning models, then generates candidate answers via a two-stream in-context learning method combined with the image context representation, finally selects the best answer from the candidate answers by means of instruction tuning. To balance the trade-off between complexity and performance, we only additionally finetune a question-aware image captioning model named InstructCap and an optimal answer reasoning model named InstructJudge. Compared to most methods that rely on LLMs with over 100 billion parameters (e.g., GPT-3 - 175B), our approach leverages the 7B-parameter LLM Mistral-7B as implicit knowledge, achieving state-of-the-art performance on multiple knowledge-based VQA benchmarks and significantly outperforming several previous state-of-the-art methods.

title: MFS-VQ-VAE: Synthesizing 7T-like MR images from 1.5T MR images with Prompt Attention in VQ-VAE
authors: XingHe Xie, LuYi Han, Yue Sun, MengZhu Liu, MingFeng Jiang, HingChiu Chang, Tao Tan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images exhibit significant quality differences at varying magnetic field strengths, such as 1.5T, 3T, and 7T. Higher magnetic field strengths generally provide better tissue contrast and richer structural details, thereby enhancing diagnostic accuracy. However, the high cost and limited accessibility of 7T MRI scanners restrict their clinical adoption, while 1.5T and 3T MRI systems are more commonly used in routine medical practice. To bridge the quality gap between low-field (1.5T/3T) and high-field (7T) MRI images, this study proposes a dynamic MRI synthesis framework that integrates a vector-quantized variational autoencoder (VQ-VAE) with a shared latent space to enable cross-field image synthesis. The proposed model extracts shared feature representations from MRI images acquired at different magnetic field strengths and, through a proposed prompt attention module combined with a field strength code, maps these features to the desired field strength, thereby achieving single-step synthesis across arbitrary field strengths. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing approaches across multiple key quantitative metrics, showing particularly strong performance in synthesis tasks between 1.5T and 3T, as well as between 3T and 7T. Furthermore, the framework enables high-quality one-step synthesis from 1.5T to 7T, effectively mitigating the cumulative errors commonly observed in multi-step synthesis methods.

title: Phase Compensator Placement and Design for Subsynchronous Oscillation Damping of Multi-loop Grid-Forming Converters
authors: Chenguang Yan, Qinzhi Liu, Ying Liu, Jun Deng, Hongxi Yang, Yichun Wang, Baohui Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Under stiff grid conditions, grid-forming (GFM) converters are prone to subsynchronous oscillations (SSOs). Phase compensators are widely used as effective means to address SSO issues. However, owing to the lack of precise design regarding the location and configuration, the performance of phase compensators within multi-loop GFM control remains underutilized. To this end, the performance of the phase compensator is quantitatively evaluated by the negative damping area in the impedance model. With a parametric analysis of this area, the SSO-critical control loops and governing parameters are pinpointed, thereby identifying four optimal embedding locations for compensators. To effectively demonstrate the robustness of SSO damping under varying grid conditions, the required configuration properties of the compensator for each location are proposed, and a feasible and general compensator form is provided. Furthermore, the proposed design proves effective in a model incorporating frequency coupling. The hardware-in-loop (HIL) experimental results validate the theoretical insights as well as the effectiveness and robustness of the presented damping strategy. The systematic design framework can be adapted to guide the phase compensator in diverse multi-loop GFM controllers for effective SSO damping.

title: Colored Traveling Salesman Problems: Models, Solutions, and Applications_supp1-3686623.pdf
authors: Jun Li
publish: N/A
abstract: A colored traveling salesman problem (CTSP) can subtly and thoroughly depict the homogeneity of salesmen and the assignment relationships between them and cities by using colors. It has been proven to be a generalization of existing traveling salesman problems (TSPs) and multiple TSPs (MTSPs). Nevertheless, its solutions and applications to various areas, e.g., logistic, manufacturing, and transporation, remain to be explored. To promote the understanding, research, and applications of CTSPs, we carry out the first survey on CTSPs by: 1) organizing the CTSP development timeline and re-formalizing the existing CTSPs as well as typical variants with different objectives and constraints in a unified way; 2) comparing CTSPs with existing TSPs, MTSPs, and vehicle routing problems (VRPs), and revealing their differences and connections; 3) introducing the main principles and procedures of the exact, heuristic, large-scale, learning-based, and parallel solutions of CTSPs; 4) reviewing the typical applications of CTSPs to scheduling a variety of multimachine mission systems; and 5) discussing the future research directions of CTSPs and their potential applications. This article is not only the first survey of CTSPs but can also serve as a reference for research on related sequencing problems such as TSPs, MTSPs, and VRPs.

title: Gain-scheduling Offline MPC for Air Path qLPV System Control in Spark Ignition Engine using Electric Supercharger
authors: Rosana C. B. Rego, Camila O. De Lima, Matheus H. R. Miranda, Jony J. Eckert, Andre V. Bueno, Grace Deaecto, Ludmila C. A. Silva
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Electric superchargers eliminate turbo lag in spark ignition (SI) engines by providing instantaneous boost pressure response independent of exhaust gas energy. However, coordinating the throttle valve and eBoost motor to track driver torque demands poses significant control challenges due to highly nonlinear air path dynamics, strict actuator constraints, and real-time execution requirements on resource-constrained automotive Electronic Control Units (ECUs). This paper presents a quasi-Linear Parameter-Varying (qLPV) modeling and gain-scheduled offline Model Predictive Control (MPC) with integral action for air path regulation in SI engines equipped with electric superchargers. A qLPV model is derived through exact factorization of nonlinear throttle flow, volumetric efficiency, and compressor torque terms into three scheduling parameters. The controller gains are computed offline via Linear Matrix Inequality (LMI)-based optimization and scheduled online through trilinear interpolation, enabling real-time implementation. Simulation results on standardized (WLTC, US06) and real-world driving cycles demonstrate superior tracking performance, constraint satisfaction, and computational efficiency compared to PID, Fuzzy, and nonlinear MPC controllers. The proposed strategy achieves fuel savings exceeding 33% relative to baseline PID control while maintaining execution times compatible with automotive sampling rates, demonstrating its viability for real-time embedded implementation on production ECUs.

title: Teachers’ Continuance Use of AIGC in Practice-Oriented Courses—Evidence from E-commerce Live Streaming Teaching
authors: Jingjing Feng, Junxi Luo, Lixuan Chang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Artificial intelligence–generated content (AIGC) is increasingly integrated into teaching practices, particularly in practice-oriented courses such as e-commerce live-streaming. However, limited research has examined teachers’ continuance intention to use AIGC in educational contexts. Drawing on the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), this study investigates the mechanisms underlying teachers’ continuance intention to use AIGC in e-commerce live-streaming courses. A research model is proposed to examine the effects of perceived usefulness, perceived ease of use, and perceived risk on continuance intention, with attitude toward using AIGC and satisfaction serving as sequential mediators. Survey data were collected from 200 teachers teaching e-commerce live-streaming–related courses in China and analyzed using PLS-SEM. Results show that perceived usefulness and perceived ease of use positively influence teachers’ attitudes toward AIGC, whereas perceived risk has a negative effect. In addition, attitude and satisfaction jointly mediate the relationship between teachers’ perceptions and continuance intention. These findings extend the application of TAM to AIGC-supported teaching contexts and offer practical insights for promoting the sustainable use of AIGC in practice-oriented higher education courses.

title: AR for Cultural Heritage: Insights from a Moroccan Educational Study
authors: Safaa Bendhiba, Houria Kelkoul, Fatima-Zohra El Aahed, Mehdi Zhar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: This article examines how the development of an augmented reality (AR) heritage platform, E-Kasbah, served as a catalyst for the creation of a new university program in heritage and visual arts technologies. Created by Moroccan students pursuing a bachelor’s degree in Audiovisual, Gaming, Design, and Interactive Multimedia, with the aim of documenting and visualizing Morocco’s traditional kasbahs, the project demonstrated technical mastery but also revealed significant gaps in the students’ training, particularly in heritage theory, research methodology, and interdisciplinary design practices. Using a mixed-method approach that included an analysis of the platform’s use, qualitative interviews with the development team, and institutional discussions, this study highlights the pedagogical and structural limitations of existing curricula. The results underscore the need for specialized academic frameworks that integrate immersive technologies into heritage studies. Based on these findings, the article outlines a new academic pathway and proposes an evolutionary model for implementing AR-based heritage education in higher education institutions.

title: Research on Multi-dimensional CIM Dynamic Modeling and Lightweight Fusion Algorithm for Facility Operation and Maintenance
authors: Xuedong Xu, Haibo Sun
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the challenges of massive multi-source heterogeneous data integration and low dynamic iteration efficiency in urban information modeling (CIM) facility operations, this study proposes a lightweight multi-modal fusion algorithm (SSG-LMF) based on spatial semantic graphs (SSG). The approach first formally defines a scenario-process-interaction (SPI) dynamic iteration model, then constructs a unified SSG to integrate BIM, GIS, and IoT data, and designs a lightweight update mechanism incorporating time-decay weights and change significance detection. Urban traffic simulation experiments demonstrate that SSG-LMF significantly outperforms traditional methods and cutting-edge semantic mapping approaches in event detection latency, state estimation accuracy, data throughput, inference time, and resource consumption, effectively supporting the closed-loop operation and maintenance of a digital twin.

title: Smart Shoe for Gaming: An Integrated Wearable Shoe for Motion-Based Interaction and Physical Activity Enhancement
authors: Satheesh Kumar, Sara Ann Mohan, Muhammed Faheem A K, Hiba S, Muhammed Sadiq K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital entertainment has significantly increased screen time, especially through video gaming. This shift often leads to less physical activity and sedentary lifestyles, negatively affecting both physical and mental health. Too much inactivity can contribute to serious issues like obesity, heart disease, diabetes, and stress. This is especially true for younger generations who depend heavily on digital platforms for fun. To tackle this issue, this project introduces the Smart Shoe. This device is designed to combine physical exercise with interactive gaming, turning passive gameplay into an active experience. The system merges wearable hardware with gaming interfaces to motivate movement while keeping users engaged and entertained. Motion-tracking tools like accelerometers, gyroscopes, microcontrollers, and power modules gather real-time movement data. They convert this data into gameplay input and track steps, distance, and estimated calories burned. By including immersive interaction through motion-based gaming or virtual reality, this solution connects boring exercise routines and enjoyable digital experiences. It promotes the idea of exergaming, where physical activity is part of the gameplay. The design focuses on being affordable, portable, and easy to use, making it suitable for students, gamers, and fitness lovers. Ultimately, the Smart Shoe shows how wearable technology can improve gaming experiences while encouraging healthier habits, lowering the risks of being sedentary, and supporting long-term wellness by merging fitness with entertainment. Index Terms: Smart Shoes, Wearable Technology, Motion Sensors, Gaming Interaction, Health Monitoring, Rehabilitation System.

title: A Boundary-Aware Attention Mechanism For Accurate Skin Lesion Segmentation And Classification Using ABR-Net
authors: J.Manoranjini, Rohini C
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Skin diseases can be effectively diagnosed and treated by making an early and accurate diagnosis. Nevertheless, traditional diagnostic techniques typically base their approach on manual dermoscopic examination, which can lead to subjective interpretation and unstable results. To overcome such drawbacks, this paper suggests a two-phase deep learning model that will improve the speed of lesion segmentation and classification. During the initial phase, the attention U-Net, which is aware of boundaries, is used to produce fine lesion masks. The segmentation network combines residual connectivity and attention to precisely reproduce the fine-grained lesion boundary and minimize artifact effects such as hair, noise, and changes in illumination. These generated masks are then added to the initial dermoscopic images to create lesion-oriented inputs highlighting the clinically important areas. The second stage involves using an Efficient Net based convolutional classifier to categorize various types of skin diseases on the segmented region of interest (ROI). Attention modules are also added to the classifier to enable it to learn fine texture and color features in the lesion. Experiments on the HAM10000 dataset show great performance, as it gives a Dice coefficient of 0.935 with segmentation and classification metrics such as 89.2 accuracy, F1-score of 0.872, and macro-AUC of 0.943, which are better than traditional single-stage architecture.

title: Model-Based Analysis and Evaluation of an Origami Encounter-type Wearable Device
authors: Monica Malvezzi, Lisheng Kuang, Claudio Pacchierotti, Francesco Chinello
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: This paper presents the analysis of an origami-inspired wearable haptic device designed for cutaneous interaction with the palm. The device employs a structured mechanism to render edges, tips, and surfaces by dynamically adjusting force and velocity parameters. A detailed evaluation of inverse kinematics and velocity-force relationships is conducted to evaluate device reachable configurations and performance in trajectory execution. The findings contribute to the development of advanced wearable haptic systems, improving tactile interaction and enhancing user experience in digital and physical applications.

title: High-Precision Positioning Control of Piezoelectric Inertial Driver Based on High-Voltage Sawtooth Wave and Segmented PID
authors: Jinqiang Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In this paper, a high-voltage nanoscale motion platform based on high-voltage sawtooth wave driving force and hierarchical PID control has been realized. A closed-loop driving-detection-control link is composed of a high-voltage power amplifier module(150VDC), a high-resolution grating ruler(with an accuracy of up to 2 nm), and a high-speed ARM processor. At the same time, a multi-level self-tuning PID parameter adjustment algorithm and a multi-level high-voltage sawtooth wave attenuation method are adopted, which can automatically identify the positioning process and adjust the control parameters to reduce the oscillation degree, significantly improving the positioning accuracy and stability. Experimental verification shows that this system can achieve precise positioning at 10 nm, and the stable error it measures is less than or equal to ± one nanometer. This level has reached the international advanced level, providing strong scientific and technological support for the development of high-end precision instruments in China.

title: BoxCall AI: An Intelligent Predictive Inventory Management System for Automotive Spare Parts Using XGBoost Regression and Advanced Time-Series Feature Engineering
authors: P. Vasuki, S Vishvavasanth, A Rahul Chandrasekar, S Dhurga, M Tharun Balaji, Y Sowmiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Automotive spare parts have a great deal of variability and are irregularly demanded. Therefore, usual statistical forecasting methods are not very suitable, so businesses often run out of stock, and also have many unneeded spare parts sitting in their warehouses. To help businesses by using artificial intelligence to create a full predictive inventory management system for spare parts, one that uses Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) regression as well as a fixed 17 engineered time-series features as input variables to produce accurate demand forecasts and classified binary (high/low) inventory risk classifications. The system has been developed using the IEEE Indian spare parts dataset, which included over $\mathbf{1 5, 0 0 0}$ product transactions from May 2023 to June 2024, and which was evaluated separately using a different set of product transactions covering the period of July 2024 through January 2025; therefore there is a temporal separation (in time) between the development and evaluation portions of the system. On the independent hold-out test set, BoxCall AI achieved an $R^{2}=$ 0.92, with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 2.15 units, a Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 3.42 units, and an accuracy of 89.3 percent in terms of high or low demand classification. BoxCall AI was found to have relatively superior prediction performance in comparative evaluations against Linear Regression, ARIMA and Random Forest, with a RMSE reduction of $58 \%, 42 \%$ and $30 \%$ for those systems respectively. BoxCall AI’s Flask-based micro-service deployment provides real-time decision support and planning assistance to procurement planners by enabling sub-15 millisecond inference latency, resulting in a projected 67 percent reduction in stockout frequency and a 42 percent reduction in overall aggregate inventory carrying costs for the business.

title: Teleoperated Robotic Mission for Sensor Calibrations in Hazardous Environments
authors: Eloise Matheson, Alejandro Diaz Rosales, Jorge Playan Garai, Carlos Veiga Almagro, Hugo Perier, Francesco Riccardi, Luca Rosario Buonocore, Mario Di Castro
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Robotic interventions in hazardous environments augment or replace dangerous work carried out by humans, and this is especially true in areas where radiation is present. These missions must be carefully designed, prepared, implemented, and analyzed in order to optimize human-robot collective performance and minimize the dangers posed to machines, operators, and support personnel. This paper describes the case study of over 200 hours of use of a redundant manipulator mounted on a movable robot in operation inside the Large Hadron Collider at CERN to validate beam loss monitor sensors using a radioactive source.

title: Quantum-Ready Education: Preparing Students for Post-Quantum Computing Societies
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The rapid emergence of quantum computing presents unprecedented opportunities and disruptions across cybersecurity, optimisation, artificial intelligence and scientific simulation. As post-quantum technologies transition from experimental research to real-world deployment, education systems must urgently prepare learners with the conceptual foundations, technical competencies and ethical awareness required for a quantum-enabled society. However, most current STEM and computing curricula remain anchored in classical computational models, lacking structured pathways for quantum literacy, quantum-safe cryptography, and future-oriented workforce readiness. This paper proposes a Quantum-Ready Education Framework (QREF) to guide the design of curricula, pedagogical strategies and assessment models that equip students for post-quantum computing environments. Integrating quantum information theory, post-quantum cryptography (PQC), quantum algorithms, and hybrid quantum-classical programming, the framework emphasises competency-driven learning, multi-modal simulation environments, and industry-aligned skill progression. A mixed-methods evaluation across pilot institutions demonstrates measurable gains in conceptual understanding, algorithmic reasoning and PQC awareness, while highlighting challenges in instructor preparedness and resource-intensive simulation workloads. The findings offer scalable pathways for national education systems seeking to build resilient, future-proof talent pipelines capable of thriving in post-quantum computing societies.

title: Exploration of Optimizing Graduate Curriculum System Based on the Concept of Personalized Learning Using Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm
authors: Tingyu Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study aims to address the inadequacy of traditional postgraduate curriculum systems in meeting personalized learning needs. A multi-objective optimization model was constructed, taking individual demand adaptability, curriculum relevance, and resource utilization as core objectives, with constraints on total credits and curriculum type ratio. An improved Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm was proposed, incorporating dynamic inertia weight and adaptive learning factors, and compared with basic PSO and Genetic Algorithm (GA) via MATLAB simulations. Results show the improved PSO outperforms the other two algorithms in convergence speed 40% faster than basic PSO and accuracy 10.0% higher than GA, with more stable performance. The optimized curriculum system significantly improves key indicators: 18.3% in demand adaptability, 42.1% in curriculum relevance, and 18.1% in resource utilization, while satisfying academic constraints. This model and algorithm provide a scientific basis for personalized postgraduate training, offering practical value for optimizing higher education curriculum systems.

title: Generative AI-Driven Multi-Vector Phishing, Vishing, and Smishing Emulation with Adaptive Defense Training Framework
authors: S. Ramamoorthi, C. Venkata Jeshwanth, P. Lakshmi Chaitanya Sai, D. Pavan Kumar, M. Mohamed Sithik, S. Priya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Phishing, smishing, and vishing attacks continue to be among the most significant threats to organizational cybersecurity, primarily exploiting human behavior rather than technical vulnerabilities. With the rapid advancement of generative artifi- cial intelligence, attackers can now produce highly realistic and scalable social engineering content, making traditional security awareness training increasingly insufficient. This paper proposes a Generative AI-Driven Multi-Vector Phishing Emulation and Adaptive Defense Training Framework designed to simulate realistic social engineering attacks across email, SMS, and voice communication channels. The framework integrates large lan- guage models for dynamic attack content generation, multilingual scenario customization, and context-aware threat simulation. The system incorporates a closed-loop training model that monitors user interactions and provides adaptive feedback through an AI- driven security coaching mechanism. Experimental evaluation using simulated enterprise scenarios demonstrates significant improvements in user defensive behavior, including increased detection accuracy, reduced click-through rates, and faster response times to suspicious communications. The results indicate that realistic attack simulation combined with adaptive feedback can significantly enhance user awareness and resilience against modern AI-assisted social engineering threats. The proposed framework provides a scalable and cost-effective approach to continuous human-centered cybersecurity training using open- source technologies.

title: Federated Learning as REINFORCE Learning
authors: Ha Son Tran, Thi Huong Nguyen, Nam Dung Tran, Dinh Song An Nguyen
publish: Proceedings of the Workshop on Privacy in Large Language Models (LLM) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) 2026
abstract: None

title: Eagle-Eye Based Pain and Myocardial Infarction Monitoring and Control System Using Hierarchical Neuro-Physiological Intelligence
authors: K Mohammed Nadeem, U Deepak, K Raghul, S Srihari, W. Nancy, T. Sripriya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: The resolution limits of contact-enabled monitoring tools, combined with delicate details in physiology, impede early-stage detection of rising acute pain and myocardial infarction. The anatomical, vascular, and morphological consistency principles are maintained using graph-constrained spectral attention modules for non-contact remote data input that captures features of more complex cardiological diagnostics, while a Vision Transformer-Convolutional model combination enables efficient modeling of longer-range dependencies in macroscopic facial expressions for non-contact micro-expressions of pain and heart attack exclusively associated with ischemia transitions, which are captured using causal dilated temporal convolution modules that eliminate recurring instabilities associated with continuous monitoring. The proposed anatomically and vascularly consistent noncontact cardiac risk assessment approach based on a Vision Transformer-Convolutional encoder combination enables calculation of cardiac risk indicators by combining distant photoplethysmography, face anatomy, and heart temperature data based on multi-modality feature representations, enabling early-stage controlled control activation and infarct prediction in a robust, continuous, real-time monitoring framework that, following simulation testing, outperforms other models to ensure a high level of sensitivity, specificity, and accuracy at over 98%.

title: Ageing Monitoring for Commercial Microcontrollers Based on Timing Windows
authors: Leandro Lanzieri, Jiri Kral, Goerschwin Fey, Holger Schlarb, Thomas C. Schmidt
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Microcontrollers are increasingly present in embedded deployments and dependable systems, for which malfunctions due to hardware ageing can have severe impact. The lack of deployable techniques for ageing monitoring on these devices has spread the application of guard bands to prevent timing errors due to degradation. Applying this static technique can limit performance and lead to sudden failures as devices age. In this paper, we follow a software-based self-testing approach to design monitoring of hardware degradation for microcontrollers. Deployable in the field, our technique leverages timing windows of variable lengths to determine the maximum operational frequency of the devices. We empirically validate the method on real hardware and find that it consistently detects temperatureinduced degradations in maximum operating frequency of up to 13.79% across devices for 60°C temperature increase.

title: The Application of the Improved AES Algorithm in Information Security of Embedded Systems
authors: Xiaoyan Gao, Zhiling Tang, Xingzhuo Meng, Jianfa Ma, Jiayin Liang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This paper proposes an improved dynamic AES encryption algorithm to enhance security in embedded systems. The fixed S-box in the original AES algorithm exhibits deterministic characteristics, which can be exploited by attackers for feature analysis, thereby weakening the security of data encryption. The proposed algorithm adopts an S-box with 7-bit input and 7-bit output, and introduces a dual-key mechanism: the master key is used to generate round keys, while the auxiliary key is employed to construct a dynamic S-box. The S-box is generated based on the SHA-256 hash function and the Fisher–Yates shuffle algorithm, with dynamically varying reserved bits incorporated into the construction process. By modifying the auxiliary key, the S-box changes accordingly, enabling key-dependent dynamic generation.

title: A Multi-Fusion Multi Staged Transfer Learning in Classification of Lung Disease Images with Applied CLAHE Algorithm
authors: Benchie L. Maribao
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This study presents a Multi-Fusion Multi-Staged Transfer Learning framework integrated with Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) for the classification of lung disease using chest X-ray images. A total of 4,862 images obtained from Kaggle were utilized and divided into training (3,403), validation (729), and testing (730) subsets. CLAHE preprocessing was applied to enhance image contrast and improve feature visibility prior to model training. The proposed framework combines MobileNetV2 and EfficientNetB4 through feature fusion and employs a three-stage fine-tuning strategy to progressively adapt the model to domain-specific features. Experimental results show that the proposed model achieved a test accuracy of 93.42% with a corresponding loss of 0.1694. Class-level evaluation indicates a precision of 0.9909 for tuberculosis detection and a recall of 0.9983 for normal classification, demonstrating strong reliability and sensitivity. The integration of CLAHE, feature fusion, and multi-stage training significantly improved model performance and convergence stability. The findings suggest that the proposed framework is effective for lung disease classification and has potential applications in computer-aided diagnostic systems, particularly in supporting rapid and accurate screening. The novelty of this work lies in the unified combination of image enhancement, dual-backbone fusion, and progressive transfer learning within a single framework.

title: Quantum-Enhanced AI Framework for Classification of Encryption Techniques in Medical Image ROIs for Secure Transmission
authors: Anindita Das Badhan, Diganth Vijayan R, K Ashwini
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Secure transmission of encrypted medical images demands trustworthy identification of the used encryption method, especially in settings where the availability of metadata is uncertain or unreliable. In this paper, a quantum-enhanced AI framework for the classification of encryption methods used in medical image Regions of Interest (ROIs) is introduced. After the extraction of the ROI and chaotic map-based encryption, a hybrid quantum-classical learning strategy is used to classify the encryption method without depending on any metadata. The proposed quantum AI framework combines variational quantum circuits as efficient latent feature encoders in CNN architectures. The high-dimensional CNN features are then compressed into low-dimensional latent spaces before the quantum embedding process, enabling the classification task to be conducted on the highly compressed feature representation. Quantum-enhanced ResNet18, VGG11, and EfficientNet-B0 models were trained and tested on encrypted ROIs extracted from dermoscopic medical images. The experimental results show that the proposed framework can successfully distinguish between different encryption methods, confirming the feasibility of quantum-assisted cipher classification for secure transmission of medical images. This work shows the potential of quantum-enhanced feature representation in privacy-preserving healthcare applications and automatic decryption routing in medical systems.

title: Research on Railway Track Foreign Object Detection of Lightweight Model Based on YOLOv11 with Fusion Attention
authors: Yafan Shi, Tao Hou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To improve detection accuracy and speed for railway foreign object intrusion under complex environments, this paper proposes SC-YOLO, a lightweight detection algorithm based on YOLOv11 with fusion attention. The model integrates a ShuffleNet V2 backbone, SC-C2PSA attention, a multi-scale fusion upsampling operator (EUCB), and C3k2-OD convolution. Experiments on the RIDD dataset show that SC-YOLO improves mAP by 5.5%, reduces parameters by 0.3 M, decreases computation by 2.4 GFLOPs, and achieves 428 FPS, meeting real-time and accuracy requirements in complex railway scenes. This study supports intelligent manufacturing and automation by enabling efficient and real-time visual inspection in complex industrial environments.

title: A Study of Enhancing C Programming Education through AIGC-Integrated Hybrid Learning
authors: Zujian Wu, Huiqin Du
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive analysis of the learning and teaching landscape of computer programming language courses, using "C Programming" as a representative case. By identifying prevalent pedagogical bottlenecks, we align the curriculum with emerging digital education paradigms and propose a hybrid, full-stack human-AI fusion reform anchored in Artificial Intelligence Generated Content. Grounded in an open-compatible instructional philosophy and leveraging modern educational technology platforms, this work seamlessly integrates online and offline resources to deliver end-to-end curricular transformation, offering a scalable solution to critical challenges in higher education teaching.

title: A Review of Control Interfaces for Telepresence Robots: Trends, Challenges, and Opportunities
authors: Ghazal Zand, Ahmed Sabbir Arif
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: We present a comprehensive review of control interfaces for telepresence robots. First, we categorize remotely operated robots by their primary functions to highlight how control needs vary across robot types. Then, focusing specifically on telepresence robots, we analyze trends in input modalities, along with the associated challenges and limitations. We identify key gaps in the literature, including the limited exploration of multimodal control and adaptive systems for dynamic environments. Finally, we outline key considerations for designing effective control interfaces for telepresence robots operating in human environments, which are largely overlooked in existing work. We conclude with recommendations for future research, including hybrid control designs, intelligent assistance, and more inclusive, context-aware control systems.

title: Agentic Fuzzy Control: A Dual-Loop Framework for Self Adaptive IoT Systems
authors: Madhavv Arul, Muhammad Farhaan M, Joe Marian A, V A Kandappan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Today most Internet of Things (IoT) deployments operate as distributed cyber-physical systems where sensing, decision-making, and actuation occur continuously in dynamic environments. This paper presents an Agentic dual-loop fuzzy control architecture for self-adapting IoT systems. The proposed approach separates real-time fuzzy control from a supervisory reasoning layer that continuously generates refinements to control objectives under classical stability constraints. Sensor data are processed using window-based aggregation to improve the robustness and enable adaptive decision-making. The supervisory layer synthesizes and evaluates multiple fuzzy controllers through real-world like simulations according to generated control objectives and ranks them based on traditional stability constraints prior to secure over-the-air deployments. Experimental validation on water tank regulation, HVAC temperature control, and vehicle speed control systems demonstrate consistent improvements in control performance and efficiency. On average the proposed controller achieves 8% lower overshoot and approximately 78% lower energy consumption when compared to static rule-based and fuzzy-only controllers, while maintaining comparable accuracy. The results indicate that agentic supervision can enhance adaptability in IoT control systems while also preserving stability and safety.

title: Deep-learning Architecture for Malaria Detection from Blood Cell Images: An Analysis
authors: C Neal Stephen Reon, Ali Hussein Ali Abu Zaid, K. Vijayakumar, K Kanishka, Sorabh Lakhanpal, P. Deepthi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Recently, the disease occurrence rate in human community is gradually rising globally and early detection and treatment is crucial. Malaria is an infectious disease caused by plasmodium and needs treatment with appropriate antimalarial drugs. The diagnosis of malaria needs clinical examination using blood samples and blood-cell (BC) examination is necessary.. Malaria can only be analyzed through a comprehensive study of the BC, and to automate this process, a deep-learning (DL) architecture is proposed in this research. Necessary phases of this work includes, image collection from the data repository, implementing DL-models for extracting the features, executing softmax-based classification, identification of best features and generating fused-features (FF) using best two-model features, and implementing the classification with 3-fold cross validation to confirm the merit of the developed system. The experimental outcome confirms that the FF-based approach help to provide $\gt98 \%$ accuracy.

title: A Framework on Automatic Speech Recognition Systems Using Natural Language Processing
authors: P. Nagaraj, P. S. Uma, S. Ummugulthum Natchiar, R. Pandi Prabakaran, B. Muralidharan, V. Virumapandi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Since antiquity, speech has been, and will continue to be, the primary form of human communication. Over the past fifty years, research into automated speech recognition by computer systems (Robots) has consistently attracted significant attention for various reasons, from language families to technical curiosity about mechanisms, to the desire to optimise simple tasks through interpersonal contact. It is well recognized that the voice signal carries not only the message's linguistic meaning but also substantial information about the speaker's identity, including gender, age, social background, and place of origin. The inclination to automate routine tasks is not a recent development; it has existed for more than a decade. Alexander Graham Bell, his cousin Chichester Bell, and Charles Sumner Tainter created a recording device in 1881 that used a rotating wax-coated cylinder, allowing a stylus to create up-and-down grooves in response to the pressure of incoming sound.

title: Real-Time Signal Quality Monitoring and Interference Detection Using RTL-SDR: A Hybrid PSD Thresholding and PSO-Adaptive Line Enhancement Approach
authors: Aejaz Ahmad Ansari, Divyesh Shanmugarajah, Manik Shrivastav, Mutyala Jahnavi Sai, Sirisha Tadepalli
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Real-time spectrum monitoring is a critical requirement in modern wireless environments due to increasing spectral congestion, unintentional emissions, and interference originating from both communication systems and low-cost receivers. Software-Defined Radio (SDR) platforms, particularly RTL-SDR devices, provide an inexpensive and flexible solution for wideband signal monitoring, but they suffer from severe hardware-induced artifacts such as DC offsets, image frequencies, impulsive spectral spikes, IQ imbalance, and non-stationary noise. These impairments significantly degrade the reliability of conventional interference detection techniques based on fixed thresholding or gradient-based adaptive filters.This paper presents a real-time signal quality monitoring and interference detection framework using RTL-SDR that employs a hybrid two-stage denoising architecture. In the first stage, a smart percentile-based Power Spectral Density (PSD) thresholding method dynamically isolates statistically significant spectral components while suppressing broadband noise and impulsive distortions. Hardware-aware artifact mitigation is explicitly integrated to eliminate DC spikes and characteristic ±420 kHz image frequencies inherent to RTL-SDR receivers, which arise due to local oscillator (LO) leakage in the direct-conversion front-end. In the second stage, an Adaptive Line Enhancer optimized using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is applied to enhance correlated and structured signal components while minimizing residual noise. The PSO-based optimization strictly follows the canonical Kennedy and Eberhart update rules, enabling global convergence in non-linear and non-stationary noise environments where traditional LMS-based methods fail.

title: Graph Contrastive Learning for Function-Level Code Vulnerability Detection
authors: Kunfeng Du
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Source code vulnerability detection is critical for software security. While Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) capture code semantics, existing methods often fail to represent deep semantic and complex structural dependencies. We propose GCL-FVD, a novel function-level detection model based on Graph Contrastive Learning. GCL-FVD first constructs an extended Abstract Syntax Trees to capture program logic. It then employs a multi-branch contrastive framework with graph augmentation and a momentum update mechanism to learn robust representations across views. Finally, a joint optimization loss enhances feature discriminability. Experimental results on public datasets demonstrate that GCL-FVD significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in detecting complex vulnerabilities.

title: An IoT-Enabled Smart Electricity Meter Integrating Advanced Analytics and Voice-Based Mobile Interaction
authors: M. Latha, Mohammed Salman M, Mohammed Zaheer Hussain M
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Modern smart grid networks require precise energy forecasts and monitoring. Real-time analytics, predictive intelligence, and user-centered engagement methods are absent from traditional power meters. The design and development of an Internet of Things-enabled smart power meter combined with sophisticated machine learning analytics and a voice-activated mobile application is presented in this study. To assure measurement accuracy and safety, the suggested system uses an ESP32 microcontroller with a multi-sensor configuration that includes a non-invasive current transformer (SCT-013), an invasive current sensor (ACS712), and an isolated voltage sensor (ZMPT101B). REST APIs are used to send energy data over Wi-Fi to a cloud backend built on Firebase and MongoDB. Several prediction and anomaly detection models, such as Multivariate Linear Regression, Bagging, Stacking, Histogram Gradient Boosting, ARIMA, SARIMAX, and Isolation Forest. Real-time visualization, billing insights, and voice-based interaction using text-to-speech and speech-to-text technologies are all offered by a Flutter-based mobile application. The suggested ensemble-based models beat conventional statistical methods in forecasting accuracy while successfully identifying aberrant usage patterns, according to experimental evaluation using real-world smart meter information. The outcomes validate the efficiency, scalability, and feasibility of the suggested intelligent energy management system.

title: Optimizing Retinal Vessel Segmentation Performance Through a Systematic Comparison of Modified U-Net Variants
authors: Fatima Ali, Bilal Gillani, Waqas Ahmed, Muhammad Anwaar, Shaista Iqbal, Haseeb Iqbal
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy, a serious complication of diabetes, is the primary cause of vision loss in adults. Diagnosing it usually requires examining fundus images, but manually screening large numbers of retinal images isn’t feasible. This challenge highlights the need for automated diagnostic systems to improve efficiency and accessibility. This study introduces a novel UNETbased deep learning framework enhanced by state-of-the-art encoder architectures—EfficientNetB0, VGG19, DenseNet169, and ResNet152—to optimize segmentation performance. A hybrid dataset of FIVES and CHASE DB1 fundus images was utilized, with data augmentation applied to mitigate overfitting during training. The DenseNet169-UNET model achieved exceptional performance, with a segmentation accuracy of $98.6 \%$, a Dice Score of 0.94, and an IOU value of 0.95, demonstrating superior capability in detecting both macro- and microvascular structures compared to traditional methods. A detailed comparison highlighted the balance between processing speed and segmentation accuracy, showing that EfficientNetB0-UNET stands out as a fast, lightweight option for real-time use, providing a scalable tool to enhance diagnostic accuracy and support clinical decisionmaking in ophthalmology and cardiovascular care.
