title: IEEE CAS Society Meets Industry in Europe [CASS Industry Outreach]
authors: Francois Rivet, Patricia Desgreys, Sonal Shreya, Mustak Erhan Yalcin, Andreas Demosthenous, Laura Bégon-Lours, Clément Dufossée, Roberto La Rosa, Dick Carrillo Melgarejo, Maide Bucolo, Costas N Psychalinos, Tomasz Marciniak, Florence Podevin
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine
abstract: “IEEE CAS Society Meets Industry in Europe” coordinated series of more than ten technical and professional events across Europe, comprising over fifty invited talks, including more than thirty-five contributions from industry representatives spanning thirty industrial companies. The primary objective was to strengthen industry-academia partnerships while showcasing the central role of circuits and systems technologies in application domains such as wireless networks, biomedical systems, artificial intelligence, Internet-of-Things (IoT) integration, energy harvesting, neuromorphic and integrated systems, automotive, AgriFood electronics, and the role of AI in next-generation communication technologies. Through this large-scale and geographically distributed initiative, IEEE Circuits and Systems Society (CASS) aims to increase its visibility within the European industrial ecosystem and to facilitate deeper, sustained engagement between the Society, industry partners, and the academic community.

title: High-sensitivity PDMS film-based Fabry–Pérot interferometer for magnetic field sensing via magnetic fluid volume effect
authors: Youlian Wang, Yinping Miao, Xiaolan Li, Shouchuan Guo, Zhi Zhang, Jianquan Yao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: reflective fiber-optic magnetic field sensor based on the magnetic fluid (MF) magnetic volume effect is proposed. The sensor incorporates a Fabry-Pérot (F-P) interference structure formed by a single-mode fiber (SMF) and a polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) film. By detecting the volume changes of MF under varying magnetic field strengths, the sensor achieves highly sensitive magnetic field measurement. When exposed to an external magnetic field, the nanoparticles in the MF undergo oriented alignment, resulting in anisotropic volume changes. These changes are transmitted to the F-P air cavity via the PDMS film, enabling precise measurement of magnetic field intensity. Experimental results demonstrate that with a 48.4 μm-thick PDMS film, the sensor achieves axial and radial magnetic field sensitivities of -2.3 nm/mT and -2.73 nm/mT, respectively, within the 0-12 mT range. Furthermore, we observed that reducing the PDMS film thickness significantly enhances magnetic field sensitivity. After optimization to 46.92 μm, the sensor achieved a radial sensitivity of -9.40 nm/mT, a detection limit of 0.0021 mT, and excellent linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9988). The sensor combines the advantages of simple structure, high sensitivity and low fabrication cost, which provides a new solution for magnetic field measurement in the fields of power equipment monitoring and aerospace.

title: A 3-D Beam-Reconfigurable Simple Liquid Dielectric Resonant Antenna by Distilled Water with High Transparency
authors: Fengyu Tang, Tao Wang, Wenbo Liu, Nan Zhang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, a 3-D beam-reconfigurable simple liquid dielectric resonant antenna by distilled water with high transparency is proposed. The proposed antenna consists of a hexagonal star-shaped radiator and a liquid ground. Moreover, the entire liquid dielectric resonator antenna (LDRA) is transparent except for the feeding structure, its high optical transparency addresses the critical demand for visually unobtrusive integration in emerging 5G/6G and IoT applications. This design combines innovative cavity design with asymmetric dielectric loading to drive the field distribution to achieve beam reconfigurability. By utilizing the fluidity of the liquid and injecting water into different radiators, different 3-D beam can be generated, achieving five 3-D beam radiation directions of ppppppppppppppppppppp15dddddddddddddd, pppppppppppppppppppp30ddddddddddddddddddd, and 0ddddddddddddddddddddd. With a total size of 1.04ttttttttttttttttt1.04tttttttttttttttttt0.26 lllllllllllllllllll0 3 (llllllllllllllllllllllllll0 is the free space wavelength at the center frequency). By measuring, the proposed antenna can achieve five 3-D beam radiation directions in the frequency range of 2.22 to 2.64 GHz, includes 2.4 GHz band-a frequency band widely employed in Wi-Fi and smart home devices due to its superior penetration capability.

title: Spatial Channel Characterization Based on Scalar Frequency Response Measurements in UAV-Assisted Systems
authors: Zhixin Huang, Fengchun Zhang, Wei Fan
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-based channel sounding and antenna measurement have been attracting significant interest. However, acquiring complex channel frequency responses (CFRs) with accurate phase is often challenging due to strict UAV payload constraints and synchronization complexities. This letter proposes a novel array signal processing framework to retrieve the power-angle-delay profile (PADP) using only scalar frequency responses (SFRs) in line-of-sight (LOS) environments. By exploiting the strong LOS path typically present in air-to-ground links, which naturally favors the minimum-phase condition, the proposed method first reconstructs the phase response via the Hilbert transform. Furthermore, with a-priori knowledge of the LOS path distance, angle of arrival (AoA), and array geometry, a LOS path calibration and relative phase compensation procedure is introduced to restore the original complex CFRs of all array elements. Experimental validation in a controlled LOS scenario demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, offering a low-cost phaseless solution for constrained UAV-assisted measurement applications.

title: Modeling Coincident Peak Pricing in Electricity Markets: Challenges and Peak Shaving Effectiveness
authors: Qian Zhang, Sadie Zhao, Lucy Diao, Conleigh Byers, Yiling Chen, Derya Cansever, Le Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Markets, Policy and Regulation
abstract: Coincident Peak (CP) pricing is widely used in U.S. electricity markets to allocate capacity and transmission costs. This paper develops a behavioral game-theoretic framework for CP-driven load shifting that couples a nonlinear cost-allocation model with day-ahead (one-shot) and real-time (sequential-learning) decision processes. We examine two update rules, namely best-response dynamics (BRD) and fictitious-play dynamics (FPD), across continuous and finite action spaces to quantify how flexibility, action resolution, and participation influence peak outcomes. Using ERCOT peak-day data, we find that FPD reliably reduces system peaks, whereas BRD is more variable and can increase peaks under tight-capacity conditions. Finer action resolution improves peak shaving, while the number of participants is largely neutral when aggregate flexibility is fixed. Meanwhile, information-provider signals can induce herding, whereas response-aware or diverse signals improve peak shaving. These results highlight both the potential and limits of CP pricing: smoothing information and enabling granular control are as important as the amount of available flexibility. The framework offers practical guidance for system operators and consumers: For ISOs, broadcasting smoothed CP signals and setting minimum controllable-capacity thresholds enhance coordination. For consumers, greater flexibility and finer control resolution improve both cost savings and peak-shaving performance.

title: Solar Photovoltaic Glare: Impacts and Mitigation Across Aviation, Transportation, Agriculture, and the Built Environment
authors: Nisha Kaur, M.R. Mohamed, Cristina Efremov, Tahta Amrillah, Mohd Fairusham Ghazali, K. Sudhakar
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Solar photovoltaic (PV) systems are rapidly expanding to meet renewable energy demands, but specular reflections from panel surfaces can produce glint (momentary flashes) and glare (sustained bright light). These glare phenomena pose safety and operational challenges across multiple sectors. This paper reviews the mechanisms of PV glare, quantifies its impact on aviation (pilots and air traffic control), transportation (road and rail users), agriculture (crop and livestock effects), and the built environment (residential and commercial areas), and evaluates mitigation strategies. Drawing on regulatory guidelines, glare simulation tools, solar geometry analysis and case study evaluations, we demonstrate that while glare risks are real, they are predictable and manageable through site-specific design, anti-reflective coatings, orientation adjustments, and screening. The results indicate that PV glare can cause visual discomfort and potential safety concerns in aviation and road transportation, while in agricultural settings it may influence crop microclimates and worker visibility. In the urban environment, glare could impact nearby buildings. The importance of glare in the planning of PV installations with sensitive receptors is emphasized in the study. Recommendations for best practices, mitigation options and future research are provided.

title: Application and Research of Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content in Routing and Switching Instruction
authors: Jun Tao, Yuting Lin, Lei Yue, Hao Xu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content (AIGC) into technical education represents a transformative approach to addressing persistent challenges in networking instruction. This comprehensive study investigates the implementation of AIGC technologies in undergraduate Routing and Switching courses, examining their impact on learning outcomes, instructional efficiency, and student engagement. Through a mixed-methods research design involving 127 computer engineering students over two academic semesters, we demonstrate significant improvements in conceptual understanding, practical configuration skills, and problem-solving abilities. The research framework incorporates AIGC across five pedagogical dimensions: personalized learning paths, dynamic lab generation, intelligent assessment, conversational tutoring, and curriculum adaptation. Quantitative analysis reveals a 31.2% enhancement in practical examination scores, a 44% reduction in configuration errors, and a 2.3-fold increase in out-of-class engagement among intervention group participants. Qualitative data from instructor interviews and student feedback further substantiate the value of AIGC in creating adaptive, scalable learning environments. This paper contributes to the growing literature on AI-enhanced technical education by providing empirical evidence, implementation frameworks, and ethical considerations for effectively leveraging generative AI in networking instruction.

title: Influence of PCB Structural Layout on Solder Joint Stress of Components
authors: Cun Zhao, Xiaolei Xu, Ming Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To investigate the influence of PCB structural layout on the solder joint stress of components, three PCB layout examples were provided. The mechanical response of the PCB under vibration loading was simulated using finite element analysis, focusing on key parameters such as modal characteristics, deformation, and stress on chip solder balls. The simulation results indicate that uneven screw distribution in conventional layouts can lead to stress concentration in the chip solder balls. Compared to the conventional layout, the optimized PCB scheme designed with uniformly distributed screws and centrally arranged chips achieved a 36% increase in the first natural frequency, a 46.5% reduction in maximum deformation, an approximately 81.8% decrease in maximum stress on chip solder balls, and about an 87.3% reduction in the standard deviation of solder ball stress. This work demonstrates that optimizing PCB layout significantly reduces maximum stress on chip solder balls, improves force uniformity on the chips, and indirectly enhances the reliability and lifespan of PCB components.

title: HERCYCLE: AI and Doctor guided Health for Women dealing with PCOD/PCOS
authors: Dhanyaja P, Nisna Haneefa K, Gopika K M, Naveen N M, Rohith P S, Anjali B
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Polycystic Ovary Disorder (PCOD), also referred to as Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS), is a condition characterized by hormonal imbalance in women of reproductive age. It requires consistent monitoring and health management. The objective of this project is to create a health application using Android and AI technology to monitor PCOD-related health conditions. The application can be used to maintain a systematic interaction between patients and their healthcare professionals. The application can be used to book appointments and consult certified professionals. The application also includes a feature whereby users can receive prescriptions and reports electronically. A chatbot is also integrated into the application using AI technology to interact with the users and respond to their queries about PCOD and other related health tips. The application can also be used to provide diet plans using AI technology. Moreover, the application is also useful in rural healthcare by allowing health workers to record information on behalf of the patients who are not yet technology-savvy.

title: VRhythmSpace: Designing a Musical Game for Play, Learning, and Performance
authors: Jiawen Li, Zheyuan Jiang, Yifei Chen, Yuwei Ren, Jingji Li, Kai Yao, Xu Sun, Bin Jia, Ningning Xu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Virtual Reality (VR) is an emerging technique that alters the way users interact with music content. In this paper, we introduce VRhythmSpace, a VR musical game prototype that enables users to engage, learn, and play with music. We first conducted a preliminary investigation with seven volunteers in three different VR musical games to examine how users engage in these games and identify key differences in game features and preferable in specific contexts. Drawing from our findings, we formulate five user requirements, followed by design and user test with eight participants. The results show that users have high expectations for rich, diverse and immersive game experience with different motivations and purposes. This work provides valuable insights for researchers and practitioners, laying the foundation for more engaging and creative music experiences.

title: Drug–Drug Interaction Prediction Using an Optimized Attention-Based TabTransformer
authors: K.R. Preethy, Mrithika Gunasekaran, Nithila Senthil
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Drug-drug interactions (DDIs) are a major challenge to clinical practice as they are associated with adverse effects and negative therapeutic results. Accurate prediction of DDIs is essential for patient safety and drug development. In this research, an optimized attention-based TabTransformer model has been developed to perform multi-class DDI prediction using a large-scale dataset derived from DrugBank. The dataset comprises 26,379 clinically relevant drug pairs categorized into nineteen interaction classes. All samples are represented using molecular structures, SMILES representations, and interaction annotations. In order to process the high-dimensional molecular features and intricate pairwise relationships, Morgan fingerprints, physicochemical descriptors and Tanimoto similarity coefficients were used. Class balancing was addressed in two stages, which reduced the effect of class imbalance, leaving a balanced dataset of 1,000 samples per interaction class. The proposed model extends the conventional TabTransformer by incorporating a feature-wise attention gating mechanism, which highlights the important features and reduces the irrelevant. The model's hyperparameters were optimized hybrid Genetic Algorithm-Antlion Optimization (GA-ALO), which takes the advantages of global exploration from GA and local exploitation from ALO. Experimental research proves the effectiveness of the proposed optimized attention-based TabTransformer model. The TabTransformer with attention showed better results compared to baseline ANN (accuracy 0.7924) and TabTransformer models (accuracy 0.8339) (accuracy 0.8613). The attention-based TabTransformer was further optimized, resulting in a prediction accuracy of 0.9061, with a corresponding precision, recall and F1-score of 0.9028, 0.9061 and 0.9038, respectively. These findings suggest that multi-class DDI prediction is enhanced by attention mechanisms and hybrid optimization, resulting in more reliable and clinically meaningful drug safety assessment.

title: Wave Former: Wavelet-Transformer Hybrid Network for Rice Disease Detection and Localization with Dynamic Feature Recalibration
authors: Yong Ran, Hong Xie, Xiao Qin Chen
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Rice diseases pose a severe threat to global food security, necessitating accurate and efficient detection methods. Traditional approaches often face challenges under complex field conditions and for subtle disease patterns. This paper proposes Wave Former, a novel wavelet-Transformer hybrid network that integrates multi-scale wavelet decomposition with a deformable attention mechanism. The core innovation is the Wavelet Coefficient Recalibration Module (WCRM), which dynamically fuses low-frequency and high-frequency features to enhance the recognition of disease-specific patterns. Extensive experiments on the public Rice Leaf Disease Dataset (Sethy2020) demonstrate that Wave Former achieves competitive performance, with a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 81.5% and an Intersection over Union (IoU) of 73.9%. The WCRM module alone increases the mAP by 9.2% compared to the baseline. WaveFormer provides a robust and interpretable solution for rice disease detection, holding significant potential for practical applications in precision agriculture.

title: A 3D Radius Compensation Method for CMM Probes Based on Position Optimization
authors: Shugui Zhou, Xu Zhang, Jinfeng Wei, Jinbo Li, Xingzhao Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In the contact measurement of turbine blades using a coordinate measuring machine, obtaining the three-dimensional radius compensation vector for the probe is difficult, and measurement coordinate system misalignment further increases this difficulty. Therefore, this paper proposes a three-dimensional radius compensation method based on position optimization. To avoid the impact of coordinate system misalignment, the method first performs position optimization on the profile points formed by the probe center in a two-dimensional plane. Then, it takes minimizing the distance from the probe center to the model surface as the objective function to obtain the three-dimensional radius compensation vector. Simulation test results show that the compensation points obtained by the proposed method are closer to the theoretical contact points. The error in three-dimensional radius compensation without position optimization increases significantly, with an average error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11.5 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>.

title: Addressing Distribution Shifts in Strength Behavior Prediction of Coarse-Grained Soils Through Model-Agnostic Sample Reweighting
authors: Ying Zhang, Ziying An, Xiaoxin Gao, Yuangeng Zhu, Yao Lu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Coarse-grained soils are widely employed in geotechnical engineering due to their favorable compaction and load-bearing properties. However, accurately modeling their strength behavior, typically characterized by the deviatoric stress–axial strain (q−ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf>) relationship, remains challenging under real-world conditions, particularly when training and testing data exhibit distribution shifts. Existing deep learning approaches often rely on the independent and identically distributed (i.i.d.) assumption, limiting their robustness and generalization to out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios. To address this limitation, we propose Model-Agnostic Sample Reweighting (MASR), a novel framework that integrates sample reweighting and invariant learning via bilevel optimization. In this approach, the inner loop optimizes the model on weighted samples, while the outer loop adjusts these weights to enhance generalization under OOD conditions. This iterative mechanism enables the model to progressively identify invariant and causally relevant features while suppressing spurious correlations. Empirical evaluations on real-world coarse-grained soil datasets demonstrate that MASR outperforms baseline models in predictive accuracy under significant distribution shifts.

title: Day-Ahead Optimal Scheduling of Virtual Power Plants Considering Electric Vehicles and Carbon Trading Mechanisms
authors: Yuepeng Ma, Changyong Yin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The VPP day-ahead optimal scheduling model for wind power, photovoltaic power, thermal power and electric vehicle clusters under the carbon trading mechanism was proposed. Probabilistic modeling is carried out using Gaussian process regression, Weibull and Beta distribution to refine electric vehicles into residential, office and commercial clusters to capture heterogeneous behaviors. Verification on the IEEE 33 bus system shows that the optimized minimum node voltage has increased by 2.82% (reaching 0.9208 p.u.), the total grid loss has decreased by 31.35%, and the peak load has decreased by 24.14%.

title: Speech-Driven Smart Home Assistant for Elderly Health and Convenience
authors: M. Kiruthiga Devi, A. Joshi, Vidhushavarshini Sureshkumar, Parthasarathy Ramadass
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: The increased elderly population worldwide is increasing the demand for intelligent and easy to access home automation systems that will increase the safety, convenience and independence of elderly individuals. Traditional smart home systems rely on visual/tactile interfaces that have barriers for elderly populations with sensory issues and/or limited mobility. Furthermore, many commercial voice assistants such as Amazon Alexa and Google Assistant, have poor performance in recognizing voices from elderly people due to their lower-pitched, slower articulated, and noisy environment. In response to these shortcomings, the current work presents a speech-driven smart home assistant for elderly health and convenience, which uses adaptive noise suppression, Mel Frequency Cepstral Coefficient (MFCC) feature extraction, and optimized Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), specifically for elderly voice characteristics. The ASR is integrated with Natural Language Processing (NLP) for command interpretation, as well as a health advisory module that can interpret user-reported symptoms to provide contextaware medical assistance. Experimental evaluations show that the system had an average speech recognition accuracy of 92% for clear speech, 85% for low volume or unclear commands, an improved signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of $\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{2 0 ~ d B}$, and an average response latency of $\mathbf{1 . 2}$ seconds. These results demonstrate the potential of the system to offer a real-time, robust and user-friendly interface for elderly users in home environments. The proposed framework provides a contribution to the development of inclusive smart home technologies through the enhancement of speech adaptability, noise tolerance, and IoT integration.

title: An Uncertainty-Aware Attention-Based Residual U-Net for Cochineal Insect Segmentation
authors: Adel Benali, Rahma Fourati, Imen Jdey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The quantification of uncertainty in image segmentation tasks has become essential to improve the reliability and applicability of computer vision systems. Two main approaches emerge: the standard training of the model and the integration of uncertainty directly into the loss function during training. This comparative study evaluates these two methodologies on the ResAtt-SE-UNet architecture for scale segmentation (Dactylopius opuntiae), a destructive pest of the prickly pear cactus. The experiment uses our original Tunisian database, comprising 451 annotated cactus images. The results show that the approach integrating uncertainty during training significantly improves accuracy by 4.19% (from 69.73% to 72.66%), while maintaining comparable performance in terms of Dice (66.78% against 66.77%). In the agricultural context, this approach offers better calibration of predictions and enhanced explainability, thanks to XAI analysis. The measured epistemic uncertainty (0.0099, 0.0505) indicates a high confidence of the model, while the random uncertainty (0.0490) mainly reveals ambiguities inherent to agricultural data. These results highlight the importance of integrating uncertainty quantification from the training phase for applications in precision agriculture, particularly in automated pest detection, where the reliability of predictions is paramount.

title: Lightweight Hybrid DNN-GNN Architecture for Network Intrusion Detection with Adaptive Late Fusion
authors: Aryan Mondal, Abhradeep Basak, Aniket Ghosh, M Lakshmi
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: With the evolution of network architectures to- wards decentralized IoT and edge computing, the computational complexity involved in deep learning-based Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) has raised significant concerns about scalability. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are capable of representing relational graph structures in network traffic; yet, their reliance on the construction of graph structures makes them less practical in resource-limited settings. This paper proposes a new hybrid network intrusion detection system (NIDS) design that combines a Deep Neural Network (DNN) with a relational multi-layer perceptron (MLP) component, connected through an adaptive late fusion strategy that uses learnable fusion weights.Based on the latest findings that MLPs are capable of learning relational patterns from tabular data, the proposed methodology overcomes the need for the explicit construction of graphs while maintaining complementary representations of features. The empirical assessment on the CIC-IDS-2017 dataset shows an accuracy of 99.64% with around 80,000 trainable parameters. These findings indicate a favorable trade-off between accuracy and complexity, thus validating the appropriateness of the proposed architecture for real-time analysis in resource- constrained networks.

title: A Novel Hierarchical Topology and Rapid Equalization Strategy for Liquid Metal Batteries
authors: Peng Peng, Yunze Liu, Xuan Gong, Wanzhou Sun, Haomiao Li, Kangli Wang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: With the large-scale development of renewable energy, liquid metal batteries hold broad application prospects in grid-scale energy storage, but the inevitable inconsistency among individual cells in series battery packs severely limits their overall performance. To address the prevalent issue of slow equalization speed in traditional equalization technologies for liquid metal batteries, this paper proposes a novel hierarchical equalization topology and designs a corresponding rapid equalization strategy. This strategy employs a hierarchical approach for equalization current distribution, which effectively enhances the objectivity and rationality of current allocation within the topology. To verify the feasibility of the proposed topology and its control strategy, a twelve-cell simulation model was established in MATLAB and Simulink, utilizing the state of charge as the equalization variable to conduct experiments under static conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to traditional equalization strategies, the proposed hierarchical strategy increases the equalization speed by 43.75 %. Simultaneously, compared to traditional single-inductor topologies, the novel topology improves the equalization rate by 36.52 %. This study fully verifies the feasibility of the improved topology and its equalization strategy, providing significant reference value for subsequent practical design and engineering applications of liquid metal batteries.

title: Agile Design of Portable Health Physical Examination System Based on Scrum model
authors: Jiujiu Yu, Yong Xu, Yanxing Yu, Pengfei Feng, Jiaojiao Zhang, Mengyue Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The research is focused on the field of home-based health physical examination, this study is devoted to describing a feasible Agile development process for a portable health physical examination system by Scrum model. The client of this system is based on Android tablet and uses Android Studio Giraffe of Java for implementation, which provides significant and visualized physical examination functions of "Blood Pressure", "Body Temperature", "Blood Oxygen", etc. for residents at home in the first executable version. System UI is friendly and portable to operate, and the usability is on available level. Application result shows that effectiveness of individual health examination for self-service mode is achieved remarkable for elderly population living at home, and independent visual physical health data is generated to forming reliable and usable medical big database for local community to promote smart healthcare positively.

title: A Hybrid Approach Combining Quantum and Neuromorphic Computing for Live City Traffic Management
authors: Shramit Sarupya Swain, Ramesh Sekaran, Girisha G S, Jeeva S, K.Vengatesan, Bharathiraja Nagu
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Even when traffic shifts, signals change just quick enough to keep cars rolling through downtown lanes. Things change fast. Old methods lag behind. Slow reactions make them weak. Speed leaves tradition behind. Fast moves break old habits. Quick changes ignore past rules. Outdated approaches fail when pace increases dealing with constant shifts in schedule. QNF fixes it – fast putting pieces together while merging separate actions simultaneously. Movement stirs awake brain-like networks-switched on solely during engines hum to life, sitting still but awake. Voltage slips downward when they wait resting, it uses less than a single watt. Near silence, the chips run just above idle creations like Intel Loihi [1], running light on need. Today yet after that, how things link shifts. Still, picks must match the moment-nothing more a faint replay fades within slow-moving delays. Roads start to when things jam up, attention fades into a shift-guided suddenly by quantum ideas. A different path appears without warning. This change moves quietly beneath the surface faster light patterns found by Grover’s method [2] - just √N tries needed. Connecting neural-style detectors through quantum cores is a pathway where pulses reshape qubit states, every few seconds, chances shift close to crowded areas. During tests, keep falling nearly a third, ups stumbled as electricity needs slipped. Almost all of the area showed changes when tested on a city layout compared to usual methods. Around 320 watts get used by DRL systems when running on edge devices [3]. This framework is still just an idea for now - bringing it into real use would take more steps than expected. What stands in the way isn’t effort, but timing. Testing comes next, though only under strict conditions. Progress waits on results, not plans. So far, nothing has moved past early checks. So far, actual hardware based on quantum principles remains unrealized..

title: 5G Enabled Smart Healthcare System for Efficient Diagnostics of Patients in Emergency Care
authors: Sivaprasath S, Kishore R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The demand for fast and reliable healthcare monitoring has increased in recent years, especially in situations where continuous observation of patients is required. Traditional systems mostly depend on cloud processing, which often becomes slow and provides discomforts such as limited patient movement, and delays in receiving critical alerts. To overcome these issues, A 5G Enabled Smart Healthcare System along with Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) is Developed. In this work, IoT sensors such as SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, temperature, and ECG are used to collect real-time health data. The values are transmitted through a 5G Evaluation Kit to the gNodeB and then processed at the MEC server, which helps in achieving low latency. A ML model (XG Boost) is developed and implemented in the MEC server to predict the patient’s condition as safe or unsafe. A dashboard is also created to display the sensor data, model output, and alerts in real time. This system aims to provide a non-invasive, efficient, and fast healthcare monitoring solution that is suitable for hospitals as well as remote healthcare environments. The project is successfully implemented for some stages which starts with the collection of the IoT sensor data with the help of 5G Evaluation board and then these data were sent to the MEC server for further processing. Then an XG Boost model is deployed in the MEC to classify the patient condition as safe or unsafe. These results were perfectly correlated with the project’s objectives.

title: Enhancing Human-Robot Connection in Space Teleoperation through Shared Mental Model Creation
authors: Luisa Mayershofer, Nesrine Batti, Ajithkumar N. Manaparampil, Anne Köpken, Florian S. Lay, Xiaozhou Luo, Adrian S. Bauer, Rute Luz, Daniel Leidner, Peter Schmaus, Thomas Krüger, Neal Y. Lii
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Previous research in human-robot-interaction in teleoperation suggests that human-robot teams with a Shared Mental Model (SMM) are able to achieve better task performances and lower the mental workload of the user. To create a SMM, the robot must be able to estimate the task model of the user and incorporate knowledge about the user into its user model. Most existing concepts use probabilistic methods or symbolic reasoning frameworks, with predefined features for estimating the user’s teleoperation goal. They are therefore highly dependent on a-priori knowledge about the task, thus limiting their flexibility. Only very few approaches mention or explicitly take into account knowledge about the individual user and lack adaptability to human behavior and user preferences. Machine Learning (ML) based concepts, on the other hand, are more flexible to adapt their SMM during the user interaction, but require a lot of data points for precise estimation. In this paper, we propose a new approach to address these limitations by combining the advantages of symbolic and subsymbolic methods, and dividing our estimation of the user’s teleoperation goal into subgoals next to the overall task goal. Furthermore, by integrating the knowledge about the individual user and human decision-making into the prediction of the user’s task goal, we can improve SMM creation of human-robot teams for teleoperation. Our approach is applied to an example space exploration task in a planetary surface habitat by a telerobotic team. The SMM implementation is described and analyzed, followed by an outlook on future development and validation.

title: Comparative Analysis of Model Interpretability in Diabetes Prediction: A Shap-Based Study of Ensemble and Distance-Based Algorithms
authors: R Ramya, K. Kavitha
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus is a severe health concern in public health, and thus, the predictive models that can be used should be precise and interpretable to enable clinical use. This paper compares four ML models, which are Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, XGBoost, and K-Nearest Neighbors on the Pima Indians Diabetes data. Through SHAP (SHapley Additive explanations) we can assess the importance of clinical features across all models like Glucose, BMI, Insulin, and Age. It has been found that SVM was more accurate (76.62%), with KNN followed closely by (66.23 percent). In all models, Glucose was always the most significant predictor, but the prioritization of the secondary features differed: tree-based models displayed BMI, and distance-based models were more concerned with Insulin. The presented results emphasize the need to address the gaps by incorporating a set of explainable AI frameworks in the context of healthcare diagnostics so that predictive performance could be accompanied by clear reasoning. The research shows the applicability of SHAP in the validation of model behavior and leading clinicians to the reliable AI-based support of decisions.

title: Triple band Split Ring Resonator Integrated Yagi Uda Antenna for LTE, ISM/WLAN and S Band Application with Multiparameter AI based Performance Prediction
authors: Niketh K A, Suraj Panayanchira Sunilkumar, Abhinav K, Prasad Jones Christydass Samuel
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This work presents the design and AI assisted performance prediction of triple band Yagi Uda antenna. The antenna is integrated with split ring resonator for achieving multiband. The antenna operates for LTE, ISM/WLAN, and S-band applications, and is fabricated on a FR4 substrate. The antenna operates in the frequency range of 1.95–1.99 GHz, 2.34–2.61 GHz, and 2.94–3.00 GHz. The VSWR is maintained below 2, the gain is maintained above 1 dBi and directivity is above 4 dB in the operating region. The driven element, reflector and director is having a optimized dimension of 0.46λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">eff</inf>, 0.54λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">eff</inf>, and 0.08–0.45λ<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">eff</inf>, respectively. To reduce the computational cost, random forest regression model is used with 11 geometrical parameters to predict the S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf> and frequency range. Feature important analysis indicates the substrate width (SW) as the dominant influencing parameter for both frequency and impedance characteristics. The proposed methodology integrates the metamaterial antenna design with high accuracy AI based predication for efficient antenna optimization.

title: Dynamic permission extension control and lightweight data lineage tracking for enterprise R&D departments
authors: Jiale Fan, Minyong Yin, Dewen Kong, Yubing Duan, Guoxin Zhong, Yulong Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the rapid digitalization and cloud migration of enterprise R&D collaboration, the sharing and circulation of sensitive documents across departments and domains have become increasingly frequent. Traditional access control models, such as Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), often exhibit response latency and audit blind spots in real-time authorization and behavior tracing. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an event-driven dynamic authorization extension and lightweight data lineage tracking framework specifically designed for enterprise R&D environments. The proposed framework leverages the Drools rule engine to automate permission propagation and revocation, while integrating metadata-based watermarking and Neo4j graph structures to construct replica-level traceability chains. This approach enables bidirectional synchronization between access decisions and lineage updates, ensuring consistent coordination between access control and provenance management. Experimental results from a Nextcloud-based prototype using a workload derived from the CERT v4.2 dataset show that the proposed framework reduces average authorization latency from 8.5 ms (ABAC baseline) to 3.8 ms, improves lineage reconstruction accuracy from 81.2% (log-based baseline) to 95.7%, and decreases CPU utilization and storage overhead compared with metadata-lineage and blockchain-based provenance schemes, thereby validating its efficiency and practicality for secure R&D data management in small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

title: Revitalizing the Tourism Industry Through Smart Web Development Solutions
authors: Vishagan D, Shaik Afzal S, Kalingarani G
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The tourism sector increasingly depends on digital platforms to support travel planning and booking, yet many systems remain text-heavy and difficult to use for first-time travelers and users with limited digital literacy. This paper proposes a smart web-based tourism solution that emphasizes visual interaction and simplified decision-making. Users upload an image (landmark, beach, monument, city view), and the plat- form predicts the destination using deep learning. The prediction is then linked to real-time flight search, followed by preference- aware ranking that considers cost, duration, baggage charges, and discounts. To reduce friction, the interface is designed with icon-driven guidance, confidence indicators, and quick correction options. Experiments on landmark classes demonstrate that reliable image-based destination prediction improves booking success and reduces repeated search attempts. The results suggest that combining AI-assisted discovery with booking-oriented optimization can improve accessibility, efficiency, and inclusiveness in modern tourism platforms.

title: A Transformer-Enhanced Deep Neural Network for Real-Time Inverse Kinematics Solution of 6-DOF Industrial Manipulators
authors: Tianyu Wang, Hao Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Inverse kinematics (IK) is critical for real-time robotic control. This paper proposes a Transformer-CNN hybrid model combining CNN-based local feature extraction with Transformer self-attention to capture global dependencies. Trained on 50,000 samples from a 6-DOF IRB 120 robot dataset, the model achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}>0.99$</tex> for key joints and outperforms MLP, Random Forest, and Linear Regression. Gradient and SHAP analyses provide interpretable, joint-specific feature importance aligned with robot kinematics.

title: Wind-Aware Smooth Path Planning for UAVs in Urban Environments Based on Improved A* Algorithm
authors: Xing Liu, Juanjuan Gao, Shengxuan Shang, Mohan Qin, Zhongyi Lin, Qichen Tan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: The widespread application of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) in urban environments faces significant challenges due to complex micro-meteorological conditions, particularly wind fields caused by high-rise buildings. Traditional path planning algorithms often ignore these dynamic wind effects, leading to increased energy consumption and potential safety hazards. In this paper, we propose a wind-aware smooth path planning method for UAVs in complex urban environments. First, a high-fidelity 3D urban environment model is constructed, and Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations are performed using COMSOL to extract accurate wind field data. Second, an improved A* algorithm is proposed by integrating a novel heuristic function that accounts for wind resistance and wind direction penalties, enabling the UAV to exploit tailwinds and avoid strong headwinds. Finally, a B-spline curve smoothing technique is applied to the discrete waypoints to generate a kinematically feasible and collision-free trajectory. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively reduces wind-induced flight costs while ensuring path smoothness and safety.

title: Haptic-Enhanced XR Classrooms for Skill Transfer in STEM and TVET Education
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Haptic-enhanced extended reality (XR) classrooms are emerging as a transformative paradigm for advancing practice-based learning in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) and technical and vocational education and training (TVET). By integrating immersive visual–auditory simulations with force, tactile and kinesthetic feedback, haptic XR environments enable learners to directly experience tool–material interactions, fine-motor constraints and dynamic task conditions essential for high-stakes vocational competencies. Recent developments in haptic devices, physics-based rendering and XR learning analytics indicate strong potential for improving psychomotor accuracy, reducing training errors and accelerating time-to-mastery across welding, robotics programming, electronics assembly and other skill-intensive domains. Despite these advances, empirical findings on long-term retention and real-world transfer remain inconsistent, underscoring the need for structured frameworks and rigorous evaluation methodologies. This paper presents a technical framework for designing, implementing and assessing haptic-enhanced XR classrooms for future-oriented STEM and TVET education. It introduces a multi-layer architecture integrating XR engines, haptic interfaces and competency-aligned analytics, and proposes an experimental protocol comparing traditional, visual-only XR and haptic XR modalities. Illustrative results demonstrate the conditions under which haptic XR most effectively enhances performance, offering evidence-based guidelines for scalable, industry-aligned adoption.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Immersive Virtual Reality Simulation System for Classroom Questioning Training
authors: Yuanxi Yang, Yongbin Hu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study explores the application of Immersive Virtual Reality (IVR) in enhancing pre-service teachers' classroom questioning skills, comparing it with traditional video-based training. The Classroom Questioning VR System was developed using the Unity 3D engine and the Photon Fusion framework, employing a dual-client architecture consisting of a Teacher Client and a Pre-service Teacher Client. It aims to provide pre-service teachers with a realistic, interactive, and low-risk classroom simulation environment to cultivate their questioning abilities. The study adopted a quasi-experimental design, randomly assigning 56 pre-service teachers from a university in China to either an IVR group (n=27) or a Video group (n=29). The results highlight the advantages of IVR in facilitating embodied interactive learning, bridging the gap between theory and practice, and enhancing learning engagement. The study suggests that Virtual Reality (VR) technology should be integrated into teacher education to promote sustainable skill development in the digital age. Future research directions should focus on the convergence of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and VR technologies to achieve personalized tutoring.

title: Multi-granularity Synergy and Nuances Mining Network for Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification
authors: Yupeng Chen, Shuli Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Visible-Infrared Person Re-Identification (VI-ReID) aims to match pedestrian images captured by visible and infrared cameras. The core challenge is to bridge the severe modality discrepancy while learning discriminative identity representations. Existing approaches largely rely on horizontal partitioning to extract modality-consistent local cues, yet the exploration of detailed multi-granularity representations and their interactions remains limited. To address these limitations, we propose Multi-granularity Synergy and Nuances Mining Network (MSNMNet), which learns modality-consistent and discriminative features by jointly exploring fine-grained nuances and cross-granularity synergy. Specifically, the Fine-grained Nuances Mining (FNM) module is designed to capture the modality-consistent nuances by mining fine-grained specific patterns, while the Salient Feature Guidance (SFG) module strengthens global representations via multi-granularity synergy. Furthermore, Adaptive Cross-center Align (ACA) loss is designed to adaptively align features to cross-modal identity centers, thereby reducing modality gaps. Extensive experiments on SYSU-MM01 and RegDB demonstrate that MSNMNet achieves competitive performance against state-of-the-art methods.

title: Prediction of High Slope Deformation Using an ARIMA-RBF Model with Residual Correction
authors: Hua Xia, Chengming Zhang, Qingda Duan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This study aims to enhance the accuracy of deformation prediction for high slopes by proposing an ARIMA-RBF model based on residual correction. First, the ARIMA model is employed for preliminary modeling and prediction of high slope deformation time series data, effectively capturing the linear characteristics of the data. However, the ARIMA model struggles to handle nonlinear components in the data, resulting in certain prediction errors. To address this limitation, an RBF neural network is adopted to model and correct the prediction residuals of the ARIMA model. Verification through actual high slope engineering cases demonstrates that compared to the residual-corrected ARIMA-BP model, as well as standalone RBF neural networks, BP neural networks, and ARIMA models, the residual-corrected ARIMA-RBF model significantly enhances the accuracy of high slope deformation prediction. This provides more reliable support for high slope stability assessment and disaster early warning.

title: Intelligent Detection of Tunnel Surrounding Rock Fractures Based on Adaptive Preprocessing and Pre-trained YOLOv8 Model
authors: Ying Qu, Mingyu Yin, Haiyue Yu, Xinyu He
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address key challenges in coal mine tunnel fracture identification and reconstruction, including geological noise interference, difficulties in geometric quantification, and limited spatial network characterization, this study proposes an integrated framework from 2D intelligent image analysis to 3D structural uncertainty optimization. An adaptive preprocessing and YOLOv8-based crack segmentation model enables robust pixel-level fracture detection. An adaptive clustering and multi-objective Bayesian optimization framework with MCMC improves sinusoidal fracture quantification beyond single-point estimation, while multi-model competitive optimization and multi-strategy sampling enhance JRC roughness evaluation with scale-effect correction. A multi-borehole fracture network reconstruction method integrating multi-physical connectivity criteria and Gaussian process regression-based borehole layout optimization forms a closed-loop workflow. As coal mining is vital to China’s energy security, accurate fracture characterization is crucial for tunnel stability and the prevention of major hazards such as roof collapse and water inrush. Due to the limitation of on-site data collection (only 10 unlabeled borehole images), the effectiveness of the proposed method is verified through visual inspection and engineering experience validation.

title: Application and Analysis of Ant Colony Algorithm in Three-Coordinate Measurement
authors: Zhen Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM), as a core testing equipment in the field of precision manufacturing, directly affects the efficiency of product quality evaluation through the optimization of measurement paths and data processing accuracy. In response to the problems of path redundancy, error accumulation, and insufficient adaptation of dynamic constraints in traditional coordinate measurement, this paper proposes to apply Ant Colony Optimization (ACO) algorithm to the key links of the measurement process. By constructing a measurement path optimization model and a data error compensation model based on ant colony algorithm, the global optimal planning of measurement point paths and accurate correction of measurement data can be achieved. The experimental results show that compared with traditional greedy algorithms and genetic algorithms, the improved ant colony algorithm performs better in path length reduction rate, measurement efficiency improvement, and error control accuracy. After path optimization, the average measurement time is reduced by 23.7 %, and the data processing error is reduced by 18.3 %, providing a new technical solution for efficient and high-precision measurement of precision components.

title: Bridging the Academia-Industry Gap: A Competency-Based Pedagogical Framework for Computer Science Talent Cultivation
authors: Rui Zhou, Tianyi Wang, Jiayi Nie, Zhiye Wang, Hao Yan, Anping He, Wenbo Chen, Qingguo Zhou
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: A significant competency gap persists between traditional computer science education and the rapidly evolving demands of the tech industry. This paper introduces a competency-based pedagogical framework, grounded in the CC2020 competency model, designed to bridge this gap. Our approach began with a diagnostic survey that confirmed computer science student deficiencies across the core competencies of Knowledge, Skills, and Dispositions. In response, we implemented an integrated, project/problem-based curriculum featuring three targeted interventions: a restructured teaching plan, embedded academia/industry-oriented open-source projects, and student-led collaborative sessions. The framework's efficacy is supported by a powerful dual validation. Internally, end-of-course evaluations revealed high student satisfaction and self-reported competency growth. Externally, students achieved tangible successes, including winning prestigious industry competitions. This study provides robust evidence for our approach, offering a replicable model for academic institutions to cultivate computer science talents. By systematically embedding competencies into the learning experience, this framework equips students with the robust capabilities needed to excel in complex, real-world challenges.

title: A Self-Supervised and Explainable Framework for Zero-Day Attack Detection and Mitigation in Windows Firewall
authors: S. Vasudevan, P. Gowtham Sai Saketh, U. Suraj, K. Boopalan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Signature-based firewall mechanisms remain a primary defense in many networks; however, their reliance on predefined signatures makes them ineffective at detecting previously unseen threats. This paper presents an intelligent security framework that augments the native Windows Firewall with a self-supervised learning approach for detecting and mitigating zero-day attacks. The proposed system learns normal traffic behavior directly from Windows pfirewall.log records without requiring labelled attack data. Masked feature prediction and contrastive learning are used to generate traffic embeddings, while a temporal representation module captures sequential communication patterns across connections to reveal stealthy attack behavior. These representations are evaluated using a Local Outlier Factor-based anomaly detector to identify suspicious activities. When anomalous traffic is detected, the framework automatically generates mitigation rules that can be applied by the firewall in real time. SHAP-based feature attribution provides interpretable explanations for blocking decisions, while a rule-conflict management component ensures compatibility with administratordefined policies. Firewall log integrity and forensic traceability are preserved through AES-256-GCM encrypted archives. Experimental evaluation on sixty days of firewall logs containing approximately 1.9 million raw entries processed into <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 6, 6 0 0}$</tex> temporal windows demonstrates strong detection capability, achieving 91.5% precision, 93.1 % recall, and an AUC-ROC of 0.967 while maintaining low computational overhead which is suitable for real-time deployment.

title: Overcoming AI Dependency: Practical Pathways and Effectiveness of Educational Agents in Python Instruction
authors: Fang Xia, Ren Ying
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The proliferation of generative artificial intelligence (AI) has given rise to the phenomenon of "AI dependency" in programming learning, where students tend to directly obtain code solutions, thereby undermining the cultivation of problem-solving abilities and computational thinking. This study developed an instructional intervention system centered on "Role-based Pedagogical Agents" for the Python Programming course, transforming AI from an "answer provider" into a "thinking support platform." Based on a one-semester quasi-experimental study, students in the experimental group significantly reduced their behavior of directly requesting complete code. They demonstrated more prominent performance in problem decomposition, algorithm design, and debugging capabilities, along with a noticeable enhancement in reflection depth. The research confirms that role-based pedagogical agents, through structured process design and agent intervention, effectively reduce AI dependency, promote the improvement of students' computational thinking and programming quality, and provide a practical pathway for programming instruction in the era of artificial intelligence.

title: Dynamic Path Planning Based on LSTM Residual Fusion Prediction and Improved Swarm Intelligence Algorithms
authors: Yutang Han
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: To address AGV obstacle avoidance in complex dynamic environments, this paper proposes a path planning framework based on LSTM residual fusion prediction and an improved DE-PSO algorithm. Utilizing a “physics-data” dual-driven mechanism, the framework employs LSTM to predict velocity perturbations, eliminating divergent cumulative effects in long-horizon trajectory predictions. An adaptive initialization strategy is designed to enhance population quality and shrink the search space through coordinate monotonicity constraints. By integrating differential evolution operators, a hybrid DE-PSO model is constructed, significantly enhancing global search and local optima escape capabilities. Simulation results demonstrate that DE-PSO exhibits superior optimality and enhanced robustness when compared to established benchmark algorithms such as DE and PSO. The study confirms that the framework robustly generates smooth, safe, and optimal trajectories satisfying kinematic constraints.

title: HX-MARL: Explainable Hypergraph-Driven MultiAgent Reinforcement Learning for Trust-Aware and Adversarially Robust Spectrum Allocation in CRN
authors: A. Shankar, P.Shyamala Bharathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: The HX-MARL model proposed presents a explainable hypergraph-based multi-agent reinforcement learning scheme of trust-aware and adversarially robust spectrum allocation in cognitive radio networks. The system combines hypergraph neural representations, trust assessment, and adversarial training to boost the coordination of agents in dynamic and hostile settings. The experiment shows a higher level of performance, with an accuracy of 99.18%, a precision of 98.74% and a recall of 98.52%, which is better than traditional models (CNN, LSTM and BERT). The hypergraph structure is efficient in capturing the effect of higher-order interactions and the mechanism of trust can reduce the impact of malicious agents. The explainability module, which is also included, enhances transparency in decision-making. The model has a high throughput, less interference, and controlled performance despite the growing adversarial conditions. These findings confirm the power of joint application of relational learning, trust modeling and reinforcement learning in efficient spectrum allocation. The framework can be deployed in the next-generation wireless communication systems to scale, be secure, and real-time.

title: Personalized Learning Pathways for Students with Special Needs Using Adaptive Learning Analytics
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Students with special educational needs (SEN) require individualized instructional approaches due to diverse cognitive, behavioural, and sensory profiles. Conventional digital learning systems often lack the responsiveness needed to adapt content, pacing, and scaffolding to each learner’s unique needs. This study introduces an adaptive learning analytics framework designed to generate personalized learning pathways for SEN learners, including those with autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, dyslexia, and mixed learning disabilities. The framework integrates multimodal data sources—interaction logs, eye-movement patterns, speech and affect indicators, and task-performance metrics—to construct dynamic learner profiles that evolve in real time. Using knowledge-tracing algorithms and reinforcement-learning models, the system tailors instructional sequences to optimize cognitive load, engagement, and skill mastery. An evaluation with 326 SEN students across inclusive and special-education environments demonstrates significant gains in learning efficiency, task completion, and sustained attention when compared with traditional learning platforms. Qualitative insights further reveal improved motivation, reduced anxiety triggers, and stronger learner autonomy. The proposed framework advances personalized education by combining explainable AI, universal design for learning principles, and ethical data-governance practices. Findings highlight the potential of adaptive learning analytics to deliver more equitable, responsive, and effective learning pathways for students with special needs, and outline directions for future large-scale deployment.

title: APLE: A Framework for Adaptable and Personalized Live Explainability for Recommender Systems
authors: Gourab K. Patro, Srimoyee Mukhopadhyay, Arsh Banerjee, Daniel Abreu, Kevin Perkins, Siddharth Kotwal, Vijay Nagaraj Sivakumaran, Aram Bidzyan, Diganta Nayak
