title: Addressing Non-Negligible Model Uncertainty in Machine Learning-assisted Measurements
authors: Leopoldo Angrisani, Maria Cacciapuoti, Sabatina Criscuolo, Mauro D’Arco, Egidio De Benedetto, Luigi Duraccio, Annarita Tedesco
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Machine Learning (ML) techniques are increasingly used in measurement science to develop novel measurement models. Such ML-based measurement models are particularly useful when conventional ones are unavailable, enabling measurement operations that would otherwise be impractical. However, the resulting relationship between the measurand and the input quantities is not directly selected by the practitioner; rather, it is inherently determined by a data-driven training process. This gives rise to an additional uncertainty component (denoted in previous work as measurement model uncertainty) which, when adherence to the Joint Committee For Guides in Metrology (JCGM) is required, shall be negligible with respect to the component associated with the measurement of the physical input quantities (denoted in previous work as physical quantity uncertainty). When this condition cannot be met, a direct application of the JCGM framework may understate the measurement uncertainty, thereby yielding coverage intervals that are not representative of the values that can be reasonably attributed to the measurand. Accordingly, to address the limitations of a direct JCGM application while remaining consistent with its framework, this work proposes a JCGM-compliant method that leverages joint probability distributions, sampled via nested Monte Carlo simulations, to obtain a fully fledged measurement result for ML-assisted measurements. Two illustrative case studies based on deterministic ML regressors demonstrate the applicability of the proposed method and show that the resulting coverage intervals explicitly account for the variability introduced by the ML model, thereby avoiding any understatement of the reported measurement uncertainty.

title: The Control for Dual-Port Wave Energy Converters Considering Both Maximum Energy Capture and Power Fluctuation Reduction
authors: Jiyu Zhang, Lei Huang, Haoran Wang, Jianlong Yang, ShiXiang Wang, Minqiang Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Under a reasonable control strategy, dual-port direct-drive wave energy converter (DPDDWEC) can exhibit characteristics of reducing power fluctuations and increasing the bandwidth for capturing waves. In this paper, a control strategy based on model predictive control (MPC) for the DPDDWEC is proposed. This control strategy employs MPC to construct a new objective function, enabling DPDDWEC to simultaneously maximize energy capture and reduce power fluctuations. The paper first outlines the structure of the DPDDWEC, followed by its mathematical modelling and derivation of the MPC control algorithm. An objective function is formulated to simultaneously satisfy reducing power fluctuations and energy capture maximization criteria. Finally, the control algorithm undergoes simulation and experimental validation. Results demonstrate that, compared to the single-port direct-drive wave energy converters (SPDDWEC), the DPDDWC control algorithm captures slightly less power but significantly reduces power fluctuations in wave power generation.

title: AI-Native Resource Orchestration for Collaborative Sensing, Communication and Computing in UAV-Enabled Space-Air Networks
authors: Hui Zhao, Mingan Luan, Pasika Ranaweera, Madhusanka Liyanage, Zheng Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Collaborative sensing, communication, and computing (CSCC) empowered Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV)-enabled air-space networks offer a promising solution for emerging wireless services demanding precise sensing and real-time decision-making in dynamic environments. In this paper, we propose a CSCC framework for space-air networks, in which the UAV both performs data sensing and serves as a relay, offering wireless access between terrestrial sensing devices (TSDs) and the Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite edge server. To enhance the adaptability and overall operational efficiency of CSCC systems in dynamic multi-task environments, multiple performance metrics, including sensing satisfaction, service duration, and UAV energy consumption, are jointly considered. Accordingly, by jointly optimizing multi-dimensional heterogeneous resources in each stage of CSCC, we formulate a multi-objective problem with the goal of maximizing the total sensing satisfaction while minimizing the service duration and energy consumption of the UAV. To avoid the curse of dimensionality caused by joint optimization across multiple CSCC stages, we decouple the original optimization problem into two subproblems. Then, we propose a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based artificial intelligence (AI)-native algorithmic framework that serves as the intrinsic core engine driving system sensing, decision-making, and execution. Within this framework, the two subproblems can be effectively addressed by using the coordinate descent (CD) based algorithm and the proximal policy optimization (PPO)-based DRL algorithm enhanced with an embedded LSTM module, respectively. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme outperforms three benchmark methods in terms of convergence, multi-objective performance, and robustness.

title: Solving the Post-Quantum Control Plane Bottleneck: Energy-Aware Cryptographic Scheduling in Open RAN
authors: Neha Gupta, Hamed Alimohammadi, Mohammad Shojafar, De Mi, Muhammad N. M. Bhutta
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: The Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) offers flexibility and innovation but introduces unique security vulnerabilities, particularly from cryptographically relevant quantum computers. While Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) is the primary scalable defence, its computationally intensive handshakes create a significant bottleneck for the RAN control plane, posing sustainability challenges. This article proposes an energy-aware framework to solve this PQC bottleneck, ensuring quantum resilience without sacrificing operational energy efficiency. The system employs an O-RAN aligned split: a Crypto Policy rApp residing in the Non-Real- Time (Non-RT) RIC defines the strategic security envelope (including PQC suites), while a Security Operations Scheduling (SOS) xApp in the Near-RT RIC converts these into tactical timing and placement intents. Cryptographic enforcement remains at standards-compliant endpoints: the Open Fronthaul utilizes Media Access Control Security (MACsec) at the O-DU/O-RU, while the xhaul (midhaul and backhaul) utilizes IP Security (IPsec) at tunnel terminators. The SOS xApp reduces PQC overhead by batching non-urgent handshakes, prioritizing session resumption, and selecting parameters that meet slice SLAs while minimizing joules per secure connection. We evaluate the architecture via a Discrete-Event Simulation (DES) using 3GPP-aligned traffic profiles and verified hardware benchmarks from literature. Results show that intelligent scheduling can reduce per-handshake energy by approximately 60% without violating slice latency targets.

title: A Secure and Transparent Multi-Agent Framework for Automated Financial Decision Systems
authors: Uthayan K.R, Shanthanu G, Santhosh V
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Traditional credit decision systems rely on opaque machine learning models that lack transparency, auditability, and secure coordination between system components. This paper proposes a secure multi-agent artificial intelligence pipeline for automated credit decisioning using a centralized orchestration layer. The architecture integrates specialized agents for digital identity verification, fraud risk assessment using interpretable fuzzy risk scoring, credit risk prediction, regulatory policy retrieval, and tamper-proof audit logging.To ensure secure and trustworthy operation, the system incorporates zero-trust communication between agents, role-based access control, authenticated message exchange, and cryptographically verifiable audit trails. The proposed pipeline enables transparent, auditable, and regulator-compliant automated credit decisioning while ensuring secure handling of sensitive financial data.This work demonstrates how secure multi-agent orchestration can enable trustworthy, explainable, and secure artificial intelligence deployment in financial web applications.

title: Context-Awareness for Dependable Low-Power IoT
authors: David E. Ruiz-Guirola, Prasoon Raghuwanshi, Gabriel M. de Jesus, Mateen Ashraf, Onel L. A. Lopez
publish: IEEE Communications Magazine
abstract: Dependability is the ability to consistently deliver trusted and uninterrupted service in the face of operational uncertainties. Ensuring dependable operation in large-scale, energy-constrained Internet of Things (IoT) deployments is as crucial as challenging, and calls for context-aware protocols, where context refers to situational or state information. In this article, we identify four critical context dimensions for IoT networks, namely energy status, information freshness, task relevance, and physical/medium conditions, and show how each one underpins core dependability attributes. Building on these insights, we propose a twostep protocol-design framework that incorporates operation-specific context fields. Through three representative use cases, we demonstrate how context awareness can enhance system dependability by leveraging context-awareness.

title: Hybrid Domain Joint Low-Rank and Sparse Constrained Framework for Simultaneous RFI Separation and SAR Imaging
authors: Jun’ao Li, Zhongyu Li, Jing Yang, Qing Yang, Junjie Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The inherent contradiction between limited spectrum resources and ever-growing spectrum demand makes it difficult for synthetic aperture radar (SAR) to avoid in-band radio frequency interference (RFI) during observation, leading to significant degradation in imaging quality. In this context, RFI suppression technology has become crucial for obtaining high-fidelity SAR images. Existing RFI suppression methods are primarily divided into pre-processing and post-processing categories, both of which have inherent limitations. Pre-processing methods often struggle to achieve sufficient suppression due to the high overlap between target echoes and RFI in the time, frequency, time-frequency and other domains. Post-processing methods, which operate directly on the imaging results, tend to cause loss of image details and weak targets. Motivated by these problems, this paper proposes a hybrid domain joint lowrank and sparse constrained framework for simultaneous RFI separation and SAR imaging. The innovation lies in leveraging the low-rank characteristic of RFI in the echo domain as well as the low-rank and sparse representation of scene targets, while coupling the echo and image domains through the SAR imaging operator to establish a unified inversion model with relational constraints. To solve this model, a deep learning network named SSI-net is constructed by integrating the alternating direction method of multipliers with deep unfolding techniques. Bedisdes, for scenarios with limited or no training samples, a gradient descent-based optimization algorithm is also proposed. Simulations and experiments with measured data demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed framework.

title: Adaptive Context-Aware Localization Network for Remote Sensing Object Detection Under Haze and Class Imbalance
authors: Yingjun Wang, Xiaopeng Yang, Jiali Wang, Zheng Liang, Wenyi Zhao, Weidong Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Object detection in optical remote sensing imagery is severely challenged by haze degradation and long-tailed category imbalance, which result in blurred appearances, noise interference, and frequent missed detections of minority classes. These challenges hinder robust feature representation, accurate cross-scale localization, and balanced optimisation. To address these issues, we propose ACLNet, a context-aware feature-enhancement and adaptive-localization-optimization framework for robust remote sensing object detection. Specifically, we first propose a context-aware poly-kernel (CAPK) module, which enhances mid- to high-level feature representations via parallel multi-kernel modelling and axial context modulation to compensate for haze-induced detail loss. Subsequently, we introduce a dual-stream dynamic feature fusion (D2F2) mechanism, which dynamically fuses local structural cues with global dependencies, to improve cross-scale alignment and discriminative consistency in complex scenes. Furthermore, we adopt an adaptive differential modulation localization (ADML) loss that suppresses noisy gradients while adaptively emphasising medium-quality and hard samples, thereby enhancing localization stability for small objects and degraded scenes without increasing inference overhead. Experimental results show that ACLNet consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the DIOR, RSOD, and Hazy-Det datasets. Additionally, ACLNet is highly deployable with only 10.7 M parameters. The source code will be available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/AmorFatio/ACLNet</uri>

title: Improved Endurance in HfZrOₓ-Based Junctionless FeTFTs Using an Asymmetric Dual-Gate Architecture
authors: William Cheng-Yu Ma, Chun-Jung Su, Kuo-Hsing Kao, Ta-Chun Cho, Jhao-Fong Deng, Yen-Lun Hsieh, Huan Hsu, Tzu-Chi Liu, An-De Xu, Che-Hsun Tsai
publish: IEEE Journal of the Electron Devices Society
abstract: This paper presents an asymmetric dual-gate (ADG) junctionless ferroelectric thin-film transistor (JL-FeTFT) featuring a HfZrO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> ferroelectric layer and a heavily doped n⁺ poly-Si channel. By exploiting asymmetric gate stacks and dual-gate biasing, the proposed ADG architecture enables effective redistribution of the vertical electric field across the gate dielectric. The influence of single-gate (SG) and dual-gate (DG) operations on device characteristics and endurance behavior was systematically investigated. The DG configuration provides enhanced electrostatic control and achieves an endurance exceeding 2 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> program/erase cycles, four times that of SG operation, while maintaining stable threshold voltage and transconductance. Energy-band analysis reveals that the asymmetric dual-gate biasing mitigates oxide stress and suppresses trap generation during cycling. Post-cycling transconductance measurements further confirm reduced oxide degradation under DG operation. These results highlight the critical role of asymmetric dual-gate field engineering in improving reliability, demonstrating the ADG JL-FeTFT as a promising candidate for high-endurance, low-voltage nonvolatile memory applications.

title: Finite control set model predictive control for current ripple reduction on BLDC motors
authors: Axel Coronado-Andrade, Luis Alvarez-Icaza
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Two new techniques are presented for finite control set model predictive control (FCS-MPC) of BLDC motors in high performance applications. The proposals are based on two design choices: a) a reduced set of admissible switching states, selected according to the electrical sector in which the motor is functioning, and b) the division of the sampling time into a fixed number of sub-intervals. These two choices, together with a proper formulation for the prediction of current values, allow to easily calculate the value of a predefined cost function at the end of the sampling time, for all possible combinations of admissible switchings during the sub-intervals, and to select the one that minimizes this cost function. The chosen combination is then applied using a standard voltage source inverter (VSI). These two techniques do not require the calculation of any switching time and keep the simplicity of the original FCS-MPC scheme. Simulation and experimental results, under similar conditions, show superior performance with a reduction between 70% to 80% of the RMSE current tracking error, when compared with the original FCS-MPC. These new techniques are complementary to control strategies that deal with load and speed variations in BLDC motors desired trajectories.

title: GenAI-Driven Quantum-Resilient Consensus Framework for Blockchain-Enabled Vehicular Digital Twins
authors: Meghna K. Bhatt, Mohammad Shabaz, Gyanendra Kumar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: Digital Twin (DT) technology is elevating the next-generation intelligent transportation systems industry to new heights, as it enables real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and adaptive control of connected and autonomous vehicles. However, the use of GenAI and DTs in interconnected vehicular technology ecosystems introduces new attack vectors, particularly from quantum computing, which can easily break classical encryption systems. This paper introduces Reputation-based Proof-of-Stake (R-PoS), a hybrid consensus mechanism tailored for lattice-based PQC operations on vehicular edge devices. The core contribution is a lightweight hybrid consensus mechanism optimized for lattice-based PQC on edge devices, enabling secure and scalable synchronization between physical assets and their digital twins. Experimental results from a containerized IoT testbed using the Open Quantum Safe (OQS) library show that the proposed PQC-BC framework achieves an average throughput of 1178 transactions per second with latency of 0.78 second. These results affirm the framework's efficacy in securing future interconnected vehicular environments and establishing a trust foundation for sustainable quantum-resistant digital twin applications.

title: A Load-adaptive Multi-pattern Switching Strategy Based on Active Gate Driver for Si/SiC Hybrid Switch
authors: Ping Liu, Mingbin Tang, Qi Guo, Qi Cao, Bin Yu, Chunming Tu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: Si/SiC hybrid switch synergistically combines the superior switching characteristics of SiC MOSFET with the cost advantage of Si IGBT, presenting an economically viable approach for enhanced power conversion efficiency. However, the performance of this hybrid configuration is primarily governed by the switching pattern, which exhibits significant load current dependence. To address this challenge, a novel load-adaptive multi-pattern switching strategy based on active gate driver (AGD) is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the switching characteristic analysis of the hybrid switch is conducted, the coupling effects of gate drive voltage and current on electrical stress and power loss are systematically analyzed. Subsequently, an AGD circuit based on transient voltage feedback is designed to dynamically regulate the gate current during switching transients. Finally, based on the device's safe operating area, three load current intervals are defined. The proposed strategy adaptively selects among three switching patterns according to the instantaneous load current, which can optimize the power loss during light-load operation while suppressing current overshoot under heavy-load conditions. Compared with the conventional 15 V and 20 V strategies, the switching loss is reduced by at least 12.69% and the current stress is reduced by up to 64.3%, respectively. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy.

title: CARGO: Compliant Analytics for Resilient Governance and Orchestration of Maritime Emissions in an Explainable Serverless Framework
authors: Mahtab Shahin, Avleen Malhi, Mahsa Khorasani, Riina Palu, Austin Kim, Sanja Bauk, Ralf-Martin Soe
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Decarbonization of maritime transport requires analytics frameworks capable of processing large heterogeneous datasets while remaining compliant with regulatory standards and explainable. Although current distributed platforms, such as Apache Spark and HPC clusters, offer scalability, they are limited in their interpretability and have high orchestration overheads. In this paper, we present CARGO (Compliant Analytics for Resilient Governance and Orchestration), a serverless framework for monitoring maritime emissions that unifies adaptive orchestration, explainable analytics, and built-in governance. In order to manage maritimeworkloads in real-time,CARGOintegrates Association Rule Mining (ARM) and clustering to identify operational and meteorological drivers of CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> surges, and utilizes AES-256 encryption, TLS 1.3, and role-based access control for secure data governance. With EU MRV CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> reports (2018-2024) coupled with ERA5 meteorology and AIS trajectories, CARGO achieves 42% lower orchestration latency and 25% higher throughput than Apache Spark and cloud-native baselines on 500 GB workloads. Furthermore, the framework provides interpretable association rules linking speed, fuel type, and rainfall to emission anomalies, providing regulatory-readable evidence for compliance decisions. Based on these results, it can be concluded that CARGO provides a deployable foundation for digital twin enabled, climate-aware maritime governance by balancing scalability, cost, and transparency.

title: No-Reference Generic Image Quality Assessment via Adaptive Fusion of Naturalness-Enhanced Colour and Appearance-Driven Spatial Metrics
authors: Muhammad Usman Khan, Imran Mehmood, Ming Ronnier Luo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: No-reference image quality assessment (NR-IQA) remains a challenging problem due to the diverse nature of image distortions and the absence of a reference image. While most existing no-reference image quality metrics (NR-IQMs) mainly target spatial distortions, comparatively few methods address appearance-driven colour renderings, and generalization across heterogeneous datasets remains limited. To address this gap, this study proposes a unified NR-IQA framework that adaptively integrates colour and spatial quality predictions through a distortion-aware fusion strategy. The framework comprises three metrics: (1) CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ZN</sub>, an enhanced colour-domain image quality metric (IQM) that extends our earlier CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Z</sub> metric by incorporating five naturalness-related statistical and texture descriptors; (2) SIQMZ, a spatial-domain IQM that derives natural scene statistics (NSS) features from the chroma and depth channels of the CIELAB colour space; and (3) GIQMZ, a classifier-guided generic metric that adaptively fuses CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ZN</sub> and SIQMZ predictions based on rendering-type probabilities. Extensive evaluations were conducted on 17,387 images from seven benchmark datasets using both same-database and cross-database protocols. The results demonstrate that CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ZN</sub> significantly improves predictive capability over CIQM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Z</sub> for both colour renderings and spatial distortions, SIQMZ achieves competitive performance on spatially distorted datasets with a compact feature set, and GIQMZ provides robust and generalizable performance across mixed-distortion datasets through distortion-aware probabilistic fusion. Although deep learning-based models achieve higher prediction accuracy on large-scale datasets, the proposed framework offers a favourable trade-off between performance, interpretability, and computational efficiency.

title: LTE signal-aided GNSS/PDR System for Mix-pose Pedestrian Positioning in Urban Environments Using Smartphone
authors: Li Cong, Jingnan Tian, Jianbiao Wei
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Smartphone-based positioning is highly promising due to its widespread availability. However, its reliance on global navigation satellite systems (GNSS) leads to accuracy degradation in complex environments such as urban canyons. While integrating GNSS with inertial sensor-based pedestrian dead reckoning (PDR) can improve performance, challenges such as cumulative positioning errors as well as reduced accuracy in step length and heading estimation due to mixed phone poses remain unresolved during GNSS outages. To address these issues, a Long-Term Evolution (LTE) signal-aided GNSS/PDR system is proposed to improve the applicability to complex environments and mixed poses. Firstly, based on the analysis of propagation characteristics of LTE signal, we establish the relationship between the reference signal received power (RSRP) variations and positions to extract position, step length parameter and heading observations. Additionally, theoretical accuracy of the position observations is derived and its influencing factors are analyzed, providing a basis for the design of subsequent availability assessment strategy. Secondly, multiple observations from GNSS are extracted. Thirdly, after compensating for PDR heading offset caused by pose switching, we employ an unscented Kalman filter (UKF) model to fuse PDR with multi-observation from GNSS and LTE signals. An availability assessment strategy is designed to adaptively adjust filter parameters to exclude moments when GNSS or LTE signals are unavailable, which can mitigate the step length and heading errors induced by environments and poses effectively. Experiments conducted at three sites demonstrate that the introduction of LTE signal significantly reduces step length and heading errors, thereby improving positioning performance.

title: Practical Time-Synchronized Model Predictive Control for Energy-Efficient Dynamic Positioning of Fully Actuated Surface Vehicles
authors: Zhihao Yu, Jialu Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: For the robust dynamic positioning (DP) of the fully actuated electric vessel, this paper presents the first practical time-synchronized (TS) Lyapunov-based model predictive control (LMPC) scheme, guaranteeing the practical TS stability and the optimal energy consumption. To ensure TS convergence of vessel states, a slack variable vector of ratio persistence is introduced into the stage cost of LMPC. To guarantee the stability of the presented scheme, a practical TS auxiliary controller based on a TS disturbance observer and adaptive compensators is designed to construct the contractive constraint for LMPC. A chattering-reduction thrust allocation algorithm is designed to smooth the thrust force commands. Supported by theoretical analyses of the feasibility and stability, all states of the closed-loop DP control system under the proposed scheme converge to their desired values with a synchronized settling time. Consequently, the vessel under the proposed scheme can precisely maneuver with optimized trajectories and reduced thruster power consumption. Comparative hardware-in-the-loop simulations on a model-scale electric vessel demonstrate the precise DP performance and the energy-saving capability of the proposed scheme.

title: Dual-Mode LSPR Biosensing of DNA and SARS-CoV-2 Spike Protein using Gold Nanorod-Coated Hetero-core Fiber
authors: Ai Hosoki
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: A gold nanorod (AuNR)–coated hetero-core optical-fiber LSPR biosensor was designed and experimentally demonstrated for label-free detection of complementary ssDNA and the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein. The sensor consists of a hetero-core fiber segment onto which AuNRs were directly immobilized, exhibiting two plasmon resonance modes. DNA recognition was achieved by introducing complementary ssDNA to the AuNR-coated fiber, which resulted in clear absorbance responses in both modes across the examined concentration range. The longitudinal mode achieved a limit of detection (LOD) of 0.11 pM. For SARS-CoV-2 spike protein detection, the sensor functionalized with the S1-binding aptamer yielded a LOD of 19.1 pM in the transverse mode. Control tests with non-complementary ssDNA verified molecular specificity. Dissociation constant (KD) analysis further revealed mode-dependent binding characteristics: the longitudinal mode exhibited a smaller KD for DNA hybridization, whereas the transverse mode showed a smaller KD for S1 binding. These KD tendencies were in reasonable agreement with the mode-dependent LOD performance observed in each assay. These results demonstrate that the AuNR-coated hetero-core fiber serves as a simple and mechanically robust platform for implementing LSPR-based biosensing, enabling label-free detection of both nucleic-acid and protein targets within a compact fiber-optic format.

title: From Traditional to Digital: A Simulation-Based Approach on the Digital Transformation of MultiTier Automotive Parts Supply Chain Based on Cloud SCM Platform
authors: Yan Dong, Song Jin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Purpose] Under the deep convergence of global economic integration and the fourth industrial revolution, the automotive manufacturing industry is undergoing a profound paradigm shift. Traditional legacy systems expose serious vulnerabilities and the “bullwhip effect” within complex multi-level global networks. [Methodology] To address these issues and bridge the theoretical gap in interdisciplinary technological integration, this study constructs a full-stack cloud-based supply chain management (SCM) platform architecture. It fundamentally synergizes artificial intelligence (multimodal LSTM, PSO), the Internet of Things, knowledge graphs, and digital twins. The proposed approach is rigorously evaluated through a hybrid simulation framework encompassing system dynamics and discrete event modeling. [Findings] The simulation results demonstrate that this digital paradigm significantly increases the network visibility of Tier-2 suppliers from $\mathbf{1 4. 2} \%$ to $\mathbf{8 7. 6} \%$. Furthermore, it structurally reduces the total global operating costs by 30.0% and compresses production line downtime to zero during extreme supply disruption stress tests. Ultimately, this framework provides both theoretical innovations and actionable guidelines for automotive enterprises to achieve optimal system resilience.

title: Wind Power Forecasting Based on TCN-Transformer
authors: Xueke Ban, Ying Han
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Accurate wind power forecasting is crucial for the safe and stable operation and grid integration of power systems. To address the challenge that traditional time-series models face in effectively capturing medium-to long-term complex dependencies within wind speed sequences, this paper proposes a wind power forecasting model based on the fusion of Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) and Transformer models. Leveraging the advantages of TCN's parallel convolutional structure, the model efficiently extracts local features and long-term trends from sequences. Furthermore, it incorporates the multi-head self-attention mechanism of the Transformer to precisely capture the global dynamic dependencies among multidimensional features such as wind speed, wind direction, and temperature. Through the synergistic operation of TCN and Transformer, the model simultaneously possesses powerful capabilities for local feature extraction and global context modeling. Experimental results based on actual data from a wind farm in China demonstrate that, compared to single TCN, Transformer, or LSTM models, the proposed TCN-Transformer hybrid model achieves significant improvements in metrics such as Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE), providing a more accurate and reliable solution for wind farm power forecasting.

title: A Comprehensive Analysis of IoT Attack Detection using a Dual-Function Dataset and Stacked Meta-Learning Models
authors: Hanen Dhrir, Chaima Aouiche, Maha Charfeddine, Mohamed Rahouti, Habib M. Kammoun, El Moundher Aouiche
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The rapid growth of internet technologies, combined with the robust and evolving infrastructure of the Internet of Things (IoT) networks, has led to the presence of a variety of sophisticated attacks, highlighting the need for advanced defensive measures. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) have become key tools to tackle a spectrum of attack vectors, as they deliver intelligent, adaptive, context-driven, and domain-specific responses. This work attempts to provide a comprehensive analysis of emerging IoT attack identification in real time by proposing an ensemble framework that incorporates two balancing strategies: Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) and Class Weights adjustment. More specifically, the framework consists of three main stages, including data collection from the CIC IoT-DIAD 2024 benchmark, data preprocessing, and flow-based feature selection. The next immediate step addresses label imbalance using SMOTE, which oversamples minority cases by generating synthetic data points, and class weights, which adjust the loss function to apply greater penalties for misclassifying minority instances. Following this, ensemble models are developed and optimized using bagging and stacking meta-learners, with hyperparameters tuned through Optuna. Models evaluation demonstrates improved performance across valuable metrics (Accuracy(0.96-0.97), Precision(0.94-0.99), Recall(0.94-0.99), F1-score(0.96-0.97) and AUC(0.99)) when the models sensitivity to the classes is adjusted, rather than relying solely on synthetic data generation. These results showcase the practical evidence of the proposed framework in enhancing the security and trust of real IoT systems, further supported by a low false alarm rate(0.000339) and false discovery rate(0.000326).

title: Protect AI Model Implementation: Building Cyber-Resilient Threat Detection Systems with a Fusion of AI and Cybersecurity
authors: Naga Sekhar Madala, Mahmoud Odeh, R. Deepa, M. Amutha, Srilakshmi Aluri, Gaurav Pushkarna
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Cybersecurity infrastructure threat detection and response have been transformed by AI. Attacks on AI models that invert models, poison data, or evade detection have increased. The cyber-resilient threat detection system in this study is protected by AI-driven analytics and cybersecurity. A robust, self-repairing system that can detect both human and artificial intelligence-based dangers is first-rate. This package includes features such as encrypted model storage, secure model training pipelines, adversarial training, behavior anomaly detection, and runtime integrity verification. The security of AI is paramount throughout the whole process, from gathering and cleaning data to training and deploying models. Through the use of rule-based security analytics, reinforcement learning, and deep learning, the hybrid detection engine is able to withstand both known and emerging threat trends and zero-day vulnerabilities. In dangerous settings, dynamic model retraining and continuous incorporation of threat intelligence are necessities. With less false positives and improved detection, the outcomes were better than standalone cybersecurity and AI solutions. Using AI and cybersecurity together forms a safe ecosystem, according to research. This paper presents a flexible and extensible design for secure AI-powered threat detection systems in the business, cloud, and critical infrastructure sectors.

title: Survey on the Current Status and Willingness of Teachers in Western Ethnic Regions to Use Digital Teaching Strategies
authors: Ying Yang, Zhijun Yang, Gaoye Cui
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the advancement of digital transformation in education, the effective implementation of digital teaching strategies has become a key element in improving teaching quality. As the main implementers of digital teaching and cultivating students' core competencies, teachers' implementation of digital teaching strategies will have an impact on the degree of implementation and teaching quality of the new curriculum reform requirements. This study employs a questionnaire survey to investigate the current status and challenges of digital teaching strategy usage among teachers in western ethnic regions. Using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), it further analyzes underlying factors affecting teachers' adoption of these strategies, including perceived difficulties and determinants of digital teaching acceptance. Based on the findings, strategic recommendations are proposed to enhance teachers' willingness to implement digital teaching strategies.

title: S2IFPTNet: Spatial-Spectral Interactive Fusion Pooling Transformer Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Haixu Yang, Shuli Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In recent years, the fusion network model combining agriculture, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and Transformers has become a research hotspot in hyperspectral image classification. However, these models applied to hyperspectral image classification typically adopt a single branch heterogeneous architecture, which combines the local details of CNN and the global attention mechanism of Transformers through simple cascading. The remaining challenge lies in the extraction of detailed and local features. To address these problems, we propose a Spatial-Spectral Interactive Fusion Pooling Transformer Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification(S2IFPTNet), which can efficiently extract multi-scale spatial-spectral features, accurately model nonlinear feature relationships, and effectively fuse multi-modal features. We design a Spatial-Spectral Matrix Fusion Module, which enables multi-scale spatial-spectral feature extraction and accurately captures the complex dependency structures between variables, thus achieving cross-scale spatial-spectral information fusion. In addition, we design a Pooling Cascaded Attention Fusion Module to hierarchically extract feature information of different scales and receptive fields, while preserving key details and global context, so as to improve the completeness and robustness of feature representation. Extensive experiments are conducted on four public hyperspectral image datasets, and the results show that the proposed Spatial-Spectral Interactive Fusion Pooling Transformer Network outperforms the state-of-the-art classification algorithms.

title: ACT-HRNet: Adaptive Consistency and Task-Aware Optimization Enhanced HRNet for Hand Acupoint Localization
authors: Wenbo Zhang, Wenxue Sun, Qiaoyi Bao, Yiting Huang, Yiting Wang, Hongbo Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Hand acupoint localization is a key task in intelligent acupuncture of traditional Chinese medicine. To address hand size variations, low acupoint feature distinguishability, and complex spatial structures, this paper proposes the ACT-HRNet model with two core modules. The Adaptive Spatial Constraint (ASC) mechanism models cross-hand spatial stability through proportional relationships, thereby effectively suppressing geometric errors. The Task-Aware Cosine Annealing (TACO) strategy adopts PCK-guided adaptive cycle reset to coordinate the learning rate schedule with the model convergence progress. Experiments show the method achieves 95.4% PCK and NME of 0.0092 on the self-built dataset, which is superior to the baseline HRNet-W32. Ablation experiments verify the individual contributions and synergistic effects of each module.

title: A Lightweight Facial Expression Recognition Method with Dual Attention Mechanisms
authors: Shuai Ma, Hong Zhu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: Facial expression recognition, as a core task in affective computing, has significant application value in fields such as human-computer interaction and mental health monitoring. However, the practical utility and real-time performance of existing methods are severely limited by challenges including illumination variations, pose occlusions in complex scenes, high inter-class similarity of expressions, and class imbalance in datasets. To address these challenges, we propose a lightweight expression recognition method that integrates dual attention mechanisms and an improved loss function. Firstly, a Cross-Attention Fusion Module (CAFM) and a Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) module are embedded into the lightweight mini_XCEPTION network, dynamically enhancing the feature representation of key facial regions from both spatial and channel dimensions. Secondly, to tackle the class imbalance problem inherent in datasets, a Dual-Scale Classification Loss Function is proposed, which combines Cross-Entropy Loss and Focal Loss through weighted summation to improve the model's sensitivity to minority classes. Comprehensive experiments on the publicly available FER2013 dataset demonstrate that our method achieves a recognition accuracy of 73.15 %, representing a 2.40 % improvement over the original mini_XCEPTION model. Furthermore, ablation studies and comparisons with state-of-theart methods from recent years further validate the effectiveness and advancement of the proposed modules and loss function. Future work will focus on evaluating the model on more diverse datasets, optimizing its computational speed for real-time applications, incorporating data augmentation techniques, and conducting latency comparisons with other lightweight methods.

title: YOLO-Plankton: A Lightweight Network for Plankton Detection in Holographic Images
authors: Xinyuan Zhang, Buyu Guo, Ziting Wang, Yuan Lin, Peiliang Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Digital holographic imaging has become an effective technique for plankton observation due to its capability of capturing three-dimensional information with a large depth of field. However, holographic images contain complex interference patterns and noise that differ significantly from conventional microscopic images, making it difficult to directly apply existing datasets and deep learning models trained on microscopy imagery. Moreover, the scarcity of annotated holographic datasets further limits the development of automated detection methods. To address these challenges, this paper constructs a synthetic plankton hologram dataset generated through diffraction propagation simulation and proposes a lightweight detection framework named YOLO-Plankton. The proposed model introduces an improved convolutional block attention module to enhance feature representation and suppress background interference in holographic images. In addition, a lightweight detection head called LiteSPHead is designed to reduce computational complexity while maintaining effective detection capability. Experimental results show that YOLO-Plankton significantly outperforms the baseline YOLOv8n and several mainstream detection models, with the mean average precision (mAP) on the synthetic dataset increasing from 0.688 to 0.939, and demonstrates robust generalization when tested on images fused with real backgrounds.

title: Adaptive Global Attention for Image Restoration
authors: Xingyu Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the limitations of existing image restoration models in global dependency modeling and computational efficiency, this paper proposes a novel image restoration network that integrates local and global feature learning. The framework introduces a dual attention convolution block, named DACB, which preserves the efficiency of convolutional operations while effectively expanding the receptive field to enhance structural consistency and contextual awareness. In addition, an adaptive attention transformer block, named AATB, is designed to enable efficient global modeling by dynamically selecting top-K attention responses based on semantic similarity. This lightweight strategy significantly reduces computational overhead while maintaining strong long-range dependency representation. The two modules are complementary, where DACB focuses on detailed texture recovery and robust local context aggregation, while AATB improves global structure reasoning with low complexity. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior reconstruction quality and faster inference speed compared with existing approaches.

title: Design of an Intelligent Pneumatic-Vacuum Harvester for Dwarf High-Density Jujube Orchards Based on Visual Servoing and Canopy Detection
authors: Longtan Hu, Yongcheng Meng, Jinhui Wang, Jiemei Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of an autonomous harvesting robot specifically engineered for dwarf high-density jujube orchards. Addressing the inefficiency of manual labor and the low throughput of single-arm manipulators, we propose a continuous pneumatic-oscillating harvesting system. The robot integrates a heterogeneous computing platform and a lightweight YOLOv8-LCE model for real-time fruit density estimation. Instead of individual picking, the system utilizes a 2DOF adjustable suction mechanism that automatically tracks the canopy's Region of Interest (ROI) using visual servoing. To ensure robust operation in GPS-denied rows, we introduce an Octreebased RRT* algorithm for the mobile chassis's autonomous navigation. Field trials demonstrate that the system achieves a mass harvest rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$308 \text{kg} / \mathrm{h}$</tex> with a damage rate of less than 8 %, thanks to a custom-designed air-cushioned separation bin. These results validate the system's efficacy in high-throughput agricultural automation.

title: A Smart Campus Transportation Framework with Real-Time Tracking and Automated Attendance
authors: R. Swathika, N. Radha, Hari Hara Sudhan R, Kishore P, Srinivas S, Yuthika Anvitha S
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Student transportation is a critical yet often overlooked component of campus operations, commonly affected by overcrowding, uncertain arrival times, and limited visibility into student boarding, leading to safety and operational inefficiencies. Scholar Commute Aid addresses these challenges by presenting an intelligent, student-centric campus transportation system that integrates real-time bus tracking, automated student attendance, and predictive pre-arrival notifications within a unified frame-work. The system ensures that only registered day scholars are permitted to board buses while continuously monitoring capacity to prevent overcrowding, thereby enhancing safety, transparency, and commuter experience. The proposed architecture comprises an edge-based hardware module deployed on buses, a mobile application for student interaction, and a web-based administrative dashboard for fleet monitoring and control. Facial recognition is executed on a Raspberry Pi using deep facial embeddings and cosine-similarity-based matching, while real-time bus location updates are synchronized through a cloud backend. Experimental evaluation demonstrates recognition accuracies of up to 96% under controlled conditions and above 90% in real-world environments, validating the reliability and practical applicability of the system for daily campus transportation management.

title: Analysis of LLMs Against Prompt Injection and Jailbreak Attacks
authors: Piyush Jaiswal, Aaditya Pratap, Shreyansh Saraswati, Harsh Kasyap, Somanath Tripathy
publish: Proceedings of the Workshop on Privacy in Large Language Models (LLM) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) 2026
abstract: None

title: A method for creating visual cues to improve depth perception in remote machine operation. How much help is needed?
authors: Victor Zhidchenko, Egor Startcev, Heikki Handroos
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: The paper describes a method for creating visual aids that might help operators of remotely operated mobile working machines perceive depth in 2D video camera frames. The method uses RGB-D data to create a virtual shadow of the machine manipulator tip on a horizontal surface below the manipulator. The shadow is depicted as a semi-transparent line across the frame. To evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of the proposed approach, an evaluation study was performed using a remotely operated mobile crane. The results of the study are also presented.

title: Analyzing Learner Experience in Mental Health MOOCs via BERTopic: Unveiling a Tripartite Framework
authors: Yaojuan Zhang, Yaqiao Mu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Learners’ comments on Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs) provide a critical resource for optimizing digital educational resources. However, existing research pre-dominantly focuses on explicit behavioral metrics, lacking a systematic excavation of the implicit thematic structures within these comments. To comprehensively analyze learner experiences in mental health MOOCs, this study employs the advanced BERTopic modeling technique to analyze 17,239 learner comments on the "Psychology and Life" course from a leading Chinese MOOC platform. Our results reveal that learner feedback can be synthesized into a tripartite framework encompassing "Teaching & Content," "Emotion & Growth," and "Administrative & Support." Specifically, the core learning experience is shaped by the instructor’s humorous teaching style and lucid explanations, the scientific rigor and practical relevance of the course content, and the acquisition of mental health knowledge and emotional management skills. Meanwhile, certification-driven extrinsic motivation emerged as a prominent participation driver. Hierarchical clustering further validated the stability and independence of these three dimensions. These findings not only offer empirical support for cognitive-affective theory in online education but also yield practical implications for designing more engaging mental health courses and optimizing platform services.

title: Simulation and Analysis of a Power Converter for Electric Vehicles
authors: R. Ramaprabha, S. Thirukkumaran, S. R. Veeresh, J. Rohit Sharavan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This work presents a simulation methodology and analysis of a bidirectional power converter for electric vehicle applications. The proposed system integrates a high gain bidirectional DC-DC converter with a single-phase DC-AC converter to enable efficient grid to vehicle (G2V) and vehicle to grid (V2G) power flow. For both buck and boost modes, the voltage gain expressions are derived by performing steady state analysis of the converter. Furthermore, comprehensive power loss modelling including switching, conduction and passive components is presented to calculate system efficiency. The system is validated using MATLAB/Simulink under a 110 V AC grid and 48 V battery configuration. Results demonstrate an overall efficiency of 96.1 %, confirming the suitability of the proposed topology for EV charging applications.

title: The Impact of BYOD and THSD Program Participation on Parental Perspectives for Students’ Digital Learning: A Case Study of Two Elementary Schools in Hsinchu, Taiwan
authors: Hsieh I-Ling, Wang Wei-Yu, Chang Pin-Shan, Chiu Fu-Yuan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of the "Bring Your Own Device" (BYOD) and "Take-Home Student Device" (THSD) programs on parental support for digital learning in two elementary schools in Hsinchu City, Taiwan. A modified version of the Parental Digital Learning Perspectives Questionnaire for BYOD & THSD Programs (PDLPQ for BYOD & THSD), originally developed by Chang and Kao [1], was used to examine five key dimensions: Pa-rental Beliefs about Digital Learning (PB), Parental Digital Competence (PC), Support systems provided by Schools and Teachers (SS), Student learning behaviors at home (SL), and Parental Involvement in Student learning (PI). A total of 338 valid responses were collected and analyzed using t-tests, ANOVA, and Pearson correlation. Results showed that School 1 significantly outperformed School 2 in SS. Parents with Fully familiar Self-Perceived Understanding Levels (SPUL) scored significantly higher across all dimensions and overall PDLPQ for BYOD & THSD scores. Grade-level differences were found in both PI and SS. In PI, parents of younger students showed greater involvement. In contrast, SS scores varied significantly across grade levels but did not follow a linear trend of higher support for lower grades. Strong correlations were observed among all five dimensions, especially between SL and SS. The findings highlight the importance of pa-rental understanding and school support in enhancing students’ digital learning at home and provide empirical evidence to inform digital learning policy refinement.

title: Innovation-Based Adaptive Kalman Fusion for Robust Localization in Highly Dynamic Environments
authors: Yixuan Tang, Yutao Xu, Zhengye Hu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To improve localization accuracy of intelligent vehicles operating under high-speed and severe bumpy conditions, this paper proposes a multi-sensor fusion localization method for highly dynamic environments. A vehicle kinematic model with Ackermann steering constraints and a quaternion-based attitude estimation method using RK4 integration and acceleration feedback correction are employed for accurate heading estimation. To address performance degradation of the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) caused by noise mismatch, an innovation-based Adaptive Kalman Filter (AKF) is developed. The proposed method estimates innovation covariance via a sliding window and adaptively adjusts measurement noise to suppress abnormal disturbances. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach significantly reduces localization errors and improves robustness compared with conventional EKF, achieving a 40% reduction in positioning error under severe bumpy conditions.

title: Instability Analysis and Mitigation for Grid-Forming Inverters under Strong Grids
authors: Yalei Yuan, Fengjiao Dai, Lei Cui, Weiming Gu, Mingyuan Ma
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: In contrast to grid-following inverters, grid-forming converter is vulnerable to small-signal instability under strong grid conditions. Due to the direct regulation of output voltages by gridforming converters, voltage regulation becomes more challenging when two voltage sources are in close electrical proximity. This paper analyzes the impact of the power control loop on the output impedance of a virtual synchronous generator (VSG). Then, this study examines the impact of grid strength on the stability of VSG, with a focus on elucidating the instability mechanisms that arise during VSG operation in robust grid environments. Furthermore, this study reveals that while virtual impedance (VI) enhances VSG stability under strong grid conditions, improper coordination with voltage loop parameters may induce high-frequency oscillation problems. Therefore, the restrictions on voltage loop parameters under different VI were studied. Finally, this paper’s parameter design method contributes to enhanced robustness for strong-grid operation.

title: UAV-Assisted MEC: A Hybrid PSO-GA for Joint Task Offloading Optimization
authors: Cheru Haile, Zheng Xiang, Long Yang, Jabar Mahmood
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Enhancing the energy efficiency of ground users and UAVs or MECs sharing tasks in offloading mode. The main challenge to creating a UAV-assisted MEC task offloading mechanism is maintaining service quality and minimizing latency, as well as reducing energy consumption. The task offloading problem in UAV-assisted MEC systems is formulated as a mixed-integer nonlinear programming problem. We propose a joint optimization mechanism to process the tasks originated from end users, which is solved by the hybrid PSO-GA algorithm. The proposed mechanism aims to minimize energy consumption and reconstruct the service quality based on a task offloading mechanism that can accept some tolerance levels of latency in both task uplink and downlink. We utilized three network scenarios, each featuring varying numbers of UAVs, ground users, and MEC. The results show that the proposed hybrid PSO-GA outperforms, compared to benchmark studies, in terms of performance improvement mechanisms, increasing 4.7% and minimizing 7.14% of the energy efficiency of an entire network.

title: Real-Time Visual Understanding for Driver Assistance in Simulated Road Environments
authors: N Shiddharth, Ganesh Kumar Chellamani
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This research outlines a real-time simulation of advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) that uses vision-based technology to simulate lane detection, object detection and tracking, forward collision warning, and predicting intentions of pedestrians. Lane detection was accomplished using UFLD (Ultra Fast Line Detector), while multi-object tracking (MOT) was performed with YOLOv8 and ByteTrack. The collision warning functionality was implemented using a rule-based system, whereas a hybrid pedestrian intention model continuously processed real-time data. In tests on actual driving video footage, our simulation resulted in 1.52 frames per second (FPS) on a single central computing unit (CPU) with 0.82 correlation between the collision warnings and the density of pedestrians. The system demonstrates the ability to estimate pedestrian crossing behavior and provide appropriate warnings to drivers. This study confirms the viability of using integrated vision-based ADAS systems in live traffic conditions. The system was evaluated on two real-world driving videos captured under daytime (720p) and nighttime (360p) conditions.

title: Developing Systematic Capabilities in New Engineering Education: Exploration and Practice Based on the "An-Yan" Intelligent Agent
authors: Jie Chen, Guangchao Yu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The widespread application of artificial intelligence in education has profoundly reshaped modern pedagogical practices, acting as a key enabler for the development of personalized and adaptive learning models. However, New Engineering Education poses unique challenges due to its complex knowledge structures, strong interdisciplinarity, and high-level problem-solving demands. Existing approaches often fail to capture the internal logic of engineering knowledge and lack sufficient reasoning ability, limiting their effectiveness in fostering core competencies. To address this gap, we developed the An-Yan model, an intelligent agent designed to cultivate students’ systematic capabilities by integrating structured knowledge with generative reasoning. This model precisely guides the learning process, facilitating systemic knowledge construction, interdisciplinary transfer, and enhanced problem-solving skills. Teaching practice at Anhui University showed that experimental classes using the An-Yan model achieved markedly higher scores in midterm and final exams compared with control groups, confirming its effectiveness. The study highlights An-Yan as a promising approach to meeting the complex demands of New Engineering Education and advancing the development of adaptive intelligent teaching systems.

title: Exponential Control Barrier Functions for Enhanced Safety during Safety-Critical Interactions in Telemanipulation
authors: Stephan Andreas Schwarz, Ulrike Thomas
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Telemanipulation is increasingly applied in many applications. Safety is a crucial aspect for interactions of the follower with humans or the environment. This work presents a mechanism to improve safety during these interactions. This approach creates a safe set for the position of the follower using only information about the location of the desired interaction and the direction from which the robot has to approach this interaction. Utilizing exponential control barrier functions (ECBF) restrains the motion when the follower is approaching the boundary of the safe set but enables free motion within. The major benefit compared to compliant control or virtual fixtures is that the follower stays in this region independently of user inputs. A design concept for such an ECBF is proposed that provides high adaptability for task-specific adjustments. The influence of the introduced design parameter is shown. In this work, the algorithm is tested on a physical telemanipulation system using a static interaction scenario. The experiment demonstrates the potential of this approach. Although the robot violates the defined constraint on average by –0.703 mm, this approach offers a real-time capable extension for existing telemanipulation systems for safety-critical interaction tasks.

title: Deformation of the panoramic sphere into an ellipsoid to induce self-motion in telepresence users
authors: Eetu Laukka, Evan G. Center, Timo Ojala, Steven M. LaValle, Matti Pouke
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Mobile telepresence robots allow users to feel present and explore remote environments using technology. Traditionally, these systems are implemented using a camera onboard a mobile robot that can be controlled. Although high-immersion technologies, such as 360-degree cameras, can increase situational awareness and presence, they also introduce significant challenges. Additional processing and bandwidth requirements often result in latencies of up to seconds. The current delay with a 360-degree camera streaming over the internet makes real-time control of these systems difficult. Working with high-latency systems requires some form of assistance to the users.This study presents a novel way to utilize optical flow to create an illusion of self-motion to the user during the latency period between user sending motion commands to the robot and seeing the actual motion through the 360-camera stream. We find no significant benefit of using the self-motion illusion to performance or accuracy of controlling a telepresence robot with a latency of 500 ms, as measured by the task completion time and collisions into objects. Some evidence is shown that the method might increase virtual reality (VR) sickness, as measured by the simulator sickness questionnaire (SSQ). We conclude that further adjustments are necessary in order to render the method viable.

title: A Chopper Instrumentation Amplifier for High-Precision EEG Acquisition
authors: Yangfan Gui, Bolong Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This paper introduces a chopper instrumentation amplifier (IA) for high-precision electroencephalogram (EEG) acquisition in analog front-end (AFE) with two-channel differential input and single-ended output. Chopper circuits are integrated into the first stage of the three-op-amp instrumentation amplifier to achieve low offset voltage and reduce 1/f noise with a programmable gain ranging from 28 dB to 60 dB. and a bandgap reference circuit is introduced to ensure the stability and high precision of the amplifier, which can provide a reference voltage of 1.65V for the DC bias. When the amplifier is programmed at a gain of 60dB and a chopping frequency of 2.5 kHz , a high common mode rejection ratio (CMRR) is achieved. With the external second-order RC low-pass filter, the input signal frequency range of amplifier is set as 0-1 kHz, the input offset voltage is as low as 5.56 μV, the equivalent input noise at 1 kHz is 0.441 μV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">RMS</inf>. Implemented in SMIC 0.18 μm CMOS process, the circuit overall consumes a current of 2.15 mA from a 3.3 V supply voltage and occupies an active area of 0.504 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. This design provides a theoretical basis and technical reference for the development of the next generation of portable EEG monitoring devices.

title: Study of an Enhanced Algorithm for Transmission Line Foreign Object Detection Addressing Complex Background Interference
authors: Xia Wang, Zhinan Wang, Wei Jiang, Sheng Huang, Yulong Li, Qingxue Liu, Xunlan Yang, Zhe Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To address the challenges of real-time detection latency, insufficient feature extraction for small targets, and complex environmental interference in transmission line foreign object inspection, this study proposes an enhanced detection framework based on YOLOv13. First, EfficientNetV2 is employed as the backbone through stage-wise search space decomposition and Neural Architecture Search (NAS) to achieve optimal balance between hardware throughput and parameter utilization while enhancing fine texture capture capabilities. Second, a Multi-Dimensional Attention Module (MDAM) is constructed to perform dual-dimensional feature weighting through sequential channel and spatial attention mechanisms, effectively highlighting target-related features while suppressing complex environmental noise. Third, Receptive Field Attention Convolution RFAConv is adopted as an alternative to standard operators to overcome inherent constraints of parameter sharing, enabling dynamic attention to key geometric features. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved model achieves an mAP50-95 of 0.611, representing a 0.8 percentage point improvement over the baseline YOLOv13 and outperforming the entire YOLO series. Specifically, the model reaches a recall R of 0.937, the highest among all comparison models, while maintaining a top-tier mAP50 of 0.965. Ablation studies further confirm the synergistic optimization effects of EfficientNetV2, MDAM, and RFAConv in feature extraction and localization accuracy - verifying the effectiveness of the proposed model in high-fidelity, real-time foreign object detection for transmission line inspection.

title: Multi-Source Collaborative Identification of AS Border Routers via Cyberspace Mapping Engines
authors: WenHang Lou, JinLong Fei, Di Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Accurate identification of Autonomous System (AS) border routers—including vendor, model, and firmware version— is critical for routing security assessment and inter-domain traffic engineering. Existing fingerprinting techniques rely primarily on initial TTL (iTTL) signatures or SNMPv3 probing, yet both suffer from structural blind spots: iTTL signatures cannot distinguish Cisco IOS from Huawei VRP , while SNMPv3 achieves only 15–18% response rates due to widespread port filtering. This paper proposes a multi-source collaborative identification framework that systematically introduces cyberspace mapping engines (FOFA, Shodan, Censys, ZoomEye) as a third fingerprint source, complementing LFP and SNMPv3 through a unified three-level model matching pipeline and a Weighted Confidence Fusion Algorithm (WCFA) with context-aware dynamic weight adjustment. Experiments on 1,500 border routers across three geographic regions demonstrate that the tri-source fusion raises vendor identification coverage from approximately 65% to 79.4%, and WCFA achieves 91.3% resolution accuracy on 188 conflicting cases—a 7.6 percentage-point improvement over simple priority-based arbitration. Nearly half of covered routers receive model-level identification, and over one quarter obtain complete vendor-model-firmware triplets.

title: A Multi-Scale Micro-Structure Residual Enhancement Network for Liver Small Tumor Segmentation
authors: Jiaxuan Ran, Zhiyong Zhang, Yixiang Ji, Xiaoping Yi
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This paper proposes a liver small tumor segmentation method based on a multi-scale micro-structure residual augmentation module. To address challenges such as small tumor size, blurred boundaries, and low contrast, improvements are made upon the BasicUNet architecture. The MSD Task03_Liver dataset is preprocessed using HU windowing and normalization, and three-dimensional CT images are converted into 512×512 slices for training. An encoder–decoder framework is adopted, where a spatial and channel Squeeze-and-Excitation (scSE) attention mechanism is embedded in skip connections to enhance feature selectivity. A multi-scale residual augmentation module with multi-branch depthwise separable convolutions is introduced in the decoder to strengthen fine-grained structural representation. Experimental results show that the proposed model significantly improves the Dice score and reduces the HD95 metric compared with the standard UNet baseline, achieving more accurate boundary segmentation and superior performance in small tumor delineation.

title: AI-Enhanced OER Observatory: A Framework for Personalized Inclusive Learning
authors: Nicia Guillén-Yparrea, Iván Miguel García-López
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This research presents a comprehensive framework for AI-Enhanced Open Educational Resources (OER) Observatory that transforms static repositories into dynamic, personalized learning ecosystems. The Observatory integrates three core components: AI-driven recommendation algorithms for adaptive learning pathways, real-time learning analytics for continuous assessment, and Universal Design for Learning (UDL) principles to ensure accessibility across diverse learner populations. The research employs a mixed-methods approach combining theoretical framework development with simulation-based validation using synthetic educational data. Three implementation scenarios (Conservative, Intermediate, and Advanced) were systematically evaluated across utility, fairness, privacy, and governance dimensions using established datasets (OULAD, EdNet) as reference models. Each scenario underwent 100 bootstrap simulation replicates to assess performance characteristics and institutional viability. Results demonstrate that the Intermediate scenario optimally balances educational effectiveness with institutional feasibility, achieving significant improvements in predictive accuracy while maintaining manageable governance overhead and high institutional acceptability. The framework addresses critical gaps in current AI-Enhanced educational systems by preserving open, collaborative OER principles while leveraging AI capabilities for personalization and analytics.

title: Comparative Analysis of YOLOv8, ResNet, and Enhanced EfficientNet-B4 + U-Net : For Automated Road Damage Detection and Segmentation
authors: Manikandan G, Dhinesh Kumar S, G. Revathy
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The changing infrastructure maintenance and smart cities apps require automatic detection and identification of road surface damage. In this paper, comparative information on techniques of image recognition has been provided at length. Particularly, the Res-UNet structure is used as a baseline of segmentation, YOLOv8m is used to identify objects in a short time, and a better encoder with a U-Net decoder (Efficient Net-U-Net) is introduced. The experiments use a selected sample of 780 image annotations of the Pothole Image Segmentation Database, stratified in a rigid proportion of 70/15/15. The mentioned Efficient Net-U-Net has the highest segmentation performance with the accuracy rate of 97.77, F1-score (Dice) of 0.9107, and Intersection-over-Union (IoU) of 0.8415. Diagrams of the workflow and architecture are included, and the literature (2021–2026) on the same studies is extensive to have a perspective. The essay shows that a combination of EfficientNet-B4 encoder and compound scaling with skip connections of concurrent spatial and channel Squeeze and Excitation (scSE) and multi-scale loss yields the best boundary fidelity. This enables the model to effectively indicate anomalies and not yield false positives in locations with water puddles but still the viable edge latency is 29.8 FPS.

title: Attack-Resilient Trust Management Framework for SDN-Enabled IoT Networks Using Blockchain
authors: Wiem Bekri, Rihab Jmal, Lamia Chaari Fourati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Cyberattacks on IoT infrastructures are increasingly exploiting the lack of coordination between detection methods and network-level response strategies. Attacks like denial-of-service flooding and man-in-the-middle poisoning can quickly disrupt communication. At the same time, existing security solutions often struggle to turn detection results into immediate actions for mitigation. This paper presents ARTM-SBIoT, an attack-resilient trust management framework based on SDN/blockchain for IoT Networks. ARTM-SBIoT framework is designed to enable a coordinated response to attacks in IoT environments. A Proof-of-Authority blockchain is used to maintain unchangeable records of trusted and blocked devices through smart contracts. SDN controllers then dynamically enforce mitigation policies. The proposed framework was implemented in a realistic emulation environment and tested under various attack scenarios. These included external and internal DoS attacks, distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, and man-in-the-middle attacks based on ARP and NDP poisoning. Traffic analysis and packet-level observations illustrate how the framework can identify malicious behavior, update device trust levels through blockchain records, and isolate compromised nodes via SDN-based policy enforcement. The results demonstrate the feasibility of coordinated trust management and network-level mitigation in IoT environments.

title: Machine Learning Approach for Alzheimer’s Classification: PCA Feature Extraction and Random Forest Modeling for Prediction and Classification
authors: Asif Raza, Chandanlal Karnani, Shardha Nand
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Timely intervention is important to the management of mental health and the management of the Alzheimer disease. This research paper suggests a machine learning system that combines both unsupervised and supervised methods to make a perfect prediction. K-Means clustering is used to conduct unsupervised learning and Silhouette scores are used to determine the best clusters. PCA is a dimensionality reduction technique that is used to reduce the number of features in a dataset but does not affect any variance, which enhances the comprehensibility of a model and its computational efficiency. The model is trained on supervised modeling using the Random Forest Classifier with an Accuracy of $95.1 \%$ which is able to produce nonlinear relationships and feature interactions with hyper parameters being optimized using Randomized Search. The data set used in the study is acquired on Kaggle and is segmented into training and validation to provide a sound evaluation. The effectiveness of the suggested method is shown by performance indicators where the weighted averages are Precision 95.3, Recall 92.1, and F1-Score 93.6, which means that it is highly predictive. The findings highlight the promise of this combined machine learning model as a viable aid in the early detection of Alzheimer to assist in making timely clinical decisions and better patient outcomes.

title: The development of board games with an augmented reality technology integration of an artificial intelligence to develop analytical thinking skills on hydrocarbon compounds for grade 12 students
authors: Chutika Srisawat, Issara Kanjug
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the context of rapid economic, social, and technological change, science education in Thailand must increasingly emphasize the development of essential 21-century skills, especially analytical thinking. However, learning outcomes in science education of senior high school student continue to reveal limitations in students' conceptual understanding and their ability to apply knowledge meaningfully. These challenges are partly associated with instructional practices that prioritize passive knowledge transmission and rote memorization rather than learning experiences that engage students in experiental activities, analysis, and reasoned decision-making. This study aimed to developing a board game integrated with augmented reality (AR) and artificial intelligence (AI), grounded in constructivist learning principles, to enhance analytical thinking skills in learning hydrocarbons in chemistry subject for Grade 12 students. The participants were 35 students from schools in the northeastern region of Thailand. A one-group pretest-posttest experimental design was applied. Data were collected using an analytical thinking skills test including three components: classification, identification of relationships, and categorization.
