The study concludes that the CIPP model provides a comprehensive framework for assessing and improving digital literacy integration in teacher education, ensuring curriculum responsiveness and educational quality in the context of 21st-century learning.

title: Research on Security and Stability Issues of Extremely Weak Sending-End Power Grid Under Access of Multi-Terminal UHV Flexible DC
authors: Wenman Gao, Shaoxuan Zhu, Xiangyi Chen, Meng Chen
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To address the security and stability risks arising from the integration of multi-terminal UHV flexible DC systems into extremely weak sending-end AC grids, and to solve the stability challenges caused by insufficient rotational inertia, weak voltage support, and control interactions among multi-terminal flexible DC systems in extremely weak grids, this paper combines the technical characteristics of multi-terminal UHV flexible DC and the operation features of extremely weak sending-end grids. It analyzes the core factors affecting the power system security and stability after integration, investigates the characteristics and criteria of different security and stability issues, and conducts case simulation studies. The results show that there exist interactive influences on active and reactive power as well as coupling effects between AC and DC faults in the hybrid system consisting of multi-terminal flexible DC and weak sending-end AC grids. The case simulations demonstrate that generator rotor angle instability occurs after DC blocking faults. The main measure adopted is to advance the action time of the security and stability control system for generator tripping.

title: Graph-Theoretic Labeling Architecture for Robust Frequency Management in Evolving Wireless Topologies
authors: Kowsalya.S, S.Buvaneswari, K.Thiyagarajan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Contemporary wireless infrastructures face escalating complexities due to volatile network topologies and erratic traffic demands. Traditional frequency allocation protocols often struggle to maintain stability amidst rapid node mobility and heterogeneous signal environments, primarily due to a lack of proactive fault-tolerance mechanisms. This research introduces a sophisticated graph-labeling framework designed specifically to enhance error resilience in such fluid network conditions. By mapping network entities as vertices and signal interferences as weighted edges, the proposed model assigns frequency channels through a distance-constrained labeling process. A pivotal innovation of this framework is its decentralized "localized correction" mechanism; rather than reconfiguring the entire network upon a failure, the system identifies and rectifies spectral conflicts within a specific neighborhood. This targeted approach significantly curtails signaling overhead and prevents cascading network instability. These refined labels are integrated into real-time scheduling policies, fostering equitable resource distribution, minimized latency, and optimized spectral utilization. Grounded in rigorous graph theory, this self-healing methodology provides an adaptive solution for dense and high-mobility deployments. Extensive simulation benchmarks demonstrate that this framework substantially mitigates co-channel interference and delivers superior throughput consistency, positioning it as a vital contributor to the evolution of 5G-Advanced and 6G communication standards.

title: TIME: A Taylor-Inspired Mixed-Effects Model for IDH Prediction_supp1-3693918.pdf
authors: Pengwei Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: Background: Intradialytic hypotension (IDH) is a critical complication in hemodialysis that increases morbidity and treatment risks, yet existing machine learning methods inadequately address session-level physiological state heterogeneity and fail to balance linear and nonlinear feature interactions, limiting predictive accuracy and interpretability. Methods: We developed TIME (Taylor-Inspired Mixed-Effects Model), which explicitly separates linear interactions, higher-order nonlinear dependencies, and residual terms inspired by Taylor series expansion, while incorporating a physiological state embedding layer to capture session-level physiological state heterogeneity revealed through hierarchical clustering on autoencoder-derived latent representations of hematologic indicators selected using SHAP analysis and clinical knowledge. Using data from 532 hemodialysis patients (18,309 records), we compared TIME against 19 state-of-the-art baseline models, including blood pressure-stratified subcohort and out-of-distribution analyses. Results: Hierarchical clustering identified two distinct hematological states with different IDH risks (57.93% vs. 55.75%, $p=6.60\times 10^{-7}$). TIME achieved 0.6929 accuracy, 0.7435 F1-score, and 0.3663 MCC on the full cohort, the highest AUC across all three subcohorts (0.7010, 0.6795, and 0.7201), and strong out-of-distribution performance (accuracy 0.7053, F1 0.7377, MCC 0.4009). Integrating TIME's architecture into baseline models improved MCC by 2.67 percentage points on average. Conclusions: TIME effectively predicts IDH by modeling session-level physiological state heterogeneity and balancing feature interactions, with robust generalizability across diverse populations. Significance: TIME advances precision medicine in dialysis care by enabling early risk identification and personalized treatment strategies, while revealing broader immune, nutritional, and fluid-related markers of IDH risk.

title: Magnetic Confinement of Dust Particles: Novel Instrumentation for Potash Mining Applications
authors: A. Okusanya, R. Paranjape
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The control of dust particles in mining environments poses an occupational health and operational challenge. Traditional mitigation strategies, including water sprays, surfactants, and baghouse systems, face limitations due to high costs, maintenance requirements, and safety concerns. This paper introduces a novel instrumentation and measurement system designed to monitor triboelectric charging of moving dust particles and employ those measurements to evaluate magnetic confinement in potash mining. The setup combines mass, differential voltage, capacitive voltage, differential pressure and air quality monitoring measurements to monitor particle charging and the resulting transport behavior. The results from these experiments demonstrated that magnetic confinement improves dust capture efficiency and reduces the outlet particle concentration by up to 75%. These findings demonstrate that combining triboelectric charging with external magnetic fields provides an effective dry method for dust mitigation. It also showcases the depth of the instrumentation and its potential to support sustainable dust control in mining operations.

title: CFMDCTCodec: A Low-Bitrate Neural Speech Codec with Noise-Prior-aware Conditional Flow Matching for MDCT-Spectral Enhancement
authors: Xiao-Hang Jiang, Yang Ai, Hui-Peng Du, Zhen-Hua Ling, Ji Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: High-quality speech coding at low bitrates is crucial for bandwidth-constrained applications, yet remains challenging due to the severe loss of quality-critical information in highly compressed representations. To overcome this challenge, we propose CFMDCTCodec, a low-bitrate neural speech codec that operates entirely in the modified discrete cosine transform (MDCT) domain. CFMDCTCodec integrates a lightweight encoder-quantizer-decoder-style MDCT-spectral codec with a noise-prior-aware, conditional-flow-matching (CFM)-based MDCT-spectral enhancer. Within this framework, the codec serves as a base module that compactly discretizes the MDCT spectrum extracted from speech and produces an initial coarse reconstruction, while the enhancer further restores fine-grained spectral details. The enhancer improves the decoded MDCT spectrum by integrating a conditional MDCT velocity-field filter with an ordinary differential equation (ODE) solver, under the guidance of an MDCT-derived magnitude-adaptive noise prior, aiming to emphasize perceptually significant high-energy regions while stabilizing low-energy and silent regions. Finally, the enhanced MDCT spectrum is reconstructed into the decoded speech using the inverse MDCT. When optimizing CFMDCTCodec, we adopt a unified non-adversarial training strategy that jointly combines reconstruction, quantization and CFM objectives. Both objective and subjective evaluations show that CFMDCTCodec outperforms competitive baselines in low-bitrate regimes, e.g., 0.65 kbps, while approaching the perceptual quality of large-scale codecs with significantly fewer parameters and computations.

title: SkyDSafe: Federated Learning Driven Security for Drone Data
authors: Soniya Rohhila, Kedar Nath Singh, Amit Kumar Singh, Brij B. Gupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: With the rapid development of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), such as drones, data can be gathered from multiple sources across various sectors. Compared with other media, images are the most vital carriers of information and are more lifelike. As illegal access to these images can result in serious privacy breaches, ensuring the authenticity of digital data remains a challenging task for drones. This paper proposes a method, called SkyDSafe, for securing image data based on federated learning and advanced encryption techniques. To address data poisoning attacks, this paper employs a cryptographic Merkle tree commitment over the training dataset, allowing auditors to detect data poisoning via Merkle proofs and each client’s training transcript. Using a multi-scale deep neural network, tamper-proof image features are effectively divided into abstract representations and secret hierarchical tensors. Subsequently, only the abstract representations of the image are encrypted through a cryptographic embedder to balance the efficiency and visual security. At the receiver end, the encrypted abstract image is decrypted and combined with the corresponding hierarchical tensors retrieved from the central server to reconstruct the original image. A comparative analysis with existing techniques highlights the advantages of SkyDSafe, including tamper-proof learning and reduced time and cost, as demonstrated by the experimental results. This aspect makes SkyDSafe a robust security solution for protecting drone data and other critical applications.

title: Robust Phase Unwrapping for Near-Field mmWave Phase Imaging: An Integrated TGV and NLSS Approach
authors: Yiming Ding, Xifeng Li, Dongjie Bi, Mingwu Tu, Libiao Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Near-field millimeter-wave (mmWave) phase imaging is a powerful non-destructive testing (NDT) technique. Phase unwrapping (PU), a critical step in this process, directly determines the accuracy of subsequent defect evaluation. Conventional two-stage denoising and PU algorithms often suffer from error propagation, while existing integrated methods are prone to generating erroneous edges when applied to near-field mmWave data. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework that integrates denoising and PU. The proposed method employs a weighted Poisson equation as the data fidelity term, regularized by a Total Generalized Variation (TGV) functional to effectively suppress noise while preserving sharp phase transitions. Furthermore, a novel quality map based on non-local self-similarity (NLSS) is introduced to adaptively weight the fidelity term, significantly enhancing performance in regions with a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through extensive experiments on real-world near-field mmWave data from diverse samples, including metallic targets, polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) blocks, and multi-layer composites. Results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high-precision phase unwrapping with superior structural fidelity, providing a highly reliable solution for complex NDT scenarios.

title: SPARNet: A Framework for Airway Invasion Tracking from Fluoroscopic Videos of Dysphagia Patients
authors: Sanjeevi G, Uma Gopalakrishnan, Rahul Krishnan Pathinarupothi, Arya CJ, Kaustuv M. Talukdar, Subramania Iyer, Srisairam Achuthan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The videofluoroscopic swallowing study (VFSS) is the clinical gold standard for evaluating dysphagia and detecting airway invasion. However, manual interpretation is time-consuming, subjective, and prone to rater variability due to the rapid and complex nature of swallowing events. Existing deep learning approaches for airway invasion are limited by relying on manual frame selection, a lack of interpretability, and black-box decision-making. In this study, we propose SPARNet (Swallowing Phase Airway Risk Network), AI pipeline integrates swallowing-phase classification, bolus and airway delineation, and airway-invasion tracking to provide a clinically interpretable assessment. SPARNet introduces a novel Airway Invasion Tracker (AIT) that partitions the visible laryngo-tracheal air column in VFSS into quadrants, tracks the location of the bolus, and computes temporal invasion parameters (event duration, total exposure time, and number of invasion episodes) to characterize airway invasion during the pharyngeal phase. We curated a VFSS-MultiLabel dataset comprising 125 clips from 75 patients, annotated for multiple tasks. SPARNet achieved strong performance across all components, including the pharyngeal phase detection (F1-score = 88%), bolus segmentation (Dice score = 0.83), laryngo-tracheal air column detection (mAP = 0.95), and airway invasion detection (F1-score = 92%). Unlike prior black-box models, SPARNet provides transparent spatial and temporal cues that align with clinical VFSS reasoning. These results demonstrate the feasibility of an system-level AI pipeline for VFSS analysis, highlighting the potential to support clinical decision-making in the management of dysphagia.

title: PCCA: A Hypothesis-Driven Prompt-Conditioned Causal Auditing Framework for Industrial Knowledge Graphs
authors: Hongwei Jiang, Jiapeng You, Jiayu Shi, Zhiyang Chen, Huaxing Gou, Xinguo Ming, Poly Z.H. Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Knowledge-driven industrial systems are brittle to dynamic conditions as their knowledge graphs (KGs) are static. This leads to costly decision failures (e.g., needless hardware replacement) and erodes operator trust. These KGs remain static because existing knowledge completion and causal discovery methods struggle with statistical correlations or complex industrial data, lacking a mechanism to proactively find their own knowledge deficiencies. To address this knowledge maintenance gap, this article proposes prompt-conditioned causal auditing (PCCA), a framework for the systematic, incremental auditing of existing KGs. PCCA translates operational hypotheses into prompts, guiding a deep graph model to efficiently evaluate their structural impact. This hypothesis-driven audit precisely locates overlooked or erroneous causal relationships. The framework is validated on both public benchmarks (Win95pts, DREAM4) and an industrial inkjet case study, demonstrating robust generalizability across diverse topologies. PCCA provides a practical process for evolving these knowledge assets, enhancing operational reliability and preventing unnecessary maintenance.

title: Reliability Class Testing Hypothesis for EBUCL*t 0 with Applications for Medical and Engineering Data Modeling
authors: Walid B. H. Etman, Rashad M. El-Sagheer, Mahmoud E. Bakr, Arwa M. Alshangiti, Farouq Mohammad A. Alam, Oluwafemi Samsonbalogun, Ali M. Sharawy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a new reliability class, the exponential better than used after age t0 in increasing convex Laplace transform order (EBUCL∗t0), and develops a corresponding statistical test for exponentiality. The test is constructed within the U-statistics framework and its theoretical properties, including asymptotic distribution and Pitman’s asymptotic efficiency, are established. Extensive Monte Carlo simulations are conducted to evaluate critical values and power performance under both complete and censored samples. Results demonstrate that the proposed procedure achieves higher efficiency and superior power compared to existing nonparametric tests. Applications to diverse engineering datasets (electrical insulation, glass fiber strength, polyester fibers, LED lifetimes, and windshields failure) and biomedical data (melanoma patients) confirm its practical utility in detecting deviations from exponentiality and modeling aging phenomena. Overall, the EBUCL∗t0 test provides a robust and versatile tool for reliability engineering and survival analysis, particularly in scenarios involving aging effects and censored data.

title: Component-Reuse Hybrid Filter Design for Effective Leakage Current Suppression in Parallel Inverter Motor Drive Systems
authors: Qiao Li, Ziyao Zhang, Yun Huang, Jiale Li, Xiaokang Zhang, Chao Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Common-mode (CM) leakage current in parallel-inverter motor drive systems degrades electromagnetic compatibility and system reliability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a component-reuse hybrid filtering topology that integrates passive and active functions through a unified design framework, eliminating the need for dedicated CM filtering components. The key contribution lies in a dual-level functional reuse strategy. At the magnetic level, the leakage inductance of the coupled inductor, originally used for circulating-current suppression, is directly reused as the CM filtering inductance, forming a unified passive attenuation path without additional magnetic components. At the circuit level, a voltage-sensing current-compensation (VSCC) active stage is implemented using a dual-channel operational amplifier, enabling shared sensing and injection while reducing hardware complexity. A frequency-domain model is developed to describe the sensing, amplification, and compensation stages, and the impedance-based mechanism of CM attenuation is analytically established. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with the interleaved baseline, the proposed filter reduces CM leakage-current RMS and peak values by 64.2% and 71.9%, respectively, and suppresses the resonant peak by 29 dB. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed component-reuse hybrid filter for compact and wideband CM suppression in motor drive systems.

title: Bdes: Blockchain-Empowered Data and Energy Sharing in Internet of Electric Vehicles
authors: Xu Yang, Saiyu Qi, Wei Wei, Hongguang Zhao, Jingxian Cheng, Xinpei Dong, Qiuhao Wang, Ke Li, Wenjia Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The Internet of Electric Vehicles (IoEV) facilitates intelligent and sustainable transportation by enabling electric vehicles (EVs) to participate in traffic data sharing and energy trading. However, the highly dynamic and decentralized nature of IoEV introduces critical challenges in identity authentication, data privacy, and trading security. Traditional centralized solutions are vulnerable to single points of failure and privacy leakage, while existing blockchain-based approaches often lack fine-grained identity control and unified handling of both data and energy interactions. To address these challenges, we propose Bdes, a blockchain-empowered data and energy sharing system tailored for IoEV environments. Bdes adopts a modular architecture that integrates three core components: a Decentralized Identifiers (DID) management module for decentralized identity registration and Verifiable Credential (VC) binding; a requirement matching module that enables automated, privacy-preserving authentication and matching between data and energy providers and consumers via smart contracts; and a trading module that decouples key exchange from data transmission, ensuring secure, tamper-proof, and traceable trading. By leveraging Decentralized Identifiers, Verifiable Credentials, and blockchain smart contracts, Bdes supports flexible, secure, and transparent sharing of traffic data and energy information across multiple untrusted entities. Extensive security analysis shows that Bdes effectively resists credential theft, inference attacks, and man-in-the-middle threats. Experimental evaluations demonstrate the system’s scalability, efficiency, and suitability for large-scale deployment in IoEV ecosystems.

title: Geometry-Driven Performance Optimization of Dispense-Printed Interdigitated Electrodes for Potassium Biosensing_supp1-3694171.pdf
authors: Shamim Torkian
publish: N/A
abstract: Printed impedimetric ion-selective sensors based on interdigitated electrodes (IDEs) enable low-cost and flexible ion sensing, yet the role of electrode geometry under practical printing constraints remains poorly understood. Here, dispense-printed carbon IDEs were systematically optimized by varying finger length and inter-electrode gap to study their effect on interfacial capacitance and impedimetric response. Capacitance measurements and analytical IDE modeling reveal a trade-off between electrical performance and printability, yielding an optimized geometry with a 7 mm finger length and a 250 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>m gap. Dispense-printed potassium sensors integrating a PEDOT:PSS solid-contact layer and a drop-cast ion-selective membrane (ISM) show stable, concentration-dependent responses from 0.1 to 100 mM. At 10 kHz, the normalized impedance change reaches up to 39% per decade. These results highlight geometry-aware IDE design for scalable printed impedimetric ion sensors.

title: Analysis of Amplitude-Frequency and Phase-Frequency Responses of Transient Electrostatic Sensor Signals
authors: Lizhi Hong, Rongxiang Xia, Shaojie Ma, Zhiyuan Ai
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In recent years, electrostatic sensors have found widespread application in fields such as human body state detection and projectile detection. Due to their simple structure, high cost-effectiveness, and adaptability to various installation conditions, electrostatic sensors are often employed as measurement solutions for specific systems, or combined with other sensors (such as magnetic or infrared sensors) to form a composite detection system. Addressing the characteristics of various conditioning circuits for electrostatic sensors and their transient signals, this paper designs an electrostatic sensor based on a potential amplifier and establishes an experimental apparatus to test its amplitude-frequency and phase-frequency responses. An equivalent circuit model is established to quantify the interaction relationship between charged objects and electrodes through coupling capacitances, and a finite element simulation is used to model the relationship between the transmitting electrode voltage and the induction electrode charge. By applying a controllable voltage signal to the transmitting electrode to generate a varying electric field signal, and consequently inducing an electrostatic signal on the induction electrode, this study investigates the factors influencing the amplitude-frequency and phase-frequency characteristics of the induced signal. Furthermore, reasonable measures are proposed to optimize electrode design and cable routing.

title: Preserving the Intangible: A Signal Processing and Deep Learning Framework for the Stylistic Analysis and Generative Archiving of Eastern Oud Heritage
authors: Mehdi Zhar, Fatima-Zohra El Aahed, Houria Kelkoul, Safaa Bendhiba
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The preservation of Eastern musical heritage faces a significant technological challenge: the inadequacy of standard Western digitization methods to capture the microtonal nuances and complex improvisational structures inherent to the region's traditions. This paper proposes a novel computational framework designed to document, classify, and preserve the playing styles of renowned Oud masters. The methodology integrates a theoretical physical modeling of the instrument's vibration dynamics with an advanced feature extraction pipeline utilizing the Constant Q Transform (CQT) to resolve quarter-tone intervals. By constructing a specialized dataset derived from the works of masters such as Munir Bashir and Nasser Shamma, we evaluate various Machine Learning architectures. Results indicate that Deep Learning models, specifically Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and Neural Networks, significantly outperform traditional classifiers (SVM, KNN) in recognizing stylistic signatures, offering a promising avenue for the generative preservation of this intangible heritage.

title: The Current Situation Analysis and Improvement Strategies for University Teachers' Digital Intelligence Literacy in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
authors: Caixia Yang, Hongming Zhang, Yanxue Zhang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Against the backdrop of educational transformation driven by artificial intelligence, enhancing teachers' digital and intelligent literacy has become the core demand of educational modernization. This study conducted a questionnaire survey among 217 in-service teachers across the country and analyzed the data using Python. It was found that there is a significant structural imbalance in the digital intelligence literacy of teachers at present: teachers perform well in the dimensions of digital intelligence awareness and ethics, but score relatively low in the dimensions of human-computer collaborative reconstruction and high-level integrated application, presenting the characteristic of "knowledge being strong but weak". In response to the above-mentioned predicaments, this paper proposes paths such as building a development community for teachers and students, implementing classified and stratified training strategies, and optimizing the external empowerment environment, aiming to provide theoretical references for the digital and intelligent transformation of university teachers.

title: A Secure Federated Learning Approach for Privacy-Aware Bank Transaction Fraud Detection Using Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Transformers
authors: Weddou Mohamedhen, Maha Charfeddine, Yessine Hadj Kacem
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Credit card fraud detection systems must balance predictive accuracy with strict data privacy requirements. This study introduces a Federated Learning framework that enables collaborative fraud detection without centralized data sharing, preserving confidentiality and regulatory compliance. Our pipeline integrates advanced preprocessing techniques including ANOVA F-score-based feature selection and data balancing via SMOTE and ADASYN with both classical and Deep Learning models. We conducted extensive experiments on a real-world imbalanced dataset, using a standardized evaluation strategy with F1-Score, Recall, Precision , and optimized classification thresholds. The strengths and limitations of various detection techniques were examined, ranging from classical machine learning models (Random Forest, XGBoost) to Deep Learning architectures (MLP,CNN, LSTM, and Transformers). Results show that Random Forest combined with ADASYN achieves the most consistent performance, especially in recall-sensitive scenarios, while MLP and LSTM models also perform competitively when enhanced with proper feature selection. Additionally, federated soft voting and federated averaging were explored as aggregation strategies,demonstrating effectiveness in both classical and neural contexts. Overall, the proposed framework provides a reproducible, privacy-preserving, and high-performance solution for modern fraud detection. Its adaptability and strong empirical results make it a promising candidate for scalable deployment in secure financial environments.

title: Monolithic Quantum Random Number Generator Containing Si-LED, SPAD and Analog Processing
authors: Horst Zimmermann, David Bugl, Bernhard Goll, Michael Hofbauer, Kerstin Schneider-Hornstein, Hiwa Mahmoudi
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: This work describes a monolithically integrated photonic quantum random number generator (QRNG) in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.35 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> CMOS. This QRNG consists of a light-emitting diode inside a deep N-well, a ring single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD), a fast cascoded active quenching and resetting circuit, and an analog evaluation circuit determining whether the random bit is a “0” or “1”. The random bits are created by comparison of two consecutive time intervals between photon detections using charging and discharging a capacitor with a constant current as well as a comparator. The time intervals can be in the range from 10 ns to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>. With <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 3.3 ~\mathrm{V}$</tex> supply voltage, the power consumption of the complete circuits is between 1.1 mW and 4.55 mW depending on the LED emission and detection rate of the SPAD. After XOR-hashing, the NIST tests were passed.

title: Vibration Analysis of a High-altitude Cable Deicing Robot
authors: Zhiyi Peng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: High-altitude cable deicing robots play a crucial role in the operation of power systems, serving as one of the guarantees for stable power supply. However, few studies have focused on vibration analysis and effective vibration reduction for such robots. This paper presents a research on a certain type of high-altitude cable deicing robot. It employs the finite element simulation method to analyze its overall dynamic characteristics, including natural frequencies and harmonic response. Based on the analysis results, the weak points of the robot are identified and corresponding optimization measures are proposed. Research demonstrates that the robot's obstacle surmounting linkages and their joints with the body need to be reinforced. The analysis process and methods in this paper provide a reference for the vibration analysis of similar equipment.

title: Intelligent Monitoring and Early Warning of Wear State of Spiral Roller Brush Based on U-Net
authors: Haitao Li, Xinquan Xu, Tengfei Jiang, Yuanjun Wang, Wei Wang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: It is very important to ensure the product quality and the stable operation of the equipment for the cleaning of the track of the storage container distributor in the tobacco industry. Aiming at the problems of traditional fixed brush cleaning device, such as fast wear of brush bristles, poor cleaning effect, easy to cause foreign body pollution and heavy maintenance workload, a new mechanical design with spiral rolling brush and directional transmission mechanism is proposed, which significantly improves the mechanical performance, but its condition monitoring still depends on manual work. In recent years, computer vision technology based on deep learning has provided a new way for predictive maintenance of industrial equipment. This work explores the application of U-Net convolutional neural network model to the intelligent recognition of the wear state of spiral roller brush. The U-Net model is trained for pixel-level segmentation by constructing a roll brush image dataset containing three levels of “normal”, “lightly worn”, and “severely worn”. The experimental results show that the proposed model achieves 98.7 % accuracy and 92.4% average intersection ratio on the test set, and the segmentation accuracy is significantly better than that of the comparison model. Furthermore, the state warning is carried out based on the wear area proportion calculated by the segmentation result, and the recall rates of slight wear and serious wear are 93.5 % and 94.8% respectively, showing extremely high fault capture rate and reliability. This work verifies the feasibility and great potential of visual recognition algorithm to achieve automated and intelligent predictive maintenance in specific industrial scenarios.

title: Research on Point Cloud Registration Algorithm Based on Bias Constraint and Feature Enhancement
authors: Bihui Jin, Hui Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In the case of transmission towers leaving the factory, the related components lie flat in sections, and the problem of “sparse point cloud features leading to insufficient registration accuracy” is prone to occur in the measurement of key parameters. Taking Angle steel towers as an example, this paper proposes a point cloud registration algorithm that integrates the deviation constraint of trial assembly of this type of tower with local feature enhancement. Firstly, the local feature recognition of the point cloud is enhanced through curvature and normal vector analysis. Then, the engineering deviation standards such as root width and crossarm spacing are transformed into the geometric constraint embedding registration optimization process, and on-site tests and actual tower scanning verification are conducted. The proposed algorithm is compared and verified with traditional ICP and FPFH+ICP algorithms based on both simulated data and actual measured data. Finally, a simulated point cloud was generated using Blender and compared and verified with the traditional ICP and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{FPFH}+\text{ICP}$</tex> algorithms. Experiments show that the proposed algorithm improves the registration accuracy of key parameters such as root opening and vertical spacing of crossloads by 48.15% to 66.14% compared with traditional ICP and by 17.48% to 41.51% compared with FPFH+ICP. Moreover, its operational efficiency meets the requirements of industrial online measurement, providing technical support for high-precision non-contact measurement of transmission towers leaving the factory.

title: Research on Lithium-Ion Battery Capacity Prediction Based on a Fusion Architecture of Adaptive Empirical Wavelet Transform and Transformer
authors: Chenglin Li, Xiaoxia Han, Wenjie Zhang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Accurate prediction of lithium-ion battery capacity is crucial for ensuring the safe operation of electric vehicles and energy storage systems. Aiming at the problems of non-stationary noise in battery monitoring data and the difficulty in capturing long-term dependencies, this paper proposes a fused prediction framework integrating adaptive Empirical Wavelet Transform (EWT) and Transformer. First, EWT is utilized to decompose the raw voltage or capacity sequence into multiple modes to separate high-frequency noise and extract intrinsic components reflecting degradation features. Subsequently, the preprocessed sequence is fed into a Transformer network based on a multi-head attention mechanism, leveraging its powerful parallel processing capability to capture the long-term trend of battery capacity degradation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed architecture achieves superior prediction accuracy and robustness compared to traditional baseline models.

title: Beyond the Pixels: Deep Learning-Based Classification of AI-Generated Images
authors: L. Jabasheela, B Hariharan, T Kathireswar, K Gowtham
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: With the use of complex models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Diffusion-based models, and text-to-image models, the development of Generative Artificial Intelligence has enabled the creation of synthetic images that are extremely realistic. Although Generative Artificial Intelligence has revolutionized industrial and artistic processes, it has significantly threatened digital trust since it has enabled the creation of manipulated and false images.As such, differentiating real images from AI-generated images has become an important problem in authenticating images and detecting possible cyber threats. The purpose of this study is to create an effective and efficient framework for detecting AI-generated images through the integration of Convolutional Neural Networks and Vision Transformers and Adaptive Optimization. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs) are integrated in the proposed hybrid deep learning framework for detecting AIgenerated images, which is further improved through the proposed Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO)-based hyperparameter tuning strategy. The proposed framework utilizes the self-attention mechanism of the Vision Transformer for learning global contextual dependencies and incorporates CNN for learning localized texture and noise-based artifacts. The optimization method proposed in this study is based on Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and is used to adaptively optimize critical hyperparameters for better convergence and efficiency. The proposed framework is tested on a large-scale Kaggle AI Image Dataset containing 60,000 images: 30,000 real images and 30,000 images generated by Stable Diffusion, MidJourney, and DALL-E.The proposed framework has shown promising results with an accuracy of 98.7%, an F1-score of 98.4%, and an ROC-AUC of 0.992. The experimental results show that the proposed framework is better than CNN and ViT models.

title: Digital Evidence Extraction And Source Image in Disk Forensics Using Parallel Data Streams
authors: Bruno A, Shivanthana S
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The speed and reliability of data extraction from a hard drive is essential for digital forensic investigations to maintain the integrity of evidence created during the investigation. Current forensic applications such as FTK and Autopsy are limited by low extraction speeds because of multiple levels of software, low hardware optimizations, and poor performance with high-capacity storage devices. In order to address these challenges, the RapidDD-Forensic (an advanced digital disk image extraction system) can be connected directly to a storage medium via hardware. Once the RapidDD-Forensic tool is installed and powered on, the operating system and middleware layers are bypassed, enabling data to be extracted from the physical storage device at a low level, which optimally accelerates the data acquisition process using C++. The RapidDD-Forensic system has the capability to perform sector-level imaging while still maintaining the forensic integrity of the data collected by using cryptographic hashing and maintaining compliance with the established standard format of forensic disk images. Optimally controlling how data is extracted and processed using multiple streams of parallel data further improves efficiency. Several experiments conducted on the RapidDD-Forensic system indicate that it can produce significantly faster imaging speeds than existing tools, increase the amount of source media utilized, improve device compatibility with different types of storage media and maintain the overall integrity of the source media. These capabilities will further enhance the incident response capabilities of law enforcement and provide a scalable, standards-compliant solution for digital forensic investigations today.

title: Systematic Comparison of LSTM, BiLSTM, GRU, and Hybrid CNN-LSTM Networks for Lithium-Ion Battery Capacity Degradation Prediction
authors: Zerui Wang, Man Lu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Accurate prediction of capacity degradation for lithium ion batteries is critical for ensuring the reliability and safety of electric vehicles and energy storage systems. Capacity prediction serves as the foundation for remaining useful life (RUL) estimation, enabling the determination of when battery capacity will cross a predefined end-of-life threshold. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of five deep learning architectures for battery capacity prediction, including Standard Long Short Term Memory, Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory, Gated Recurrent Unit, Convolutional Neural Network combined with Long Short Term Memory, and Stacked Long Short Term Memory networks. The proposed models are evaluated using the NASA battery dataset, which contains discharge cycle data from four lithium ion batteries. Experimental results demonstrate that the Gated Recurrent Unit model achieves superior performance with a root mean square error of 0.0231 Ah, mean absolute error of 0.0150 Ah, and coefficient of determination of 0.9734. The findings provide valuable insights for selecting appropriate deep learning architectures for battery health management applications.

title: Decoding Autonomic Stress Responses Using Multimodal Photoplethysmographic Representations and Boosted Learning Frameworks
authors: Kabilaesh M, Lingesh Karthikeyan M E, Akilandeswary G
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Stress is one of the significant determinants in physical and mental health where it must be detected properly and without the necessity of employing invasive techniques so that appropriate interventions can be implemented in good time. The paper deals with the problem of credible stress classification using the photoplethysmography (PPG) signals and the latest machine learning methods. A time-frequency-nonlinear scheme is suggested, which distorts time domain, frequency domain and nonlinear characteristics of PPG signals to reflect stress related physiological changes. The classification of these features with CatBoost and LightGBM algorithms is due to the capability of dealing with intricate feature interactions and helping to increase the accuracy of prediction. The suggested technique increases sensibility to noise and intersubject variation, which are typically found in wearable sensor data. The experimental analysis has shown a better classification performance than the conventional models with LightGBM having faster computation and CatBoost having better generalization. The results validate the usefulness of the PPG based stress detection and emphasize the appropriateness of the gradient boosting models in relation to the real-time monitoring of the state of physiology through wearable health care systems.

title: An Intelligent Vehicle Path Planning Algorithm Integrating Artificial Potential Field Guidance and D3QN
authors: Ertai Ban, Jilin Gu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To mitigate the issues of slow convergence, low sample efficiency, and blind initial exploration inherent in traditional Deep Q-Networks (DQN) for intelligent vehicle path planning, this paper proposes an improved D3QN algorithm integrated with Artificial Potential Field (APF) guidance. First, the Dueling Double DQN architecture is adopted to decompose the Q-function into state value and action advantage streams, while decoupling action selection from evaluation to enhance decision stability in complex environments. Second, a Prioritized Experience Replay (PER) mechanism is integrated to optimize the utilization of critical samples by prioritizing high-error experiences. Finally, the APF method is introduced to reshape the reward function, employing a target attractive potential field to guide the intelligent vehicle's exploration direction. Extensive simulation results validate the model's feasibility and accuracy, demonstrating superior performance compared to several classical algorithms in terms of path quality and convergence speed.

title: Comparative Analysis of Hardware Accelerator Architectures for Performance and Energy Efficient Deep Neural Network Execution
authors: Salsabil Saoudi, Mario Barbareschi, Alberto Bosio
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Deep neural networks (DNNs) have high computational and memory demands because they rely on multiplyaccumulate (MAC) operations and the amount of data that needs to be moved from the main memory to the compute unit. Since 2016, Systolic Arrays have emerged as a popular architecture for accelerating DNNs inference, although their performance is affected by multiple parameters, including the amount of used Processing Elements (PEs) and the dataflow. In this study, we perform a systematic evaluation of Systolic Array-based DNN accelerators using the ScaleSIM simulation framework to analyze different array sizes and dataflow configurations across different DNN models, measuring the effects of memory transfers, and execution latency across all DNN workloads, founding that dataflow option for routing data through an array is dependent on the specific workload; thereby, using moderate array sizes can achieve better performance than larger arrays, offering useful insights for Systolic Array-based DNN accelerators design.

title: Effect of Harmonic Distortion on the Design and Performance of Distribution Transformers
authors: Kamran Dawood, Zekiye Esra Şahin, Semih Tursun
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Transformers are key components in electrical distribution systems, responsible for regulating voltage, power quality management, and ensuring reliable power delivery. With the increasing presence of nonlinear loads to the grid these days, harmonic distortions are becoming increasingly common, and they can negatively impact how transformers perform. To handle these challenges, transformer designs, including winding configurations and core materials, are adjusted to accommodate different harmonic conditions while trying to keep losses about the same. In this study, two identical 1000 kVA oil-immersed distribution transformers with a primary voltage of 33 kV and a secondary voltage of 0.69 kV are designed. Both transformers have the same design specifications for no-load losses, leakage impedance, and load losses. However, one transformer operates in an environment with less than $5 \%$ total harmonic distortion (THD), while the other faces a much higher THD of 33%. Through experimental measurements of core and copper losses, impedance, and weight, this study provides a quantitative assessment of how different harmonic levels influence transformer performance and design characteristics. Mathematical analysis of the measured data was conducted to ensure accurate interpretation and to establish reliable relationships between harmonic distortion and transformer performance indicators. The results show that higher harmonic distortion leads to increased weight. These results can assist transformer designers in enhancing the design of transformers to perform more effectively and last longer in environments with varying harmonic conditions, thereby making power systems more reliable and efficient.

title: Class-Imbalance-Aware ECG Classification Using a Multi-Block CNN with Advanced Data Augmentation
authors: Nilancy Agarwal, Priyam Prasad Sahoo, G. Balamurugan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database is a well-known database for automatic cardiac rhythm analysis. Multiple techniques have been suggested for classifying cardiac rhythms by researchers using this database. Previously published techniques perform well in some arrhythmia types; however, reliable performances across all the arrhythmia types remains a challenge. A key limitation is the severe class imbalance and diversity of ECG signals. To address this issue, this study proposes a deep learning-based ECG classification framework. This paper proposes a framework that employs data augmentation via a multi-block CNN for ECG classification. The implementation of the multi-block CNN combined by data augmentation aims to enhance the representation of the minority classes while preserving the morphology of ECG signals. Using 109,446 ECG samples from the MIT-BIH dataset, the model is trained with a balanced data strategy and evaluated using cross-validation to ensure robustness. The experiments showed high overall performance of classification. Further, when compared to baseline approaches, detection of the overall minority class has increased with a considerable margin. Class-wise evaluation was performed using standard metrics such as precision, recall, and F1-score. As evidenced by these results, the proposed approach effectively diminished the impact of imbalanced data while not affecting the overall classification results. The suggested framework gives a trustworthy way for automated ECG classification in imbalanced settings. Validation of the framework on other datasets and deployment in real-world clinical settings is necessary to determine its overall generalizability and feasibility.

title: Research on the Control of the Islanded Microgrid System Based on the Consensus Algorithm
authors: Yusong Wang, Long Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional droop cont rol-specifically the disproportionate reactive power sharing caus ed by line impedance mismatches and the resulting voltage deviat ions-this paper proposes a consensus-based control strategy for i slanded microgrids. Initially, a dynamic consensus algorithm is e mployed to identify reactive power mismatches among DG units. This deviation is subsequently processed by a PI controller to ada ptively adjust the virtual impedance, thereby ensuring proportion al power distribution. While the introduction of virtual impedanc e helps balance power, it inevitably causes a voltage drop at the sy stem output. To counteract this effect, a secondary voltage contro 1 loop based on the consensus algorithm is integrated to restore th e voltage and maintain stability. The proposed strategy utilizes a distributed approach that eliminates the need for global communi cation or precise line impedance measurements, relying instead o n local data exchange between neighboring units. Finally, the effe ctiveness of this strategy is validated through comprehensive simu lation case studies. Considering line impedance differences and lo cal real-time communication, it shows robustness against small m easurement noise, suitable for practical use.

title: Characterization on the flyer velocity of vaporizing foil actuator forming process by a combination of high-speed photography and finite element modeling
authors: Zhigao Jiang, Changxing Li, Mengyuan Gong, Liang Li, Zhipeng Lai
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: High-speed forming, particularly vaporizing foil actuator forming (VFAF), has received considerable attention for its potential to overcome the limitations of traditional quasi-static methods when processing high-strength, low-ductility materials. However, VFAF’s extremely short vaporization timescale and intense plasma radiation create significant challenges for in-situ velocity measurement. To overcome these constraints, an indirect velocity calibration method is proposed for measuring flyer velocity in VFAF, combining high-speed imaging with finite element (FE) modeling. To shield the observation area from intense light and debris during foil vaporization, the experimental configuration was modified by introducing a flyer plate and a polyurethane block. Velocity measurements of steel balls propelled through the polyurethane by the vaporizing foil enabled the development of an FE-based inverse method for predicting flyer plate velocity, thereby allowing for calibration without specialized optical filters. Validation tests in both air and water environments revealed that vaporization intensity and flyer plate driving force increase with higher discharge voltage, thus improving forming efficiency. Notably, forming velocity in water exceeds that in air by over 200% under equivalent voltages.

title: An Instruction-Level On-Chip Side-Channel Analysis Platform Based on RISC-V
authors: Jiayi Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: The traditional side-channel analysis (SCA) has the drawback of relying on expensive oscilloscopes, imposing limitations in practical applications. This paper designs and implements an instruction-level on-chip side-channel evaluation system which is integrated in the RISC-V architecture. Based on the open-source tinyriscv CPU core, this system achieves the application of a hardware-trigger mechanism based on instructions address by making modifications to the pipeline execution units. As a result, it can capture specific encrypted instruction intervals with zero latency. A 512-level time-to-digital converter (TDC) is constructed by utilizing the carry chain within the FPGA. This component converts the transient voltage fluctuations that occur during the encryption operation into a digital power trajectory, thereby preserving the fine side-channel information. This system employs the PopCount compression algorithm, significantly reducing the data transmission bandwidth. Experimental results conducted on the Xilinx Artix-7 platform indicate that this system eliminates trajectory alignment errors. Without the need for an external oscilloscope, the complete AES-128 key was successfully recovered through Pearson Correlation Power Analysis (CPA).

title: Research on Development of Specialized Literary Criticism Agents and Effectiveness in Education
authors: Tingrui You, Jiayang Wu, Shurou Xiao
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Based on the Wenxin agent platform, this paper uses knowledge base citation and prompt engineering and other technologies to design an agent for literary criticism education. The agent adopts a three-level module architecture, with system parameters fixed after testing, which can stably analyze texts and produce literary criticism, providing a new way of thinking for the feasibility of literary education large models based on the Wenxin agent platform, and providing an alternative framework for intelligent tools in literary education.The authors collected 50 articles from novice writers on the Chinese Young Writers Network, including 30 essays and 20 novels, and analyzed the core literary techniques and genres of these 50 articles with DeepSeek R1, Kimi K2.5 Thinking, and our literary criticism agent developed based on the Wenxin Agent platform, respectively, and manually collected the dataset. Due to the scarcity and opacity of external review data, this study has cited the core concepts of "Consensus", finally prove that the specialized agent based on prompt engineering and large knowledge base filling has excellent performance in literary criticism ability.

title: Physics-Informed Battery Modeling: Coupling Electrochemical Dynamics with Stochastic Usage Patterns for Smartphone Time-to-Empty Prediction
authors: Jianhua Tian
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Smartphone battery life prediction remains a critical challenge despite well-understood electrochemical principles governing lithium-ion cells. Existing approaches suffer from three key limitations: (1) reliance on constant-voltage approximations that fail to capture voltage collapse at low state-of-charge, (2) neglect of bidirectional thermal-electrical coupling under varying ambient conditions and power loads, and (3) insufficient quantification of uncertainty arising from parameter estimation and stochastic user behavior. We propose PhysBat, a continuous-time differential-algebraic equation framework that integrates Shepherd voltage dynamics, temperature-dependent capacity degradation, and SEI-LAM aging mechanisms. Our approach introduces three innovations matching these limitations: (1) implicit voltage-current coupling through Newton-Raphson iteration to resolve polarization-driven voltage collapse, (2) lumped-capacitance thermal modeling with Joule heating and entropic effects to capture thermal feedback, and (3) maximum likelihood estimation with bootstrap resampling to propagate parametric uncertainty through Monte Carlo simulation. Validation on commercial lithium-ion discharge data achieves voltage RMSE of 48.2 mV with R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.956, while sensitivity analysis reveals that network connectivity and battery capacity dominate time-to-empty variance (S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">i</inf> = 0.32 and 0.40, respectively), providing actionable insights for power management optimization.

title: Digital Group Therapy: Interdisciplinary Challenges and Opportunities for Biomedical Circuits and Systems
authors: H. F. Jelinek, H. Al-Nashash, F. V. Kusmartsev, J. Barreiro-Gomez, T. Aminosharieh Najafi, I. M. Elfadel
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine
abstract: Ahstract Digital Group Therapy (DGT) combines clinically-led group psychotherapy with continuous multimodal sensing, edge AI analytics, and online interaction platforms. This article synthesizes five perspectives presented at the IEEE CASS Workshop on Digital Group Therapy: neuro-dynamic modeling of group processes, cognitive workload management, next-generation edge—AI wearables, game-theoretic modeling of group behavior, and physics-inspired models of social interaction (sociophysics/social physics). We frame DGT as an open, multi-layer system of systems in which neural, physiological, cognitive, and social dynamics co-evolve under continuous feedback. We then connect this framework to recent evidence on online and mixed-reality group therapy and internet-delivered Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and to classical and modern work in sociophysics and opinion dynamics. The resulting architectural view reveals concrete circuits-and-systems challenges in scalable signal acquisition, robust edge intelligence, human-in-the-loop adaptation, incentive design, and validation of closed-loop DGT interventions.

title: A Framework for Chiplet Authentication During Post-Stacking Test
authors: Juan Suzano, Anthony Philippe, Fady Abouzeid, Philippe Roche, Giorgio Di Natale
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The semiconductor industry is adopting chiplets as an alternative to integrated circuit design. With adoption of off-the-shelf chiplets, supply chain attacks are likely to increase, making chiplet authentication essential. This work identifies post-stacking testing as the best phase for authentication and proposes a framework using existing industry standards. The framework includes a flexible authentication IP integrated into the chiplet design, compatible with various authentication methods; an IP insertion flow compatible with commercial DFT insertion tools; a secure module for the test environment; and the corresponding test procedure. Results show that chiplet authentication can be implemented without disrupting industry practices, with multiple authentication methods available based on the context.

title: Multi-objective Workflow Scheduling Strategy for Edge Computing Based on an Improved Whale Optimization Algorithm
authors: Mingwei Sun, Huaiying Sun, Xinlong Jiang, Liqiong Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the growth of application of latency-sensitive and resource-constrained in edge computing, multi-limit in complicated workflows has drawn attention as an important challenge affecting quality of service and resource utilization. To solve the problems in existing models of being trapped in local optima, slow convergence rate, and low multi-objective collaborative optimization capability in heterogeneous edge environments, the multi-objective workflow scheduling model for edge computing based on edge computing paradigm for traditional workflow scheduling model and whale optimization algorithm. The proposed model describes task dependencies using a directed acyclic graph and comprehensively considers optimization objectives such as execution latency, energy consumption, computing cost and load balancing as optimization objectives to build the model. Meanwhile, by chaotic mapping and heuristic rule combination population initialization strategy and adaptive nonlinear convergence factor to improve the algorithm global search capability. Moreover, by integrating key path task priority allocation and dynamic resource matching mechanisms to achieve efficient collaborative scheduling in complex edge scenarios. Experimental results indicate that the proposed model outperforms comparative algorithms in completes time, energy consumption, cost and load balancing.

title: Thematic Structure and Technological Evolution of Artificial Intelligence in Autism Spectrum Disorder Research
authors: Juan Ang, Yuan Chen, Weiyi Liao, Jiahui Rao, Yanwen Ji, Haijing Xu, Zirui Song
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies, their application in autism spectrum disorder (ASD) research has expanded continuously. To systematically characterize the research structure and developmental patterns in this field, this study conducts a bibliometric and visualization-based analysis of AI-related ASD research based on publications indexed in the Web of Science Core Collection. A total of 322 articles published between 2001 and 2024 were analyzed using annual publication statistics, keyword co-occurrence analysis, clustering analysis, and keyword burst detection. The research landscape is examined from multiple perspectives, including temporal distribution, thematic structure, and technological evolution. The results indicate a sustained growth trend in AI-related ASD research, with major research themes mainly focusing on technical methods, diagnosis and assessment, and intervention and development, while strong interconnections exist among these themes. From an evolutionary perspective, the research focus has shifted from early conceptual and descriptive studies toward data-driven modeling based on intelligent methods, and further toward multi-technology integration and application-oriented research. This study provides a macroscopic overview of the thematic structure and evolutionary trajectory of AI in ASD research, offering insights into the development trends and research organization of this interdisciplinary field.

title: Bridging the Gap between Construction and Application: A Policy-Driven Model for Enhancing MOOC Effectiveness in Universities
authors: Chen Gu, Haiyan Wang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: significant gap persists between the scale of Massive Open Online Course (MOOC) construction and its practical integration into teaching, limiting the realization of its educational potential. To explore systematic strategies for enhancing MOOC effectiveness, this study proposes and examines a "Policy-Driven MOOC Ecosystem Model." Based on an in-depth, longitudinal case study of a Chinese research university, the research employs a mixed-methods approach combining policy text analysis, quantitative performance data, and qualitative interviews.The findings indicate that bridging the "construction-application" gap requires synergistic institutional design at the university level. The validated model comprises four core, interacting mechanisms: An Incentive and Recognition Mechanism formally links MOOC outcomes to faculty evaluation and reward systems; a Quality and Standard Mechanism regulates the course development lifecycle with clear technical and pedagogical specifications; a Support and Service Mechanism provides comprehensive, proactive assistance from training to technical implementation; and an Assessment and Feedback Mechanism utilizes learning analytics to drive evidence-based, continuous improvement of both courses and policies.Empirical evidence shows that under this framework, the university launched over 200 MOOCs, adopted by nearly 200 institutions nationwide with high enrollment, facilitating resource sharing and blended learning. The study concludes that transforming MOOCs into integrated teaching practices requires strong top-down policy architecture. This model provides a systematic approach for global higher education to turn digital course investments into tangible educational outcomes, supporting quality and equity.

title: Research Evolution of High-Fidelity Modeling and Simulation Methods for Digital Twins in Intelligent Manufacturing Equipment
authors: Yijun Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The digital transformation of smart manufacturing equipment relies on high-precision digital twin technology, where high-fidelity modeling and simulation are central to accurately reproducing equipment behavior and predicting performance. Using tools such as VOSviewer and CiteSpace, this study analyzes relevant literature from the Web of Science Core Collection (WoS Core Collection) published between 2015 and 2025 to systematically map the research landscape and knowledge evolution in the field. The results show a highly concentrated research effort, organized around several distinct collaborative clusters centered on key scholars and institutions, with a global pattern of “dual-core leadership by China and the United States.” The research trajectory has evolved through three stages: conceptual and architectural exploration, technological integration and application, and high-fidelity intelligent deepening. Current work focuses on advancing multi-physics coupling modeling, hybrid-driven methods, and AI-enhanced simulation. Today, high-fidelity modeling and simulation have become essential for implementing and managing digital twins across the full life cycle of smart manufacturing equipment. Future research should tackle challenges such as multi-scale coupling modeling, adaptive simulation, and cross-platform integration to further the adoption of this technology in high-end equipment sectors.

title: Concept of Telemetry Spectrum Monitoring and Management for Flight Test
authors: Shan Shou, Hui Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Addressing the needs of telemetry spectrum monitoring and management in flight tests, this paper elaborates on the necessity of establishing a telemetry spectrum monitoring and management system from multiple perspectives, including telemetry spectrum resources, telemetry signal modulation, external interference, and spectrum management methods. It details the fundamental functions, system architecture, unit functionalities, and management approaches of the proposed system, laying the groundwork for the development of a telemetry spectrum monitoring and management system for flight tests.

title: Comparative Analysis of Human and AI Learning from Feedback Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Mahesh Muthulakshmi R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Comparing human and artificial intelligence (AI) learning is essential for understanding how feedback influences decision-making and performance improvement in adaptive systems. Humans learn primarily through experience, reward, punishment and gradual behavioral adjustment, while AI systems rely on structured mechanisms such as training data, loss functions, and optimization strategies. Existing studies often analyze human learning and AI learning independently or focus on static accuracy measures, limiting insight into dynamic feedback-driven learning behavior. This work presents a Reinforcement Learning-based simulation using a Multi-Armed Bandit model to compare human-like and AI learning across four experimental cases within a unified framework. Both agents interact with the same environment and update their decisions based on feedback, enabling a fair and consistent comparison of learning dynamics. The results demonstrate that both human-like and AI agents improve through feedback-based learning. The AI agent achieves higher and more stable performance, with a mean accuracy of 0.835 and an F1-score of 0.835, reflecting balanced and consistent decision-making. Human-like learning shows slightly lower accuracy and greater variability. Furthermore, the study supports advancements in intelligent adaptive systems that can enhance personalized education and contribute to improved mental well-being through feedback-driven learning environments.

title: CMTAN: A Computationally Efficient Multi-Scale Temporal Attention Network for Detecting Vulnerable Posts on Social Media
authors: Seethalakshmi R, Sheik Mohammed A, Karthikeyan B
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The rise of user-generated content on social networking sites have increased the need for reliable and efficient automated systems to identify suicide-related expressions and separate them from non-suicidal content. Early detection of suicidal thoughts is crucial for timely intervention. However, current deep learning methods often rely on complex architectures that require a lot of computing power, which limits their ability to scale for real-world use. While recent models, such as Attention-based Bidirectional Temporal Convolutional Networks, perform well in classifying suicide risk, their use of temporal self-attention leads to complexity that grows rapidly with sequence length. To overcome this issue, this paper presents a Computationally-efficient Multi-scale Temporal Attention Network for classifying suicide and non-suicide text. The proposed model combines multi-scale convolutional features extraction, stacked dilated temporal convolutional networks, and a channel-wise self-attention mechanism. This approach allows for effective modeling of context with linear computational complexity. DistilBERT embedding are used to generate small yet significant descriptions of social media text. Experimental results on the Reddit suicide-related dataset shows that our model achieves competitive performance, with an accuracy of 91.97% and an F1-score of 92.03%, while using about half the number of trainable parameters compared to AL-BTCN. These results demonstrate that our approach is suitable for efficient and scalable detection of suicide risk in large social media environments.

title: Usability of Integrated Scoring System for Competitions with Rubric and Evaluation (I-SSCORE)
authors: Leonard L. Alejandro
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This research presents the development and evaluation of an Integrated Scoring System for COmpetitions with Rubric and Evaluation (I-SSCORE), a web-based platform designed to streamline the judging process for academic and non-academic competitions. I-SSCORE incorporates key features such as user role management, category management, and integrated rubrics to enhance efficiency, fairness, and transparency in conducting competitions.The system, built using the Blazor framework on the.NET Core platform, was developed using Agile Methodology that prioritized user feedback throughout the design and development process. Three main user levels were identified: the Admin, the Organizer and the Judges. The Admin account will have full control over the system, including user management, competition creation, category management, and overall maintenance. Organizers will have the ability to create categories, manage entries, and oversee the judging process. Judges will have access to real-time judging results, scoreboards, and participation data. Additionally, a live scores page can be viewable once shared by the organizer.Initial testing was done by Quality Assurance Specialists before it was tested to three school based academic and non-academic competitions. Usability testing, conducted using the System Usability Scale (SUS), demonstrated high user satisfaction and ease of use. Specifically, the judges' access features, category management functionality, and the integration of rubrics and criteria all received high usability scores.I-SSCORE offers a valuable tool for competition organizers, judges, and participants, promoting a more efficient and equitable evaluation process.

title: BilimALL AI: An Intelligent Platform for Integrating Computer Science and Natural Sciences in Digital Education
authors: Meruert Yerekesheva, Adilet Darmenov, Ryskul Zhakhina, Anar Tashimova, Moldir Muratova
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the modern education system, the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) technologies into the learning process has become one of the key priorities. This study focuses on the development of the BilimALL AI platform, which was created based on the concept of connecting informatics with natural science subjects and enhancing digital learning practices. BilimALL AI is an intelligent system that identifies interdisciplinary links between informatics and natural sciences, assisting teachers in automatically generating educational materials and visual slides. The platform introduces a novel approach by combining AI-driven content generation with automated detection of interdisciplinary connections, a feature that has not been extensively explored in current educational technologies. The platform aims to modernize the teaching process, automate lesson preparation, and reduce teachers’ workload. During the research, informatics teachers tested the BilimALL AI system in practice and evaluated its functional capabilities. The results revealed that the platform positively impacts lesson quality, increases students’ interest in science subjects, and improves teachers’ productivity through automation. The findings confirm that the BilimALL AI platform is an effective digital tool for promoting interdisciplinary integration, enhancing teacher efficiency, and advancing the use of AI in education, representing a significant contribution to the digital transformation of teaching practices.

title: Efficient YOLOv8n: An Empirical Analysis of Architectural Redundancy and Quantization on Edge Devices
authors: Devadharshan D, Karthika R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Running accurate object detectors on hardware like the Raspberry Pi is difficult because standard models are simply too computationally heavy. This problem was addressed by building a custom compression pipeline for YOLOv8n specifically for the Raspberry Pi 5. Instead of using traditional optimization, a combined surgical pruning (physically removing redundant layers), fine-tuning, and INT8 quantization. The tests demonstrate that this hybrid approach achieves a 34% reduction in inference latency compared to the baseline, maintaining 99.7% of the original accuracy (0.354 mAP). This provides a practical approach for identifying and removing architectural bottlenecks on resource-constrained edge devices.

title: When Discourse Meets Behavior: Cross-Platform and Multilingual Fandom Dynamics during High-Impact Events
authors: Rian Wagner Costa, Lívia Dâmaso, Carlos Henrique Ferreira, Carolina Xavier, Vinícius Vieira
publish: Proceedings of the 18th ACM Web Science Conference 2026
