abstract: With the rapid growth of the electric vehicle (EV) population, vehicle-to-grid (V2G), especially AC V2G, is considered a key enabling technology for constructing a new electricity system due to its immense potential for large-scale application. However, the development of AC V2G is facing a significant challenge, which is the lack of national standards unifying the digital communication specifications for bidirectional charge-discharge AC interfaces. Based on the latest guidelines released by the China Electricity Council and a group standard by the Guangzhou Power Industry Association, this study systematically reviews existing technical solutions for AC V2G EVpile communication. It first analyzes the superiorities of AC V2G compared with DC V2G in terms of resource scale, user experience, and cost-effectiveness. Then, it identifies two categories of EV-pile communication pathways. One is the asynchronous serial communication scheme, which relies on Control Pilot (CP) lines and requires no hardware modifications to existing systems. The other is the “hardware upgrade” scheme, which expects to achieve high-reliability communication by incorporating dedicated communication modules (e.g., CAN or LIN transceivers). This paper elaborates on the communication principles, workflows, advantages, and limitations of both solutions, as well as their standardization needs and future challenges, in an attempt to provide a theoretical reference for the standardization and industrial development of AC V2G.

title: Research on Intelligent Management Strategy of Graduation Project Moving Forward for "Optimizing Course Connection and Improving Topic Quality"
authors: Jin-Gang Jiang, Jin-Xu Zhao, Tao Shen, Yu-Dong Bao, Jiang-Long Xiong, Kai-Rui Wang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: To address common challenges in undergraduate thesis management—including inadequate course system support, inconsistent topic quality, and lax process oversight—this study proposes an intelligent pre-thesis management strategy focused on "optimizing course alignment and enhancing topic quality." By establishing a systematic implementation framework centered on course integration, supported by topic repository development and interdisciplinary resource integration, and implementing a comprehensive evaluation system based on AHP (Analytic Hierarchy Process), the strategy achieves quality monitoring and continuous improvement throughout the pre-thesis phase. The research innovatively introduces Knowledge Graph technology to construct a "course-competency-thesis" relationship graph, enabling precise course system support and personalized topic recommendations. Pilot implementation in related majors at Harbin Institute of Technology demonstrates that this strategy effectively improves the alignment between course content and thesis requirements, enhances topic relevance and cutting-edge relevance, and provides a systematic solution for reforming undergraduate thesis quality management.

title: Early-Stage Fire Recognition and Localization Based on YOLOv8 and Binocular Vision Fusion
authors: Yifan Gan, Linsen Xiong, Weiming Xu, Shengli Chu, Jianfeng Li, Wenpei Zheng, Dantong Zhu, Ruisheng Yong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: To address the critical need for early and accurate identification and localization of oil spill fires, this paper proposes a fire detection and localization method that integrates YOLOv8-based object detection with binocular stereo vision. A multi-scenario flame image dataset was constructed to train the YOLOv8 model for high-precision flame detection. Meanwhile, an improved Semi-Global Block Matching (SGBM) algorithm was adopted to achieve precise three-dimensional localization. Experimental results indicate that the proposed system exhibits strong robustness in complex environments, achieving a maximum mean Average Precision (mAP) of 98.74%, a localization error of less than 7%, and an effective detection range of 3 to 15 meters, with localization accuracy surpassing 93%. This study provides a reliable technical solution for intelligent monitoring and emergency response in oil spill fire scenarios.

title: Machine Learning and Reinforcement Learning for Production Scheduling: A CiteSpace-Based Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Hanchuan Li, Peng Pan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Production scheduling is being revisited through data-driven methods as shop-floor connectivity and operational logging improve. This paper maps the (2020-2025) research landscape of machine learning (ML) and reinforcement learning (RL) for production scheduling through a CiteSpace-based bibliometric analysis. We collected 1,541 records from the Web of Science Core Collection (retrieved on 29 December 2025) and constructed collaboration, co-citation, and keyword co-occurrence networks with yearly time slicing and g-index selection. The results show a clear publication take-off after 2022, with China and the United States leading both output and network connectivity. Topic evolution indicates a shift from algorithm demonstrations on benchmark job-shop instances toward deployment-driven themes, including rescheduling under disturbances, digital-twin-supported training, energy-aware objectives, and graph-based representations of job-machine relations. Burst detection further highlights growing interest in hybrid pipelines that combine learning with heuristic or evolutionary search. These findings provide an evidence-based snapshot of the field and point to open challenges in robustness, generalization, and reproducible evaluation of ML/RL schedulers.

title:
Intelligent Modelling of Maritime Fuel Consumption and CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Emissions Using Integrated Attention-based LSTM -FNN Neural Networks
authors: Shruthi Merine S., Shakthinee S., Kumari Deepika P
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Predictive CO2 emission and Fuel consumption of maritime transport can only be reliable in alleviating the efficiency and environmental sustainability of maritime transport. Some of the data it consists of are vessel types such as fishing trawlers, oil service boats, surfing boats, and tanker ships, and operational data such as navigation route, engine performance data, weather conditions, and fuel consumption history. The LSTM models in the suggested model identify time variations of sequential data of maritime operations. The LSTM design has an attention mechanism that gives priority to the most critical aspects in its operations, e.g., route conditions, weather, and engine dynamics, etc., in terms of fuel consumption. The model aims to learn spatial and operational differences between the navigation routes in order to gain route awareness and refine the prediction of fuel consumption and CO2 emissions. Experimental research indicates that the attention-based LSTM model forecasts better than regular LSTM and machine learning predictors. The framework approximates the cost of business operations based on calculated fuel consumption, and this forms a smart resource for managing maritime energy. The recommended solution will be added to the existing AI-based maritime analytics to enhance fuel management, emissions evaluation, and sustainable maritime transportation planning.

title: EchoGuide: A Low-Cost, Wearable Dual-Ultrasonic Travel Aid for Enhanced Mobility and Safety for Individuals with Visual Impairments
authors: Himanshu Singh, Vipin Jain, Paras Tripathi
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Personal freedom and quality of life is based on the ability to move independently, and it is still a serious issue of millions of people with visual disabilities across the globe. Conventional tools such as the white cane, although necessary, possess certain inherent drawbacks such as inability to detect any obstacles that may be placed above the ground and unpredictable weather conditions. The present paper presents EchoGuide, which is a new Electronic Travel Aid (ETA) that will fill these gaps. It is a low priced, non-obtrusive, wearable gadget which is designed to be placed on the chest as an ID card. It combines a microcontroller ESP32 and a pair of HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensors. The two sensors offer forward-facing obstacle detection and downward-angled, respectively, offer ground-level hazard detection, like curbs, steps, and potholes. This information is processed by the system in real-time to provide clear and concise voice notifications, which the user receives through common earbuds, and informs them about the distance to an identified obstacle. The design also focuses on user-centric principles such as a low cognitive load created by the use of simple audio cues, social acceptability with a minimal form factor, and accessibility to the device through the incorporation of low-cost off-the-shelf components. The paper describes the entire system architecture, the hardware implementation, the algorithms used in software including a sequential triggering mechanism to control sensor interference and a proposed user-friendly evaluation protocol. Offering a holistic approach to environmental awareness, in a practical, convenient bundle, EchoGuide offers a bright future to the safety and confidence levels as well as the level of independence of visually impaired pedestrians.

title: Design and Optimization of a High-Pressure Flare Gas Recovery System in Deepwater Gas Fields Based on Cascade PID Control
authors: Xu Sun, Han Shi, Yong Lai, Yong Yang, Fuming Xiang
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Recovery of high-pressure flare gas in deepwater gas fields is a key measure for energy saving and emission reduction. However, conventional gas-phase compression schemes suffer from large footprint and high energy consumption. A “liquid-phase pressurization coupled with flash vaporization” recovery process based on cascade PID control is proposed to achieve efficient and stable recovery of high-pressure flash gas. A dynamic model of the controlled object-separator pressure and liquid level-is established. A cascade PID control structure is adopted, with the master loop controlling pressure and the slave loop controlling liquid level, incorporating feedforward compensation and anti-windup mechanisms. Critical system parameters are obtained via the relay feedback method, PID parameters are tuned using Ziegler–Nichols rules, and further optimized with actual operating data. Field application shows that the system achieves pressure fluctuation within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2 \%$</tex>, liquid level control accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5 \%$</tex>, a recovery rate of high-pressure flare gas of 98.7%, and an annual gas recovery of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 3 2} \boldsymbol{\times} \mathbf{1 0}^{\mathbf{4}} \mathbf{m}^{\mathbf{3}}$</tex>. In conclusion, the cascade PID control strategy effectively resolves the strong pressure-liquid level coupling issue, providing a high-precision control solution for high-pressure gas recovery under space-constrained conditions on offshore platforms.

title: Filter Modeling and Twin Implementation of Unknown RLC Circuit Based on Digital Twins
authors: Yizhe Zhao, Qinding Chen, Canhao Li, Hongyan Jiang, Shuaiqiang Li, Tao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Filters are pivotal for noise suppression and signal integrity in high-precision sensor conditioning. Concurrently, non-intrusive digital twin technology has emerged as a significant research focus, utilizing virtual modeling of physical entities to enable real-time monitoring and fault diagnosis. The system integrates two modules: filter design derived from known transfer functions, and rapid digital twin simulation of unknown passive RLC circuits. The method for constructing filter circuits with known voltage transfer functions involves first confirming the circuit architecture, then calculating component values via the transfer function. For modeling unknown RLC second-order circuits, the Direct Digital Synthesizer (DDS) module performs frequency sweeping. The amplitude-frequency characteristics are analyzed using the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). Subsequently, the DDS module generates output signals consistent with the unknown model through inference, thereby achieving a digital twin of the unknown circuit.

title: MCP:A Time Series Prediction Model Integrating Multi-Cycle Modeling and PatchTST
authors: Shi Bo Cui, En Qing Chen, Pei Li, Xin Guo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Periodic patterns in time series data are key to longterm prediction. However, most models struggle to accu-rately capture complex data periodicity, and traditional Transformerbased models have limited adaptability to periodic perturbations. To address these issues, this paper proposes a time series prediction model called MCP (Multi-Cycle-PatchTST), which integrates an improved CycleNet and the PatchTST model. First, based on the learnable periodicity mechanism of CycleNet, the model introduces a multi-cycle modeling strategy and uses a multicycle queue to explicitly capture stable patterns across different time scales. Then, the prediction mechanism of PatchTST is applied to forecast the residual component after removing the periodic part. The complete prediction result is obtained by merging the residual prediction with the periodic sequence, and the final prediction is generated through weighted fusion after evaluating the outputs of different periodic branches. Experimental results show that MCP achieves excellent performance on multiple public datasets such as ETT and Weather, with particularly significant improvements in long-term prediction. Its perfor-mance is approximately 8% better than that of the original CycleNet and PatchTST models, verifying its effectiveness and robustness in predicting time series with complex periodicity.

title: A Convolutional and Bidirectional GRU Framework for Intrusion Detection to protect IoMT Environments
authors: Nivedhidha M, Ramkumar M P, Emil Selvan G S R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: IoMT has transformed the healthcare sector by allowing high-quality monitoring and care of patients due to the interconnection of devices; however, it also opens the healthcare sector to high risks of cyberattacks. One of the most significant threats is ARP spoofing, which helps to instigate man-in-the-middle attack, modify the data, and seize devices in a critical hospital network, which may negatively affect the lives of patients. A novel hybrid DL model CBG-IDS which combines PCA, CNN-BiGRU to detect ARP spoofing in real-time and specifically to the IoMT ecosystem is developed. PCA reduces dimensionality of features, thus reducing redundancy and enhancing computational efficiency. CNN component identifies spatial irregularities in the structure of packets. An advanced dual-task learning system integrates binary classification using the focal loss which gives more attention to difficult cases, and a reconstruction element that uses the mean squared error promoting robustness, interpretability and visualization of anomalies. The proposed model CBG-IDS produced high accuracy compared to traditional approaches. This reduces instances of false positives, ensures high reliability and assists easy real-time integration into healthcare systems to counter the emerging cyber threats and maintain patient safety and data integrity.

title: NavShip-5V: A Multi-view Dataset and Lightweight Framework for Autonomous IMO Inspection
authors: Zhi-Bo Cao, Duanxiong Wan, Zeliang Sun
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Autonomous maritime inspection by Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) requires precise relative viewpoint estimation to locate identity markers (specifically, the International Maritime Organization (IMO) number) on vessel sterns. However, existing datasets lack navigational semantics, and visual ambiguity arising from vessel symmetry poses significant challenges for robust control. To address this, we propose a lightweight, hierarchical perception framework tailored for embedded edge deployment. First, we introduce NavShip-5V (Navigation-oriented Ship 5-Viewpoints), a novel multi-view dataset annotated with a function-oriented protocol. Second, we design a viewpoint estimation network based on MobileNetV3-Small. By optimizing the classification topology (merging symmetric side views) and employing a class-balanced weighted loss (20:1), we effectively resolve the visual ambiguity issue. Counter-intuitively, our lightweight model achieves a Top-1 accuracy of 90.9%, significantly outperforming the heavier ResNet-18 (84.1%) and the unstable ShuffleNetV2 in both accuracy and convergence stability. Finally, integrated with YOLOv8-nano (90.8% mAP@0.5) for local ROI detection, the proposed system demonstrates a complete, safe, and efficient Find-Navigate-Read pipeline, paving the way for fully autonomous maritime law enforcement.

title: A Binary Chernobyl Disaster Optimizer Algorithm for Feature Selection and Its Applications to Meteorological Radar Data
authors: Douglas Rodrigues, Leandro A. Passos, Danilo S. Jodas, Kelton A. P. Costa, Clayton R. Pereira, João P. Papa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Feature selection is a fundamental step in high-dimensional learning problems, aiming to reduce dimensionality while preserving predictive performance and improving model interpretability. Metaheuristic optimization methods have been widely adopted for this task due to their ability to explore large combinatorial search spaces. In this work, we propose a binary adaptation of the Chernobyl Disaster Optimizer for wrapper-based feature selection. The continuous dynamics of the original optimizer are mapped to binary selection masks through an S-shaped transfer function, and candidate subsets are evaluated using a Naïve Bayes classifier. The proposed method is evaluated on six benchmark datasets from different domains and on four high-dimensional meteorological radar datasets derived from NEXRAD Level II volumes for hail detection. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves competitive predictive performance compared with well-established binary metaheuristic algorithms, including Binary Bat, Binary Black Hole, Binary Equilibrium Optimizer, Binary Firefly, and Binary Multi-Verse Optimizer. Statistical tests indicate that performance differences are often dataset-dependent, whereas the proposed method exhibits stable behavior across multiple scenarios. Beyond predictive metrics, we introduce a frequency-based analysis of feature selection behavior across multiple runs and optimization methods. This analysis reveals which features are consistently selected, which are robust across metaheuristics, and which exhibit method-dependent selection patterns. In meteorological datasets, the progressive inclusion of gradient, texture, and convective descriptors yields consistent performance gains and clearer identification of robust radar features. The results demonstrate that the proposed Binary Chernobyl Disaster Optimizer is an effective and interpretable tool for feature selection in both benchmark and real-world high-dimensional applications.

title: HTTPS Man-in-the-Middle Attack Detection Based on Contrastive Learning and Multimodal Feature Fusion
authors: Kun Liu, Wei Lin, Di Jin, Ding Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In the contemporary digital era, the cybersecurity threat landscape has become increasingly critical, with diverse attack vectors emerging continuously. Man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks against The Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) protocols pose severe threats to both the confidentiality and integrity of data transmission. To address the challenges prevalent in HTTPS MITM attack detection—specifically, the difficulty in characterizing encrypted traffic, the proliferation of attack variants, and the scarcity of labeled training data—this paper proposes CLET-MITM, a novel detection model integrating contrastive learning with multimodal feature fusion. The model employs a Transformer-based architecture that incorporates multimodal representations of encrypted traffic, encompassing raw bytes, packet length sequences, and 32-dimensional composite temporal features. Through dual-task pre-training combining masked sequence prediction and enhanced contrastive learning, the model learns traffic representations with superior generalization capability. During the fine-tuning stage, an encoder freezing strategy is adopted to enable few-shot adaptation. Experimental results demonstrate that CLET-MITM achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple test datasets, with overall detection accuracy exceeding 98%, significantly outperforming existing baseline methods.

title: A Hand-Eye Calibration Method Based on SCARA Manipulator
authors: Yanhong Fu, Weiting Ma, Changlu Zhang, Wenbo Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Based on the self-developed SCARA robotic arm's mechanical structure and corresponding self-developed controller algorithm, in order to achieve the automatic grasping of industrial products by the robotic arm, this paper proposes a hand eye calibration method that can accurately locate the product and grasp it to the designated position to complete production. This method involves multiple movements of a robotic arm equipped with a vision system to take photos of a calibration board, record pose and image data, create a calibration model, input camera internal and external parameters, input image and pose data, calculate the calibration result (transformation matrix) based on the calibration model, and apply the result to the grasping algorithm, ultimately achieving high-precision industrial automation production.

title: Reformulate, Retrieve, Localize: Agents for Repository-Level Bug Localization
authors: Genevieve Caumartin, Glaucia Melo
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 7th International Workshop on Bots and Agents in Software Engineering
abstract: None

title: Beyond constant attention: Why VR’s relaxation-dominant patterns better support inclusive learning ecosystems
authors: Kai-Chun Hou, Kuan-Ying Chen, Ping-Han Cheng, Ping-Lin Fan, Kang-Ming Chang, Jui-Yao Tsai, Po-Wei Huang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As educational technology evolves, Virtual Reality (VR) demonstrates significant potential in advancing inclusive education by surmounting physical barriers and providing equitable experiences. However, the impact of immersive media on the core cognitive states of learners requires empirical validation. This study evaluates the effects of different display media on participants' Attention and Relaxation brainwave averages to assess their suitability for future inclusive learning environments. A within-subjects experimental design was employed with 36 participants, who performed virtual exploration tasks under three conditions: Resting (R), 2D Monitor (M), and VR Headset (V). Statistical results revealed a significant interaction between media condition and brainwave type (F(2, 70) = 4.908, p = .010 ). Crucially, the VR condition exhibited a unique "low-pressure immersion" pattern: it was the only condition where the Relaxation average (52.08) exceeded the Attention average (43.81), contrasting with the Attention-dominant patterns observed in the Resting (64.11) and Monitor (55.86) conditions. This physiological reversal suggests that VR immersion effectively filters environmental distractions and induces a "flow" state with reduced cognitive anxiety, providing an empirical foundation for future inclusive digital courseware development.

title: Construction of Dynamic Pathway for Graduation Project Topics Selection Under the "Industry-Science Education"
authors: Jin-gang Jiang, Rui Zhang, Tao Shen, Yu-dong Bao, Jiang-long Xiong, Jian-peng Sun
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As a crucial component of undergraduate education, the graduation project plays a key role in cultivating students' comprehensive abilities. The quality of its topic selection directly impacts the development of students' holistic competencies and their future professional adaptability. To address issues such as delayed updates to topics databases, insufficient personalized recommendations, and subjective evaluation criteria in the process of selecting graduation project topics, this study proposes the construction of a dynamic pathway and evaluation system for graduation project topics selection. This system is driven by a dual mechanism integrating "industry-education collaboration" and "scientific research-education synergy." By integrating enterprise demands, scientific research projects, and competition outcomes, a multi-dimensional topics sourcing mechanism is established. Furthermore, a multi-layer intelligent framework based on a knowledge graph—incorporating BiLSTM-CRF, TransE, and GAT—is introduced to achieve dynamic and personalized topic recommendations. Meanwhile, an evaluation index system encompassing four dimensions—innovativeness, practical value, feasibility, and comprehensive educational effectiveness—was developed. The analytic hierarchy process (AHP) was employed to determine the weights of each indicator, forming a scientific and quantifiable quality evaluation model. Empirical results demonstrate that this system significantly enhances topic-matching satisfaction, industry relevance, and students’ comprehensive capabilities, thereby providing an effective pathway for optimizing graduation project topic selection in higher education institutions.

title: Consistent Conjugated Variables Choice for TDPA Passivated Teleoperation Architectures
authors: Camilla Celli, Francesco Porcini, Andrea Bini, Alessandro Filippeschi, Antonio Frisoli
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: In bilateral teleoperation, the operator acts on a local robot to command the motion of a remote follower robot and receives back a force feedback generated by the interaction of the follower robot with the environment. The delay of the communication channel as well as the architecture are sources of instability of the teleoperation loop. Passivation of the active blocks of this loop is a commonly used solution with the Time Domain Passivity Approach (TDPA), and, among the methods that use this solution, many (e.g., Time Domain Power Network - TDPN) exploit electro-mechanical equivalence to calculate the energies that may cause instability and that need to be dissipated. These methods rely on the Maxwell’s equivalence, which provides an overall consistent representation of the system’s dynamics. However, this work highlights that this consistency does not occur for the definition of energies when the low-level controllers include feedforward terms. In these cases, Maxwell’s method is ambiguous and may lead to the definition of conjugate variables that fail to represent energies consistently, thus jeopardizing passivity and, eventually, stability. This work shows that Firestone’s method for electro-mechanical equivalence solves the ambiguity and provides a physically consistent estimation of energies, thus always preserving passivity. After the formal identification of this issue for low-level controllers that include feedforward terms such as compensations, this paper proposes a simulation to highlight the issue. The simulation includes a one Degree of Freedom (DoF) leader and a one DoF follower, and considers TDPN architecture and energy reflection TDPA. The simulation results show the stability obtained with the proposed approach and the difference in the computed energies for both architectures when using Maxwell’s and Firestone’s methods.

title: Multimodal Fusion of Speech and Handwriting Biomarkers for Parkinson’s Disease Diagnosis
authors: Mohana Priya S, Shamyukthaa P, Mary Joseph
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Parkinson’s Disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disease that progresses by affecting both motor and non-motor capabilities such as speech, writing, and exhibiting one's feelings. Early detection enables timely intervention, but subjective single-modality assessments dominate most of the current diagnostic approaches. In this paper, we present a novel multimodal framework that combines the power of paralinguistic voice with handwriting data to improve the accuracy and reliability of PD diagnosis. On the voice modality, emotional speech instability can be captured with features extracted using Mel-Frequency Cepstral Coefficients (MFCCs), prosody features (pitch, jitter, shimmer), and spectral characteristics from speech signal, that are further processed with CNN on spectrograms, GRU on temporal sequences, and XGBoost on classical acoustic features. Fine motor impairments could be captured by handwriting modality through spiral drawing data, processed either by CNN or MLP model. A late fusion layer combines predictions from the voice and handwriting stream to estimate the final probability of Parkinson's. Moreover, Grad-CAM and SHAP/LIME support explainable AI and give insights into the features most influential for the detection. Our experiments on publicly available datasets, namely the Parkinson Speech Dataset (UCI), NeuroVoz emotional speech corpus, and Spiral Drawing Dataset, reveal that the proposed multimodal and emotion-aware approach significantly outperformed state-of-the-art single-modality solutions, resulting in a robust, reproducible, and interpretable solution for the early-stage diagnosis of PD.

title: From Pain to Poetics: Suffering as a Generative Force in AI-Assisted Award-Winning Cinematic Creation
authors: Faten Ridene, Imen Samet
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The rapid development of generative artificial intelligence has introduced new aesthetic and narrative possibilities in contemporary animation cinema, particularly within experimental short films that emulate handcrafted techniques. While existing research has extensively examined the technical efficiency, originality, and production workflows of AI-generated animation, limited attention has been given to its capacity to mediate personal suffering and psychological transformation. This paper addresses this gap by analysing two AI-generated animated short films (A Breath Apart and Lily) by Zoubeir Jlassi, whose work has received major international recognition, including the Google Gemini One Billion AI Film Award. Drawing on interdisciplinary frameworks from generative AI studies, animation theory, and psychology, the study explores how lived experiences of illness, vulnerability, and parenthood are transmuted into symbolic visual narratives through AI-assisted cinematic creation. Grounded in theories of post-traumatic growth, resilience, and shattered assumptions, the analysis demonstrates that AI functions not merely as a production technology, but as an aesthetic and psychological mediator that enables the sublimation of trauma into meaning. The stop-motion, like visual language which is characterized by restrained movement, tactile textures, and contemplative pacing, emerges as a deliberate expressive choice aligned with themes of fragility, healing, and emotional suspension. The findings suggest that AI-generated animation can support deeply human, autobiographical, and emotionally resonant storytelling when guided by strong authorial intent. By situating AI cinema within a framework of creative resilience, this research contributes to ongoing debates on originality, authorship, and the human role in intelligent creative systems, particularly within underrepresented Arab and African contexts.

title: Enhanced XGBoost Model for Predicting Credit Risk in Micro Finance Lending
authors: CH. Rushikesh, D. Sri Sai, D. Naveen Reddy, R. Karthikeyan, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Credit risk assessment is very essential in maintaining environmentally friendly lending practices in consumer credit and microfinance, where default rates are naturally high. The paper introduces an AI-based credit risk prediction system that utilizes real data from applications and credit repayment to categorize borrowers into safe and risky groups. Firstly, the presented method combines the applicant's demographic and financial information with their past repayment behavior to create a reliable single binary default indicator. They calculated features to create more descriptive variables like age and length of employment and then transformed the categorical variables and cleaned the data. In order to solve the problem of severe class imbalance in default events, the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) is used. A classifier based on Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) is created using the balanced dataset in order to capture complicated non-linear relationships between borrower characteristics and default risk. The proposed model achieved an accuracy of 95.1%, precision of 93.6%, and recall of 93.5% on the test dataset. Additional evaluation metrics such as F1-score and ROC-AUC are used to further assess model performance under imbalanced conditions. Multiple machine learning models such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, LightGBM, and XGBoost were implemented and compared to evaluate.

title: Multi-Similarity Aware Progressive Gap Minimization for Unsupervised Person Re-Identification
authors: Jun Zhang, Shuli Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Currently, most unsupervised person re-identification (ReID) techniques, despite achieving significant progress, still face several issues. These methods often focus excessively on positive samples, neglecting the benefits of negative samples. They also fail to leverage the potential complementary information among samples, centroids, and between centroids and anchors. This shortcoming prevents them from fully capturing the complex and dynamic relationships between samples and pseudo-labels, leading to suboptimal model optimization. To address this, we propose a Multi-Similarity Aware Progressive Gap Minimization (MAPGM) strategy. MAPGM leverages multi-level similarity to design a novel contrastive learning framework that softens pseudo-labels and smooths probability distributions, alleviating overconffdence in single-sample predictions. Furthermore, it simulates positive augmentation within multi-level similarities by selecting appropriate positives and negatives, and adaptively adjusting weights to manage sample diversity and complexity. Experiments on Market-1501 and DukeMTMC demonstrate that our method signiffcantly outperforms most unsupervised ReID approaches.

title: Towards Transparent and Secure Biometric Authentication: Leveraging Deep Learning and XAI
authors: Mude Giri Naik, Minuku Vijay Sai, Naik Tarunsai Kishor, Mopidevi Yashwanth, S. Dilli Babu
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Biometric authentication has become an essential safeguard for sensitive digital interactions, yet conventional systems remain vulnerable to sophisticated spoofing attacks, including high-resolution images, video replays, and realistic 3D masks. Traditional security mechanisms, reliant on static features or rigid rules, fail to adapt to modern adversarial attempts. This research introduces a novel Enhanced Deep Learning and Explainable AI Framework (EDLX), architected as an adaptive feature-fusion ensemble rather than a simple combination of standalone networks. By integrating advanced transfer learning (leveraging InceptionV3 parameters) with interpretability-driven model evaluation, the EDLX framework fortifies liveness face detection. The model is fine-tuned on the Spoof in the Wild with Multiple Attacks Version 2 (SiWMv2) dataset, encompassing 14 diverse spoofing techniques. The framework uniquely utilizes Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations (LIME) to provide actionable transparency in decision-making by visually highlighting the discriminative facial regions utilized for classification. Experimental comparisons demonstrate superior detection performance, with the EDLX ensemble achieving an intradataset accuracy of 99.8%, an Attack Presentation Classification Error Rate (APCER) of 0.2%, and a Bona Fide Presentation Classification Error Rate (BPCER) of 0.2%. Furthermore, rigorous cross-dataset validation against unseen attacks verifies the framework’s resistance to overfitting, sustaining a robust accuracy above 94%. By combining high-performance deep learning models with interpretable XAI techniques, the EDLX framework offers a scalable, transparent, and resilient solution to modern biometric authentication challenges. This dual focus on standard biometric metrics (ACER) and interpretability establishes a clear path for secure, real-world deployment in high-stakes security environments.

title: Integrating Haptic Vest to the Virtual Reality
authors: Gillian Culberson, Xiaoli Zhang, Qiaojie Zheng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: Human sensory integration is the bridge between technology and humans. Through sensations like tactile sensory input using technological means, a human can experience a more robust virtual simulation while optimizing performance levels. This study investigates how haptic vibrations can effectively impact performance, resulting in a higher hit rate and lower average miss margin than without vibrations, within a virtual baseball simulation. During the baseball simulation a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5}$</tex>-ounce virtual baseball was thrown at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 0}$</tex> miles per hour with a. 01 angular drag. In the baseball simulation, a Haptics vest was used to portray different vibrations during the duration of the ball spawning to when the ball reached the subject. The vibrations were separated into two different trials, with each vibration tested 15 times in random order. The first trial tested various vibrations of left-toright, single continuous, bottom-to-top, slant, and no-vibrations while the baseball followed a consistent trajectory. The second trial tested three different vest vibration locations (left, right, and middle), which corresponded with the baseball trajectory. Both trials resulted in a higher total hit rate and lower average miss margin for each set of vibrations compared to non-vibration. Through observing the total hit rate and average miss margin using a virtual reality baseball simulation, there was a greater performance and hit accuracy with the incorporation of a haptic stimulus.

title: Research on the Operation and Maintenance Decision Support System for Power Transmission Tower Foundations Based on State Perception
authors: Yang Zhang, Yu Zhang, Haichao Peng, Qi Xu, Yuhang Liu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To improve the intelligent identification and early warning capabilities of hidden defects in power transmission tower foundations, this study proposes a state perception-based operation and maintenance decision support system. The method constructs a three-layer collaborative perception system, integrating multi-source data from ground-based InSAR, nearground sensors, and drone inspections. A time-series-physical correlation feature construction method is then designed to extract deep-level indicators, such as the “rainfall response coefficient,” which can sensitively indicate defects. Finally, an innovative gradient boosting decision tree evaluation model is established with physical information constraints, embedding industry safety rules as soft constraints into the machine learning process, achieving highly reliable and interpretable state evaluations. In an 18 -month real-world scenario covering 86 towers, the system achieved a recognition precision of 88.2 % and a recall rate of 85.7 % for “severe defects,” with ablation experiments showing that the introduction of physical constraints reduced false alarms by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 4 \%}$</tex>. Feature attribution analysis revealed that the constructed “rainfall response coefficient” and “simulated differential settlement” were key decision factors in over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0 \%}$</tex> of correct early warning cases. A complete case review showed that the system successfully issued accurate early warnings 21 days before routine inspections. The results indicate that the proposed system effectively closes the loop from multi-source perception to intelligent decision-making, providing reliable technological tools for predictive maintenance of transmission line towers.

title: LeafVascSense-AI: A Dual-Spectrum Embedded Imaging System for Early Detection of Vascular Obstruction and Phenological Stage Identification in Hydroponic Crops
authors: Nasreddine Somaali, Mohamed Hayouni, Hatem Zgallai, Fethi Choubani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Timely detection of physiological stress is critical for optimizing growth and yield in hydroponic crops. This paper introduces LeafVascSense-AI, a dual-spectrum embedded sensing and AI system for early vascular stress detection and phenological classification. The framework integrates thermal and RGB imaging with convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to enable multimodal analysis, supporting robust and autonomous monitoring under low-power and low-bandwidth constraints.In this preliminary study, validation is performed using publicly available thermal datasets to assess the feasibility of stress detection. Results show that physiologically relevant patterns can be effectively captured, allowing localization of stressed leaf and canopy regions. Full dual-spectrum integration and proprietary dataset acquisition are currently underway.The system architecture combines an ESP32-based embedded node, LoRa communication, and fault-tolerant local storage, providing a scalable and energy-efficient solution for continuous monitoring. While end-to-end deployment and embedded inference remain in development, the design establishes a solid foundation for real-world implementation.LeafVascSense-AI advances plant phenotyping by coupling multimodal sensing with edge-oriented AI and embedded system design, enabling early stress detection and phenological analysis, and paving the way for future work on multimodal fusion, real-time decision support, and pilot-scale hydroponic validation.

title: Trustworthy and Transparent AI for Mitigating Insider Threats in Critical Infrastructure Security (CIS)
authors: Syed Muhammad Hasan Danish
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Insider threats pose a persistent challenge to critical infrastructure security due to their stealthy nature, privileged access, and the difficulty of distinguishing malicious actions from legitimate user behavior. Traditional rule-based monitoring systems often fail to capture subtle patterns of insider compromise, while complex artificial intelligence (AI) solutions risk becoming opaque “black boxes” that limit operator trust and accountability. This paper proposes a theoretical framework for trustworthy and transparent AI-based insider threat detection that integrates principles of explainable AI (XAI), fairness, and robustness into cybersecurity operations for critical infrastructure. Using a lightweight machine learning pipeline executed on Google Colab, simulation of insider activities with synthetic log data demonstrates how classifiers (e.g., logistic regression, random forest) combined with SHAP-based interpretability can highlight anomalous access patterns while maintaining human-interpretable insights. Results indicate that transparent models achieve competitive detection performance (AUC $\gt0.85$) while enabling accountability and operator trust, an essential requirement in mission-critical sectors such as energy, healthcare, and transportation. The proposed framework advances the global discourse on responsible and secure AI deployment, addressing both technical challenges (adversarial robustness, bias mitigation) and governance requirements (transparency, accountability) in critical infrastructure cybersecurity.

title: Unified Energy Efficiency Index (UEEI): A Holistic Framework for Energy-Aware Object Detection
authors: Ala Eddine Hsairi, Asma Ben Letaifa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Modern object detection models achieve remarkable accuracy but often incur substantial computational and energy costs, posing challenges for deployment on edge devices and other resource-constrained platforms. While prior works report isolated metrics such as FLOPs, latency, or mAP, there is a lack of a unified framework to evaluate models holistically across energy, performance, and efficiency dimensions. In this paper, we introduce the Unified Energy Efficiency Index (UEEI), a novel metric that integrates energy per inference, latency, computational efficiency, and detection performance into a single scalar index. UEEI enables analytical comparison of models, guides architectural design choices (e.g., convolution vs. attention layers), and provides a standardized evaluation framework for energy-aware object detection. Using representative models including YOLOv5, YOLOv7, and YOLOv13, we demonstrate that UEEI captures non-intuitive trade-offs: models with slightly lower accuracy but substantially lower energy and latency can achieve higher overall efficiency. The proposed framework offers both theoretical insights and practical guidance for designing and selecting energy-conscious object detection architectures, paving the way toward more sustainable and efficient AI systems.

title: SE-HybridNet: Squeeze-and-Excitation Enhanced Hybrid Filter Based on EfficientNet for Haze Scene Classification
authors: Soorya K, Cynthia J
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Visual recognition systems undergoing outdoor scene classification in the presence of atmospheric impurities like haze and smoke from wildfires face huge challenges. The imposition of real-world conditions such as contrast degradation, low visibility, and inconsistent illumination has led to traditional CNN-based models not being able to perform very well in terms of accuracy. This study puts forth SE-HybridNet as an image classification framework that is both efficient and robust and which combines the methods of hybrid image filtering, Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) attention, and an EfficientNet backbone. The hybrid preprocessing pipeline acts sequentially integrating those four processes: bilateral smoothing, Gaussian blur, median filtering, and sharpening that so to achieve improving visibility, texture, and structural details. The SE attention block dynamically readjusts channel responses making it possible for the model to indicate and give the importance of haze-relevant features prior to classification. The features after processing are captured by the EfficientNet backbone and sorted into categories like Thick Haze, Thin Haze, Night Haze, and Wildfire. The results of the experiments provide evidence of the claim that SE-HybridNet with 96.4% validation accuracy is superior to the conventional CNN and attention-based methods in terms of both precision and robustness. The methodology put forward indicates that hybrid enhancement along with channel-aware learning can have a significant positive impact on environmental scene understanding.

title: Empowering English Writing Assessment and Feedback through Large Language Models: A Hybrid Framework Integrating Local Fine-Tuning and Prompt Engineering
authors: Huijun Ma, Charuni Samat, Mingjie Li, Jiajun Zhang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study investigates the application potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) in English writing assessment and feedback. Experiments were conducted to compare a fine-tuned local small-scale LLM with the web-based DeepSeek-R1 model, optimized via prompt engineering. Results indicate that for composition scoring, the fine-tuned local model achieved moderate consistency with human grading, validating its feasibility. Conversely, regarding instructional feedback, while the local model underperformed, the DeepSeek-R1 model combined with optimized prompt engineering yielded significant improvements, demonstrating that high-quality feedback is attainable through prompt engineering. Consequently, provided privacy risks are controlled, teachers and students can leverage such LLMs to effectively enhance English writing instruction and learning outcomes.

title: Detection of Filled Marks of Answer Sheets by using Deep Learning for Automated Scoring
authors: Li Dandan, Zhang Xinyu, Zhang Yingjie
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Aiming at alleviating teachers' daily burden of grading exams, an object detection method is proposed by using deep learning for the automatic recognition of filled marks on answer sheet images, which are captured by mobile cameras, such as smart phones, cameras, and CCD embedded on laptop etc.). Considering that detection of filled marks on answer sheets is key to achieving automated scoring, in this paper, a Faster R-CNN architecture-based neutral network model is established. To solve the difficulty in the collection of the training dataset, a dataset generating program is developed to simulate the marks filled by human beings, and image augmentation is also used to avoid overfitting. Further, 3000 images are generated for training and validating the neural network model. To validate the pre-trained model, the model evaluation is conducted to verify its properties and the indicators, including recognition accuracy of 97.16%, average IoU of 95.19%, weighted IoU of 97.91%, and average BF-Score of 99.93%. Finally, several answer sheets written by varying tools, including pencil, pen, sign pen, and brush pen, and captured under varying light conditions, are used for testing. The results demonstrate the reliability and robustness of the proposed method. t.

title: Study on the Evolution of Physicochemical and Electrical Properties of Low-Proportion Mineral Oil Blended with Natural Ester Insulating Oil
authors: Feng Tang, Zhenfa Gao, Hongmin Zou, Chunlei Guo, Zhenyang Yang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: In the green transformation and retrofilling projects of transformers, a small amount of mineral oil will inevitably remain in natural ester insulating oil, and the law of its performance evolution urgently requires systematic evaluation. This paper takes FR3 natural ester insulating oil and Karamay 25# mineral oil as the research objects, prepares blended insulating oil samples with different mineral oil volume fractions $(1 \% \sim 11 \%)$, and systematically investigates the variation characteristics of their physicochemical properties, electrical properties and combustion properties. The results show that the incorporation of low-proportion mineral oil can reduce the acid value, density and pour point of the blended insulating oil, while increasing the breakdown voltage and volume resistivity and decreasing the dielectric loss factor. Meanwhile, the flash point, fire point and limiting oxygen index (LOI) decrease with the increase of mineral oil proportion, but still maintain a high fire safety margin under the condition of low-proportion incorporation. The research results can provide experimental basis for the retrofilling of mineral oil transformers with natural ester insulating oil.

title: Research on the Electromagnetic Interference Coupling Mechanism and Quantitative Suppression of Hall Sensors in DC Brushless Motors
authors: Ke Mei, Zhifeng Li, Jian Yang, Xiaofei Li, Guofeng Zhang, Xiang Li, Chao Gu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The Hall position sensor serves as the core component for electronic commutation in brushless DC motors (BLDCMs). However, its operation is susceptible to interference from the stator winding electromagnetic field, leading to position measurement errors, disrupted commutation timing, and ultimately causing phase current surges and overcurrent shutdowns. Existing anti-interference solutions primarily rely on passive adjustments, lacking quantitative design principles.Based on an in-depth analysis of interference mechanisms, this paper conducts systematic comparative tests between Hall sensor mounting positions and winding phase currents. It comprehensively acquires interference data under various operating conditions, establishes a quantitative interference assessment system, and innovatively proposes a dual-dimensional active anti-interference technology combining “mounting phase optimization” and “phase current waveform regulation. ”Experimental validation demonstrates that this technology achieves a maximum suppression rate exceeding 80% for Hall sensor phase angle deviation, while enhancing motor load capacity by over 40%. It effectively resolves motor operational stability issues caused by electromagnetic field interference.

title: Research on Macro Placement Optimization Algorithm for Integrated Circuits Based on Improved Q-Learning
authors: Hongyu Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the continuous increase in integrated circuit scale, macro placement has become an extremely challenging NP-hard problem in physical design. Traditional placement algorithms struggle to simultaneously minimize wirelength, satisfy density constraints, and control congestion. This paper proposes DQPlace, an improved Q-Learning-based macro placement optimization algorithm. The placement problem is modeled as a Markov Decision Process. Double DQN is adopted to eliminate Q-value overestimation, ResNet-18 extracts canvas image features, a 4-layer 8-head GAT captures netlist hypergraph structural features, and a macro priority sorting mechanism is introduced. The reward function comprehensively considers half-perimeter wirelength increment, density violation, and congestion penalty to achieve multi-objective immediate optimization. Experiments on the ISPD-2005 benchmark suite show that, compared with the PPO method, DQPlace reduces HPWL by 8.2% on average, peak density by 6.6%, congestion rate by 17.1%, and runtime by 19%, while demonstrating strong generalization on unseen circuits. Ablation studies further verify the effectiveness of the Double DQN, GAT, and priority sorting modules. This work provides a new perspective for the application of deep reinforcement learning in the EDA field.

title:
Interface-regulated ultra-Sensitive, highly moisture-resistant, low-temperature H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    S gas sensing platform based on SnO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -sensitized In
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
authors: Zhenghan Zhong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This work successfully synthesized SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> heterojunction nanosheet materials via a two-step hydrothermal method and investigated their gas-sensing performance toward H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S. Experimental results indicate that at 100 ℃, the S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> sensor exhibits a response value of 239 to 10 ppm H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S, representing a 12.6-fold enhancement compared to pristine In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">a</inf>/R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf>=19). Concurrently, the incorporation of SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> significantly improves material selectivity and lowers the detection limit to 10 ppb. Furthermore, the sensor maintains 60% response at 80% relative humidity, demonstrating excellent moisture resistance. The performance enhancement primarily stems from three mechanisms: the n-n heterojunction formed at the SnO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>/In<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> interface promoting electron migration; the strong adsorption of H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S by Sn<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4+</sup> acidic sites; and the increased oxygen vacancy density facilitating surface reactions. This study provides an effective approach for developing high-sensitivity, high-selectivity, low-temperature, and moisture-resistant H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>S gas sensors.

title: An Innovative Application for E-Waste Collection and Recycling Marketplace
authors: Sarah Susan Abraham, Gowtham Padmanabhan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Electronic waste (e-waste) constitutes the most rapidly expanding category of hazardous waste on a global scale. In developing nations such as India, over 90% of e-waste is managed by the informal sector utilizing unregulated practices like open burning, thereby presenting significant environmental and public health hazards. To mitigate this socio-environmental dilemma, we propose the EcoCycle Marketplace, an innovative web-based platform constructed on the MERN stack (MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js). In contrast to conventional disposal platforms, Eco-Cycle incorporates Client-Side Edge AI (TensorFlow.js) for instantaneous e-waste identification and automated heuristic weight assessment. The system effectively connects consumers with formal recyclers through a dual-dashboard architecture that features a secure logistics escrow mechanism. To promote user engagement within the system, the platform employs a closed-loop token economy ("Green Points") along with real-time micro-impact metrics that incentivize users to responsibly dispose of items both financially and psychologically. This formalization of the recycling lifecycle, in conjunction with the deployment of a locally executed Natural Language Processing (NLP) Eco-Bot and the establishment of immutable digital traceability, aligns this initiative closely with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production).

title: Research on Coordinated Optimization Strategies for Electro-Gas Coupled Microgrids
authors: Youzhong Wu, Jinzhong Li
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: With rising renewable energy penetration, the stochastic nature of distributed generation has intensified wind and solar curtailment, causing economic penalties. To address this, this paper proposes a coordinated optimization strategy for an integrated electricity-gas microgrid incorporating Power-toGas (P2G) technology. A microgrid model comprising microturbines, renewables, storage, and P2G is established. Subsequently, a dispatch model minimizing comprehensive operating costs-including specific curtailment penalties-is formulated and solved using the Genetic Algorithm (GA). Case studies demonstrate that P2G effectively absorbs surplus renewable energy, significantly reducing penalty costs and enhancing both the system’s economic efficiency and energy utilization rate.

title: UART Design and Verification of Compatible Baud Rate Communication
authors: Esakki Rajavel S, Kashwin M S, Pravin A, Vinoth Rajkumar G, Viniston Suthahar J, D J Joel Devadass Daniel
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter commonly know as UART is serial, asynchronous, full-duplex communication protocol which is widely used in the embedded systems and microcontrollers. This paper uses Verilog HDL to design the advanced UART architecture to enhances the auto baud rate detection using adaptive control logic. The design modules contain baud rate generator, transmitter, receiver, Asynchronous FIFO and adaptive control logic and the majority of conventional UART designs has only 8 -bit data transfer in this design we extend the data size to 32-bit and Most of the existing UART has fixed baud rate communication here we proposed the dynamic baud rate detection which the baud rate will be changed upon the data size, if the data size is < 8 it will select 9600 and if it is >14 it will select 115200 for the reliable transmission and receives and verifies the functionality using system verilog and functional verification and constraint random verification, to verify the functionality of the design. All the codes written in Verilog HDL and System verilog for verification using Xillinx vivado and EDA playground.

title: CAE-YOLO: An Improved Detection Algorithm for Steel Surface Defects
authors: Nini Xiao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the limitations of traditional YOLOv5s in steel surface defect detection, including inadequate positional information capture, imbalanced global-local feature representation, and suboptimal small object detection, this paper proposes an improved algorithm with three key optimizations: (1) integration of a Coordinate Attention (CA) mechanism into the backbone network to enhance sensitivity to defect orientation and location; (2) introduction of an Explicit Visual Center (EVC) module for preserving local features and corner information while maintaining global context; and (3) adoption of WIoU loss to specifically optimize small object detection. Experimental results on the NEU-DET dataset demonstrate that the proposed model achieves a mAP@0.5 of 77.8%, representing a 13.25% relative improvement over the baseline YOLOv5s while maintaining real-time performance and fulfilling the requirements for industrial surface defect inspection.

title: Design and Implementation of an ISO-Compliant Life Cycle Assessment Framework for Environmental Footprint Evaluation of Corrugated Packaging
authors: Prem Lohia, Aryan Somanna, S Gagan, Venkatesh K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Corrugated packaging is currently common in contemporary supply chains due to its recyclability, low cost, and versatility. However, despite its environmentally friendly nature, the production of corrugated packaging is characterized by a number of labor-intensive processes, energy-intensive processes, and water that have negative impacts on the environment. Therefore, it is important to have an accurate and transparent quantification of these impacts for sustainability reporting, regulatory compliance, and eco-design decision-making. This paper describes the design and development of a prototype calculator for the environmental footprint of corrugated packaging based on the Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) approach. The calculator is ISO 14040/14044-compliant and follows the cradle-to-gate approach with industry benchmark data from FEFCO 2023. A reverse mass balance method is used to simulate material flows, and mathematical expressions are used to calculate the carbon footprint, primary energy demand, and water scarcity. The prototype calculator is shown to be capable of identifying hotspots stage-by-stage and to be effective in the proposed methodology.

title: A Dual-Driven Model for Cultivating Innovative Control Postgraduates: Integrating Interdisciplinary Knowledge with Generative AI
authors: Junjie Liu, Lipeng Zhang, Yuehui Ji, Qiang Gao
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The rapid evolution of the intelligent era presents significant challenges to postgraduate education in control disciplines, notably rigid disciplinary boundaries and a lag in incorporating cutting-edge technologies. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel training model designed to enhance the innovation capability of postgraduate students. This model is propelled by a dual-drive mechanism: "Interdisciplinary Integration" and "Generative Artificial Intelligence." It achieves its objectives through a two-pronged approach: first, by deeply embedding generative AI tools into the core curriculum to broaden students' knowledge base and reinforce systems thinking; and second, by systematically integrating these tools into the entire scientific research training workflow to improve research efficiency and deepen innovative outcomes. The ultimate goal is to cultivate a new generation of control specialists who are proficient in leveraging intelligent technologies to solve complex, interdisciplinary problems at the forefront of the field. This work aims to provide a replicable and scalable paradigm for reforming postgraduate education in control and related discipline.

title: Techno-Economic Analysis of a Hybrid Microgrid-Powered Reverse Osmosis Desalination System Using HOMER Software: A Case Study for Thanjavur, India
authors: Ramkiran.B, Ruma Arora Soni, Ahmad Fitri Yusop, Jayaseelan Veera Sundaram, Shanmuga Priya, Sudhakar Kumarasamy
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The twin challenges of energy shortages and freshwater scarcity represent some of the most pressing concerns facing modern society, particularly in rapidly developing regions of South Asia. Because energy is an essential prerequisite for water purification, these two crises are inherently interlinked. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of a hybrid microgrid-powered reverse osmosis (RO) desalination system, simulated in HOMER Pro, for a real-world case study in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu, India. Leveraging the abundant solar irradiation in the region, various hybrid renewable energy configurations, including photovoltaic (PV) panels, wind turbines, and battery storage, are evaluated and compared based on net present cost (NPC) and greenhouse gas (GHG) emission reduction. Two principal system architectures are investigated: configurations with battery energy storage and those without. A systematic sizing and selection methodology for microgrid components is proposed to optimally match the energy demand of the RO plant. Results demonstrate that appropriately designed hybrid renewable energy systems can deliver cost-competitive freshwater production while substantially curtailing carbon emissions relative to diesel-only baselines. The findings provide actionable guidance for sustainable water infrastructure planning across similar water-stressed, solar-rich regions in India and beyond

title: Early Feature Identification of Thermal Runaway in New Energy Vehicle Battery Packs Based on Lightweight YOLOv9
authors: Boran Ren
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper addresses the delayed response of electrical signal-based warning systems for thermal runaway in new energy vehicle batteries. It proposes an early warning method using infrared thermal imaging and a lightweight YOLOv9 model for visual feature recognition. An efficient network structure is designed to meet real-time onboard processing requirements. A multimodal warning model fuses time-series temperature distribution and spatial hotspot features to capture subtle pre-runaway temperature anomalies. Experiments on a real-vehicle thermal dataset show the optimized model achieves 97.5% <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{m A P} {@} 0.5$</tex>, while reducing parameters by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim 80.6 \%$</tex> and computational complexity by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${\sim 8 5. 2 \%}$</tex>. It provides an average early warning time of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{3 7. 5}$</tex> minutes, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\sim\text{2 2. 3}$</tex> minutes earlier than the original model, significantly outperforming traditional electrical monitoring methods. This offers an efficient and reliable technical path for battery safety management.

title: Distributed Computing for Real-Time Traffic Analysis - A Kafka-Spark Integration
authors: B.L. Velammal, Samyuctaa Sriram, Sahana S, Meenakshy A
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Modern urban transportation systems produce huge amounts of real-time data that need processing power beyond what traditional centralized computing can offer. This paper introduces a distributed computing setup that uses Apache Kafka and Apache Spark to process and analyze streaming traffic data across several cluster nodes. The system features a three- tier setup, including data ingestion, distributed processing, and analytical layers, built on a master-worker configuration with one master node and two worker nodes. Apache Kafka acts as the messaging backbone, providing reliable data streaming. Apache Spark delivers scalable, parallel processing for real-time traffic analysis. The system includes smart algorithms for congestion detection and dynamic load balancing, which improve traffic distribution across different routes based on current conditions. The evaluation shows that the implementation achieves high processing speed with low delay, successfully managing continuous data streams that update every five seconds. The distributed architecture offers fault tolerance and horizontal scalability, enabling the system to keep running during node failures and to increase capacity by adding more worker nodes. This work confirms the effectiveness of open-source distributed computing tools for smart transportation uses, laying a strong groundwork for scalable traffic management solutions in smart cities.

title: Design and Analysis of External Rotor High Speed Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor for Flywheel Energy Storage
authors: Deyun Pan, Fengge Zhang, Yanwen Zheng, Bin Rui
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: This study takes a flywheel energy storage system as the application background and focuses on the stringent requirements it imposes on the drive motor in terms of high power density, high efficiency, and high reliability. Accordingly, a highspeed outer-rotor permanent magnet synchronous motor with a rated power of 100 kW and a rated speed of $20,000 \mathrm{r} / \mathrm{min}$ is designed. Under high-speed operating conditions, the eddy current loss in the permanent magnets increases significantly, which not only reduces the motor efficiency but also leads to magnet overheating and an elevated risk of demagnetization. To address this issue, the work investigates the influence of permanent magnet segmentation on eddy current loss. By varying the number of magnet segments and comparing the results with those of a conventional motor employing integral magnets, the variation of eddy current loss in the magnets under different segmentation schemes is systematically analyzed, and its impact on the overall loss distribution and efficiency is examined, aiming to achieve an optimal trade-off between eddy current reduction and structural complexity. A two-dimensional finite element model of the motor is established in Ansys Maxwell, and electromagnetic field as well as eddy current loss simulations are carried out for different segmentation numbers. The internal loss density distribution of the magnets and the quantitative relationship between total loss and segmentation number are obtained, from which the optimal segmentation scheme suitable for the targeted operating conditions is determined.

title: Equipment Selection Optimization of Oilfield Distribution Network by Improved Interval Multi-Objective Optimization Algorithm
authors: Anping Liu, Kun Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the continuous development of oilfield area exploitation, the periodic start-stops of intermittent wells and the random derating of faulty wells lead to continuous load disturbances. This makes it difficult for traditional static equipment selection to match real-time well conditions and continuously achieve the goal of energy saving and efficiency improvement. In addition, there may be sudden changes in well conditions and uncertainty information in different oil wells under actual working conditions. To this end, this paper proposes the DNSGA-III-CL algorithm with central point and autoregressive prediction functions. Firstly, this paper constructs a dynamic interval multi-objective optimization model with the goals of minimizing power loss and maximizing system efficiency. Secondly, a first-order autoregressive dynamic prediction model is constructed using the central point sequence of the previous P generations to predict the center of the feasible region at the next moment, thereby quickly locating the new search direction after sudden changes in well conditions. Individuals are sorted according to non-domination and elitism, and the top 80% of high-quality individuals replace the worst 20% of the parent generation to achieve a dynamic balance between rapid convergence and continuous exploration. Experimental results show that the DNSGA-III-CL algorithm significantly enhances the energy-saving effect and adaptability of the oilfield distribution network and has good prospects for on-site promotion.

title: Lightweight Cryptographic Framework for IoT-Enabled Healthcare Environments
authors: V. Ceronmani Sharmila, K. Vedhasakthi, S. Suthaviji, D. Velmurugan, D. Priyadharshini, Lavanya Settipalli
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Modern healthcare applications started using the expanded version of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies and significantly enhanced continuous patient monitoring, emergency response, and remote clinical decision-making. But, interconnected medical devices are deployed in large-scale which introduces serious challenges related to data confidentiality, integrity, latency, and resilience against evolving cyber threats. These challenges are addressed through the proposed SecHealth-Lite, a lightweight and secure framework tailored for IoT-enabled healthcare environments. The important elements of this framework are: (i) a lightweight advanced ECC-based encryption protocol, LECCEP with replay protection, which ensures low-latency and entropy. augmented secure data exchange among resource constrained medical devices; and (ii) a robust Hybrid Anomaly Detection and Trust Management System by combining LSTM, VAE, and SVM models with adaptive trust aggregation to detect both known and unknown attacks in real-time which can be dealt with under this proposed framework. Python-based simulation was used for testing under scenarios of various attacks and with different numbers of malicious nodes. Performance is assessed using key metrics including detection accuracy, false positive rate, latency, and throughput. Experimental results demonstrate that SecHealth-Lite achieves 97% detection accuracy, 2% FPR, 80-95 ms latency, and 150-180 Kbps throughput, consistently outperforming two baseline models by 5-8% in accuracy and 25-40% in operational efficiency. These results confirm that SecHealth-Lite provides an effective, scalable, and practical security solution for real-world healthcare IoT deployments.

title: Mechanism and Circuit Design of Intelligent Explosive Disposal Robot
authors: Bailong Li, Dan Zhou, Qian Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: In recent years, modularity, high mobility and fast task adaptation have become important research directions of special robots (such as explosive ordnance disposal robots).
