title: Multimodal Anemia Detection Using Nail and Eye Images with Numerical Parameters
authors: A. Nibitha Arul, W. Jeba Wincy Deborah, G. Gifta, I.V. Binola K Jebalin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: One of the common blood disease is Anemia that creates difficulty for the body to carry oxygen because it lowers the levels of haemoglobin. Traditional methods of diagnosing anaemia requires invasive blood tests performed in laboratories by trained professionals, which can be time-consuming and painful for patients. In this work, we propose a more patient-friendly approach: a multimodal machine learning system that uses non-invasive images of the eye and nail, with blood parameters such as Mean Corpuscular Volume (MCV), Haemoglobin (Hb), Mean Corpuscular Haemoglobin (MCH), and gender. There are two branches of deep learning in the system. One branch, based on DenseNet169, collects features from pictures, and the other works with numbers. Final prediction is done by a fusion layer puts the two branches together. After being trained on a larger dataset with TensorFlow, the model got an accuracy of 94-95%. With OpenCV, you can click photos and make predictions right away to check for anaemia. It is quicker, less intrusive, and easier to get than regular tests. This approach is a huge step forward in helping clinicians diagnose anaemia more easily.

title: Design of a Cloud Platform Antenna Array Millimeter-Wave Radar
authors: Xiaofei Xu, Jie Miao, Yutian Hu, Wenyi Zhang, Mianchen Huang, Zhiqiang Fang, Qian Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the challenges of occlusion, interference, and the lack of a systematic cloud-edge collaborative framework in fall detection within real home environments, this paper designs and implements a cloud-edge collaborative perception system based on a 24GHz antenna array millimeter-wave radar. First, the design of the core sensing front-end is completed. A circularly polarized microstrip patch antenna with a slit-coupled cross-slot ground structure is optimized through HFSS simulation. It resonates at 24.15GHz with a gain of 7.3dB, effectively reducing multipath interference. The system uses ESP32 as the edge computing node to perform local signal denoising, feature extraction, and fall judgment, uploading only abnormal events and feature vectors to the cloud platform, significantly reducing network load and alarm delay. Tests indicate that the system performs well in key metrics such as fall recognition accuracy (≥93%), false alarm rate (≤4%), and alarm delay (≤1.2s), and achieves multi-tenant management, remote alarms, and open integration via the Alibaba Cloud IoT platform, providing a highly reliable and scalable solution for smart elderly care.

title: Gaussian Process-Based Adaptive Multi-Fidelity Fusion with Cost-Aware Active Learning
authors: Jun Lu, Sheng Jiang, Fanghua Bai, Xin Yang, Liang Zhou, Wei Hu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To address the challenges of high computational costs in obtaining high-fidelity data and suboptimal sample distribution in engineering applications, this study proposes a multi-fidelity data fusion method based on Gaussian process regression. An Ensemble Multi-fidelity Gaussian Process (EMFGP) model integrated with a novel Expected Improvement criterion (NEWEI) is developed to achieve adaptive sampling and effective fusion of multi-fidelity data. Validation results from numerical examples and a battery pack side pole impact case demonstrate that the proposed method maintains prediction accuracy while significantly reducing computational costs and highfidelity sample requirements. Compared with existing methods such as Multi-Fidelity Neural Network (MFNN) and Ensemble of Two-Level Neural Networks (E2NN), EMFGP shows remarkable advantages in computational efficiency, sample utilization, and training stability, providing an effective technical solution for the optimization design of complex engineering problems.

title: Design of A DVOR Loop Reconfigurable Antenna for Aircraft Navigation System
authors: Ruru Wang, Lifan Wang, Yifan Zhang, Yongmou Yan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Aiming at the requirements of high efficiency and anti-interference ability of aircraft navigation system, this paper designs a Loop Reconfigurable Antenna for Doppler VHF Omnidirectional Radio-range (DVOR) antenna system from the perspective of frequency reconfiguration. The main body of the antenna consists of three parts: Alford loop radiator, two pairs of tunable capacitor dials and High-power capacity matching network. The antenna adopts a narrow-band point tuning method, and the impedance matching of each frequency point is realized by adjusting the length of the end vibrator and the spacing of the tunable capacitor dials at the same time through the U-shaped Balun impedance transformation characteristics. In the whole working frequency band of 108MHz-118MHz, the standing wave ratio is better less than 1.15, the maximum Gain is 1.7dBi, the amplitude difference of the horizontal radiation field is better than 0.8dB, and the phase difference is less than 6°. It has the characteristics of High-power capacity, Strong environmental stability, Frequency reconfiguration and Better horizontal omnidirectionality, which meets the needs of aircraft navigation system.

title: Strain Modulation of Terahertz Emission from Flexible Pt/CoFeB/Ta Heterostructures
authors: Tianyu Hong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This study systematically investigates the modulation of terahertz (THz) emission characteristics of Pt/CoFeB/Ta heterostructures prepared on flexible polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) substrates by uniaxial strain. Through the "pre-strain-deposition-release" technique, controllable residual strain was successfully introduced along the magnetization direction (x-direction) of the heterostructures, and their THz radiation was characterized using terahertz emission spectroscopy (TES). The experimental results indicate that strain applied along the magnetization direction exerts a significant and anisotropic modulation on THz emission. In the strain direction (x-direction), the peak of the THz time-domain signal is significantly enhanced by more than 37%, while the central frequency and spectral bandwidth remain stable. In the perpendicular direction (y-direction), due to the opposite strain induced by the Poisson effect, the THz peak exhibits a regular decrease. These phenomena suggest that strain primarily induces and enhances the in-plane uniaxial magnetic anisotropy of the CoFeB ferromagnetic layer through the inverse magnetostriction effect, thereby optimizing the spin-to-charge conversion efficiency and influencing the post-excitation relaxation dynamics, without altering the fundamental excitation mechanism of THz radiation. This work elucidates the coupling relationship among strain, magnetic order, and terahertz emission, demonstrates the effectiveness of strain engineering in achieving efficient and anisotropic terahertz modulation in flexible spintronics, and provides critical insights and experimental foundations for the development of novel strain-tunable terahertz devices.

title: CASAB-Net: Direction-Aware Strip Convolution and Hybrid Attention for Real-Time Autonomous Driving Object Detection
authors: Zhiqian Qin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Real-time object detection is a fundamental component of autonomous driving perception systems. However, existing lightweight detectors often struggle to achieve a satisfactory balance between detection accuracy and computational efficiency when dealing with complex driving scenarios, such as small objects, background clutter, and large-scale variations. To address these challenges, this paper proposes CASAB-Net, a lightweight real-time object detection framework built upon YOLOv11 for autonomous driving environments. The proposed network introduces two key modules to enhance feature representation while maintaining efficient inference. First, a Channel–Spatial Aggregated Boosting (CASAB) module is designed to improve feature discrimination during multi-scale feature fusion. By combining channel attention and spatial attention mechanisms, CASAB enables the network to emphasize informative semantic channels while suppressing irrelevant background noise, thereby enhancing the detection of small and distant objects. Second, a Direction-Aware Strip Convolution (StripConv) module is integrated into the backbone network to replace conventional square convolutions. By sequentially applying horizontal and vertical strip convolutions, the module effectively captures long-range directional dependencies with lower computational overhead, improving structural feature modeling and localization accuracy. Extensive experiments are conducted on the KITTI and BDD100K datasets. The results demonstrate that CASAB-Net achieves superior detection performance compared with the baseline YOLOv11 while maintaining real-time inference speed and lightweight computational complexity. These results indicate that the proposed method provides an efficient and practical solution for real-time perception in autonomous driving systems.

title: Optimization method of dynamic multi-robot picking scheduling based on reinforcement learning
authors: Mengmeng Xie
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This study formulates the dynamic multi-robot picking scheduling problem as a Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP) and applies reinforcement learning for decision-making, with a tailored state encoding and reward mechanism. Field tests with six robots in a real warehouse (normal, peak-load, abnormal conditions) demonstrate that the method outperforms three classical scheduling approaches in efficiency, task responsiveness and robustness, verifying the practical potential of reinforcement learning in dynamic robot scheduling.

title: A Detection Method for Text Input-Induced Non-Crashing Functional Bugs in Mobile GUI Testing
authors: Qijun Liang, Chuanyu Li, Fengyu Yang, Qionglong Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In mobile GUI testing, non-crashing functional bugs (NCFBs) induced by text input can lead to severe problems such as data errors, logical confusion, and display anomalies. Existing detection methods mainly rely on predefined heuristic rules or specific test oracles, which are difficult to adapt to diverse bug types, resulting in limited detection scope and low efficiency. To systematically address this challenge, this paper proposes an automated testing method based on multimodal large language models (MLLMs). We first construct a three-stage interactive "generation-exploration-verification" automated testing framework: using LLMs to deeply understand the semantics and constraints of input components for generating abnormal inputs; planning test paths through knowledge base construction and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) technology; and utilizing MLLMs to fuse GUI screenshots and semantic information for identifying and classifying NCFBs. We comprehensively evaluated the method on a dataset consisting of 315 real bugs and 45 injected bugs. Comparison with 21 baseline methods demonstrates its overall effectiveness, and ablation experiments further confirm the contribution of each module. Additionally, 19 previously unknown NCFBs were successfully discovered in real-world application testing, 10 of which have been confirmed or fixed by developers, proving its practical value.

title: Administrative AI Chatbot Embedded in a Learning System
authors: Gabriela Kiryakova
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The digitalization of processes within educational institutions is a priority for university management. Learning and teaching are supported by platforms that offer flexible access to educational resources and activities for both students and instructors. Working in a feature-rich environment can be difficult and confusing, making routine tasks challenging to execute. Administrative processes at a university, even when largely digitalized, are often unfamiliar and difficult for students to follow. Modern AI-based technologies are well-suited to addressing such issues. They enable rapid, flexible creation of Chatbot assistants that provide timely, accurate guidance for both academic and administrative tasks. The aim of this paper is to propose a model for developing an administrative AI Chabot that can be integrated into Moodle.

title: A Sustainable Agriwaste-Based Microgrid Integrating Biogas Energy Conversion and Soil Nutrient Recycling
authors: B Dhanam, B Mohan, K Ramalakshmi, K Agnesh, M Selvamani, G Sri Krishnan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: This paper presents a sustainable solution for rural electrification by utilizing agricultural waste as a renewable energy source through an agriwaste-based microgrid. Locally available farm residues, including straw, husk, and animal manure, are converted into energy using anaerobic biogas generation. The produced biogas is utilized as a thermal source for thermoelectric power generation, and the generated electrical energy is distributed through a decentralized microgrid to supply power for rural households and small-scale agricultural applications. The proposed system reduces dependence on fossil fuels, minimizes greenhouse gas emissions, and improves energy accessibility in remote areas. In addition to energy generation, the by-product of the biogas process, known as digested slurry, is recycled as an organic fertilizer, enhancing soil fertility and reducing reliance on chemical fertilizers. By integrating energy production with nutrient recycling, the system follows circular economy principles, ensuring efficient resource utilization and zero-waste operation. The proposed approach contributes to sustainable agriculture and aligns with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals related to clean energy, responsible consumption, and climate action.

title: Automated Vision Transformer Framework For Intelligent Crime-Scene Evidence Detection
authors: T Nithishwar, R Samuel N, Mary Joseph
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Criminal investigations involve processing vast amounts of heterogeneous information, such as criminal records in structured form and narrative representation of criminal events in unstructured form. Such information is laborious to process manually and may actually be constrained in time and mental processes. The paper introduces COPS (Criminal Operandi Prediction System), which is the AI-based decision-support model that allows engaging with the investigative data on a natural-language level. The system combines NLP-based entity extraction, semantic reasoning, and fuzzy matching, to reveal contextual links between suspect, location and behavioral pattern associated to suspects and the location. Vision Transformers work with crime-scene and surveillance images, whereas LSTMs are necessary to identify temporal patterns and repeat-offense sequences. Deep Belief Networks also indicate latent characteristics in past crime data. COPS creates the use of React, FastAPI, Python, and MongoDB to allow secure offline deployment and provide increased investigative speed, predictive reliability, and the results of intelligence-led policing.

title: Development and Design of an Intelligent Anti-slip Robot for Freight Trains: Structure Design
authors: Souliya Phetsaphangthong, Liguo Shuai, Guo Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: The unintended movement of freight trains while stationary is a recurring issue in railway systems, leading to severe safety and operational risks. This research focuses on the design and development of an intelligent anti-slip robot to address this problem. The proposed system uses a robotic mechanism for the automatic installation and removal of wheel chocks, a critical antislip device for preventing train runaway. The design is based on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0 N}$</tex> (CHN60) rail specifications commonly used in freight railway systems in China. The robot's structural framework, locomotion mechanisms, and wheel chock gripping system are optimized for safe and reliable operation under various environmental conditions. Finite element analysis (FEM) is employed to evaluate the structural integrity and performance of key components, including the wheel chock and the robot's frame. The system aims to improve operational efficiency, reduce human intervention, and enhance safety in freight yards. Future work includes prototype development and testing to validate the design's functionality in real-world railway operations.

title: Modeling and Analysis of Primary FrequencyRegulation in Thermal Units Considering InterTemporal Coupling Mechanisms
authors: Pengfei Qi, Huan Ma, Qun Chen, Xiaotong Zhang, Yangyang Ge
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: In power systems with high renewable penetration, the primary frequency regulation capability of thermal power units is crucial for grid frequency stability. However, traditional simplified models often neglect the coupling of working fluid states and boiler thermal inertia, failing to accurately reflect the unit's regulation potential. To address this, this study proposes a thermodynamically consistent first-principle dynamic model, which explicitly characterizes the pressure feedback mechanism during the release of boiler thermal storage. This formulation enables the analysis of inter-temporal coupling effects under diverse temporal loading trajectories. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed model accurately reproduces the power attenuation characteristics caused by thermal storage exhaustion. Quantitative analysis reveals that within the critical 30-second assessment window, the traditional model overestimates the energy contribution by 83.7 % and the peak power by 82.2 %. These findings confirm that neglecting thermal inertia leads to a significant overestimation of power support in long-term simulations, whereas the proposed model provides a physically consistent safety boundary for grid frequency stability assessment.

title: Research on PLC Control of Circular Conveying System Based on Static Path Predefinition and Dynamic Path Reservation
authors: Junge Guo
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Aiming at the key problems of frequent path conflicts, complex scheduling logic and operation efficiency bottlenecks in large-scale circular conveying systems without Rail Guided Vehicles (RGV) under multi-task concurrent scenarios, this paper proposes a collaborative control strategy of “static path predefinition + dynamic path reservation” and a three-layer distributed control architecture. The upper-level WMS/WCS management layer is responsible for global task scheduling and optimal sequencing. Programmable Logic Controller (PLC), as the core execution unit, realizes fast path matching through an offline static path predefinition library, completes real-time resource coordination combined with a dynamic phased reservation mechanism, and is supplemented with conflict avoidance rules and deadlock prevention design. It ensures the consistency of independent decision-making of multiple vehicles and global collaboration, and effectively avoids collision and deadlock risks. Engineering applications on Siemens S7-1500 PLC show that the scheme supports up to 18 concurrent tasks with a peak throughput of 105 pallets per hour, and the conflict rate is reduced to 0. With the engineering characteristics of simple logic, convenient maintenance and strong fault tolerance, it provides a reliable technical solution for the efficient multi-task control of large-scale circular conveying systems.

title: Robust Few-Shot Fake Ad Detection via Conflict-Aware Multi-Slice Feature Aggregation
authors: Panzhi Zhao, Yu Zheng, Lingmin He
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the rapid growth of e-commerce, fake advertisements have become a serious threat to the security and credibility of the online ecosystem. Existing multimodal detection methods typically rely on image resizing, which leads to the loss of fine-grained details in long-image advertisements. Meanwhile, fake advertisement detection faces instance-level noise and severe few-shot constraints. To address these issues, we propose a conflict-aware multi-slice feature aggregation framework for long-image fake advertisement detection. First, we treat each long image as a bag of slices and employ a Slice Attention Fusion module to aggregate local slice-level representations under few-shot settings, thereby mitigating the impact of instance-level noise. Second, motivated by the common phenomenon of semantic inconsistency between text and image in fake advertisements<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">[1]</sup>, we design a Semantic-Aware Dynamic Gating (SADG) module. By explicitly modeling cross-modal discrepancy features and integrating a residual calibration mechanism, SADG transforms multimodal inconsistency into discriminative evidence rather than treating it as mere noise.Experiments conducted on a real-world e-commerce advertisement dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a Macro-F1 score of 0.5677 ± 0.015 under the 8-shot setting, significantly outperforming existing baseline methods. These results verify the effectiveness of explicit multimodal conflict modeling for few-shot long-image fake advertisement detection.

title: RouteXpert: An Explainable Symbolic AI Framework for Multi-Intersection Adaptive Traffic Control
authors: Arjun Jayakrishnan, Tirtha Nath, Baskar M
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Traffic congestion is a major challenge which is globally prevalent. Most of the existing systems are built for the current traffic conditions and as cities continue to grow, they fail to keep up. Most of the solutions propose machine learning due to its better throughput but its high infrastructure costs and their black-box nature remain a problem. To counter this, we introduce RouteXpert, an Explainable Symbolic AI (Artificial Intelligence) Framework which addresses the limitations of the existing implementations and provides additional features. A rule-based logic is used here which also supports multi-intersection coordination. This framework has seven components- a traffic input layer which accepts and processes the inputs, traffic network model layer which represents the entire traffic network as a graph, a Knowledge Base for rule storage, an Inference Engine for symbolic reasoning, an Emergency Pre- emption Module for providing instant routes to emergency vehicles, a signal execution module which makes the decision whether signal should be RED or GREEN, and an explainability and performance analysis layer for generating human-readable decision logs. This approach keeps transparency and semantic consistency intact. The framework proves that a reasoning-based, coordinated system performs much better than traditional fixed-time or machine learning based solutions. This work builds a scalable and secure base for managing traffic in modern cities with uses in autonomous grid management, and emergency response provision.

title: Node2vec-Based Graph Embedding for Deep Learning Graduation Project: Advanced Management
authors: Jiang Jin-Gang, Fu Kang, Shen Tao, Bao Yu-Dong, Kang Fu-Wei, Dong Jing-Hao
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Graduation projects serve as a crucial assessment of undergraduate students' academic achievements over four years, playing an irreplaceable role in talent cultivation. Course instruction, as the primary vehicle for imparting theoretical knowledge, directly influences students' ability to effectively apply learned theories to solve complex engineering problems when undertaking graduation projects. However, current teaching practices face challenges including disconnect between theory and practical application, poor alignment between course content and graduation project requirements, inadequate guidance system coordination, and insufficient personalized instruction. This paper employs the Node2vec algorithm to optimize the course alignment mechanism for advancing graduation projects, ensuring tight integration between course content and design requirements. It establishes a topic selection model that enhances quality by incorporating innovative achievements such as outstanding competition awards, outcomes from undergraduate innovation and entrepreneurship training programs, converted faculty research project results, and completed enterprise-demand projects. This study provides a scalable implementation scheme for cultivating application-oriented, innovative, and interdisciplinary talents in the new era.

title: A Compact Geometry-Modified Dual-Band Planar Antenna with Full-Wave Electromagnetic Analysis for C-Band Applications
authors: S. Rajasekaran, P Yashwanth, D Venkata Suresh, M Vinay Kumar
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This paper presents a compact geometry-modified square planar antenna integrated with multi-sided split-ring resonators and a circular slot structure to improve radiation performance and bandwidth characteristics.The proposed antenna has overall dimensions of 30 × 30 × 1.6 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> and is fabricated on an FR-4 substrate using a 50-Ω micro-strip feed line. The antenna is suitable for compact wireless communication systems.The antenna structure supports multi-band operation across multiple frequency ranges.Two distinct antenna configurations are analyzed. The first design displays dual-band operation at 4.7 GHz and 6.1 GHz, while this second design operates at 1. 6 GHz and 2.4 GHz. This suggested antenna employs the modified ground plane, which allows for effective size reduction, efficient impedance matching, and cancellation of undesired surface current distribution. Throughout the operating band, the VSWR retains below 2, having minimum value 1.02 at resonance, to ensure proper impedance matching. The electromagnetic performance of the proposed antenna is analysed using CST Studio Suite through full-wave simulation.Due to its compact structure,stable radiation characteristics, and improved performance metrics, the new structure, square-shaped, is perfect for a variety of jobs connected to the application of the new technology of 5G communications,WLAN,wireless, or related microwave technology.

title: Exploring an AI-Empowered "1+M+N" Advanced Mathematics Teaching Model Based on a Three-Tier Collaborative Architecture
authors: Lina Jia
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Addressing key challenges in Advanced Mathematics education—its abstractness, insufficient student interest, and lack of humanistic cultivation—this study constructs an innovative AI-empowered "1+M+N" teaching model. Here, "1" is the main thread of mathematical history, "M" denotes core mathematicians’ stories, and "N" represents fundamental mathematical ideas. Based on a three-tiered architecture (resource, technology, teaching layers), a multimodal resource repository and AI-driven functional modules are built to achieve in-depth integration of AI and teaching practice. A case study on "The Concept and Applications of Derivatives" among engineering undergraduates verifies the model. The results show that it effectively resolves abstract teaching difficulties, improves student engagement and knowledge mastery, and facilitates the cultivation of mathematical literacy and inquiry abilities. Moreover, the three-tiered architecture’s synergy ensures sustainable model implementation, providing a feasible path and practical reference for advanced mathematics education reform in the AI era.

title: Techniques of Artificial Intelligence used in the Tailoring Process for Project Management
authors: Samia Saidi, Latifa Ben Arfa Rabai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Having that there is an increase interest on the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in project management and that projects are carried out in complex environments that must balance potentially competing demands, project management, particularly in Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs), requires adapting their project management approach to the specific context in order to maximize the value of production and organization.This paper presents a summary of the application of AI techniques that can enhance the tailoring approaches and processes in project management. The Systematic Literature Review (SLR) was conducted to synthesize the relevant existing studies and highlight trends and inconsistencies. Our main aim is then to analyze framework and articles in the field in order to further improve tailoring process by enhancing it by the use of the AI technique that will lead to successful project outcomes and successful project management processes.

title: Integrating AI Practices in Higher Education: Perspectives from the Islamic University of Technology, Gazipur, Bangladesh
authors: Abdullah Binta Suleiman
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The study examined the integration of IUT Bangladesh AI (Artificial Intelligence). AI technology has been in persistent use over recent years. The survey used a triangulation method, involving 200 IUT students, 60 faculty members, and 10 semi-structured interviews with administrators, curriculum designers, and staff. A review of published documents, ranging from journal articles to archives from the Ministry of Education and other institutional guidelines, was also carried out. Assessment of AI literacy, Technology use attitude, perceived risks, benefits, and self-reported usage were conducted quantitatively. In the meantime, thematic analysis explored institutional readiness, practice considerations, and barriers to the implementation of AI technology. The results determined the potential for integrating AI into curricula, institutional policies, and capacity-building initiatives tailored to IUT and similar Bangladeshi universities. The findings contributed to the open literature through observed data from a South Asian higher education context, addressing a significant geographic and cultural gap in AI integration implementation in education. Limitations involved sampling from a single institution and the fast-changing landscape of AI technology, which limited generalization and timeliness. Recommendations highlighted institution-led strategies, faculty growth, and a nationwide synchronization approach for AI in higher education [1] This research focused on educators, policymakers, and researchers on liable AI incorporation practices for universities in Bangladesh and similar scenarios.

title: Dynamic Collaborative Scheduling Optimization of Multi-Robot Based on PPO in Air-Ground Collaboration Scenes
authors: Zihui Peng, Jintao Zhu, Lingzhi Li, Xincheng Liu, Hailong Jiang, Jifei Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: The Open Robotics Middleware Framework (RMF) provides universal navigation and task collaboration management for multi-robot systems, serving as an ideal carrier for multi-robot collaboration in dynamic scenarios. Aiming at the air-ground collaboration scenario of UAV inspection and UGV disposal, we propose a domain knowledge-guided PPO scheduler and implement a full-process closed-loop system of “perception-localization-scheduling-collaboration-disposal” based on RMF. The proposed method introduces three key strategies—lookahead action pruning, task queue reordering, and nonlinear reward shaping—that jointly guide the PPO agent to achieve efficient and robust global optimization. We formulate the scheduling problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), with a 54-dimensional state space and a dual-objective nonlinear reward mechanism that explicitly encodes efficiency and load balancing. Experimental results show that compared with the greedy strategy, the proposed scheduler reduces the total task completion time by 29.52% on average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{p}<0.05)$</tex> and decreases the variance of UGV task queue lengths by 67.16%. Ablation studies further validate the synergistic effectiveness of the three strategies. We demonstrate the compatibility of data-driven scheduling algorithms with the RMF framework, providing a reusable technical pathway for airground collaborative scheduling in dynamic scenarios.

title: Trust but Verify approach based on Blockchain Towards Secure and Privacy-Aware Metaverse
authors: Arwa Badri, Rihab Jmal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The metaverse is built upon large-scale, shared virtual environments where users interact through digital identities, creating significant challenges related to security, trust, and content moderation. Traditional centralized moderation mechanisms are often opaque and raise concerns regarding accountability, censorship, and privacy. To address these limitations, this paper introduces "Trust but Verify", a blockchain-based moderation framework designed for decentralized metaverse platforms. The proposed approach leverages smart contracts to enforce transparent, tamper-resistant moderation rules while enabling verifiable decision-making without relying on centralized authorities. User actions are evaluated against predefined policies, with reputation and behavior-driven mechanisms regulating access, privileges, and sanctions. Privacy is preserved through cryptographic techniques that limit unnecessary disclosure of user information. A prototype implementation is developed and experimentally evaluated to assess feasibility, scalability, and resilience against malicious behaviors. The results demonstrate that blockchain-enabled moderation enhances accountability and trust while maintaining user privacy, making it a promising foundation for secure and decentralized metaverse governance.

title: Class-Weighted Federated Averaging for Intrusion Detection: An Evaluation-Driven Analysis
authors: Amutha Jeba Princy Ayyadurai, K.B. Sundhara Kumar, Dhivya S
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) play a critical role in protecting modern networks against an increasingly diverse range of cyberattacks. However, the centralized training of IDS models often raises serious concerns related to data privacy, regulatory compliance, and data sharing across administrative domains. Federated Learning (FL) has emerged as a promising paradigm that enables collaborative model training without exposing raw network traffic data. Among existing FL approaches, Federated Averaging (FedAvg) is widely adopted due to its simplicity and communication efficiency. FedAvg implicitly assumes balanced and homogeneous data distributions across clients—an assumption that rarely holds for IDS datasets. Network traffic data are typically highly imbalanced, where benign traffic and a few dominant attack types overshadow rare yet security-critical intrusions. As a result, models trained using FedAvg may achieve high overall accuracy while failing to detect minority attack classes. This limitation raises concerns regarding the reliability of accuracy-centric evaluation in federated IDS settings. To address this issue, Class-Weighted Federated Averaging (CWFedAvg) incorporates class-level weighting during model aggregation, explicitly enhancing the influence of under-represented attack classes. In this paper, a comparative study of FedAvg and CWFedAvg on IDS dataset is performed, emphasizing the need to shift evaluation focus from global accuracy to class-sensitive metrics. The results demonstrate that improved accuracy alone does not guarantee effective intrusion detection and that class-aware aggregation yields better detection coverage for rare attacks, which are often the most critical in real-world deployments

title: Interpretable Deep Learning Models for Rare Genetic Disorder Detection in Low-Resource Settings
authors: G. Uma Devi, Balajee Maram
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: There are obstacles in the identification of rare genetic disorders in resource-limited settings: diagnostic tools and specialists are often not available. This paper discusses the development of a novel deep learning framework which optimally understands and accurately detects rare genetic disorders. The framework consists of a convolutional neural network and attention mechanism on specific important genetic features. The framework also provides explanations for its predictions. The framework was trained on a sample set of 5,000 and validated with an accuracy of 92.5% and an F1 score of 0.91; it also outperformed competing frameworks by a margin of 5%. The absence of competing frameworks illustrates the significance of the framework's explainable architecture. The framework was designed for resource-limited settings to assist non-expert health care workers with the explanatory and prescriptive features of the framework. The framework also emphasizes the importance of providing the model with the ability to be used in various locations around the globe. The framework's adaptability and ability to optimize its design are essential for the detection of genetic disorders in resource-limited settings.

title: Context-Aware Cognitive Fingerprinting for Real-Time Detection of Deceptive Multilingual Websites
authors: K. Inbakumari, S. Aakash, A. Anstron Graif, M. Anand
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The growing expansion of fake pages and false URLs has exposed the inadequacy of existing detection systems, based on static features and language-oriented statuses like maliciousness, geolocation, or external reputation databases. To this aim we define an original real-time detection method that is grounded on Cognitive Website Fingerprinting (CWF), a human-centric technique based on the model of user-perceived trust evolution instead of relying only upon syntactic or lexical indicators. CWF-TDM (Cognitive Fingerprinting with Trust Drift Modeling) is a new technique to uncover intent-action coherence, semantic mismatch, cognitive pressure signals, and temporal trust departure occurring in multilingual online content. It does so through the real-time, dynamic analysis of anomalies in trust acceleration or degradation to discover anything abusive without relying on WHOIS, DNS, or blocklists. An empirical evaluation over a multilingual set of genuine and fake websites reveals an accuracy of around 89.2 %, a precision of 88.6 %, a recall of 90.1 %, and an F1-score of 89.3%. The average inference latency of the system is less than 500 ms, which is compatible with browser extensions and gateway-level security systems. Results indicate that trust modeling inspired by cognition is a novel approach that is effective in identifying zero-day as well as previously unknown bogus websites.

title: Design and Analysis of an Actively Swinging Variable-Diameter Wheel-Leg Robot for ComplexTerrain Locomotion
authors: Haoran Zheng, Bin Lü, Yufeng Liang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: Mobile robots for complex unstructured environments must achieve both efficient flat-ground locomotion and reliable obstacle-surmounting capability. This paper proposes an actively swinging variable-diameter wheel-leg robot for complex terrain locomotion. The mechanism integrates a swinging leg for body lifting and support adjustment with an active variable-diameter wheel for controllable changes in effective wheel radius, allowing adaptation to steps, trenches, and uneven terrain. Planar kinematic and dynamic models are established to analyze the motion characteristics and driving requirements. Simulations and experiments verify the feasibility and terrain adaptability of the proposed design.

title: Emotion Detection Using ECG Signal
authors: Amit Bosco, G Gifta, IV Binola. K. Jebalin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Human emotions have a large effect on our behavior, physical responses and decision making. Accurately identifying and interpreting emotions is one important area of research within biomedical engineering and artificial intelligence. Current techniques for emotion detection (e.g., fidelity, tone of voice and questionnaires) rely on subjective factors, which makes them highly susceptible to the influence of seasonality, conscious control and other things related to the environment. To overcome the limitations of those subjective measures we will develop a valid and objective method for detecting emotions through analysis of Electrocardiograms (ECG). ECG based analysis of the heart's electrical activity provides stable and non-invasive physiological markers that show small changes when emotional states change; thus, making ECG an attractive option for emotion recognition, due to the high level of consistency and resistance to manipulation associated with ECG when compared with traditional methods of recognizing emotions.

title: Co-Optimizing Performance and Security of Analog Integrated Circuits
authors: Abdullah Bayram, Haralampos-G. Stratigopoulos, Engin Afacan
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The globalization of the integrated circuit (IC) supply chain has increased the risk of IC piracy. Analog and mixed-signal (AMS) ICs are particularly vulnerable, as their design demands specialized expertise and multiple tapeouts to meet specifications, while they have limited automation support and inflexible portability across technology nodes, making them valuable intellectual assets and attractive targets for piracy. IC locking has emerged as an effective countermeasure, with several AMS IC locking paradigms proposed to date. The core idea is to embed a key mechanism within the AMS IC to control its functionality, treating the key as the designer's secret. Correct operation is achieved only when the valid key is applied, while any incorrect key renders the circuit non-functional. Existing locking solutions for purely analog blocks face the challenge of ensuring this dual objective. In this work, we propose an optimizationbased methodology that jointly synthesizes the target circuit and its key mechanism, ensuring the objective is deterministically achieved. The methodology is validated on two widely used AMS circuits: a bandgap reference and an operational amplifier.

title: Digital Twin Assisted Dynamic Task Assignment for Multi-UAV Systems Using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hamza, Syeda Sadaf Zaidi, Farzana Kulsoom, Wazir Ali Haideri, Iram Baig, Hassan Nazir Chaudhry
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: In this work a dynamic task allocation for the fleet of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) using a digital twin and multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL) is presented. The main focus is to enhance real-time task assignment and to improve decision-making capabilities during the mission. Traditional approaches based on single-agent learning methods have limited scalability and efficiency in dynamic and crowded environments. To address this a cloud-based infrastructure is proposed in which the digital twin acts as a replica of the UAV and continuously synchronizing its operational states with the environmental data. This process helps in prediction and evaluation and it gives refined control strategies. In this architecture, the MADRL aids cooperative policy learning among UAVs by doing efficient task assignment with the objective of minimizing energy consumption and also adapting quickly to dynamic mission requirements. Rather than relying on localized computation resources in this work task allocation is performed dynamically and training is conducted centrally using the cloud based system. This work also uses adaptive prioritization by considering key parameters such as UAV state, velocity and current task execution by each UAV. Simulation results show notable improvements in system performance, such as increased task completion ratio, reduced energy consumption and better UAV fleet management. Overall the proposed framework improves the coordination and operational efficiency of UAV task assignment even in complex environments.

title: Event-Triggered Optimization Method for Path-Tracking Controller of Gliding Vehicles
authors: Xiaotian Zhang, Kun Qian, Yiyang Peng, Changchun Zhao, Ningyu He
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Currently, gliding UAVs are widely employed in large-scale flight missions such as monitoring and communication under complex environments. During flight missions, the environment is complex and variable, the impact of nonlinear terms in the aircraft model is amplified, and unknown terrain can lead to abrupt changes in the desired path-tracking state of the aircraft. Adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) is a data-driven method that uses neural networks for training and is prone to overfitting. To address the problems of nonlinear effects, abrupt changes in tracking states, and ADP overfitting in large-scale flight missions of aircraft, this paper proposes an optimized pathtracking controller method for gliding aircraft with an eventtriggered mechanism. First, based on the concept of ADP, two BP neural networks are used to approximate the value function and control strategy, and the gradient descent method is adopted to simulate the strategy iteration process, such that the optimal parameters of the controller are output by the execution network. Second, based on the principle of Lyapunov's second method, an event-triggered mechanism is established for the pitch and yaw channels, and the trigger condition is determined when the number of times the training data required by ADP fails to satisfy the stability principle exceeds a defined threshold. Finally, simulation results verify that the proposed algorithm can effectively reduce response time and improve control accuracy.

title: Comparison of SMOTE and Class Weight Balancing in Deep Learning for Breast Lesion Classification Using Ultrasound Images
authors: Muhammad Omar Cheema, Zia Mohy-Ud-Din
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: This paper compares the efficacy of SMOTE (Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique) with ADASYN (Adaptive Synthetic Sampling), which have been combined with class weight balancing to deal with the issue of class imbalance during the classification of benign and malignant breast lesions. Based on a small set of ultrasound images, we have trained two Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models and compared them. The model composed of SMOTE attained a test accuracy of $64 \%$. Though it gave a recall of 0.37 in the malignant class, its accuracy was very poor at 0.25 thus giving an F1-score of 0.30. This indicates that this model overfitted; therefore, the number of false positives was great, and its general discriminating power was low, as indicated by an ROC AUC score of 0.512. Conversely, the ADASYN-based model was much more effective, and its test accuracy was $83 \%$. It also performed better in terms of balance on the malignant class, having precision of 0.57, recall of 0.40, and $F 1$-score of 0.47. The higher ROC AUC score of 0.728 when compared to the other models depicts the greater classification power that the model has. These findings show that, in this medical imaging task, ADASYN is a better method than SMOTE. The adaptive methodology of ADASYN in generating synthetic samples of hard-to-classify minority examples resulted in a more robust and balanced model, which illustrated a strong benefit over the class imbalance with a small amount of medical samples.

title: AI-enhanced Design of Interdisciplinary Thematic Learning in High School Biology: From the Perspective of Integrated STEM Education
authors: Rutian Chen, Chunyan Feng, Xinyue Shen, Zhe Zhou
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the context of Industry 4.0, traditional single-subject teaching is unable to meet the demand for cultivating innovative and interdisciplinary talents. Integrated STEM education, by placing disciplinary knowledge in real problem-solving contexts, provides good support for the cultivation of 21st-century competencies. This study adopts a mixed-methods research design to verify the effectiveness of the AI-enhanced design pathway for interdisciplinary thematic learning in high school biology and its teaching practice in improving students’ disciplinary core competencies and interdisciplinary competencies. The results indicate that the experimental group (EG) achieved significant improvements in disciplinary core competencies, including scientific concepts, scientific thinking, scientific inquiry, and social responsibility, as well as in interdisciplinary competencies such as innovation and collaboration. Furthermore, the proposed design pathway effectively enhances student initiative and engagement, addressing their diverse inquiry needs. This study offers new insights for implementing interdisciplinary thematic learning in high school biology and provides practical evidence for future research in related fields.

title: Leverage Quantum Computing and Artificial Intelligence for Complex Problem Resolution: Innovative Optimization Framework
authors: Suresh Kumar Pithani, Hebatullah Awwad, J. Lethisia Nithiya, Shobana D, Rajini Parimala, Amit Dutt
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Optimization problems that are computationally intractable can be tackled with the help of AI and quantum computing. Standard optimization techniques are ill-equipped to deal with high-dimensional search spaces and non-linear constraints in domains such as logistics, finance, energy management, pharmaceutical research, and large-scale industrial scheduling. Using quantum computing with AI-driven learning, this research presents a hybrid optimization strategy that enhances the efficiency and accuracy of problem-solving. Adaptive optimization, parameter changes, and pattern discovery are all part of machine learning models, whereas quantum annealing and variational quantum eigen solvers simultaneously explore massive solution spaces. The proposed method uses quantum probabilistic computation and heuristic refining based on AI to reduce computational complexity and speed up the convergence of global optimums. While quantum computers optimize computationally expensively at the quantum-classical interface, deep learning models preprocess and extract features. Compared to metaheuristics, simulation-based performance analysis improves both the quality of solutions and the speed of convergence. The study demonstrated that AI frameworks boosted with quantum computing may solve combinatorial optimization problems and NP-hard problems. Using a computational paradigm that is both scalable and future-proof, this work reimagines optimization in the scientific, industrial, and engineering spheres.

title: A Lightweight YOLO11n-Based Fall Detection Method with Wavelet and Attention Mechanisms
authors: Liguo Zhang, Chongwei Hu, Mei Jin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Human fall detection in real-world scenarios is challenged by complex backgrounds, occlusions, and limited computational resources on edge devices. This paper proposes WAL-YOLO, a lightweight fall detection framework based on YOLO11n that integrates Wavelet Transform Convolution (WTConv), Adaptive Downsampling (ADown), and Large Selective Kernel Attention (LSKA) to improve multi-scale feature representation and enlarge the receptive field while reducing computational cost. Experiments on the public Falling Posture dataset show that the proposed method achieves 82.2% mAP@0.5, outperforming YOLO11n by 1.1%, with only 2.08M parameters and 5.2 GFLOPs, demonstrating a favorable balance between accuracy and efficiency for real-time edge deployment.

title: Multi-grained Hybrid Metric Guided Pseudo-neighbor Augmentation for Unsupervised Person Re-identification
authors: Jun Zhang, Shuli Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Current unsupervised person re-identification (ReID) research frequently confronts challenges related to unreliable similarity metrics and a lack of sufficient supervision signals during the early training stages, which induces instability in model optimization. To address these challenges, we propose an unsupervised learning framework named MHMNSA, which integrates a Multi-grained Hybrid Metric with Neighbor-aware Semantic Augmentation. Specifically, the Multi-grained Hybrid Metric (MHM) constructs a more stable similarity evaluation model by fusing multi-grained representation clues with neighborhood structural evidence. This module utilizes neighborhood consistency calibration to stabilize the initial geometric topology and subsequently incorporates Jaccard distance to reinforce structural constraints, thereby laying a reliable foundation for pseudo-label generation. Building upon this, the Neighbor-aware Semantic Augmentation (NSA) exploits local geometric relationships induced by nearest cluster centers to synthesize boundary-oriented pseudo-neighbors, mitigating the issue of sparse boundary supervision. Furthermore, it employs a soft neighbor label loss to impose marginal constraints on hard boundary samples, effectively suppressing misleading updates arising from uncertain neighborhoods. Experimental results on the Market-1501 and MSMT17 datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms most existing unsupervised Re-ID methods.

title: Dendrite-Inspired DRL Scheduling With Interpretable Semantic Receptive Fields in Adaptive Fog Computing
authors: Leka Habte Lejebo, Kenea Ayantu Tesfaye, Mohammad Shabaz, Hamad Aldawsari, Muhammad Asim Saleem, Shijie Zhou
publish: IEEE Network
abstract: Efficient task scheduling in fog computing environments is challenging due to highly dynamic workloads and strict latency requirements of modern applications. Existing container orchestration approaches typically rely either on heuristic rules that enable fast decisions but lack adaptability, or deep reinforcement learning models that provide adaptability but often incur high inference overhead due to dense neural architectures. These limitations can significantly affect scheduling responsiveness and overall system performance. To address this challenge, we propose SAC-dANN, a bio-inspired scheduling framework that integrates discrete Soft Actor–Critic reinforcement learning with dendritic artificial neural networks. The proposed architecture introduces semantic receptive fields to enforce structured sparsity and employs a dynamic adaptive sparsity mechanism that adjusts dendritic activity according to the global system state. This design enables efficient policy learning while reducing unnecessary computation during scheduling decisions. Experimental results using real-world fog workload traces derived from the BitBrain dataset demonstrate that SAC-dANN improves scheduling latency by 15–24%, reduces service-level objective violations by 10.8%, and decreases response time by 25% compared with state-ofthe- art scheduling approaches. The proposed architecture also scales effectively from 10 to 200 hosts while maintaining low scheduling overhead. Furthermore, the dendritic structure improves interpretability by enabling policy decisions to be associated with meaningful feature groups such as container demand and system load. Overall, SAC-dANN provides an efficient, scalable, and interpretable scheduling framework for dynamic fog computing environments.

title: A Computational Economic Decision-Support Model for Evaluating Collaborative Robots in Service Systems
authors: Asmaa Rashed Alahmari, Wadee Alhalabi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study developed a computational decision-support model for evaluating collaborative robots in service systems under various techno-economic constraints. Although collaborative robots have achieved substantial success in industrial environments, their adoption in service contexts remains limited because of the absence of integrated approaches that jointly assess investment feasibility, operational sustainability, and collaboration design. To address this gap, the proposed model structured deployment decisions into three analytically connected levels: capital feasibility under budget constraints, operational recovery based on amortization dynamics, and collaboration design modeled through a continuous collaboration-intensity variable. A parametric computational analysis was conducted across varying robot acquisition costs, labor wages, and operating schedules to systematically evaluate alternative deployment scenarios and identify economically dominant collaboration configurations based on cost–throughput trade-offs. The results showed that capital feasibility does not guarantee operational recoverability, with long-term viability strongly influenced by wage structure and payback horizons. At the design level, modeling collaboration intensity as a continuous variable revealed a nonlinear cost–throughput trade-off and identified an economically dominant region before diminishing returns emerge. The model was demonstrated through a representative service-oriented case study in robotic coffee preparation, illustrating its applicability as a practical and structured decision-support tool for evaluating and designing human–robot collaboration strategies in real-world service environments.

title: Dual-Band Hybrid Biaxial Signal Injection for Sensorless Control of SynRM under Low-Speed Operation with Improved Torque Performance
authors: Jinpeng Liu, Xiuhe Wang, Tingli Qiao, Lingling Sun, Zezhi Xing, Han Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: This work presents a sensorless control strategy for SynRMs based on dual-band hybrid biaxial high-frequency signal injection (Bi-HFSI) strategy, aiming to enhance torque performance. The proposed approach concurrently mitigates two dominant sources of torque ripple: inherent torque ripple caused by motor tooth-slot structure and high-frequency (HF) components induced by signal injection. To suppress these effects, a compensation signal is designed via nonlinear function fitting, and a hybrid HF voltage waveform is synthesized and injected using biaxial pseudo-random (Bi-PR) excitation. This configuration effectively reduces HF current noise while maintaining accurate rotor position estimation. The proposed strategy is validated through both finite element analysis and experimental testing, exhibiting significant torque ripple reduction under various load conditions, particularly under high-load operation. Although the integration of the compensation signal adds some complexity to the analysis of HF currents, it significantly enhances torque output quality and ensures reliable rotor position tracking. Power spectral density (PSD) and noise analysis confirms that the proposed method achieves lower noise levels than conventional square-wave injection, demonstrating its feasibility for high-performance sensorless SynRM drive applications.

title: Compact Metasurface Antenna with Wide 1-D Near-Field Focal Scanning Range
authors: Zirui Yu, Juan Chen, Chunhui Mou, Kaihang Fan
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A compact near-field focusing antenna with wide 1-D near focal scanning range is presented. The design utilizes a phase decomposition strategy, where the required focusing phase is theoretically decomposed into independent linear and quadratic terms. This design is realized by a three-layer structure: the bottom two layers generate a linear phase for beam steering, while the top layer imparts a passive quadratic phase for focusing. The design closely integrates all layers of the structure, significantly reducing the profile height (0.21λ). Simulations and experimental measurements validate that the antenna maintains wide-angle scanning capability (80.2°) while preserving compactness and low-cost advantages.

title: SLIM: High-Throughput Watermarking with Stationary Latent Manifolds
authors: Quan Yan, Zhige Chen, Yang Liu, Zhiping Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: The rapid proliferation of generative artificial intelligence necessitates efficient and reliable copyright protection for large-scale digital content. Existing deep learning-based watermarking methods often suffer from a throughput bottleneck due to heavy per-image encoding, as well as optimization instability caused by the drifting manifold problem in end-to-end coupled training. To address these issues, we propose SLIM, a high-throughput watermarking framework with stationary latent manifolds. SLIM uses a frozen pretrained autoencoder to provide a stationary latent target for supervision, reducing training to lightweight hiding and reveal networks under latent feature alignment. It further adopts an offline-online design that converts a watermark image into a reusable spatial perturbation offline and embeds it online through a single residual addition, enabling amortized computation over large collections. A compound objective integrating latent feature consistency and perturbation energy regularization is introduced to jointly optimize watermark recovery and visual imperceptibility. Extensive experiments on diverse datasets demonstrate that SLIM achieves over 14,000 images per second on a laptop-class CPU while maintaining high visual fidelity (PSNR > 41 dB) and reliable watermark recovery. Code is available at https://github.com/QuestaYan/High-Throughput-Watermarking-with-Stationary-Latent-Manifolds.git.

title: Wideband Dielectric Dispersion Analysis of Non-Crosslinked GelMA Hydrogels Using a Micro-Container Coaxial Probe
authors: Shabbir Chowdhury, Amir Ebrahimi, Kamran Ghorbani, Francisco Tovar-Lopez
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: This letter presents a three-dimensionally (3D) printed fixture enabling accurate, wideband complex permittivity measurements of micro-liter-scale liquids using an open-ended coaxial probe. By integrating a well-defined micro-reservoir directly at the probe tip, where the field intensity is maximum. The proposed fixture allows reliable characterization of viscous liquids at micro-scale volumes, which is not achievable using conventional microfluidic microwave sensors. The probe is calibrated using standard reference liquids, and the system is applied to wideband permittivity measurements of non-crosslinked GelMA hydrogels with varying water concentrations. High measurement accuracy is demonstrated through validation against the two-pole Debye dielectric model of isopropanol. The extracted data are further used to develop Debye-2 relaxation models describing the frequency-dependent complex permittivity of GelMA hydrogels.

title: AI-Based Approaches for Drunk Driving Detection for Traffic Safety: In-vehicle and Out-vehicle Techniques, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Razan Alsulieman, Richard Preston Swiley Jr, Ahmed Sherif, Mohamed Elsersy, Mohammad Mahbubur Rahman Khan Mamun, Ahmad Abdel-Aliem Imam, Rabab Abdelfattah, Kasem Khalil
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Drunk driving remains a critical public safety concern, causing a significant number of traffic accidents and fatalities worldwide. Traditional methods of detecting impaired driving, such as breathalyzers and sobriety tests, are often reactive and fail to prevent accidents before they occur. This paper provides a comprehensive review of AI-driven technologies for detecting drunk driving, including in-vehicle and out-vehicle systems for traffic safety. These systems leverage data from sensors, image recognition, iris recognition, and driving-behavior analysis to identify impaired driving in real time. While in-vehicle systems focus on physiological and behavioral indicators within the vehicle, out-vehicle systems monitor driving patterns and driver facial features from external sources, such as roadside cameras and drones. This paper also introduces a novel classification framework for existing detection methods, highlights their limitations, and proposes directions for future research.

title: Memory-Gain-Scheduling of Fuzzy Cyber-Physical Systems: A Dual-Mode Polynomial-Based Framework and Its Applications to Active Vehicle Suspension Systems
authors: Xingchen Shao, Xiangpeng Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: This paper investigates the critical challenges of mode information loss and complex transition probability uncertainties, collectively termed as dual-domain uncertainty, in stochastic nonlinear cyber-physical systems (CPSs). Oriented towards the emerging wireless architectures in future vehicles, a memory-gain-scheduling framework is developed, which strategically incorporates memory-dependent membership functions to enhance controller design by leveraging past and present scheduling information. Unlike existing scaling-based methods, our approach utilizes a polytopic reconstruction of the transition probability matrix to fundamentally eliminate structural conservatism. This is integrated with a dual-mode polynomial controller that adaptively switches based on real-time mode information availability, exhibiting enhanced robustness against data loss disruptions. Furthermore, by employing homogeneous polynomial techniques, this memory-gain-scheduling strategy effectively enlarges the feasible solution region by guaranteeing structural consistency. The practical effectiveness and superior performance of the proposed method are conclusively validated through rigorous hardware-in-the-loop experiments on an active vehicle suspension system, demonstrating significant improvements in ride comfort and stabilization under various road disturbances.
