title: Invariant Deformation Fields: Enabling Real-Time Visible-Infrared Image Alignment for UAVs
authors: Zixuan Xiao, Ruigang Fu, Jiajun Xiang, Xingyue Liu, Pengcheng Wan, Ping Zhong
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) are increasingly equipped with dual-modal sensors, making visible- infrared image alignment a task of significant practical value. However, precise cross-modal alignment remains a challenge due to disparities in field of view, radiation characteristics, and other factors. Existing image-based alignment methods often overlook the imaging mechanisms of dual-modal sensors and struggle to balance efficiency with accuracy. In this paper, by investigating the imaging mechanism of a fixed visible- infrared sensor, we demonstrate that when its visible and infrared imaging planes are coplanar (a practical and feasible approximation), an ideal deformation field exists that can warp the visible image into alignment with the infrared image. This deformation field remains nearly invariant with respect to both UAV state (e.g., altitude, longitude, latitude) and sensor pose (e.g., pitch, yaw, roll). To accurately estimate this ideal deformation field, we propose PDFNet, an end-to-end pixel- wise dense matching network. Unlike traditional methods that rely on sparse feature matching or parametric transformations, PDFNet directly learns a dense displacement field from visible to infrared images, enabling it to address complex geometric distortions and non-rigid deformations. In our alignment pipeline, images are first roughly aligned through manual annotation before being fed into the network. Then by integrating complementary information from multiple rounds of manual annotation, the network effectively suppresses subjective errors and stably converges to a globally optimal deformation field that is invariant to input variations. This enables direct application of a single precomputed deformation field at inference, requiring no network forward pass and reaching 33 FPS. Experiments on real-world UAV datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms existing learning-based methods such as RoMa and SuperGlue in alignment accuracy. Continuous UAV videos aligned using the same deformation fields across varying altitudes and pitch angles are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/xzx1302/Alignment-Performance-Based-on-Pixel-wise-Deformation-Field.</uri>.

title: Photoacoustic Excitation and Defect Characterization Using a Directional Bent Single-Mode Fiber Transducer
authors: Shengnan Zhou, Hongxin Zhang, Yongkang Dong, Yong Yao, Jiajun Tian
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: This study investigates a directional photoacoustic transducer based on a bent single-mode fiber for ultrasonic excitation and analysis of guided acoustic waves in plates of varying material types, thicknesses, and defect conditions. The directional behavior of the proposed transducer is validated through simulations and experiments, and the relationship between bending radius and bend-induced loss is established quantitatively. Leveraging the transducer's directional emission characteristics, experiments were conducted on aluminum(Al) and carbon fiber-reinforced polymer(CFRP) plates with thicknesses of 1, 2, and 4 mm. The measured time-frequency responses, interpreted together with the theoretical dispersion curves, suggest thickness-dependent participation of multiple Lamb-wave components in the tested plates. For defect characterization, the Continuous Wavelet Transform was employed to visualize the signal by preserving its time and frequency information. Results revealed high-frequency attenuation in Al plate due to isotropic flaws, and selective low-frequency phase suppression in CFRP– characteristic of its anisotropic delamination behavior. The testing system of interlayer delamination demonstrated a baseline axial resolution of 0.230 mm and successfully resolved interlayer gaps as small as 72.3 μm via a Fabry-Pérot resonance model. Additionally, a lightweight, physics-informed classification framework was developed for automated defect identification. By extracting features such as envelope decay rate and spectral centroid, and applying linear discriminant analysis with a leaveone- out cross-validation scheme, the model achieved 100% accuracy in distinguishing rectangular notches from circular blind holes in Al specimens. These findings demonstrate the laboratoryscale feasibility of compact fiber-optic photoacoustic systems for high-resolution non-destructive evaluation.

title: Integrated Fiber Optic FPI-MMI Sensor for Simultaneous Temperature and pH Monitoring in Estuarine Environment
authors: Zi-Ting Lin, Ran Gao, Chao-Fan Li, Yong Zhao
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: A dual-parameter optical fiber sensor for pH and temperature suitable for estuarine acidic environments is proposed. The sensor adopts a cascaded structure of tapered fiber and hollow fiber (HCF) to construct a hybrid interferometer. The HCF is filled with polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) to form a Fabry-Perot (FP) interferometer (FPI) for temperature sensing. Alternating layers of a polyacrylic acid/chitosan (PAA/CS) pH-sensitive hydrogel are deposited on the surface of the tapered no-core fiber (TNCF) by layer-by-layer self-assembly, forming a multimode interference (MMI) for pH detection. Experimental results show that, within the pH range of 2.79–9, the wavelength exhibits a blue shift as pH increases, following a quadratic relationship (R2 = 0.984). The sensitivity is -2.53 nm/pH at pH 4 and -1.13 nm/pH at pH 6. In the alkaline range of pH 7–9, the sensitivity decreases to -0.27 nm/pH, indicating a relatively weak response. This weak response is beneficial for suppressing background seawater interference. In the temperature range of 5–35°C, the temperature sensitivity is -0.36 nm/°C. The sensor shows favorable repeatability and stability. This work provides a solution for tracking acidic pollution and monitoring ecosystems in estuarine environments.

title: AMSA-Net: Adaptive Multi-Scale Attention Net for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Arafat Al-Jawari, Li Fang, Radhwan A. A. Saleh, Huifang Shen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral image classification (HSIC) remains a challenging task due to the complex relationship between space and spectrum in hyperspectral images, in order to improve the classification accuracy. This paper presents the Adaptive Multi-Scale Attention Net (AMSA-Net), a novel deep learning architecture that integrates multi-scale feature extraction with dynamic spectral-spatial attention for robust HSIC. AMSA-Net combines hierarchical encoding-decoding with a Dynamic Spectrum-Aware Attention (DSAA) block at its bottleneck, which processes features through three complementary pathways: global self-attention for long-range dependencies, local depthwise convolution for channel-specific patterns, and patch-based attention for intermediate-scale relationships. A learned gating mechanism dynamically fuses these branches based on local spectral-spatial characteristics. Comprehensive experiments on four benchmark datasets (Pavia University, WHU-Hi-HanChuan, WHU-Hi-HongHu, and QUH-Tangdaowan) demonstrate that AMSA-Net outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Ablation studies validate the contribution of each model's component, showing that AMSA-Net is particularly crucial for handling spectral variability in agricultural scenes. Visual analyses confirm AMSA-Net's superior spatial coherence and ability to preserve fine boundaries compared to CNN and transformer-based approaches. The proposed architecture provides an effective solution for hyperspectral analysis, particularly in applications that require precise discrimination among spectrally similar land-cover classes. The source code is available here <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Arafat-Aljawari/AMSA-Net</uri>.

title: Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm Driven by Differential Observation Learning for Design of Wide Operating Range THz Mixers_supp1-3691539.docx
authors: Hongji Zhou
publish: N/A
abstract: Terahertz harmonic mixers (THMs) are pivotal in terahertz systems, yet their design is hampered by high-dimensional coupling and extreme sensitivity as wavelengths approach device dimensions. Meta-heuristic algorithms offer a robust alternative for global optimization without the need for extensive pre-training. However, the standard Sparrow Search Algorithm (SSA) often suffers from premature convergence in complex design spaces. Existing SSA-DE hybrids frequently suffer from a functional mismatch between operators and evolutionary roles, or incur computational redundancy through static stacking. This paper proposes SSA-DOL, a search architecture integrating a Differential Observation Learning (DOL) mechanism and a Computing Resource Allocation (CRA) model. By constructing role-aware specialized operators, SSA-DOL aligns evolutionary semantics with THz physical constraints without increasing computational complexity. CEC2017 benchmark tests, including ablation studies, show that SSA-DOL outperforms state-of-the-art SSA variants, achieving competitive win rates of 68.97% and 86.21% against recent SOTA hybrids in terms of optimization accuracy. Using a fully automated MATLAB-HFSS-ADS framework with no manual intervention, a wideband mixer covering the 220-330 GHz frequency range a 110-GHz ultra-wideband mixer (220-330 GHz) was implemented. This design achieves a typical SSB conversion loss better than 12 dB with only 25% of the evaluation cost of traditional optimization methods. One-Factor-at-a-Time (OAT) sensitivity analysis confirms performance fluctuations remain below 0.1 dB under micrometer-level tolerances. Experimental measurements show an average deviation of only 1.45 dB from simulations, validating SSA-DOL as a robust and efficient solution for the automated synthesis of complex terahertz components.

title: Uni-RSNet: A Unified Recognition-Sorting Framework for SAR Interference Signals in Complex Electromagnetic Environments
authors: Qianyue Xue, Bing Han, Jianbing Xiang, Shangyu Zhang, Jiayuan Shen, Di Yin, Xiaochen Wang, Yuxin Hu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) often receives interference from multiple emitters operating within the same frequency band. These interference signals are densely overlapped in time and frequency domains and strongly coupled with SAR echoes, which substantially increases the difficulty of signal recognition and sorting. In conventional processing chains, recognition and sorting are treated as independent modules with task-specific inputs, resulting in severe information fragmentation and cumulative-error propagation under dense interference conditions. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a unified recognition-sorting network for overlapping SAR interference signals, termed Uni-RSNet. Based on continuous multi-frame time-frequency representations, Uni-RSNet jointly captures high-dimensional intra-pulse features and inter-pulse temporal correlations of signals from the same emitter, enabling simultaneous modulation classification, pulse-parameter estimation, and emitter attribution. This framework extends interference recognition from isolated pulses to entire frames, while simultaneously imposing modulation-aware constraints on the signal sorting process. Furthermore, a statistically consistent contrastive objective is designed to enhance intra-emitter compactness and inter-emitter separability, while a geometric consistency constraint improves the robustness of pulse localization. The unified architecture compresses the conventional serial processing chain and eliminates the need for task-dependent input adjustments. Extensive experiments on both simulated and GaoFen-3 (GF-3) satellite data demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed method under dense interference conditions. The code and datasets are publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/QianyueXue/Uni-RSNet</uri>.

title: Publishing With Impact: Insights From an IEEE Journal Editor-in-Chief in a Lecture Series at Chinese Universities
authors: Leilei Yang, Yan Yan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: Between October 2025 and January 2026, a series of high-level Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Author Workshops titled “Tips and Best Practices on How to Get Published Based on insights from actual IEEE Editors” was successfully conducted at Southwest Minzu University, Suzhou Institute of Technology, and Jiangsu University. Jointly organized by IEEE China, iGroup China, and the respective university libraries, this educational tour aimed to empower graduate students and early-career researchers with strategic insights into the IEEE publishing ecosystem. Prof. Hanwen Yu, who is an IEEE Fellow and the Editor-in-Chief of the IEEE Journal on Miniaturization for Air and Space Systems (J-MASS), delivered the keynote presentations. Drawing from an editor’s perspective, Prof. Yu provided comprehensive guidance on selecting publication venues, structuring high-quality manuscripts, navigating the peer-review process, and upholding research ethics. These workshops fostered interactive dialogues between the IEEE editorial leadership and local research communities, providing practical tools to enhance the global visibility and impact of scientific contributions from these institutions.

title: Suppression Strategy of Torque Ripple for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors Based on Artificial Intelligence Algorithms
authors: Yan Hu, Kai Yang, Xiaojun Wang, Qiong Liu, Fei Xiong, Yixiao Luo, Cheng Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: To suppress torque ripple, stator tooth shaping and rotor auxiliary slot opening on a permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) are conducted in this paper. Meanwhile, to avoid excessively affecting other performance characteristics of the motor, a multi-objective optimization algorithm based on an artificial intelligence model is employed to explore the most optimal scheme possible. First, finite element analysis (FEA) is used to establish models of the motor before and after optimization design. Subsequently, a parametric sweep is performed on the optimized motor model to calculate the average output torque and torque ripple for each design scheme, followed by a comparative analysis of the motor’s performance before and after optimization design. The results demonstrate that the stator tooth shaping and rotor auxiliary slot opening, along with the subsequent multi-objective optimization, significantly reduce the torque ripple of the motor. Moreover, the average output torque remains almost unaffected.

title: Impact Path of Government Excessive Behavior on Investors' Rational Conformity in PPP Projects: Demand and Government Control Rights
authors: Jiaqi Liu, Li Zhang, Peifen He, Jicai Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: When governments make excessive behavior, investor conformity may hinder effective risk management. Elucidating the reasons behind investors' conformity to government's excessive behavior is crucial for enhancing decision-making efficacy in high-stakes scenarios. Drawing on field dynamics theory, hypothetical paths are proposed to elucidate the impact of government excessive behavior on investor conformity, with a focus on investor demand and government control rights. The study employed rigorous questionnaire design, valid respondent screening, reliability and validity tests, as well as robustness testing including competitive model analysis and multi-group analysis to ensure methodological rigour. Through hypothesis testing and path analysis of 244 data, the study found that under excessive government behavior, investor demand response contains the inherent logic of technological capabilities and long-term development driving decision-making, reflecting a proactive response pattern within apparent passivity. Government control rights exerts a dual effect on investors' conformity: economic benefits and market access act as “hard levers”, while trust and reputation serve as “soft bonds”. Moreover, industry capability need dominates compliance decisions, revealing that implicitly conveyed “capability threshold” signals carry more weight than explicit rules. These findings show that investor conformity is a rational choice based on balancing dual government control rights and multidimensional interests, reshaping the traditional cognitive framework that regards conformity as irrational. This insight offers valuable guidance for mitigating the risks associated with investor conformity and significantly enhances the theoretical framework of project risk management.

title: Threshold Voltage Modulated ITZO-Based Capacitorless Memory With Multibit Capability
authors: Xiangcheng Liu, Xiaocheng Wang, Lin Xu, Mei Yang, Peizheng Yang, Wei Huang, Delang Lin, Rongsheng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Metal oxide thin-film transistors (TFTs) exhibit low leakage current due to their unique band structure, making them a promising material for two-transistor zero-capacitor (2T0C) dynamic random access memory (DRAM) production. Compared to InGaZnO (IGZO), InSnZnO (ITZO) offers higher electron mobility—enabling faster read speeds—and requires lower processing temperatures, thus ensuring back-end-of-line (BEOL) compatibility. We employ a threshold voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\textit {th}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-modulated ITZO process to fabricate ITZO-based 2T0C DRAM cells featuring distinct <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\textit {th}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> values for the write and read transistors. mobility (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu _{\textit {fet}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of ~30 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s and a read current (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${I}_{\textit {read}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.4~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>A/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m. We provide a comprehensive elucidation of the mechanism behind the performance evolution of ITZO thin films, examining it from the perspectives of oxygen vacancy (V<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {O}}\text {)}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> concentration, internal bonding configuration, and band structure. With <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\textit {th}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> modulation, the ITZO-based 2T0C cell demonstrates a data retention time of 200 s and maintains 2-bit storage for over 50 s. Furthermore, we have implemented a neural network model suitable for edge image processing with extremely constrained resources, achieving an accuracy of 93.15%. These results demonstrate the strong potential of ITZO-based 2T0C DRAM for future in-memory computing applications.

title: A Novel Unobservable Subspace Online Estimation Method based on the Variable-Dimension Kalman Filter
authors: Longjie Tian, Yinzhou Zhi, Linghai Kong, Jiawei Wu, Junyang Zhao, Hongcai Li, Yangqiang Yang, Haoyang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Gyroscope scale factor errors play a critical role in determining the dynamic performance of fiber-optic inertial navigation systems and directly affect measurement accuracy. However, the observability of scale factor errors is highly maneuver-dependent, and unobservable subspaces may arise under insufficient excitation. Conventional approaches typically adopt high-dimensional Kalman filters to enhance estimation stability, which may retain weakly observable scale factor states and adversely affect overall filtering performance. In this study, a novel variable-dimension Kalman filtering algorithm is proposed. A gyroscope confidence factor derived from prior noise characteristics is introduced to assess maneuver sufficiency and autonomously identify unobservable scale factor states in real time. Unlike existing methods that rely on structural modification of system or measurement models, the proposed approach realizes online dimension adaptation solely through covariance initialization and propagation, preserving filter continuity and implementation simplicity. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method improves heading accuracy from 0.1° to 0.02° and achieves a horizontal positioning accuracy of 0.89 nmile/h, corresponding to a 55.5% performance enhancement compared with conventional fixed-dimensional filtering approaches.

title: SurgNavAR: An Augmented Reality Surgical Navigation Framework for Optical See-Through Head Mounted Displays
authors: Abdullah Thabit, Mohamed Benmahdjoub, Rafiuddin Jinabade, Hizirwan S. Salim, Marie-Lise C. van Veelen, Mark G. van Vledder, Eppo B. Wolvius, Theo van Walsum
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Augmented reality (AR) devices with head mounted displays (HMDs) facilitate the direct superimposition of 3D preoperative imaging data onto the patient during surgery. To use an HMD-AR device as a stand-alone surgical navigation system, the device should be able to locate the patient and surgical instruments, align preoperative imaging data with the patient, and visualize navigation data in real time during surgery. Whereas some of the technologies required for this are known, integration in such devices is cumbersome and requires specific knowledge and expertise, hampering scientific progress in this field. This work therefore aims to present and evaluate an integrated HMD-based AR surgical navigation framework that is adaptable to diverse surgical applications. The framework tracks 2D patterns as reference markers attached to the patient and surgical instruments. It allows for the calibration of surgical tools using pivot and reference-based calibration techniques. It enables image-to-patient registration using point-based matching and manual positioning. The integrated functionalities of the framework are evaluated on two HMD devices, the HoloLens 2 and Magic Leap 2, with two surgical use cases being evaluated in a phantom setup: AR-guided needle insertion and rib fracture localization. The framework was able to achieve a mean tooltip calibration accuracy of 1 mm, a registration accuracy of 3 mm, and a targeting accuracy below 5 mm on the two surgical use cases. The framework presents an easy-to-use configurable tool for HMD-based AR surgical navigation, which can be extended and adapted to many surgical applications. The framework is publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/abdullahthabit/SurgNavAR</uri>.

title: Evaluation of the Consistency of Several XAERDT Aerosol Products over Land and Ocean
authors: Xin Pei, Leiku Yang, Ping Zhang, Muhammad Bilal, Xiaoran Lv, Xiaoxia Xue, Xiaolong Niu, Yizhe Fan, Yuxuan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Recently, NASA released a specialized aerosol dataset based on the unified Dark Target (DT) algorithm framework, known as XAERDT. This dataset provides aerosol products from eight satellite sensors (covering 2019–2022), including the Moderate Resolution Imaging Spectroradiometer (MODIS) aboard the Aqua satellite, as well as the Visible Infrared Imaging Radiometer Suite (VIIRS) aboard the Suomi-NPP (SNPP) and NOAA-20 satellites. While this unified framework aims to ensure consistency in aerosol algorithms across multiple sensors, it differs in some respects from the standard MODIS DT Collection 6.1 (C6.1) algorithm. This study evaluates the impact of sensor and algorithm differences on aerosol retrieval consistency by analyzing MODIS/Aqua (DT and XAERDT), VIIRS/SNPP (XAERDT), and VIIRS/NOAA20 (XAERDT) aerosol products, thereby providing insights for multi-sensor data fusion and data continuity. The performance of aerosol optical depth (AOD) and Ångström exponent (AE) products from these four DT datasets was validated against Aerosol Robotic Network (AERONET) measurements, and their spatiotemporal patterns were intercompared using the predefined consistency thresholds (AOD: ±0.03, AE: ±0.4). The results indicate that: (1) differences between the two MODIS algorithms primarily affect aerosol retrievals over land, with a much smaller impact over the ocean; (2) sensor characteristics and calibration differences strongly influence the consistency of three sensors' XAERDT AOD products. VIIRS/SNPP generally yields higher AOD than MODIS/Aqua, whereas VIIRS/NOAA20 shows slightly lower values but performs comparably to MODIS/Aqua. The difference between the two VIIRS products is more pronounced; (3) wavelength-dependent calibration differences impact AE retrievals over the ocean, with the VIIRS/NOAA20 AE product performing best, significantly reducing the overestimation at low AE values observed in other products; and (4) over the ocean, monthly AOD and AE offsets between sensors remain within the consistency thresholds. Over land, however, significant AOD differences persist, especially between the two VIIRS sensors.

title: Construction and Practice of an AI-Empowered "Four-Dimensional Integration and Five-Stage Progressive" Experimental Teaching System for Mechanical Engineering Programs
authors: Xiangfu Fu, Guochao YU, Xuebing Li, Yaping Wang, Yuqi Fan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In response to the demand for interdisciplinary and innovative mechanical engineering talent in the new era of engineering education, and to address persistent issues in traditional experimental teaching such as disciplinary barriers, theory-practice disconnection, and unidimensional assessment, this paper proposes and implements a novel AI-empowered "four-dimensional integration and five-stage progressive" experimental teaching system. Driven by artificial intelligence technology, this system constructs a new pedagogical framework based on knowledge graphs, digital twins, and intelligent agents, deeply integrating four dimensions: "discipline, theory-practice, university-industry, and ideological-political education," while systematically designing five progressive stages: "cognition, foundation, specialization, innovation, and advanced application." Practice demonstrates that this system effectively enhances the intelligence, personalization, and engineering orientation of experimental teaching. Students' comprehensive practical abilities, innovative spirit, and complex engineering problem-solving capabilities have been significantly strengthened, providing a replicable pathway for experimental teaching reform in mechanical engineering programs.

title: An ICT-Driven Framework for Course Information Development towards Outcome-Based Education (OBE) Approach
authors: Rosmayati Mohemad, Anuar Abu Bakar, Hamimah Ujir
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The transition from traditional to outcome-based learning poses a consider-able challenge for the academic community, as they must strive to under-stand and enhance their skills to successfully implement the intended learning outcomes. However, the existing framework of Outcome-Based Education (OBE) does not offer explicit guidelines for attaining the desired outcomes. Currently, there is a lack of an ICT-based framework that guides academics in designing course information to ensure alignment of teaching, learning, and assessments with OBE requirements. The construction and documentation of the course outlines is conducted manually, utilising basic ICT tools such as Microsoft Word and Excel. Manual processes are laborious, unstructured, time-consuming, and tedious because they require proper management and organization of a multitude of interrelated documents. Different iterations of the format for course outlines, which are subject to constant change, are also challenging to implement. The substantial quantity of administrative duties involved in documentation is a critical factor hindering scholars' comprehensive understanding of the notion of constructive alignment. Therefore, the objectives of this study are to pro-pose an ICT-based framework of designing course information and to demonstrate the applicability of the proposed framework with real data captured from courses information database of Universiti Malaysia Terengganu. Research methodology contains three phases: planning and design, development and evaluation. An ICT-based framework for course information development is developed, which assists academician in designing course information that aligns with the OBE requirement. The research significance output is to improve education quality when the implementation of ICT in OBE could increase academics' readiness and comprehension of outcome-based learning.

title: Privacy-Enhanced Fraud Detection in Credit Card Transactions Using Federated Learning
authors: A. Lakshmi, K. Nashroon Fazila, S. Sanchavi Priya, R. Sowmiya
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Diabetic foot complications like Peripheral Artery Disease (PAD) and Diabetic Foot Ulcers (DFU) can occur slowly due to poor perfusion and abnormal plantar pressure. A sensor–based smart insole for continuous, non-invasive diabetic foot risk assessment is presented in this paper. To identify structural alterations linked to foot ulcers, the insole incorporates a temperature sensor, Light Dependent Resistor (LDR)-based plantar pressure sensors, and a bone strength sensing module. Vascular parameters like blood flow and oxygen saturation are monitored using a non-contact liquid level sensor for blood flow measurement and a MAX30102 sensor for SpO2 and heart rate monitoring in order to detect ischemic conditions associated with peripheral artery disease (PAD). An anomaly detection algorithm based on Isolation Forest is used to analyze sensor data obtained with a Raspberry Pi. This system enables early detection using a low-cost wearable monitoring system by classifying conditions into normal, PAD risk, foot ulcer risk, or combined risk.

title: Analysis of Reactive Power Droop Coefficient and Power Angle Stability Under Large Disturbances
authors: Mu Li, Shouyuan Wu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper investigates the transient power-angle stability of virtual synchronous generators (VSGs) in power systems with high penetration of renewable energy under severe grid voltage sags. Theoretical analysis establishes that the total deceleration area of the system is a monotonically increasing function of the point of common coupling (PCC) voltage. To systematically examine the impact of different reactive power droop coefficients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{k}_{\mathbf{q}}$</tex> on the dynamic response of the VSG under large disturbances, a single-machine infinite-bus system model is developed in MATLAB/Simulink. Simulation results demonstrate that increasing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{k}_{\mathbf{q}}$</tex> significantly enhances the VSG's reactive power regulation capability, thereby strengthening PCC voltage support. This effectively enlarges the total deceleration area, facilitating satisfaction of the equal area criterion (EAC) for transient stability and markedly improving the transient stability of the VSG-integrated system.

title: Advanced Machine Learning and Deep Learning Framework for Multiple Sclerosis Lesion Detection and Classification in Multimodal MRI
authors: R. Sangeetha, K.V. Kanimozhi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Multiple Sclerosis (MS) is a long-lasting nervous system disorder that is marked by the establishment of demyelinating lesions in the central nervous system and the backbone, which interfere with the transmission of nerve signals. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is an important process of detecting and monitoring these lesions yet they exhibit different appearances with respect to shape, size, texture and intensity when observed in different imaging sequences. Moreover, MRI data usually have noises, artifacts, and interscientific differences across patients, and there are few labeled medical datasets. Such problems complicate the detection of MS lesions through the traditional methods in terms of accuracy and early onset. This paper provides a detailed design of automated lesion detection of MS by implementing high-level image processing and deep learning techniques. MRI data is first subjected to preprocessing processes such as grayscale normalization, skull stripping, coregistration of two or more MRI sequences and augmentation of data to enhance consistency of the image and minimization of noise. During the feature extraction step, the texture based descriptors are used to identify salient structural features of lesions which include Gray Level Co-occurrence Matrix (GLCM), Local Binary Patterns (LBP), and Haralick features. More details are retrieved by means of wavelet based decomposition, intensity histograms and shape based features. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is used to reduce the dimension to eliminate duplication of data and only meaningful features are retained. Moreover, deep learning models, like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) VGG, ResNet, and DenseNet, are used to automatically adapt to complex patterns using MRI images. The limitation of small datasets is addressed by transfer learning and spatial feature learning and anomaly detection are investigated using capsule networks and autoencoders. According to the experimental findings, the methods based on deep learning prove to have a high potential in aiding clinicians in early diagnosis and effective follow-up of multiple sclerosis by providing high scores of lesion detection accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity.

title: An AI-Driven Cloud-Native Freelance Marketplace for the Gig Economy
authors: Puvvada Sai Yaswanth, Shreeman Muruganandam, Chakka Venkata Akhilesh, M Manickam
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The gig economy has been identified as a new and popular paradigm of employment that enables flexible and project-based work through digital platforms. Freelance marketplaces are an important part of this infrastructure that enables talent discovery, communication, and payment processing. However, most existing freelance marketplaces are still quite transactional and lack intelligent automation, trust awareness, and effective user support. This paper presents an AI-driven and cloud-native freelance marketplace that leverages the power of advanced technologies to enhance automation, transparency, and user experience. The proposed system uses BERT-based resume parsing for structured skill extraction, aspect-based sentiment analysis for trust assessment, blockchain-based escrow payment systems for secure transactions, and a retrieval-augmented conversational chatbot for intelligent user support. The system is hosted on Amazon Web Services (AWS) with a modular design that supports scalable microservice-based deployment of AI components. Experimental results on the resume parsing component of the system show the effectiveness of structured information extraction from unstructured documents, and the potential of combining AI and decentralized technologies in modern freelance marketplaces.

title: RepoScantinel: An Intelligent Multi-Language GitHub Repository Vulnerability Scanner with Unified Risk Scoring
authors: Muruganantham.U, Palanivel.N, Lakshmipriya.V, Karunya.R, Vaishnavi.E, Angel Rani.A
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Open-source software repositories frequently contain security vulnerabilities that go undetected for extended periods, posing serious threats to the applications that depend on them. This paper introduces RepoScantinel, an intelligent web-based platform designed to automatically identify and assess such vulnerabilities across GitHub repositories written in multiple programming languages. The system combines two complementary static analysis engines—Bandit for Python-specific scanning and Semgrep for broader multi-language coverage—with a weighted risk scoring mechanism that produces a unified security assessment per repository. A key feature of the platform is its interactive dashboard, through which developers can submit repository URLs and receive detailed reports covering vulnerability distribution by severity, file-level breakdowns, and actionable remediation suggestions. Experimental evaluation on 50 publicly available repositories yielded a precision of 92.1% and a recall of 89.7% (F1-score: 0.909), with a mean scanning time of 45 seconds per 10,000 lines of code—a marked improvement over individual standalone tools. Across the evaluated repositories, the system detected a total of 347 vulnerabilities, establishing its practical utility for both developers and security auditors.

title: Emotional Support in Digital Classrooms: How Teachers Foster Emotional Connection in Online EFL Learning
authors: Kien Trung Nguyen
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Given the shift to online education during the COVID-19 pandemic, emotional connection between teachers and the students became a critical aspect of maintaining engagement of the students and their academic motivation. This study used a convergent mixed-methods approach examining quantitative data from 220 students by questionnaires and qualitative data from 5 teacher interviews to understand how the students perceived the teachers emotional behaviours, and how the teachers described their behaviours to create emotional connections, in their online learning context. The findings, which were based on O'Hare's (2020) framework of five positive emotional support behaviours show that students typically acknowledged high levels of emotional support from their teachers. Sincerity and personal sharing were the least recognised behaviours, indicating areas for improvement, while positive communication and passion were the most frequently reported behaviours. To promote emotional connections, teachers discussed using techniques like sharing personal stories and engaging in dynamic online activities. The study emphasises how crucial it is to incorporate emotional connections into online education. To ensure that emotional connection continues to be a key component of student engagement and motivation in online education, it also highlights the necessity of professional development programs to assist teachers in improving their emotional support behaviours in online classrooms.

title: Neuromorphic Filtering: Memristive Anisotropic Diffusion Coupled with STDP-Driven Spiking Networks for Biomedical Edge Computing
authors: Marwa Chaabene, Fakhreddine Zayer, Anouar Ben Khalifa
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel neuromorphic filtering framework that integrates memristive gradient diffusion with spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP)-driven spiking neural networks (SNNs) for efficient biomedical edge computing. The proposed approach leverages memristive anisotropic diffusion to perform in-memory, local pixel computations across multilevel memristor grids, enabling pseudo-parallel processing and efficient multiply-accumulate operations. Brightness changes are computed directly within the memristive crossbar, with flux propagation determined solely by local neighboring nodes, allowing a single memristor to serve as both a computing and storage element. The diffusion-filtered outputs are then encoded into spikes and fed into a memristive STDP-based SNN, where synaptic weights adapt to temporal correlations, facilitating robust feature extraction and signal enhancement. Experimental evaluation on noisy biomedical CT images demonstrates consistent improvements of 2.1–3.4 dB in PSNR and 4–7% in SSIM over digital Perona–Malik and recent neuromorphic filtering baselines. Hardware-level benchmarking further shows 38–52% reduction in energy consumption and 30–45% lower inference latency compared to state-of-the-art memristive and hybrid edge architectures. These results establish MAD as a learning-aware, hardware-native filtering layer that significantly improves the accuracy–energy–latency trade-off for real-time biomedical signal processing at the edge.

title: Research on IMU/GPS Integrated Navigation Based on Extended Kalman Filter
authors: Xiaolong Xu, Fuhua Liu, Xiao Sun, Jiale Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the requirements for high-precision and real-time navigation of unmanned aerial vehicles , this paper proposes an EKF-based IMU/GPS fusion algorithm. A 16-dimensional state vector including the carrier’s position, velocity, attitude quaternion, and IMU biases is designed. The discretized state equation and the linear GPS measurement equation are derived, and the data fusion is realized through the iterative "prediction-correction" process of the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF). Simulation results show that the proposed algorithm significantly suppresses the cumulative drift of the IMU, greatly reduces the position and velocity errors compared with the pure IMU integration and GPS interpolation methods, and improves the navigation accuracy and reliability.

title: Design and Investigation of PV-Based Versatile E- Scooter Technologies for Sustainable and Affordable Urban Mobility
authors: R.Sankar Ganesh, M.Subaramani, K.Sathiyasekar, Manoj K, Vishnu L, Gokul Raja K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This paper presents in design and perform evaluation on a photovoltaic (PV)-assisted electric scooter developed at promote sustainable and affordable urban mobility. Then recommended system integrates of the 60 V lithium-ion battery pack a 6 kW brushless DC (BLDC) hub motor of the dual-source solar charging topology unit comprising an on-vehicle 80 Wp panel and a portable 250 Wp foldable module. A field-oriented control (FOC) motor controller joined and the ESP32-based vehicle control unit (VCU) enable multi-mode operation, regenerative braking and CAN-based communication to BMS, inverter and telematics unit .Analytical and process the investigation was conducted for an 80 kg payload to determine the relationships among vehicle speed, gradient and energy consumption .The e-scooter achieved an energy usage of 18–19 Wh/km an a range of up to 115 km per charge with a drivetrain efficiency of ≈90 %. The PV subsystem supplied 0.28–1.05 kWh/day extending range by 12–50 km/day thereby reducing grid dependence by ≈ 40 %. The system complies with AIS-156 Phase II safety requirements and demonstrates an effective combination of renewable-energy integration, compact design, and intelligent control offering a practical blueprint for future green micro-mobility solutions.

title: Conditional Variational Autoencoders for Statistical MOSFET Modeling
authors: Dawid Sudowski, Dominik Kasprowicz
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Monte Carlo circuit simulation is impossible without statistical compact models capable of faithfully reproducing the probability distributions of the circuit components' electrical characteristics. While such models do exist for devices fabricated using established processes and working under typical operating conditions, this is not always true of emerging devices or ones working at cryogenic temperatures. Although approximation MOSFET models can be used in place of physics-based ones, making them suitable for statistical simulation usually requires detailed information about the distributions of the transistors' physical parameters, which are difficult to extract. We propose an alternative approach that relies solely on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I-V$</tex> measurements of a set of transistors of various geometries. Based on these data, a conditional variational autoencoder (CVAE) is trained. It is subsequently used to generate arbitrarily large <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I-V$</tex> datasets that match the statistical properties of the training data. The model can smoothly generalize between the training cases, which also enables reliable generation of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I-V$</tex> data for other transistor geometries than those used for training. This approach has been shown to reliably reproduce the probability distributions of various figures of merit of an operational amplifier.

title: Self-Optimizing Dual-Band Ku/Ka RF-VLSI Beamforming Architecture with Embedded AI for Next-Generation SATCOM and 6G NTN
authors: Vijayalakshmi.R, M. Saranya., Adinath M, E.Munuswamy, P Kaviya, Shamili E, Sarath R, Sharavanan R, Keerthanaa.L
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: We propose a novel RF-VLSI phase-shifter architecture enabling dual-band Ku/Ka satellite communications (SATCOM) with on-chip AI-assisted calibration. By combining a wideband analog phase-shifter network and embedded machine-learning feedback, our design dynamically compensates PVT and thermal variations for resilient beam steering. Silicon implementation (65 nm CMOS) is experimentally validated. The prototype achieves <2° RMS phase error and 6–8 dB insertion loss across 13–18 GHz and 27–31 GHz bands, while AI optimization yields ~30% reduction in beam-pointing error. Adaptive control of switch bias and varactor settings maximizes linearity and energy efficiency under dynamic tracking loads. Key contributions include a dualband hardware phase shifter with integrated AI calibration, a mixed-signal control loop with real-time error sensing, and measured SATCOM beam-steering performance. This work demonstrates that ML-driven RF co-design can overcome the limitations of conventional fixed-calibration phase-shifters, paving the way for next-generation LEO/MEO SATCOM terminals and 6G NTN integration.

title: Frequency Regulation Strategy and Economic Analysis of Thermal-Storage Coordination for Power Systems with Variable Inertia
authors: Yurui Li, Sipeng Hao, Yijiang Feng, Yue Yuan
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: The integration of a high proportion of renewable energy has led to decreased grid inertia, posing severe challenges to frequency stability. Issues such as the large dead band and delayed response of traditional thermal power units in frequency regulation have become more prominent. To address this, this paper proposes a coordinated frequency control strategy for thermal-storage systems, based on dead band optimization and adaptive system inertia. Under small disturbances, the energy storage system is set with a smaller frequency regulation dead band than thermal units, and droop and virtual inertia coefficients are tuned to suppress high-frequency, small-magnitude fluctuations. For large disturbances, thermal and storage systems jointly regulate frequency, with power allocation adaptively adjusted based on equivalent system inertia to provide dynamic power support and avoid overshoot or undershoot. A costeffectiveness analysis of frequency regulation services indicates that using energy storage alone is more economical in small disturbance scenarios. Simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy under different disturbance scales and system inertia conditions. Finally, the applicability of this strategy in future low-inertia and even zero-inertia grids is discussed, paving the way for its broader implementation.

title: Design and Application of a Multi-Sensor Fusion ZigBee Wireless Fire Alarm System
authors: Junnan Tian, Meiyan Quan, Ming Cai, Yuncai Yan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)

title: Mitigating TIA Noise Impact in Impedance Measurement via Digital Lock-In
authors: Muye Jin, Binbin Chen, Zhi Liang, Tao Liu, Hongyan Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Impedance measurement of resistors, inductors, and capacitors is fundamental for component characterization and sensor applications. In wideband systems, the transimpedance amplifier (TIA) used to convert device-under-test (DUT) current to voltage suffers from increasing high-frequency noise gain due to finite operational amplifier bandwidth and parasitic capacitances. This effect significantly amplifies broadband noise, degrading amplitude and phase accuracy, especially for low-level signals. This paper presents a digital dual-phase lock-in amplifier architecture combined with multistage filtering and decimation to specifically suppress TIA-induced high-frequency noise. Implemented on an FPGA, the system achieves precise synchronous I/Q demodulation and efficient noise filtering while maintaining low hardware complexity. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate amplitude and phase errors below one percent across a wide frequency range, validating the approach’s effectiveness. The proposed method offers a low-cost, high-accuracy solution suitable for automated wideband impedance measurement in modern electronic testing environments.

title: A Triboelectric Pressure Sensor with an Ordered Multi-scale Hierarchical Structure for Non-Invasive Cardiovascular Monitoring
authors: Yulong Deng, Xiaoke Wang, Lincan Deng, Yangpeng Lu, Hongyuan Gao, Mingjun Zou, Fang Xu, Zhiming Lin
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Flexible pressure sensors have attracted growing investigative focus for their applicability in real-time cardiac signal acquisition and the preliminary screening of cardiovascular pathologies. Nevertheless, it is still a major challenge to realize both high sensing sensitivity and the comprehensive acquisition of cardiovascular characteristic parameters in a single sensor. In this study, we designa brand-new triboelectric pressure sensor integrated with a well-ordered multi-scale hierarchical microstructure for non-invasive cardiovascular monitoring applications. The optimized structural design allows the sensor to precisely identify slight pressure fluctuations induced by vascular dilatation and contraction, and the sensor achieves a high sensing resolution of 3.24 V/kPa, a quick response speed of 80 ms, as well as good operational stability after 4000 repeated loading-unloading cycles. Through the analysis of high- precision pulse waves obtained by the sensor, key cardiovascular metrics such as cardiac rate and the K value can be derived with high reliability, thus building a solid analytical system for non-invasive cardiovascular health evaluation. With excellent sensing performance and skin conformability, the sensor exhibits great application potential in personalized healthcare, especially real-time uninterrupted pulse detection and high-precision cardiovascular hemodynamic analysis.

title: SmartPCB: An Efficient PCB Defect Detection Framework Based on Deep Learning
authors: Yuyan Xue
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Printed circuit board (PCB) quality is crucial to electronic product reliability, yet defect detection remains challenging due to diverse defect types and subtle visual features. Although deep learning models such as YOLO have achieved strong performance in object detection, small defects and background interference still limit their effectiveness in PCB inspection. This paper proposes SmartPCB, an efficient YOLOv12-based framework incorporating a Multi-Scale Information Fusion (MSIF) module, an Adaptive Feature Selection Mechanism (AFSM), and a Small-Object Guided Defect Augmentation (SGDA) module to enhance multi-scale representation, suppress background noise, and improve small-defect detection. Experiments on a PCB defect dataset show that SmartPCB achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 94.5%, outperforming existing methods and demonstrating strong effectiveness in complex inspection scenarios.

title: CME2Net: Cross-Modal Edge-Enhanced Fusion Network for Optical and SAR Image Semantic Segmentation
authors: Yukang Wang, Xiaolin Wei, Luping Wang, Wuming Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In remote sensing image processing, multi-modal semantic segmentation algorithms based on optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images have drawn growing academic attention. However, current methods generally have the following issues: 1) Most existing methods address inter-modal feature misalignment via global information, with suboptimal segmentation performance at edge details. 2) Semantic edge-related information is insufficiently exploited in feature fusion of current algorithms. To tackle these issues, we propose a Cross-Modal Edge-Enhanced Fusion Network (CME2Net), which incorporates an edge branch to enhance the capability of extracting semantic edge information. Additionally, we present three modules: Dual-Domain Feature Rectify Module (DFR), Deformable Cross-Attention Fusion Module (DCAFM), and Dynamic Graph Edge Feature Enhancement Module (DGEFM). DFR enables interaction and rectification between dual modalities. DCAFM improves the capability of spatially aligned feature fusion. DGEFM performs edge-enhanced feature decoding. Experimental results demonstrate that CME2Net achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance on two datasets.

title: Human–AI Collaboration and Creative Behavior among Chinese College Students
authors: Xiao Zhou
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study examined the levels of human–AI collaboration and creative behavior among Chinese college students and explored the relationship between the two constructs. Data were collected from 558 undergraduate students across five universities in Guangdong Province, China. Using descriptive statistics and partial least squares structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM), the results indicated that students demonstrated relatively high levels of both human–AI collaboration and creative behavior. furthermore, human–AI collaboration was found to have a significant and positive effect on creative behavior. The findings support augmentation-based perspectives, suggesting that AI can enhance higher-order cognitive outcomes when used collaboratively rather than substitutivity. This study contributes empirical evidence to the growing literature on AI-supported learning and provides insights into how AI integration may foster creativity in higher education contexts.

title: Adaptive Intelligent Protection Model for Distribution Network based on State Prediction
authors: Yue Yuan, Sipeng Hao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To address the reduced selectivity and sensitivity of traditional three-stage current protection in distribution networks with high PV penetration, an adaptive protection strategy based on local voltage tracking is proposed. The method establishes a linear relationship between voltage sag depth and the stage II protection setting, and combines Kalman filter prediction with multimodal recognition to achieve voltage-driven and currentverified intelligent decision-making. A 10 kV PV-integrated distribution network model is built in Matlab/Simulink for validation. Results show that the proposed method reduces protection settings and improves sensitivity to remote and highresistance faults, effectively avoiding protection refusal. Relying only on local electrical measurements, it is simple, reliable, and suitable for engineering application.

title: Obstacle Perception Algorithm Based on LIDAR-Visual Fusion and Multi-Model Filter for Autonomous Guided Vehicles
authors: Yang Li, Yaohui Ma, Cuifeng Ren
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In order to improve the accuracy and recall rate of obstacle perception of automotive vehicles and ensure sufficient safety and robustness in autonomous driving, this paper proposed an obstacle detection, tracking and prediction algorithm based on early fusion of LIDAR and camera. Firstly, a LIDAR obstacle detection algorithm based on improved Euclidean clustering is proposed. The clustering parameters are adjusted according to the detection distance of obstacles by adaptive distance threshold method. Then, the trained YOLOv8n model was used for visual object detection, and the result of point cloud obstacle was fused to obtain the potential dynamic obstacle. Finally, a dynamic obstacle tracking and prediction algorithm based on multi-model filter is proposed to adjust the probability ratio of different models in real time according to the obstacle state. Experiment results demonstrate that the proposed method boosts the recall rate of LIDAR-based obstacle detection by 7.7 % across varying distances, enables accurate tracking of dynamic obstacles. In comparison with the single-model Kalman Filter (KF) tracking algorithm, a 10% improvement (RMSE) in obstacle tracking accuracy is achieved under high-dynamics scenarios, it also contributes to higher object prediction accuracy.

title: Gastric Cancer Detection in Histopathology Images Combining Transfer Learning and Graph Neural Network (GNN)
authors: S. Vasuki, VG. Janani, M. Jeya Shree, V. Lakshmi Deepika, K. Srikamu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: One of the main causes of cancer-related fatalities globally is gastric cancer, and survival rates are considerably increased by early detection. Histopathology images are crucial for identifying cancer, but manual analysis is time-consuming and prone to variability. This study presents a hybrid deep learning model combining Transfer Learning and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) for accurate gastric cancer detection and survival prediction from histopathology images. A pre- trained CNN extracts high-level features, leveraging transfer learning to reduce training time and improve feature quality. These features are used to build a graph representing cellular regions and patient data, which a GNN analyzes to capture complex spatial and relational patterns. The approach enables precise tumor segmentation, reliable tumor size estimation, and accurate survival predictions. Results show the hybrid model outperforms traditional CNNs, offering improved accuracy, survival prediction, and tumor detection. This research highlights the potential of combining feature extraction with graph-based analysis for advancing gastric cancer diagnosis and prognosis in histopathology.

title: Multipath Flow Routing For Battery Life Enhancement in Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: A. Lakshmi, I. Aswathi, S. Kavya Priya, L. Maheswari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) commonly confront issues related to limited energy resources and fluctuating network conditions. To ensure high data delivery performance and increase network longevity, effective routing techniques are crucial. In order to improve throughput and battery life, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A$</tex> Multipath AODV (Ad hoc On-Demand Distance Vector) routing protocol is presented in this study. NS2 simulations are used to compare the suggested protocol with the traditional DSDV (Destination-Sequenced Distance-Vector) protocol under various network conditions. Analysis is done on performance indicators such packet delivery ratio (PDR), energy usage, throughput, and end-to-end delay. Simulation results reveal that Multipath AODV achieves greater throughput and PDR while reducing energy consumption, hence increasing the network lifetime compared to DSDV. These findings indicate that the proposed approach is effective for energy-aware and reliable data transmission in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{W} \boldsymbol{S} \boldsymbol{N} \boldsymbol{s} \boldsymbol{.}$</tex>

title: Histopathologic Analysis of Canine Tumors Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Douglas Rodrigues, João P. Papa, Faviana A. M. León, Alessandre Hataka, Rebeca Scalco, Clayton R. Pereira
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: Veterinary pathology plays a fundamental role in clinical decision-making, with histopathological examination serving as the gold standard for diagnosing canine neoplasms. Among these, mast cell tumors and squamous cell carcinomas are highly prevalent, and their accurate differentiation is essential for prognosis and treatment planning. However, traditional grading workflows remain subjective, labor-intensive, and susceptible to interobserver variability. This study investigates deep learning–based approaches for automated classification of canine mast cell tumors and squamous cell carcinomas using a curated dataset of hematoxylin-and-eosin (H&E) stained images. Thirteen state-of-the-art Convolutional Neural Network architectures were systematically evaluated under two learning-rate configurations to assess the influence of network depth, connectivity patterns, and optimization hyperparameters on model performance. The results show that Xception and ResNet-152 achieved the best performance at lr = 0.001, whereas InceptionResNetV2 and DenseNet-121 attained the highest accuracies and perfect ROC–AUC scores at lr = 0.0001. These findings highlight that both architectural choice and learning-rate selection critically affect convergence stability and predictive accuracy. Explainability analyses based on Grad-CAM and feature activation visualization confirmed that the models focused on histologically meaningful regions, supporting the biological plausibility of their decision processes. Overall, this study demonstrates the potential of deep learning to enhance diagnostic consistency, objectivity, and scalability in veterinary pathology, paving the way for more reliable computational support tools in clinical workflows.

title: Transforming Agricultural Knowledge Dissemination: A SMART Platform Powered by Agentic AI for New Farmers
authors: Riming Liu, Ratanachote Thienmongkol, Natirath Weeranakin
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The urgent issue of proliferation and fragmentation regarding the agricultural knowledge among new farmers in China was tackled in this study, which established a context-aware SMART platform driven by Agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI). It is inspired by continued rural digital divide, where the existing mode of traditional knowledge diffusion cannot deliver timely, accurate and context-aware information. We take a mixed-method approach where we incorporating qualitative insights from experts and domain specialists with quantitative data from 30 new farmers in Nanning City, Guangxi Province to develop and evaluate the platform. The introduced system combines IoT, big data and AI in order to provide full-chain agricultural services from production to sales with an ultra-simple interface presented by dialectic visual content. The results show that 70% farmers are suffering from fragmented knowledge and 80% have focus on real-time data monitoring and intelligent decision support. Subsequent evaluations show high user acceptance with 63.33% of participants trusting the AI-based service, while 33.33%, though not averse to using our system, are neutral towards it – establishing credibility is required. The modular design of the platform effectively brings multi-source data together and can deliver dynamic knowledge by means of autonomous AI agents. This study brings about a collaborative knowledge ecosystem that empowers farmers out of assemblage and makes them no longer the recipient only, which is in line with China's National Smart Agriculture Action Plan. The results demonstrate the potential of agentic AI to act as a bridge between smart technology and everyday agricultural use, while underscoring trust and localization as key determinants of lasting uptake.

title: Digitalizing Math Instruction: The Synergistic Effect of the L.E.A.R.N.E.R. Strategy on Sixth-Grade Metacognitive Development and Computational Fluency in Philippine Public School
authors: Jereco F. Cultura, Samira P. Jalandoni, Jeanchil Gonzaga, Luchie P. Guinsod, Ariel U. Cubillas
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study addresses the persistent challenge of low mathematics proficiency among Filipino learners, as evidenced by the Philippines ranking 77th out of 81 countries in the 2022 PISA results. Specifically, at Alviola Integrated School–Elementary, Grade 6 pupils demonstrated a mean percentage score of only 53% in multiplying decimals, identifying it as the least mastered competency due to traditional, procedure-heavy instruction. To bridge this gap, the research evaluates the L.E.A.R.N.E.R. (Launch–Engage–Apply–Relate–Narrow–Examine–Reflect) Strategy integrated with Wayground. Utilizing a quasi-experimental design with 49 participants, results revealed that the experimental group achieved significantly higher posttest scores compared to the control group. The study’s contribution lies in providing a scalable, learner-centered model that aligns with the DepEd MATATAG vision, offering a practical solution to numeracy gaps. Its novelty is the unique synergy of gamification and a structured seven-phase reflective cycle that fosters deep metacognitive awareness. Standardized assessments of metacognitive development were employed alongside qualitative data coding of reflective journals. A follow-up assessment conducted two months later demonstrated the strategy's efficacy in long-term knowledge retention and transfer. The findings suggest that L.E.A.R.N.E.R. is an effective intervention to improve students' mathematical skills while fostering self-regulation and metacognitive awareness.

title: Phase-Programmable RIS Using Varactor Diodes for Beam Steering in 6G Applications
authors: Sherene Jacob, Vasudevan Karuppiah, Devi S, Arun Thanga Saravanan J, Rajesh Mooda
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RISs) has significant attention as a transformative technology for 6G communications. This is due to the capability of offering real-time control over wireless signal propagation. This work introduces a phase-programmable RIS designed for the 6–7 GHz band. The work employs varactor diodes in each unit cell to enable dynamic phase adjustment. The proposed design achieves a phase shift of up to 240° with high linearity, supporting precise beam steering across the operating spectrum. A full RIS array is designed and validated through simulations, showing effective beam direction control and minimal reflection losses. The results demonstrate that the design provides a compact, low-complexity, and efficient approach for enhancing link performance in 6G wireless networks.

title: Automated Image Based Classification for Early Detection of Amoebic Keratitis using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Abinaya Govindarajan, Gomati R, Kissokar R, Perazhagan S, Arun Kumar T, Kiruthikha M
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Amoebic Keratitis is the rare vision-threatening corneal infection caused by Acanthamoeba and hence early diagnosis is significant to prevent severe visual impairment. Nowadays, deep learning–based medical image analysis has shown promising outcome, wherein the transformer-based architectures have demonstrated strong capability in capturing long-range spatial dependencies in images. Still, traditional transformer attention mechanisms have limitations in balancing local feature extraction and global contextual understanding by maintaining computational efficiency. To address the issues, this research introduced the novel Residual Hybrid Attention Swin Transformer (RHA-SwinT) for automated classification of Amoebic Keratitis. The proposed model devisedthe pre-processing of corneal images using the Anisotropic Diffusion Filter to enhance lesion boundaries and suppress noise by preserving critical corneal structures. The enhanced images are then fed into the proposed RHA-SwinT architecture designed with hybrid attention composed of block and grid attention mechanisms and residual feature learning based FFN to capture both local infection patterns and global corneal structural relationships. Thus, the proposed RHA-SwinT method acquired improved diagnostic accuracy for early detection of Amoebic Keratitis from images.

title: Controlled Wet-Process Fabrication and Performance Investigation of Nanocomposite Aramid Insulation Paper
authors: Xu Zhao, Bowen Liu, Shuo Wang, Ruiyang Wang, Jiakang Guo, Xiaozhou Fan, Fangcheng Lv
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To address the increasingly demanding multifunctional integration requirements of insulation materials for advanced power equipment, this study develops a novel controllable fabrication process for nanocomposite aramid insulation paper based on wet-forming technology. Nano-sized alumina was employed as a functional filler, and by integrating key steps such as mechanical-ultrasonic synergistic dispersion and high-speed hydrodynamic homogenization, the issues of nanoparticle agglomeration and loss during the wet process were systematically resolved, enabling uniform dispersion and stable incorporation of the fillers within the aramid fiber network. Experimental results demonstrate that the prepared nanocomposite paper exhibits excellent overall performance: compared with pristine aramid paper, the characteristic breakdown strength increases from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$33.8 \text{kV} / \text{mm}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$40.1 \text{kV} / \text{mm}$</tex>, the in-plane thermal conductivity rises from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.152 \mathrm{W} /(\mathrm{m} \cdot \mathrm{K})$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.221 \mathrm{W} /(\mathrm{m} \cdot \mathrm{K})$</tex>, while the tensile strength is well maintained at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 3. 7 ~ M P a}$</tex>.

title: Geospatial Deep Learning Approach for Roof Type Mapping Using UAV Images
authors: Shoba B, Venkatesh J, Saravanan R, Janaki E, Surendran R, Deepa R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: In the current era of the development of Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) technologies, building structures, being the object of geospatial analysis, can be studied precisely due to high-resolution aerial imagery. Nevertheless, current roof classification procedures usually have recourse to low-resolution satellite data or visual interpretation, which leads to a low degree of extracting structural information and low robustness. Moreover, the traditional deep learning models often overlook spatial-geometric correlations and multiple-scale contextual features, which results in error of the classification of similar type roofs. In a bid to overcome these constraints, this paper suggests a Multi-Scale Geo-Aware CNN with Spatial Feature Fusion (MSGCNN-SFF) framework. The model combines the multi-scale feature learning and geospatial awareness to learn local texture features and global structural features of roofs using UAV images. The spatial feature fusion module improves the reliability of classification through integrating geometric, spectral, and contextual spatial features and therefore overcoming the problem of scale variation and structural ambiguity in the current approaches. Experimental results show that MSGCNN-SFF is much more effective than the conventional deep learning methods in classifying, establishing, and maintaining high classifier accuracy, spatial consistency, and strength in complex urban environments. The framework offers credible roof-type mapping that facilitates risk analysis and decision-making during urban development that is climate-resilient. It achieved higher classification accuracy (94%), spatial consistency (0.86±0.02), structural similarity detection (84%), and multi-scale robustness (0.88±0.02).

title: DNSFormer: A Multi-Objective Transformer Model for Stealthy DNS Attack Detection
authors: P. Senthil, S Kishore Kumar, P. Karthikeyen, S Gunaseelan, M Manobala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The Remote Access Trojans and the data exfiltration represent some of the persistent and hidden threats in modern cybersecurity as, the Domain Name System has become a major threat particularly affecting corporate, government and high value infrastructure. Threats such as DNS amplification and DNS Fast-flux play a major role in exploiting DNS for remote access trojans and data exfiltration through tunnelling, which is one of the stealthiest methods as it hides the command-and-control communication within legitimate DNS traffic, making detection challenging as it is bypassing firewalls and IDS. The traditional IDS often show high false alarm and moreover they lack in balancing between efficient detection and system performance, which eventually not suitable for real-time deployment. To overcome limitations, this study proposes a DNSFORMER model for DNS classification and it enhance through multi-objective optimization with NSGA-2(Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II). NLP-based packet sequence modelling, statistical analysis, reinforcement learning are employed to identify and block unauthorized data transfer channels. The optimization specifically targets 12 objectives: seven classification metrics (Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score, True Positive Rate, False Positive Rate, and False Negative Rate) and five Quality of Service (QoS) metrics (Response Time, Latency, Throughput, Reliability, and Availability). The DNS amplification and the fast-flux are being captured In separate algorithms approaches, as how IP rotation occurs and they malform as an victim's IP, in order to cause Denial of Service (availability attack) and Stealthy hosting & persistence (confidentiality + integrity attack). Comparative analysis shows how an NSGA-2 and other algorithms perform on that 12 objective. By incorporating layered security intelligence, this research presents a robust, scalable, and future-ready Defence mechanism against DNS-based RAT infiltration and hidden data exfiltration. There by strengthening cybersecurity resilience in mission-critical environments.
