title: Network Topology Dynamic Variation Method Based on Matrix Efficiency Model for Online Path Optimization of WPTN System
authors: Xiaomin Wang, Xin Dai, Shunsheng Hong, Yanling Li, Udaya Kumara Madawala
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Wireless power transfer network (WPTN) extends conventional WPT into distributed multi-node systems with relay-based power routing. However, the complexity of network topology poses significant challenges for path optimization. This paper proposed a matrix-based efficiency path optimization (MEPO) strategy that enables online adaptive routing without offline training or iterative solving. A unified frequency-domain model is established, allowing path efficiency and voltage feasibility to be determined analytically from the network parameters. This model captures all cross-coupling effects and yields expressions for optimal operating parameters. The mutual inductance matrix is reconstructed in real time using NSGA-II, which requires only coil current measurements, enabling adaptive operation under varying topologies. Experiments on a four-node prototype validate the method under dynamic topology variations, achieving up to 90% efficiency.

title: PC-FT: A Lightweight and Utility-Preserving Private Fine-Tuning Framework for Edge-Based Agentic AI
authors: Peisheng Zhang, Renao Zhang, Zefang Lv, Qing Fan, Shuai Yuan, Zhitao Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based agentic AI systems are increasingly moving to the network edge, driven by the need for intelligent services with minimal latency. However, the resource-constrained and data-local nature of edge nodes imposes higher demands on both privacy protection and computational efficiency. Existing differentially private fine-tuning methods often incur high overhead or substantial utility loss. We propose PCFT, a lightweight and utility-preserving differentially private fine-tuning framework designed for LLM-based agentic AI operating at the network edge. PC-FT improves the privacy–utility trade-off through contribution-guided adaptive noise perturbation, enabling parameter-efficient private adaptation. Specifically, it introduces an objective-coupled contribution score derived from a first-order Taylor proxy to quantify each parameter's influence on the training objective. This score partitions parameters into high- and low-contribution groups to enable differentiated noise injection. Furthermore, a lightweight dynamic partial update mechanism is introduced to periodically adjust the partition during training using only already-privatized gradients without incurring additional privacy cost. When benchmarked on E2E and DART, PCFT provides measurable utility improvements over current DP-PEFT baselines without relaxing the privacy guarantee or incurring substantial additional overhead. Privacy attack evaluations confirm that PCFT does not amplify information leakage and often reduces it, thereby achieving a favorable privacy–utility balance for agentic AI deployed at the network edge.

title: MoSA: Modality-Aware Spatially-Adaptive Adaptation for Multimodal Semantic Segmentation
authors: Xing Zhang, Kunlei Dong, Wei Wang, Huasheng Yang, Lei Chen, Zhengpin Li, Jian Zhang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multimodal semantic segmentation enhances semantic perception by fusing complementary features from multiple modalities. However, the contribution of each modality to segmentation predictions varies across spatial locations depending on local content characteristics, an issue that has been insufficiently addressed by existing methods. To tackle this challenge, we present MoSA, a novel framework for adapting vision foundation models to Multimodal semantic segmentation via Modality-aware Spatially-Adaptive learning. MoSA introduces a Modality-Specific Spatially-Modulated Adapter (MS-SMA) to capture modality-specific representations while dynamically adjusting adaptation intensity based on both global scene context and local content information. Furthermore, MoSA proposes Reliability-Guided Cross-Modal Fusion (RGCF), which estimates per-location modality reliability using feature statistics and performs adaptively weighted fusion. MoSA is a model-agnostic framework that can be seamlessly applied to various multimodal combinations without task-specific modifications. MoSA outperforms state-of-the-art methods in mIoU by 2.3% on the NYU Depth V2 dataset and by 2.1% on the MFNet dataset, requiring only 7.7% of the trainable parameters.

title: Developing Plug-and-Play Interoperability: an approach through IEEE 11073-10206 and the Bluetooth General Health Sensor Profile and Service
authors: Malcolm Clarke, Hulya Gokalp
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Plug and play interoperability is viewed as an essential feature for Personal Health Devices; however commercial devices remain proprietary. This paper describes the research undertaken in the development of the IEEE 11073-10206 standard to provide plug and play interoperability and its implementation as the Bluetooth General Health Sensor (GHS). It explains the concepts adopted in the IEEE 11073-10206 information model and the use of IEEE 11073-10101 nomenclature to support a plug-and-play semantic architecture. It describes how the concepts of IEEE 11073-10206 have been implemented as the services defined in the GHS profile. It reports findings from an implementation of a platform that integrates proprietary, Bluetooth and GHS to form an eco-system of interoperable devices and its extension to an interoperable end-to-end system based on IHE PCD 01 or FHIR (profiles of HL7) to send semantically interoperable data to the enterprise. Gaps in the GHS specifications are identified and recommendations made.

title: Execution-Time Valid Control for Dynamic LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Mahmoud Sami, Mohammed Atiquzzaman
publish: IEEE Networking Letters
abstract: Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite networks are a promising solution for global connectivity, Internet access, disaster management, and remote sensing applications. This letter defines execution-time feasibility under delayed and partially observed telemetry based on a telemetry-consistent belief set. We show that, under bounded state evolution and non-zero execution intervals, decision-time validation is insufficient. An execution-time feasibility framework is formulated that can adapt to existing control plane mechanisms in LEO satellite networks. The simulation results show that delay and uncertainty induce violations in the decision-time validation scheme, while the proposed execution-time feasibility framework certifies that network constraints remain satisfied throughout the execution interval.

title: Open-set Anomaly Segmentation in Complex Scenarios
authors: Song Xia, Yi Yu, Henghui Ding, Wenhan Yang, Shifei Liu, Alex C. Kot, Xudong Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Precise segmentation of out-of-distribution (OoD) objects, herein referred to as anomalies, is crucial for the reliable deployment of semantic segmentation models in open-set, safety-critical applications, such as autonomous driving. Current anomalous segmentation benchmarks predominantly focus on favorable weather conditions, resulting in untrustworthy evaluations that overlook the risks posed by diverse meteorological conditions in open-set environments, such as low illumination, dense fog, and heavy rain. To bridge this gap, this paper introduces the ComSAmy, a Complex Scenarios Anomaly segmentation benchmark. ComSAmy encompasses a wide spectrum of adverse weather conditions, dynamic driving environments, and diverse anomaly types to comprehensively evaluate the model performance in realistic open-world scenarios. Our extensive evaluation of several state-of-the-art anomalous segmentation models reveals that existing methods demonstrate significant deficiencies in such challenging scenarios, highlighting their serious safety risks for real-world deployment. To solve that, we propose a novel energy-entropy learning (EEL) strategy that integrates the complementary information from energy and entropy to bolster the robustness of anomaly segmentation under complex open-world environments. Additionally, a diffusion-based anomalous training data synthesizer is proposed to generate diverse and high-quality anomalous images to enhance the existing copy-paste training data synthesizer. Extensive experimental results on both public and ComSAmy benchmarks demonstrate that our proposed diffusion-based synthesizer with energy and entropy learning (DiffEEL) framework serves as an effective and generalizable plug-and-play method to enhance existing models, yielding an average improvement of around 4.96% in AUPRC and 9.87% in FPR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">95</sub>.

title: Research on the Construction of AI-Based Personalized Learning Paths and Curriculum Design
authors: Yuan Sun, Bojia Chen, Changhua Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence and data-driven technologies, the education sector is being transformed from traditional uniform teaching models to student-centered personalized learning approaches, and the development of personalized learning paths and curriculum design has been identified as a critical direction for promoting high-quality educational development. This paper systematically reviews the current applications of artificial intelligence and data-driven technologies in personalized learning, thoroughly analyzes the opportunities and core challenges in technology implementation, and focuses on the mechanisms of AI-driven personalized learning path design and data-supported curriculum setting methods. Research indicates that the synergistic application of artificial intelligence and data-driven technologies can effectively achieve "teaching determined by learning," providing technical support for personalized education; however, addressing issues such as data security, algorithm fairness, and teachers' technological adaptation capabilities is considered a core prerequisite for successful technology implementation. This study can provide theoretical references for advancing personalized education practices in the educational field, optimizing the integration of technology and teaching scenarios, and facilitating the digital and personalized transformation of education.

title: Exploring the Relationship between AI Trust and AI Literacy among Chinese College Students
authors: Xiao Zhou, Zhixiang Pan, Liu Li
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the rapid integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into higher education, understanding factors that shape students’ AI literacy has become increasingly important. This study investigates the relationship between AI trust and AI literacy among Chinese college students and examines the extent to which AI trust predicts AI literacy. Using a questionnaire survey, data were collected from 485 college students in Guangdong and analyzed using partial least squares structural equation modeling (PLS-SEM). The results reveal a significant and positive relationship between AI trust and AI literacy. Further analysis indicates that AI trust demonstrates moderate explanatory power and strong predictive relevance for AI literacy, highlighting its substantial role in students’ AI competence development. These findings suggest that trust-related beliefs toward AI are important psychological antecedents of AI literacy. The study contributes to the literature by linking AI trust with AI literacy and offers practical implications for designing educational interventions that foster both students’ trust in AI technologies and their AI-related competencies.

title: Hybrid Naïve Bayes–ID3 Model for Phishing Classification Using Lexical and Behavioural Features
authors: Jayshnav S, G Suseela, G Niranjana, Archana Mahendran
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Malicious URLs are primary vector for cyber threats such as phishing, delivery of malware, financial scams, etc.,. Essentially blacklist-based, traditional methods of detection struggle to identify recent and hidden threats and sometimes require downloading entire web pages before they can be examined, thus wasting a lot of time and money. This research work proposes a machine learning-based hybrid detection system integrating static and dynamic analysis to classify URLs in real-time so as to overcome these limitations. The proposed approach leverages Naïve Bayes classification and an enhanced ID3 decision tree algorithm to improve detection accuracy. Analysing important URL characteristics including lexical features (special characters, domain structure, subdomains, and path analysis) and behavioural features (hosting information, TLD reputation, and attack parameters) will reveal the threats before they can directly affect users. The system also uses anomaly detection methods to offset cybercrime evasion techniques. Using several realworld sources, the approach is evaluated on a 600 URLs dataset comprising 400 hostiles and 200 legitimate URLs. The system detects hitherto unidentified threats, consequently surpassing conventional blacklist-based techniques and attaining a high success rate. With much lower false positives, experimental results reveal an $87 \%-95 \%$ accuracy rate. This work is rather successful in improving web security by means of a fast, light, real-time system for the identification of harmful URLs using limited computational capacity. Future work will primarily focus on adding deep learning models and extending the dataset for better performance.

title: Load Transfer Optimization Method Based on Neural Networks Under Substation Total Outage Faults
authors: Yutong Guo, Shufan Shi, Cong Wang, Xiangyu Gao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: Due to neglecting factors such as the real-time status of the power grid and dynamic changes in load, the transfer efficiency is low. Therefore, this paper studies a load transfer optimization method based on neural networks under substation total outage faults. Comprehensive load monitoring is performed during a substation total outage fault to acquire multi-dimensional data and preprocess it. A load transfer optimization model is constructed using a convolutional neural network to accurately predict evaluation indicators under different load transfer schemes. A multi-objective particle swarm optimization algorithm is used to determine the optimal load transfer scheme from the perspective of multiple objective functions. Experimental results show that, in terms of power supply reliability, this method achieves a load recovery rate of 97%, far exceeding the expected 95%; in terms of actual fault handling timeliness, under low, medium, and high load conditions, this method determines the optimal load transfer scheme within 8 minutes; in performance comparison, after applying the method, the peak-to-valley difference decreased by 40%, the load standard deviation decreased by 43.75%, the transfer cost decreased by 28.6%, the line overload rate decreased by 20%, the calculation error rate decreased by 9%, and the load transfer rate increased by 22%, demonstrating excellent performance and effectively improving power grid performance and power supply reliability, possessing high practical and promotional value.

title: A Scalar Field–Based Toolpath Generation Method Using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Kun Li, Guanghui Zhou, Chao Zhang, Zenghui Wang, Zhijie Wei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Finish machining is the final operation in numerical control (NC) machining and directly determines the surface integrity and dimensional accuracy of the workpiece. Its toolpath governs the motion of the cutting tool along the workpiece surface, thereby serving as a critical factor influencing both machining efficiency and quality. Conventional toolpath planning methods often suffer from non-uniform path spacing and excessive machining redundancy. In order to overcome the above limitations, a scalar field-based toolpath planning framework is developed, where self-supervised learning is employed to construct an optimal scalar field and toolpaths are extracted from its iso-level lines. The method starts with the generation of a maximum machining strip width direction field and then formulates two optimization objectives—alignment with the target direction field and preservation of uniform scallop height-which serve as the loss functions for the learning model. Furthermore, a novel convolutional module is introduced to effectively integrate the spatial geometry, curvature, and directional vector features of the surface. Based on this module, a Scalar Field Generating - Convolutional Neural Network (SG-CNN) is developed. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the scalar fields generated by the SG-CNN model are well aligned with the desired geometric and machining criteria, and the resulting toolpaths exhibit high continuity.

title: Haptic-Enhanced Shared Control for Collaborative Dual-Robot Telemanipulation in Large-Scale Object Handling
authors: Mohammad Sadeghi, Inderawes Khalil, Konstantina Orlandatou, Thorsten A. Kern
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: The Material handling is often a repetitive, labor-intensive process that can be optimized through automation. However, while humanoid robots provide dexterity, they face limitations in handling large-scale objects, particularly in extreme environments where the transportation of hazardous materials demands stringent safety measures. This paper presents the development of a shared control strategy for the telemanipulation of large objects using dual mobile manipulators. A high-level impedance control layer is integrated into the shared control framework to ensure smooth and adaptive movement in complex environments. Progression through the phases of object manipulation is governed by a finite state machine (FSM) integrated within the coordinator node. To enhance operator situational awareness, the system incorporates haptic feedback and a virtual reality (VR)-based Hapto-Vision interface, enabling real-time visualization of force dynamics, positioning, and interactions. System performance is evaluated using key parameters such as repeatability, latency, and resilience to external disturbances. The proposed software framework enables adaptable, safe, and precise Telemanipulation across different mobile platforms with minimal hardware changes.

title: A Multimodal Summarization Framework Based on Dynamic Retrieval and Adaptive Querying
authors: Wenxian Zeng, Shicheng Hu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Multimodal summarization aims to distill concise and accurate summaries by integrating multi-source information, such as text, images, and videos, to address the rapid growth of digital media content. However, in long-context or real-time scenarios (e.g., news reporting and live broadcasting), existing static fusion and retrieval strategies frequently suffer from cross-modal information loss and computational inefficiency. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel multimodal summarization framework incorporating a dynamic multimodal retrieval mechanism. By tracking the intermediate summary generation process in real-time and injecting relevant images or video frames from an external knowledge base, this approach effectively bridges information gaps and enhances the robustness of long-sequence processing. Additionally, an adaptive query optimization module is designed. This module leverages modality-specific encoders and contextual attention mechanisms to generate refined hybrid query vectors, thereby facilitating efficient knowledge matching and significantly reducing inference latency. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed DRAMS framework outperforms existing baseline methods across multiple datasets, achieving an average improvement of 5% to 10% on evaluation metrics including ROUGE-1, F1 score, and cosine similarity, while effectively reducing inference time by approximately 20%.

title: Automatic Alignment Control Strategy for GIL Flanges Based on Laser-Vision Fusion
authors: Yaojun Zhou, Xiaoming Xu, Yang Li, Zhihao Shen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To address the efficiency and safety challenges of manual Gas-Insulated Line (GIL) flange assembly in confined spaces, particularly under strong optical interference, this paper proposes a robust automatic alignment control strategy utilizing a hierarchical laser-vision fusion framework. First, a geometric constraint-based laser algorithm is employed for coarse alignment to overcome Field-of-View (FOV) limitations caused by large initial deviations. Subsequently, an anti-interference visual servoing method integrates adaptive morphological filtering with least-squares circle fitting to achieve robust, high-precision pose estimation despite metal reflections. A hierarchical state machine ensures smooth switching between coarse and fine control modes. Experiments on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$300-\text{kg}$</tex> pipe sections demonstrate that the system achieves an alignment error within 0.5 mm, a success rate exceeding 95%, and an average docking time of 10 seconds. This multi-agent collaborative approach offers a robust solution for intelligent power facility construction.

title: Enhancing Grade 7 Students’ Conceptual Mastery of Free-Body Diagrams and Force Analysis Using CK-12 and PhET: A Mixed-Methods Investigation
authors: Apple Jane S. Lima, Rolando S. Estose, Zio V. Tutor, Josiery Keziah L. Uayan
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This mixed-methods, quasi-experimental study investigated the effectiveness of CK-12 and PhET Interactive Physics Simulations in enhancing Grade 7 students’ conceptual mastery of Free-Body Diagrams and force analysis. In the quantitative phase, students were assigned to an experimental group (CK-12) or a comparison group (PhET) and assessed using pre- and post-tests. Results of an independent samples t-test indicated that students using CK-12 achieved significantly higher post-test scores, suggesting that structured explanations and guided representations more effectively support conceptual understanding. In the qualitative phase, students’ perceptions were gathered through open-ended questionnaires and reflective responses. Thematic analysis highlighted complementary strengths: CK-12 was valued for its clarity and stepwise guidance, while PhET fostered interactivity, visual engagement, and exploratory learning. These findings suggest that strategically integrating CK-12 and PhET can reinforce foundational physics concepts while promoting engagement and experiential learning. Educators may leverage CK-12 to strengthen structured understanding and PhET to encourage inquiry-based exploration. Future research could examine long-term retention, hybrid platform use, and the role of teacher scaffolding in optimizing simulation-based learning outcomes.

title: Motion Planning Methods for a Mobile Manipulator in Underground Coal Mine Auxiliary Operations
authors: Ziyan Ma, Dejian Liu, Jie Zhou
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: Coal mine robotics technology serves as a key technical support for achieving reduced manpower, enhanced efficiency, and intelligent mining. However, the practical application of mobile manipulators in underground coal mine roadways faces significant challenges, including complex spatial environments, difficulty in system decoupling, and abrupt environmental variations. To address the requirements for precise control of mobile manipulators and multi-objective motion trajectory planning in underground coal mine operations, a dynamic model of the mobile manipulator is established. Based on the geometric characteristic parameters of auxiliary operational tasks, path and trajectory planning of the mobile manipulator are conducted. Furthermore, a coordinated control strategy for the redundant degree-of-freedom system composed of the mobile base and the manipulator is investigated. The proposed approach enables precise control of the mobile manipulator system for underground coal mine operations, thereby improving the operational performance and environmental adaptability of coal mine robotic systems.

title: Research on the Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Teachers' Educational Values
authors: Yaling Wu, Fuxing Zheng
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As artificial intelligence becomes increasingly embedded in educational practice, teachers are required to reconsider their educational values alongside the adoption of new technologies. This qualitative study examines how teachers interpret and negotiate their educational values in response to artificial intelligence integration across different school contexts. Semi-structured interviews were conducted with teachers from well-resourced, ordinary public, and under-resourced or remote-area schools. Guided by sociocultural theory, the data were thematically analyzed to understand artificial intelligence as a mediating tool in teachers’ professional thinking. The findings reveal three interconnected shifts in teachers’ educational values. Teachers increasingly prioritize guiding students to evaluate and apply information rather than focusing on knowledge transmission. They also emphasize fostering students’ moral judgment in response to challenges such as algorithmic bias and misinformation, and promote autonomous learning and higher-order thinking over procedural skill acquisition. Overall, artificial intelligence contributes to a gradual transformation of teachers’ educational values toward students’ holistic development.

title: The Effects of Blended Learning in University Science Courses: A Meta-Analytic Review
authors: Lingna Li, Nan Yao, Xinyue Hu, Lili Wang, Junhong Xie, Guopeng Zeng
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: As digital transformation in education advances, blended learning is evolving from an innovative approach into a mainstream format in higher education, significantly reshaping traditional teaching ecosystems. Current research presents inconsistent findings on how blended learning affects student outcomes in undergraduate science courses. To address this, a meta-analysis was conducted, synthesizing 28 empirical studies from China and abroad. The results indicate that: (1) Blended learning has a moderately positive overall effect on learning outcomes in university science courses; (2) Regarding instructional design, full-semester duration, small teaching scale, and a moderate proportion of online learning show stronger effectiveness; (3) In terms of instructional support, videos and reading materials play the most essential foundational role, while learning management systems prove more effective than online meeting tools; (4) Regarding assessment, formative evaluation and big data-based comprehensive evaluation outperform summative evaluation. Based on these findings, this study proposes building a blended learning system centered on "scientific cognitive principles", aimed at systematically enhancing students’ disciplinary literacy and higher-order thinking skills through optimized course structure, deeper technology integration, and diversified assessment practices.

title: Project-Based Flipped Classroom Reform with Interactive and Collaborative Approaches
authors: Ning Ye, Xiaoshi Song, Rong Geng, Ce Ji
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This paper presents a novel project-based flipped classroom model that synthesizes two core innovations: Guided Learning-Mutual Growth and Symbiotic Co-Creation. The three-stage, four-dimensional teaching architecture integrates teacher demonstration, collaborative research, and student-led practice with competency development in critical thinking, engineering implementation, innovation breakthroughs, and collaborative leadership. By embedding authentic industrial case studies and implementing AI-assisted scaffolding, the framework enables dynamic optimization of instruction and competency cultivation. The model demonstrates significant empirical outcomes: students achieve an autonomous task completion rate of 85% or higher, which notably exceeds the 51% observed in traditional classrooms. Furthermore, team innovation proposal acceptance shows a 60% increase from a baseline of 34%, while enterprise evaluation of job-role alignment improves by 40% from a baseline of 27%. These results validate the effectiveness of applying the gradual release of responsibility teaching theory to engineering education.

title: MTBT: Multi-Target Bit Trojan Attack for Quantized Neural Networks
authors: Akiha Kusumoto, Mohammad Hasan Ahmadilivani, Jaan Raik
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Quantized Neural Networks (QNNs) are widely deployed in safety-critical edge applications. In this regard, understanding the severity and mechanisms of security threats is essential for safeguarding the systems during deployment. This paper presents Multi-Target Bit Trojan (MTBT), the first bit-flip backdoor attack capable of simultaneously hijacking multiple target classes through minimal weight modifications. Unlike prior work that targets a single class, MTBT jointly optimizes trigger generation and weight perturbations to enable multi-target attacks with a single Trojan in CNN models. Our evaluation on an 8-bit quantized ResNet-18 demonstrates that MTBT achieves over 95% Attack Success Rate (ASR) for two target classes with only 55 bit-flips, with a minimal impact on the baseline accuracy. Furthermore, MTBT successfully scales to three simultaneous targets, achieving a mean ASR exceeding 85% across all classes in CIFAR-10.

title: The Construction of an AI-based chatbot to Provide Formative Feedback on Argumentative Writing
authors: Jing Wen, Xiaoyu Wang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The study explores students’ perceptions of the efficacy of an AI-based chatbot "IELTS Writing Tutor" in providing formative feedback on argumentative essays to first-year engineering students at a China-UK collaborative undergraduate program. Built on the Smartutor platform, the chatbot delivers structured feedback aligned with IELTS Writing Task 2 criteria, model answers, and reflection prompts. 29 students engaged in the research and received feedback on the three writing responses they wrote. This study employed a mixed-methods approach. Quantitative data were collected via a 5-point Likert scale questionnaire measuring perceived usefulness and ease of use of the chatbot, followed by SPSS-based descriptive analysis. Qualitative data included open-ended feedback from students, which was analyzed using thematic analysis. The study found participants rated the chatbot highly on both usefulness and ease of use, with all mean scores exceeding 4.2. While its core strengths are recognized in promptly identifying errors and delivering learning-focused feedback, the findings indicate that future development must focus on two key areas: advancing personalized learning experience and diversifying modes of interaction.

title: Power Prediction and Efficiency Optimization of Photovoltaic Power Generation Systems Based on Deep Learning
authors: Xiao Qin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: The degradation of power prediction accuracy in photovoltaic power generation systems under data privacy constraints remains unresolved. To address this issue, this study constructs a deep prediction and optimization model that integrates Differential Privacy - Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) with physical constraint mechanisms. A Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network are jointly used to extract meteorological-power sequence features, and privacy protection is achieved during gradient pruning and Gaussian noise injection. Simultaneously, Model Predictive Control (MPC) is employed to solve for the optimal solution for energy storage and inverter power allocation under the coupling of prediction output and environmental conditions, thereby improving operational efficiency. Experimental results show that the model achieves a short-term power prediction error of 3.02% Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) and a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 27.95 kW under clear weather conditions, while maintaining a MAPE of 4.12% under a privacy budget of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon=0.5$</tex>. In the coupled prediction and optimization scenario, the energy storage dispatch efficiency reaches 95.12%, and the total system energy utilization rate reaches 93.65%. The above research forms a unified technical path for photovoltaic power prediction and efficiency optimization under differential privacy constraints.

title: Cloud-Integrated Lucky Imaging Pipeline [CLIP]: A Distributed Approach for Real-Time Astronomical Image Processing
authors: Lakshya Singh, K Akshanth Sai, Charan Aditya, Aditi Kora, Narayanan Prasanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Atmospheric turbulence introduces wavefront distortions and limits achievable spatial resolution, which is a fundamental limitation of ground-based astronomical observations. By collecting thousands of short-exposure frames and carefully stacking only the sharpest subset to recreate a high-resolution image, Lucky Imaging overcomes this constraint. However, real- time operation, automation, and scalability are not feasible on traditional local systems due to Lucky Imaging's data volumes and computational demands. The Cloud-Integrated Lucky Imaging Pipeline (CLIP), a cloud-native framework that combines real-time distributed image stacking with Lucky Imaging, is presented in this paper. With the help of the suggested system, telescopes can send short-exposure frames straight to the cloud, where large-scale automated frame quality evaluation, selection, and stacking take place. Through continuous data streaming and edge-based pre-filtering, the framework makes sure that only high-quality frames are transmitted for processing and thereby reducing bandwidth and latency. This also enables multiple telescopes to contribute to a shared processing environment and supports collaborative observations.

title: Platforms for Sharing Threat Intelligence: Improving Teamwork in Cybersecurity by Disseminating Threat Indicators and Countermeasures in Real-Time
authors: Rajesh Jagadeesan Ravikumar, Charulatha Umashankar
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: In today’s interconnected digital economy, conventional cybersecurity measures are ineffective due to the proliferation and complexity of cyber threats. Cybersecurity teams are better able to work together when they share threat data. Through the real-time sharing of threat indicators, vulnerabilities, and countermeasures, these systems enable cybersecurity specialists, government agencies, and businesses to work together in the battle against cyber assaults. By examining these models, we can identify those that effectively convey threat intelligence. Methods like these automate methods for exchanging data, standardize data formats, and use machine learning techniques to glean insights from shared datasets. In this research, we look at problems with implementing platforms. Issues include data privacy, information overload, and the maturation of corporate threats. These issues with the architecture can be resolved by strong data governance and user-centered design. This method makes it possible to convey information in a timely and relevant way. Additionally, the implementation of threat intelligence platforms is covered in case studies. The cybersecurity and incident response were both enhanced by these solutions. This study found that cybersecurity is best achieved through collaboration and trust. Companies can fight new cyberattacks better if they share threat intelligence.

title: Multi-Scale Residual Dilated Temporal Convolutional Network (MRD-TCN) for Interaction-Aware Drug Recommendation in Breast Cancer
authors: R.T Charulatha, T. Aravind
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the most common causes of cancer-related deaths worldwide, with a high level of molecular heterogeneity and therapeutic responses. Despite the use of specific agents such as Trastuzumab and Tamoxifen, which are known to improve survival, the appropriate choice of drugs is complicated by the issues of Drug-Gene Interactions (DGI) and Drug-Drug Interactions (DDI). Several current computational methods, such as traditional machine learning and conventional deep learning models, have not been successful in effectively modeling multi-scale nonlinear dependencies and interaction-sensitive patterns inherent in high-dimensional multi-omics data. In addition, several existing approaches are intensively based on graph-based or attention-based designs, which might be insufficient to capture hierarchical temporal relationships in the combined space of molecular and interaction features. To address these drawbacks, this work proposes an Interaction-Aware MultiScale Residual Dilated Temporal Convolutional Network (MRD-TCN) to recommend breast cancer drugs. The first step was data preprocessing to guarantee data consistency and reliability by normalizing, filling in the missing values, and filtering interactions. Molecular fingerprints of the drug, gene expression patterns, and an encoded DGI/DDI matrix were then extracted and formatted as sequential embeddings. The proposed MRD-TCN utilizes parallel multi-scale convolutional kernels to learn both local and global patterns of interactions, dilated convolutions to learn long-range relationships, and residual connections to learn important biological data. The channel-wise gating mechanism further augments the profound interaction properties during pre-classification. The experimental analysis proves that the proposed framework has a high performance compared with the existing models: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN, 89.12%), Regional Graph Convolutional Network (RGCN, 90.34%), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU, 91.08%), and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM, 91.76%), and the proposed MRD-TCN is the most accurate (95.63%).

title: AI-Driven Optimization of Cascaded Seawater-Cooled PV-CSP Hybrid Systems for Arid Coastal Environments: Coastal Saudi Arabia as a Model
authors: Yara Almaghrabi, Reena Alqahtani, Lamar Alzahrani, Hemalatha Mahalingam, Marwa Ahmed
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: This study presents a novel, Artificial Intelligence (AI)-enhanced hybrid Photovoltaic (PV)-Concentrated Solar Power (CSP) system designed for arid coastal environments (using coastal Saudi Arabia as a model), which fundamentally transforms seawater-based PV cooling from a conventional operational expense into a productive thermal input for CSP generation, thereby increasing overall conversion efficiency by up to 30%. By employing a sophisticated random forest-based machine-learning model to optimize site selection, the research achieves over 90% classification accuracy and reduces analysis time by more than 50% compared to traditional manual methods, identifying Tabuk as a prime location. The suggested integrated, cascaded architecture eliminates reliance on freshwater resources while ensuring reliable, dispatchable power through advanced thermal energy storage and bidirectional power conversion, offering a scalable and sustainable solution that aligns with Saudi Vision 2030 energy objectives.

title: Dynamic Modeling of a 4-DOF Coaxis Parallel Manipulator
authors: Xiaocong Hu, Bin Liao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Kinematic and dynamic modeling of a parallel manipulator serves as the fundamental basis for mechanism performance evaluation and motion control. In this paper, the inverse kinematic and dynamic models of a novel 4-degree-offreedom (DOF) coaxis parallel robot are established, and the validity of these models is verified via simulation experiments. First, by constructing the position closed-loop equations for each subchain, the geometric constraints of the mechanism are transformed into a system of quadratic equations with driving angles as variables, enabling the analytical solution of the inverse kinematic problem. Furthermore, the velocity and acceleration expressions of the joints are derived using the vector composition method, and expressed in the operational space. Subsequently, the rigid-body dynamic model of the mechanism is derived based on the principle of virtual work, yielding the general dynamic equations in the operational space. Finally, the accuracy of the proposed models is validated through simulation experiments.

title: Assessing the Vulnerability of Open-Source RISC-V Processors to Transient Execution Attacks
authors: Elia Lazzeri, Gianluca Furano, Luca Cassano
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: The rapid adoption of the open and extensible RISCV instruction set architecture across diverse computing domains requires a thorough understanding of its security posture, particularly against hardware threats. While performance optimizations like speculative and out-of-order execution enhance throughput, they concurrently introduce vulnerabilities to Transient Execution Attacks (TEAs), which can bypass traditional security mechanisms. The inherent flexibility of RISC-V leads to significant microarchitectural variation among implementations, suggesting that susceptibility to TEAs is not uniform. However, systematic comparative assessments across prominent processors are still lacking. This paper addresses this gap by presenting a comparative vulnerability analysis of three widely used open-source RISCV cores: the high performance BOOM, the reliability-oriented NOEL-V, and CVA6. We ported and evaluated a set of TEAs, including variants of Spectre and Meltdown and the most recent attacks, such as Speculative Code Store Bypass (SCSB) and Indirector. Our experiments demonstrate the successful execution of different attack subsets on each processor, revealing distinct vulnerability profiles tied to their underlying microarchitectures, offering valuable vulnerability data and practical insights that may be crucial for guiding the design of more secure processors. One of the key insights of our analysis is that, contrary to the popular belief, in-order processors are vulnerable against TEAs.

title: Direct Power Control Strategy for Three-Phase PWM Rectifiers Based on Synchronous Rotating Vectors
authors: Ying Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In high-power settings, three-phase PWM rectifiers often have slow dynamic response and poor steady-state accuracy. This paper proposes a direct power control strategy using synchronous rotating vector principles to solve these issues. Traditional methods in renewable energy systems have flaws like big switching frequency variations and weak low-frequency harmonic suppression [1]. This paper adopts the synchronous rotating vector approach, using grid voltage as a reference to control input power. A PI controller and hysteresis comparator are added to improve transient response. Simulation shows it reduces switching losses, raises power factor, and ensures stability.

title: CAPred: An effective prediction strategy for allergy through machine intelligence
authors: T.Padmavathy, M.Thenmozhi, Subha K J
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: An allergy is a sudden response of the immune system that can happen when someone is exposed to allergens, including proteins, peptides, or chemicals. This work presents the development of an efficient approach called CAPred, which demonstrates enhanced precision in predicting chemicals having allergic potential. The training and testing datasets were gathered, and feature descriptors were chosen using a variance threshold and a correlation-based technique. Various machine-learning methods were employed to create a model with improved accuracy. The Decision Tree exhibited superior predictive performance, with a maximum accuracy of 88% and an AUC of 0.76 on the training datasets. The SHAP visualisation was employed to identify the predominant features contributing to the model's effective performance. The t-SNE technique effectively displayed the probabilistic features. To conclude, CAPred is an efficient prediction tool specifically designed for the identification of allergens. A web server might be created to employ the model for subsequent research endeavours.

title: A Visual Servoing Method for Harvesting Robots Based on Bytetrack and YOLOv11
authors: Zhonghua Miao, Qianlong Bu, Teng Sun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Addressing the limitations of traditional automated harvesting systems in complex field operations—such as dynamic target recognition accuracy, and tracking efficiency—this paper presents a vision-based servo system for tomato harvesting robots. The system employs the YOLOv11 algorithm to accurately detect tomato clusters and stems in complex environments, supported by a dedicated dataset to ensure generalization. Integrated with the ByteTrack algorithm, it achieves stable tracking of dynamic targets. A position-based visual servo (PBVS) module transforms visual data into motion commands through coordinate transformation and fuzzy PD control, enabling precise dynamic target picking. Experiments show that the system maintains high object tracking performance and grasping success across tomatoes of varying shapes, sizes, and maturity, demonstrating efficient dynamic response and offering a reliable solution for large-scale automated harvesting.

title: Smart Campus Safety and Energy Management System Using IoT
authors: R Prathipa, S Saranya, Varshini Selvakumar, I Poonguzhali, M Arun, Karishma Dillibabu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Educational facilities require a significant quantity of electrical power because they need to keep their lighting systems their ventilation systems and their safety systems operational at all times. The systems on many campuses require manual control which leads to energy wastage because classrooms and hallways remain empty. Security needs to be maintained during non-working hours because of energy waste which results from traditional monitoring systems unable to detect unauthorized entry and dangerous situations such as fire outbreaks. This study presents an IoT-enabled multi-sensor system for campus safety and energy management which delivers both energy efficiency solutions and essential safety requirements. The proposed system includes various sensors which consist of PIR sensors ultrasonic sensors LDR modules temperature sensors flame sensors and a real-time clock (RTC) module that connects to an ESP32 microcontroller. The system achieved better accuracy in detecting occupancy because it used multiple sensor inputs instead of relying on one sensor input. The system uses environmental data together with time data to control lighting and fan operations which helps to achieve optimal energy efficiency. The experimental results demonstrate that this approach effectively decreases unnecessary energy use while enhancing campus security throughout the entire campus area. The system which we developed offers cost-effective solutions that can be easily expanded for practical use in schools. The researchers examined the proposed system through tests conducted in environments that simulated classrooms and hallways using various operational conditions. The system achieved $90 \%$ occupancy detection accuracy with 355 ms fire detection response time and an estimated $58 \%$ reduction in device operating hours which can be deployed at low cost.

title: Spectral Data Processing of Centimeter-Wave Solar Radio Signals
authors: Sha Li, Yihua Yan, Linjie Chen, Chengming Tan, Zhichao Zhou, Wei Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In the decimeter and centimeter wavelength bands, solar radio bursts are typically generated by gyrosynchrotron radiation. Compared with the meter and ten-meter wavelength bands, the radiation source regions in the decimeter and centimeter bands are closer to or even located at the initial energy release regions of solar eruptions. A typical signal produced by gyrosynchrotron radiation exhibits an inverted "V" shape in its frequency spectrum, often reaching a frequency peak around 10 GHz. The corresponding data can provide crucial information for the study of solar eruptions. Based on the spectral subtraction method, work has been carried out on spectral data processing, optimization of time-frequency resolution, and accelerated computation of two-dimensional inverse Fourier transform.

title: AI-Driven EST Translation Teaching: A Human-AI Synergy Framework for New Engineering Talents
authors: Jing Xiao, Yong Luo
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Amid China’s "New Engineering" initiative and growing global technical communication needs, EST translation teaching demands enhanced efficiency and precision. This study proposes an AI-assisted teaching framework integrating core computing technologies, including NMT engines, context-aware terminology retrieval algorithms, and LLMs. Targeting bottlenecks in specialized translation instruction for engineering students, the framework combines these technologies with tailored pedagogical strategies. Experimental results indicate the model boosts translation accuracy by 26.5% and terminology standardization by 46.2% compared to traditional methods. This work provides a scalable technical solution for EST teaching optimization, offering empirical evidence for human-AI synergy in engineering-oriented language education.

title: Structured Latent Factor Decomposition in Deep Probabilistic State-Space Models for Interpretable Knowledge Tracing
authors: Tianbao Xue, Quanxiang Lan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Modeling student learning as a partially observable dynamical process is central to intelligent tutoring systems. Although recent neural knowledge tracing models achieve strong predictive performance, they typically rely on opaque latent representations that lack structural interpretability and calibrated uncertainty estimation. This paper introduces a Structured Cognitive State Modeling System (S-CSMS), a deep probabilistic state-space framework that explicitly decomposes the latent cognitive state into three semantically grounded components: skill mastery, misconception intensity, and transient learning condition factors. By embedding structured inductive bias into latent transition dynamics and leveraging amortized variational inference, the framework enables uncertainty-aware prediction while preserving cognitive interpretability. Experiments on the widely adopted ASSISTments benchmark demonstrate consistent improvements in negative log-likelihood, probability calibration, and predictive robustness over strong neural and probabilistic baselines. Furthermore, the structured decomposition supports fine-grained diagnostic analysis of mastery progression and misconception dynamics. These results highlight the effectiveness of integrating structured cognitive constraints into probabilistic state-space models for reliable and interpretable student modeling.

title: Distribution network Reconfiguration under multi objective using Barber’s optimization Algorithm: comparative study on IEEE Test Systems
authors: T.D. Sudhakar, K Ramya, M Umamageshwari
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: This manuscript suggests a methodology for Distribution Network Reconfiguration (DNR) employing the Barber Optimization Algorithm (BOA), a modern metaheuristic intuitive to barber-customer dynamics. The foremost aims are to lower real power losses, enhance voltage profiles, and keep network in radiality condition. However, most existing metaheuristics algorithms often suffer from premature convergence and scalability. However, BOA has good optimization capability but not explored in DNR problem. The BOA is assessed on IEEE 33-bus, 69-bus, and 118-bus distribution systems and checked with optimization procedures such as Grey Wolf, Fireworks, Ant Colony, Genetic Algorithm, Particle Swarm Optimization, Whale Optimization Algorithm, etc. The IEEE 33-bus system indicates that BOA lowers real power losses from 202.67 kW to 134.61 kW while bettering minimum bus voltage from 0.9131 p.u. to 0.9604 p.u., outdoing most methods and matching best reported values acquired by the Gravitational Search Algorithm. Furthermore, IEEE 69-bus and 118-bus systems, BOA achieves loss reductions, with a substantial voltage profile boost. Simulation results demonstrate that BOA achieves faster convergence and loss reduction compared to other algorithms. Additionally, BOA exhibits speedier convergence and effective adaptability compared to conventional metaheuristics, exhibiting its capability as a trustworthy and effective solution for the DNR problem. Unlike existing swarm and evolutionary approaches, BOA introduces a human-inspired service-queue interaction mechanism, enabling improved convergence stability for large-scale DNR problems.

title: Real Time Haptic Guidance for Obstacle Avoidance in Tele-Interaction
authors: Luuk B.L. Lenders, Nils Rublein, Douwe Dresscher
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: In this paper, we present a novel method for providing haptic guidance for obstacle avoidance to an operator of a tele-manipulation setup. The proposed method is based on the potential field approach without using inverse kinematics or (pseudo-)inverse Jacobians. The method is demonstrated in simulations to work in multiple scenarios and achieves update speeds between 825 and 1000Hz on the used hardware. The high update rate makes the method particularly suitable for usage as haptic guidance for obstacle avoidance and haptic guidance in tele-operated environments.

title: Space Design and Path Optimization for Intelligent Parking Systems Under Unit Layout
authors: Haozhou Yan, Shaolin Li, Zhengjie Peng
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of urbanization, the number of cars has also surged, making the problems of parking difficulties and limited parking spaces urgent issues for the public. To address this problem, a smart parking system based on a cell layout has been developed. This system utilizes a cell layout, eliminating fixed aisles and ineffective gaps, and is designed with corresponding intelligent path planning. An improved ant colony algorithm is used for optimizing path planning, enhancing the operational efficiency of the parking system, and its effectiveness has been verified through simulation.

title: Enhancing Semantic Code Search and Navigation Systems Using Dual Embedding Representations
authors: Shakthinee S, Shruthi Merine S, Kumari Deepika P
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Searching for relevant source code in large repositories is often difficult and the actual intent behind the developer’s query is not captured by traditional keyword-based methods because of the differences in naming conventions, programming languages, and coding styles. In order to overcome these problems, this paper introduces CodeSense, a semantic code search system letting developers to find code snippets using natural language queries by using dual-embedding method, which encodes source code and text queries separately using pretrained models into vector representations and are mapped into a shared vector space enabling meaningful semantic comparison between queries and code. These vector representations are stored in a vector database with HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World)-based indexing for fast and scalable retrieval, consistently outperforming traditional keyword-based methods. The proposed solution CodeSense achieves over 20% improvement in precision and recall while maintaining query latency below 250ms, highlighting the effectiveness of semantic embeddings in improving code search which in turn increases developer productivity.

title: Learning A Causation-driven Retrieval Model for Effective Time Series Augmentation and Forecasting
authors: Bohan Zhang, Dixin Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: For time series forecasting models, retrieving internal or external time series as auxiliary information may help enhance the models' predictive power and robustness when forecasting target time series. However, retrieving time series based on their observed parts often leads to unreliable predictions because of their natural randomness: the time series similar in the current time window may behave differently in the future. To overcome this issue, in this study, we propose a simple but effective causation-driven retrieval method, called CaRTS, for time series augmentation and forecasting. In particular, we propose to “retrieve causes from effect”, matching time series based on the similarities between their prediction parts. To overcome the inaccessibility of the prediction parts, we train a dual-tower retrieval model to encode the observed parts of matched time series, making the similarities between their latent representations inherit the similarities between their prediction parts. By leveraging this retrieval model, we can enhance the prediction model's performance by augmenting each target time series with retrieved data likely to exhibit similar future behaviors. To provide concrete evidence of its effectiveness, we rigorously evaluate the CaRTS method on real-world datasets. Experiments show that CaRTS is a promising approach to retrieving time series with high temporal correlations, leading to higher prediction accuracy, improving the model performance and robustness significantly.

title: Nonlinear Mechanism of Passive Intermodulation Power Jumps Induced by Mechanical Shocks
authors: Weiling Zhuang, Junyu Luo, Jinchun Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: To elucidate the mechanism behind the passive intermodulation (PIM) power jump phenomenon observed during dual-tone intermodulation (IM) testing, the work investigated the nonlinear transient behavior of contact interfaces induced by relative micro-movements. The concept of PIM power jump was formally introduced, accompanied by a time-domain transient analysis. A second-order parallel RLC circuit was used to fit the waveform of PIM power jumps. A time-domain model for PIM power jumps was theoretically developed by coupling mechanical dynamics, microscopic surface mechanics, and electrical contact theories. To support this model, an equivalent spring-mass system model of the contact interface, a statistical finite element model of micro-asperities, and an equivalent contact impedance model were established to predict the PIM power jumps. In addition, a series of PIM power jump observation experiments under different mechanical shocks was conducted with connectors of varying tightness. The predicted responses show strong agreement with experimental measurements, validating the accuracy and applicability of the proposed model. This analytical approach provides a solid theoretical foundation for mitigating detrimental PIM power jump effects in radio frequency (RF) systems.

title: CMNC-Net: A Cross-Modal Dual-Branch Network with Noise Awareness for Depth Completion in Transparent Scenes
authors: Baiyang Wang, Xujian Wang, Ming Fang, Xindi Wang, Hongjun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Depth sensing in scenes containing transparent or semi-transparent objects remains a challenging problem for vision-based measurement systems. Conventional depth sensors often produce incomplete measurements, noise contamination, and boundary distortions due to complex optical effects such as refraction and reflections, leading to increased measurement uncertainty and reduced reliability. To address these limitations, this paper proposes CMNC-Net, a Cross-Modality and Noise-aware Completion Network for robust depth reconstruction in transparent scenes. CMNC-Net adopts a lightweight dual-branch RGB–depth encoder–decoder architecture to jointly exploit complementary texture and geometric information across multiple scales. A cross-modality feature fusion module is designed to align RGB and depth representations at both spatial and semantic levels, enhancing structural consistency and shape fidelity. To explicitly handle measurement perturbations induced by transparency, a Reliability-Aware Noise Predictor is introduced to learn a latent reliability-aware noise prior from the input depth observations. Rather than directly regressing the ground-truth error map, this branch produces a scale-free auxiliary representation that is statistically associated with error-prone regions and is injected into the depth stream as reliability guidance. Furthermore, a residual attention refinement mechanism dynamically recalibrates feature responses to suppress specular noise and enhance valid geometric cues, enabling accurate boundary reconstruction. Extensive experiments on multiple transparent object datasets demonstrate that CMNC-Net achieves strong depth completion performance, with clear advantages on the TransCG benchmark and competitive generalization on ClearGrasp. These results indicate that the proposed approach provides a reliable and metrologically robust solution for vision-based 3D measurement in complex transparent environments.

title: Coupling Analysis and Quantization Among Control Loops of Grid-Following and Grid-Forming Inverters
authors: Hao Luo, Zhe Zhang, Yinxiao Zhu, Yi Xiao, Hao Ruan, Yongheng Yang, Frede Blaabjerg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Unlike the grid-following (GFL) inverter functioning as a current source, the grid-forming (GFM) inverter, as an independent voltage source, is an effective solution for mitigating potential instability associated with the increasing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) in the grid. However, the GFM inverter may introduce power oscillations due to the coupling among the control loops of parallel inverters and interactions with the grid. To analyze the coupling effect, several methods have been established to explore the complex interactions in a multi-inverter system. Nevertheless, recent strategies are either not comprehensive or incapable of handling parallel GFL and GFM inverter systems. Accordingly, in this paper, a dynamic relative gain array (DRGA)-based quantization strategy is proposed to analyze the coupling effect among different control loops with a full-order state-space model. Subsequently, the impact of grid strength, control parameters, line impedance and capacity ratios on the coupling is explored with the proposed strategy. It is revealed through the exploration that the grid strength, control parameters, line impedance, and the capacity ratio significantly affect the low-frequency interactions, especially under weak grid conditions. The efficacy of the proposed method and the accuracy of the analysis are verified through extensive experimental tests.

title: Analysis of a Half-Wave Dipole in Free and Half-Space Revisited: Analytical and Numerical Modeling vs. Measurements
authors: Josip Salinovic, Dragan Poljak, Zoran Blazevic, Maja Skiljo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper revisits the determination of the current distribution and input impedance of a half-wave dipole in free space and half-space by employing analytical and numerical methods based on image theory and the Fresnel reflection coefficient. An analytical solution for the input impedance in half-space was derived using an analytical approach based on a sinusoidal current distribution and a average reflection coefficient. By contrast, the numerical method computes the current distribution and input impedance by solving Pocklington’s integro-differential equation using the Galerkin–Bubnov scheme of the Indirect Boundary Element Method. Finally, the analytically calculated and numerically computed results of the input impedance were benchmarked against the NEC and FEKO software packages and compared with the measured values. This study demonstrates that integrating the analytical electric field with a sinusoidal current distribution accurately predicts input resistance in free space and half-space, with the experimental discrepancies below 15%. In conclusion, this analytical closed-form solution offers a practical alternative to computationally demanding numerical methods.

title: End-to-End 3D Spatiotemporal Perception with Multimodal Fusion and V2X Collaboration
authors: Zhenwei Yang, Yibo Ai, Weidong Zhang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Multiview cooperative perception and multimodal fusion are essential for reliable 3D spatiotemporal understanding in autonomous driving, especially in cases with occlusions, limited viewpoints, and communication delays in vehicle-to-everything (V2X) scenarios. In this paper, XET-V2X, a multimodal fused end-to-end tracking framework for V2X collaboration that unifies multiview multimodal sensing within a shared spatiotemporal representation, is proposed. To efficiently align heterogeneous viewpoints and modalities, XET-V2X introduces a dual-layer spatial cross-attention module based on multiscale deformable attention. Multiview image features are aggregated to enhance semantic consistency, followed by point cloud fusion guided by the updated spatial queries, enabling effective cross-modal interaction while reducing computational overhead. Experiments based on the real-world V2X-Seq-SPD dataset and the simulated V2X-Sim-V2V and V2X-Sim-V2I benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements in detection and tracking performance under varying communication delays, with XET-V2X achieving up to 15–20% relative gains in mAP and AMOTA over single-view or single-modal baselines, while also outperforming representative tracking-by-detection cooperative perception methods.

title: Enhanced Deconvolved Beamforming via Adaptive Subspace Separation to Improve Underwater Target Detection
authors: Zhang Haisong, Yan Shefeng, Song Xueqin
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Using sonar for underwater target detection holds importance for underwater sensing. In underwater target-detection scenarios, conventional beamforming (CBF) is commonly used to estimate the direction of arrival (DOA) of targets. And deconvolved CBF (DCBF) further enhances CBF’s angular resolution by deconvolution. However, DCBF suffers from severe resolution degradation at low signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs) due to insufficient output gain during beamforming in the element domain. To address this issue, this paper proposes an adaptive subspace separation DCBF (ASS-DCBF) to enhance DCBF performance under low SNRs. Firstly, an optimized source number estimation is employed to adaptively partition the signal and noise subspaces, thereby enhancing the accuracy of subspace separation under low SNRs. Secondly, the CBF is performed in the signal subspace domain rather than the element domain, consequently improving the input SNR of the CBF. Finally, deconvolution is applied to further enhance the output resolution of the sub-space domain CBF. Theoretical analysis and numerical simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm in maintaining resolution capabilities under even lower SNRs and snapshots. Furthermore, water tank experiments provide additional empirical validation of the algorithm’s practical efficacy.

title: Compact High-Temperature Superconducting Dual Ultra-Narrowband Bandpass Filter Using Spiral Stub-Loaded Resonators
authors: Baoping Ren, Guanfa Chen, Xuehui Guan, Haiwen Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: In this paper, a novel sixth-order high-temperature superconducting (HTS) dual ultra-narrowband (UNB) bandpass filter (BPF) using a miniaturized spiral stub-loaded resonator (SSLR) is proposed. By extending the upper loaded-stub of the resonator leftward and rightward, then bending them downward to partially enclose the lower stubs, the coupling between two adjacent resonators is effectively reduced. Lower stubs of the resonator adopt a spiral design, yielding a more compact resonator structure and further reducing the coupling between resonators. Two resonant frequencies of the proposed resonator can be independently tuned by stubs, while the coupling coefficients for the two passbands can be independently controlled by adjusting the length of the upper stubs and the distance between resonators. Additionally, external quality factor can also be independently tuned via the feeding structure. To validate the design approach, a sixth-order dual UNB HTS BPF was designed with center frequencies of 1.34 GHz and 1.69 GHz, corresponding fractional bandwidths of 0.25% and 0.22%, achieving a compact size of 0.165 λg × 0.088 λg. Measured results demonstrate good agreement with simulations, validating the feasibility of the proposed design.

title: Spectral characterization and performance of SPT-SLIM on-chip filterbank spectrometers
authors: C. S. Benson, K. Fichman, M. Adamic, A. J. Anderson, P. S. Barry, B. A. Benson, E. Brooks, J. E. Carlstrom, T. Cecil, C. L. Chang, K. R. Dibert, M. Dobbs, K. S. Karkare, G. K. Keating, A. M. Lapuente, M. Lisovenko, D. P. Marrone, J. Montgomery, T. Natoli, Z. Pan, A. Rahlin, G. Robson, M. Rouble, G. Smecher, V. Yefremenko, M. R. Young, C. Yu, J. A. Zebrowski, C. Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: The South Pole Telescope Shirokoff Line Intensity Mapper (SPT-SLIM) experiment is a pathfinder for demonstrating the use of on-chip spectrometers for millimeter (mm) Line Intensity Mapping (LIM). We present spectral bandpass measurements of the SPT-SLIM spectrometer channels made on site using a Fourier Transform Spectrometer during the first deployment of SPT-SLIM in the 2024-2025 Austral summer season. We note the effect of FTS systematics on measurements of on-chip filterbank spectrometer resolutions and demonstrate a technique for measuring the narrow band passes of the SPT-SLIM filterbanks that improves beyond the intrinsic resolution of a Fourier Transform Spectrometer.
