title: Leveraging Text-Modulated Semantic Guidance for Low-Light Endoscopic Image Enhancement
authors: Hong Wang, Zhijian Wu, Haodu Fang, Dong Wei, Jinghan Sun, Yefeng Zheng, Jianhua Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Low light conditions in endoscopic imaging would lead to poor visibility, reduced contrast, and increased noise, which may hinder accurate diagnosis and surgical guidance. Against this low-light endoscopic image enhancement (LLEIE) task, inspired by the remarkable performance of pretrained CLIP in downstream vision tasks, in this paper, we carefully investigate the pretrained priors of CLIP and embed them into a text-modulated semanticaware discriminator (TMSD). Through the adversarial learning mechanism, the discriminator can be easily integrated into different low-light enhancement baselines for helping them accomplish better visual restoration effects without incurring any extra inference cost. Specifically, to make the foundation model CLIP suitable for the LLEIE task, we initially propose a prompt learning procedure to obtain the text embedding and image semantics corresponding to the normal-light endoscopic imaging scenario. Building upon the acquired text prior and image semantic priors, we devise a text modulator to synergize these two priors, yielding a richer semantic representation. Leveraging the convolutional modulation and cross-attention mechanisms, we blend this semantic guidance information into the discriminator, thereby fostering the fine-grained distribution learning of normal-light endoscopic images in visual semantics and guiding different enhancement baselines achieving higher visual quality. Based on five public benchmark datasets, including three synthetic datasets, one real clinical dataset, and one clinical downstream segmentation dataset, we comprehensively evaluate the effectiveness of our proposed TMSD. Extensive experiments substantiate that the integration of the proposed TMSD enables seven representative baselines to obtain better perceptual quality, especially in the cross-domain clinical generalization scenario. Besides, the downstream segmentation accuracy can be evidently improved, showing the favorable application potential of the proposed TMSD. Moreover, to comprehensively evaluate the generality of our TMSD framework, we successfully apply it to a new and classic metal artifact reduction task. It is worth mentioning that our TMSD does not incur any extra computational cost during inference.

title: Joint Optimization of Model Partitioning and Resource Allocation for Anti-Jamming Collaborative Inference Systems
authors: Mengru Wu, Jiawei Li, Jiaqi Wei, Bin Lyu, Kai-Kit Wong, Hyundong Shin
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: With the increasing demands of deep neural network (DNN) inference on resource-constrained devices, DNN partitioning-based device-edge collaborative inference has emerged as a promising paradigm. However, the transmission of intermediate feature data is vulnerable to malicious jamming, which significantly degrades inference performance. To counter this threat, this letter focuses on an anti-jamming collaborative inference system in the presence of a malicious jammer. In this system, a DNN model is partitioned into two distinct segments, which are executed by each wireless device and an edge server, respectively. We first analyze the effects of DNN partitioning and jamming on inference accuracy via data regression. Based on this, our objective is to maximize the system’s revenue of delay and accuracy (RDA) by jointly optimizing computation resource allocation, each device’s transmit power, and DNN partitioning. To address the mixed-integer nonlinear programming problem, we propose an efficient alternating optimization algorithm, which decomposes the problem into three subproblems that are solved via Karush-Kuhn-Tucker conditions, convex optimization methods, and a quantum genetic algorithm, respectively. Extensive simulations demonstrate that our proposed scheme outperforms baselines in terms of RDA.

title: A Multimodal Learning Framework for Detecting Systemic Hypertension in Sleep Apnea Patients Using ECG and PPG Signals
authors: Hisham ElMoaqet, Rami Janini, Mutaz Ryalat, Ghaith Al-Refai, Matthew Salanitro, Martin Glos, Thomas Penzel
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Systemic hypertension (HTN) is a major cardiovascular comorbidity in patients with obstructive sleep apnea (OSA), yet these conditions are often diagnosed and managed independently, limiting integrated cardiovascular risk assessment. There is a significant gap in scalable automated frameworks for the screening of hypertension using routinely collected polysomnography (PSG) signals. To address this, we introduce FusionNet, a novel deep learning architecture to detect established HTN in patients with OSA. The proposed framework leverages a Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) approach to capture long-term temporal dynamics from overnight PSG recordings. It integrates electrocardiogram (ECG) and photoplethysmogram (PPG) signals through a gated fusion mechanism for robust multimodal analysis, further enriched by frequency-domain heart and pulse rate variability (HRV/PRV) and clinical context features, including sleep stages and apnea events. The model was evaluated using Leave-One-Patient-Out (LOPO) cross-validation on a clinically annotated dataset of 33 patients to ensure subject-independent generalizability. FusionNet achieved an AUROC of 87.34%, accuracy of 79.03%, specificity of 81.25%, and an MCC of 0.5801, indicating a balanced and clinically conservative detection profile through adaptive integration of complementary cardiac and vascular information. This work establishes a proof-of-concept for a dual diagnostic paradigm, demonstrating the feasibility of deriving hypertension-related cardiovascular signatures from standard sleep studies. These findings present an initial step towards automated cardiovascular risk assessment and require validation in larger independent cohorts prior to clinical deployment.

title: Developer Challenges in the Adoption of Model Context Protocol in AI Agent Development
authors: Oluwatoyosi Oyelayo, Samuel Abedu, SayedHassan Khatoonabadi, Emad Shihab
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 7th International Workshop on Bots and Agents in Software Engineering
abstract: None

title: Identifying At-Risk Learners in Engineering Education: A Self-Regulated Learning Perspective Based on Digital Traces
authors: Ning Zhao, Qin Fu
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: In the context of digital transformation in engineering education, precisely identifying at-risk learners through learning analytics has become essential for enhancing teaching quality. Based on Self-Regulated Learning (SRL) theory, this study extracts a six-dimensional feature vector from students' online behavioral traces. Using the K-Means algorithm, four distinct strategic prototypes were identified. This paper specifically focuses on the "Effortful Low-Achiever" and "Surface Participant" profiles to develop an early-warning model based on centroid distance. Results indicate that the model effectively identifies students with potential academic risks during the midterm stage, providing an empirical foundation for precision intervention.

title: Exploring the Needs and Perspectives of Chinese Special Education Teachers on Digital Learning
authors: Yubin Zhang, Samiullah Paracha
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This article uses qualitative research methods to explore Chinese special education teachers’ perceptions, experiences and expectations of digital learning. Through in-depth interviews and theme analysis of seven special education teachers, the study found that while affirming that digital technology improves classroom efficiency and student participation, teachers always uphold the humanistic education concept with relationships, emotions and ethics as the core. The study further reveals the structural obstacles faced by the digitalization of special education, including insufficient hardware and resources, a lack of institutional support, faults in the training system, and limited technological inclusion. Teachers show strong reflection and initiative in practice, and respond to challenges by making their own courseware and building sharing mechanisms. The study points out that the key to the success of digital education lies in building an educational ecosystem based on teachers’ professional wisdom and oriented to educational equity.

title: Research on the Construction and Application of an AI-Based Personalized Intervention Model for Early Childhood Games
authors: Rui Ren
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This paper addresses issues in traditional early childhood game interventions — such as subjective observation, inefficient recording, and delayed intervention—by constructing an AI-based personalized intervention model. It aims to drive the educational paradigm shift from "experience-driven" to "data and intelligence dual-driven" through human-machine collaboration. Integrating techniques from the Zone of Proximal Development, adaptive learning, multimodal integration, and deep learning-based behavior recognition, the study establishes a comprehensive four-layer framework encompassing data acquisition, intelligent analysis, intervention decision, and application service. This model employs multimodal perception networks to capture real-time behavioral data, utilizes deep learning algorithms for contextual analysis and profiling, and integrates expert rules with reinforcement learning to generate personalized intervention strategies. Through case analyses across four dimensions—environment and materials, interaction and guidance, evaluation and reflection, and home-school collaboration — the study validated the model’s effectiveness in enhancing teacher observation objectivity, intervention precision, and support for children’s personalized development. It provides a viable technical pathway and practical example for fostering personalized development in early childhood education.

title: LLM-Guided Gym Exercise Feedback from Video: A Feasibility Study
authors: Samet Ucuncu, Haibin Cai, Hui Fang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Automating gym exercise self-assessment has the potential to significantly enhance routine coaching for professional athletes, ultimately improving their performance. In this paper, we present a feasibility study towards this goal by combining 3D body reconstruction, temporal data processing, and LLMbased reasoning into a unified, end-to-end solution. Using a monocular video of a bench press as input, we leverage a state-of-the-art 3D human reconstruction model and propose to extract joint-level kinematic descriptors, including elbow range of motion, inter-limb symmetry, head stability, and repetition tempo, for action quality assessment. To address frame-to-frame reconstruction inconsistencies, we design a lightweight temporal refinement module that generates more stable and reliable trajectory data for movement repetition analysis. Once these structured biomechanical metrics are obtained, they enable a Large Language Model (LLM) to provide insightful coaching feedback for subsequent training. Experiments conducted on a collected gym dataset demonstrate reliable repetition segmentation, consistent kinematic estimation, and effective translation of geometry-derived features into actionable language feedback. The study establishes the feasibility of geometry-driven, temporally consistent, and explainable action quality assessment for realworld automated coaching systems.

title: An Improved YOLOv11-seg Underwater Net Cage State Instance Segmentation Method with Dual Attention Mechanism Fusion
authors: Nan Yu, Wenlin Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Deep-sea cage aquaculture is an important direction for the transformation of China's marine fishery towards intensification and intelligence. As a core component of the aquaculture facilities, the netting is constantly exposed to complex underwater environments, which makes it prone to biofouling and physical damage, directly affecting the safety of aquaculture and economic benefits. Traditional manual inspection methods have problems such as low efficiency, high risk, and strong subjectivity. With the maturity of remote-controlled underwater robot technology, the use of visual systems for automated inspection has become an inevitable trend. However, the underwater environment poses challenges such as light attenuation, turbid water, and interference from suspended matter, which makes the detection of netting conditions based on vision face problems such as difficult feature extraction, easy loss of small targets, and high real-time requirements for models. In response to these issues, this study proposes an improved instance segmentation model. This model takes YOLOv11-seg as the base architecture, introduces the receptive field attention convolution (RFAConv) module in the backbone network to enhance the extraction ability of blurred and edge features; uses the parallel patch-aware attention (PPA) module in the feature fusion layer to retain multi-scale defect information; and significantly improves detection accuracy by optimizing the model structure. The experimental results show that the improved model achieves a mean average precision (mAP)@0.5 of 96.7% for Mask on the self-built dataset, which is nearly 6 percentage points higher than that of YOLOv11-seg. The single-frame inference time meets the real-time requirements, providing an effective technical solution for the visual perception system of the deep-sea cage cleaning and inspection robot.

title: IoT-Enabled Bioenergy Generation from Food Waste for Sustainable Power Systems
authors: V Sathyajith, H Kathir, R Cibiraj, S Lokeshwaran, L. Hubert Mary, T Karthika
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: The growing need to have smart and clean bioenergy conversion and the need to handle food waste effectively require smart bioenergy conversion technologies. Food waste has great potential for high organic content in decentralized electricity generation, but traditional bioenergy systems have the disadvantages of unstable processes and a lack of system control. To overcome these issues, a bioenergy generation scheme based on IoT is designed, encompassing real-time monitoring, intelligent control, and maximized anaerobic digestion to generate electricity. The system utilizes constant control over biochemical and electrical values to control the conditions of digestion and energy conversion processes. Experimental analysis shows that the rate of biogas generation is $218 \mathrm{~m}^{3} /$ day with a methane content of 62.7%, which gives a specific energy production of $1.34 \mathrm{kWh} / \mathrm{kg}$ of food waste. The energy conversion efficiency is 41.6%, the voltage deviation is minimized to $\pm 2.1 \mathrm{~V}$, and system uptime is 99.1%. Relative comparison ascertains better waste adaptability, shorter latency of control, and a carbon offset efficacy of 78.9%. These results confirm the usefulness of bioenergy systems that use IoT to generate sustainable power.

title:
Trialing telepresence robots as part of a volunteer support scheme to encourage social interaction and physical activity
                    <sup>*</sup>
authors: Praminda Caleb-Solly, Kirsty Brown, Richard Solly, Karen Lloyd
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: More than one-in-three people over-65 have a fall, resulting in a fear of falling, leading to more inactivity and frailty, with greater risk of social isolation. Resources for support in the country are scarce. Telepresence robots are an emerging technology which enables two-way video communication, without the older adult needing to set-up any software or have any digital skills. This paper reports on a trial in the voluntary sector to enhance social communication and encourage physical activity. The main objective of this study was to better understand the barriers, challenges and opportunities for using telepresence robots as part of volunteer support scheme to encourage social interaction and increase physical activity to address frailty. Its secondary objective was to iteratively design materials and protocols that could be used to set up a future sustainable framework to address the acute staff shortages in the health and social care sector which is currently leaving people socially isolated and without any human support to encourage engagement with physical activity. Volunteers recruited and screened via Age UK Bristol’s volunteer service were paired up with an older person to engage in regular social interactions via a telepresence robot situated in the older person’s home over a 6-week period, two to three times a week. The volunteers were trained on using the telepresence robot, and how best to safely guide and encourage the older person they were buddied-up with through a personalized physical activity exercise plan developed by a fitness instructor. Pre-, during and post-assessments of the person in the home, as well as usability and user-experience evaluations were conducted to evaluate the effectiveness of the scheme, as well as refine the materials and protocols. The assessments show small reductions in physical frailty, and improved confidence in the older person to begin to socialize outside their home, as well as increased digital literacy for volunteers and older adults. The findings provide experiential insights into emotional, logistic, and technical challenges, together with a deployment framework, including protocols and materials to support larger studies in community settings.

title: Application of an AI-Assisted Gait Detection System for Improving Children’s Sports Interest in Preschool Physical Activity
authors: Min Feng, Shu Hong, Liqian Liang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study aimed to examine the effectiveness of an AI-Assisted Gait Detection System integrated into preschool physical activity curricula in improving children’s sports interest. A smart insole–based system with multi-dimensional plantar pressure sensors and real-time AI feedback was developed and embedded into a 16-week physical activity intervention for 100 senior kindergarten children in Shanghai, using a quasi-experimental pretest–posttest control group design. The experimental group participated in AI-assisted physical activity sessions with real-time gait monitoring and corrective guidance, while the control group followed a conventional curriculum. Results indicated no significant baseline differences between groups, whereas post-test findings showed that the experimental group demonstrated significantly higher sports participation, positive interest, and attention to physical activity, along with significantly lower negative interest compared to the control group. Within-group analyses further confirmed significant improvements only in the experimental group. Overall, the study provides empirical evidence that integrating AI-assisted gait detection technology into preschool physical education offers a data-driven and developmentally appropriate approach to promoting sports interest and active participation in early childhood.

title: Nighttime pedestrian re-identification method based on high-frequency component reproduction
authors: Xinyang Wang, Yaling Liang
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: Due to issues such as insufficient lighting and uneven distribution of light sources, nighttime pedestrian images contain more noise and are more blurred, making nighttime person re-identification more challenging. To address this problem, we propose a novel nighttime person re-identification network called the High-Frequency Reproduction Vision Transformer (HFRViT). First, we employ discrete wavelet transform to extract high-frequency components of the image, which are then superimposed onto the original image for enhancement. Second, we utilize a ViT-based feature extraction network and extract features from the second network layer of ViT, which is rich in high-frequency components. These features are fused with the output features from the final layer to further enhance the high-frequency characteristics of pedestrians. Moreover, the proposed module does not introduce any additional parameters. Extensive experiments have validated the effectiveness of our method.

title: Inclusive Education through the integration of 3D Animation Technology in Teaching
authors: Víctor Manuel Zamudio García, Glendamira Serrano Franco
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: 3D animation has emerged as an innovative technological solution to facilitate the learning of Mexican Sign Language (LSM). This technology enables precise visualization of the characteristic movements of LSM, improving communication and supporting the development of accessible educational tools. Along with other technologies such as mobile applications, 3D animation represents an alternative to overcome the limited availability of adequate educational resources, a factor that contributes to the educational lag among deaf children. The main objective of this study was to analyze users’ perceptions of 3D-based applications for learning LSM and their impact on sign language acquisition. Specifically, the study evaluated the most valued features by users, such as interactivity, clarity of educational content, and perceived ease of learning, as well as the relationship between these variables. The research followed an analytical methodological approach with descriptive and correlational components. The sample consisted of 120 participants, including both deaf and hearing individuals. Data were collected through a structured questionnaire with multiple-choice questions designed to measure the variables of interest. Results indicate a positive perception of 3D-based applications for learning LSM. Additionally, a statistically significant correlation (r = 0.669, p = 0.031) was found between users’ perception of these applications and their impact on sign language learning, highlighting the importance of interactive and accessible technological resources.

title: IoT-Enabled Automated Building Integrated Agrivoltaics (BIAV) : A Symbiotic System for the Built Environment
authors: Hariram N P, Mohd Fairusham Ghazali, Sumaiya Bt Zainal Abidin, Nadzirah Mohd Mokhtar, Vidya Chandran, K. Sudhakar
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The increasing demand for smart sustainable urban buildings urges novice solutions that address the nexus of food, energy and water. Building Integrated Agrivoltaics (BIAV) represents a transformative symbiotic approach, merging photovoltaic energy generation with agricultural production within the built environment. This conceptual paper introduces an IoT-enabled automated BIAV system concept, enhanced through artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) enabling real-time monitoring, adaptive control, and predictive optimization of agrivoltaic operations to maximize energy and food yield by facilitating decentralized energy production, localized food systems, and efficient resource use. The primary objective of the study is to conceptualize an automated BIAV framework where sensors monitor real-time parameters. IoT-enabled sensors collect environmental and operational data such as solar irradiance, soil moisture, crop health, water level, temperature, humidity, nutrient level, and microclimate conditions, which are processed using AI/ML algorithms. AI / ML algorithms process this data for predictive analytics, enabling dynamic adjustments like photovoltaic panel tilting, shading modulation, and automated irrigation through actuators, to optimize irrigation, shading, nutrient delivery, and energy utilization, thereby maximizing energy yield while enhancing crop productivity under partial shading. The study go through BIAV System perspectives and basic principles and typologies like Building Integrated - Agrivoltaics, Rangevoltaics, Aquavoltaics, Algaevoltaics, Seaweedvoltaics and Apivoltaics, then further discussed structural Integration of BIAV on to buildings. BIAV system can be integrated in vertical facades vertical agrivoltaics, rooftop agrivoltaic greenhouses, atrium-integrated Agrivoltaics; and balcony/pergola and sunshade systems for utilizing maximum built-up area and analyzed in terms of spatial adaptability, energy efficiency, and crop compatibility within urban residential and commercial structures. By enabling closed-loop resource flows, rainwater harvesting, including wastewater reuse, bio-waste to biogas conversion and material upcycling this symbiotic BIAV system reduces external resource dependence, lowers carbon emissions, mitigates urban heat islands, enhances resilience, climate adaptability and supports SDG as well as net-zero goals through decentralized, smart and regenerative integration of agriculture, energy and building.

title: An Area-Efficient S-Box Using Transmission Gate Logic
authors: Arulmurugan Azhaganantham, S.Bhavadharini
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Cryptographic hardware with high performance, low silicon area, and low power consumption is in high demand due to the rapid expansion of secure communication systems. The Substitution Box or S Box is a crucial component that introduces nonlinearity and strengthens resistance against cryptanalytic attacks in the widely used symmetric encryption algorithm known as the Advanced Encryption Standard. However, traditional lookup table based S Box implementations suffer from large memory requirements, high power dissipation, and increased area overhead. This work proposes an area efficient S Box circuit utilizing Transmission Gate logic based on Composite Field Arithmetic to overcome these limitations. Logic complexity is reduced by decomposing the inversion operation into simpler operations. Cadence Virtuoso was used to design and simulate the proposed architecture in CMOS technology. Simulation results show that the Transmission Gate based S Box uses fewer transistors than conventional CMOS and lookup table based architectures, making it suitable for lightweight cryptography and Internet of Things applications.

title: Lightweight Underwater Acoustic Target Recognition Method Based on Multi-Scale Feature Fusion
authors: Jiaru Chen, Jian Hu, Rongyao Zhao, Yaotian Shi, Tongsheng Shen, Jia Liu, Chengpeng Hao, Feng Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the challenges of hydroacoustic target recognition in complex marine noise environments under constrained computational resources, this paper presents a lightweight hydroacoustic target recognition method using Multi-Scale Feature Fusion to overcome complex noise and computational constraints in marine environments. Log-Mel spectrograms are extracted from audio, and a parallel multi-scale convolutional network captures diverse frequency features. With depthwise separable convolutions and label smoothing, the model reduces parameters by 96.8% (to 0.35M) and computational load from 1.82 to 0.71 GFLOPs, while achieving 91.36% accuracy on DeepShip. The approach enables efficient and accurate recognition suitable for real-time underwater monitoring platforms.

title: Refining L2 Learner Agency through Multi-role Prompting: A Coordination Framework for AI-Mediated Writing Peer Feedback
authors: Jie Pan, Yanan Hu, Huimei Chen
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: While AI feedback has become common in writing instruction, its typically authoritative and prescriptive nature often stifles learner agency. To address this, our study introduces a multi-role prompting framework that shifts students from passive recipients to active decision-makers. Instead of relying on a single, homogenized AI voice, the system coordinates simulated personas—such as specialists in logic, language, and cohesion—to provide diverse perspectives. Crucially, the design does not aim for consensus; instead, it organizes disagreements into visible options by surfacing rhetorical trade-offs and "cognitive frictions" that require students to evaluate competing suggestions. Qualitative interviews with six college students suggest that this approach transforms AI from a directive "corrector" into a mediator of critical inquiry. By implementing strict interaction boundaries that prevent automated rewrites, the system keeps the intellectual work of interpretation and synthesis with the student. The findings support the pedagogical value of persona-driven prompt orchestration in scaffolding metacognitive engagement and helping learners with different proficiency levels make more intentional revision choices in AI-supported environments.

title: Lightweight Multi-Class Vehicle Detection for Urban Road Scenes: Incorporating SE Channel Attention and ASPP Context Enhancement into YOLOv8n
authors: Yilun Luo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the challenges of multi-class vehicle detection in urban traffic scenarios—specifically high visual similarity between categories, large scale variations, and complex occlusions—this paper proposes a lightweight improved model: YOLOv8n+SE+ASPP. While maintaining compatibility with the original framework, we innovatively incorporate the Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) channel attention mechanism to enhance feature discrimination and introduce a parallel Atrous Spatial Pyramid Pooling (ASPP) module to aggregate multi-scale contextual information. Experimental results on a custom urban traffic dataset show that the proposed model achieves an mAP@0.5 of 0.977 and a peak F1 score of 0.96. Compared to the baseline, it significantly reduces the inter-class false detection rate between similar vehicles (e.g., cars and light trucks) while keeping computational overhead low. The model balances high accuracy, environmental robustness, and real-time performance, making it suitable for edge deployment.

title: Privacy-Preserving Media Sharing with Scalable Access Control and Secure De Duplication in Mobile Cloud Computing
authors: Harish Chamarthi, Konet Sai Dheeraj
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The rapid growth of mobile-shared media in cloud environments requires a unified system of privacy protection, access control and data storage. The demand of such requisites by the previous methodologies has been done separately, thus creating operational inefficiencies and creating critical gaps in security. The current work postulates a unified framework that integrates attribute-based access control schemes that are scaled with ownership-sensitive secure de duplication algorithms suited in the mobile cloud environment. Client-side encryption is used to assure data confidentiality; fine-grained access control is implemented independently, without having to rely on trusted third parties; storage redundancy is eliminated with cryptographic proof-of-ownership protocols. The new query-fragment caching scheme is proposed, which is successful in terms of minimizing access latency on mobile end-users. Empirical security evaluations as well as simulation research reveal that the framework proposed would reduce computational overhead by about 30 % and communication expenses by about 25 % compared to the benchmark systems, and prevent collusion and brute force attacks, thereby providing an optimal trade-off between privacy, operational efficiency, and scalability.

title: AI-Powered Wearable for Fatigue and Health Risk Detection
authors: S. Dilip Kumar, V.S. Swathi Mirra, B. Vishwa, R Vishwa
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Rescue workers work in dangerous and chemically polluted areas, so they tend to experience physical overload, which puts them at risk of fatigue and poor health. Real-time and continuous monitoring of physiological parameters is thus required in an attempt to improve the safety of responders and to help them intervene in time. The current paper describes a wearable device based on an embedded platform of ESP32 to monitor fatigue and health risks through AI. The suggested system has a combination of various physiological sensors such as heart rate, blood oxygen saturation (SpO 2), galvanic skin response (GSR), respiratory rate, temperature, and pressure sensors, which constantly check the physical state of the wearer. The sensor data are delivered wirelessly to the ThingSpeak cloud system to store, visualize and access the data remotely. The data-driven approach of fatigue assessment is a lightweight method used to evaluate the physiological trends and identify the abnormal patterns related to fatigue and possible health hazards. Under artificial operating conditions, experimental validation of the system will show that it can effectively monitor physiological variations and provide timely alerts. The proposed solution provides highly scalable, economical, and resources-saving framework to enhance safety, situational awareness during rescue and industrial monitoring and disaster management applications.

title: InsulatorDefect-Net: A Deep Learning-Based Algorithm for Insulator Defect Detection
authors: Yudan Zhu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Insulators operating in outdoor environments are vulnerable to defects such as cracks, breakage, contamination, and aging, which may cause line faults if not detected in time. However, defect detection in transmission line inspection is challenging due to small target size and complex backgrounds. To address these issues, this paper proposes InsulatorDefect-Net, an improved YOLOv12 (You Only Look Once)-based network for insulator defect detection. The model integrates a Multi-Scale Insulator Feature Fusion (MSIF) module to enhance small-object perception, a Transmission-Line Context Attention (TLCA) mechanism to suppress background interference, and a Fine-grained Defect Representation Enhancer (FDRE) to improve subtle defect representation. Experiments on a public dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves 97.2% mAP while maintaining real-time performance, confirming its effectiveness for complex transmission line inspection scenarios.

title: Wide Load Range LDO Design
authors: Tairan Ning, Aiying Guo
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To power display driver circuits, low-dropout regulators (LDOs) must achieve high load capacity, fast stabilization, and resistance to power supply disturbances. Therefore, a high-stability, high-load LDO was designed using the HL55nm CMOS process. This LDO design incorporates a bandgap reference circuit, an error amplifier, and a transient enhancement circuit. The transient enhancement circuit improves tolerance for high-current loads by introducing a novel feedback loop. The main loop of the error amplifier employs Miller capacitance and multiple compensation methods for frequency compensation, ensuring circuit stability under full-load conditions. Simulation results indicate that with a load capacitance of 3μF, when the output voltage is 1.2V and the load current transitions between 10mA and 2A within 1μs, the overshoot and undershoot voltages are 12.8mV and 11.3mV respectively. Recovery time is approximately 6μs for both transitions. The linear regulation is approximately 14.6 μV/V, the load regulation is about 32 μV/A, and the power supply rejection ratio at 1 kHz is approximately -77 dB.

title: Predictive Modeling of Solar Irradiance Under PM2.5 Pollution and AQI Variations for Energy Yield Forecasting in PV Systems
authors: Muhammad Yousuf Irfan Zia, Rukaiya Rukaiya, Rabia Siddiqui, Syeda Iqra Hassan
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: The solar photovoltaic (PV) technology offers a sustainable way of producing clean power. However, its performance is subtle to atmospheric conditions, particularly air quality, which can lower solar irradiance through light absorption and scattering. In this study, we examine the tradeoff between the Air Quality Index (AQI) and solar irradiance to quantify its impact on potential PV power output. To do this, we used an AirLink air quality monitor and a Vantage Pro2 Plus weather station to collect real-time measurements of solar radiation, PM2.5 levels, and overall AQI. We considered periods of constant peak irradiance to guarantee data dependability and insightful comparison. The findings showed a strong inverse correlation between solar irradiance and AQI, implying that increased pollution levels significantly lower the amount of available solar energy. We used a predictive model to find trends and irradiance under various polluted conditions. The importance of including AQI in PV forecasting systems was validated by the model’s high accuracy in predicting irradiance attenuation based on air quality trends. This study benefits the scientific community by integrating environmental science and renewable energy modelling for sustainable energy planning, reliable deployment, and optimization of solar infrastructure in polluted atmospheres. It lays the basis for pollution-aware energy forecasting, particularly in Urban areas of Pakistani and other developing settings.

title: A Robust Visual Intelligence System for Intelligent and Connected Vehicle Manufacturing
authors: Ziyan Hui, Chuanwei Duanmu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: With the rapid development of China's intelligent and connected vehicle (ICV) industry, the manufacturing process faces unprecedented pressures in terms of efficiency, quality, flexibility, and safety. Currently, numerous manufacturing stages still rely on manual visual inspection or traditional vision equipment with insufficient anti-interference capability, making it difficult to achieve millimeter-level precision. Concurrently, tasks such as equipment patrol inspection, instrument monitoring, and material counting remain largely manual, leading to low efficiency and safety hazards. To address these challenges, this paper proposes and constructs a robust visual intelligence system tailored for ICV manufacturing scenarios. The system first realizes a full-process closed-loop for automated production line quality inspection through a “Perception-Decision-Evolution” framework: it utilizes multi-source signals (PLC/Encoder) for precise image acquisition triggering, enhances stability under dynamic interferences (e.g., strong light, vibration) and fine defect recognition capability via a cascaded AI analysis engine incorporating physical models, and achieves millisecond-level quality risk control through real-time PLC linkage and structured alarm feedback. Secondly, this study preliminarily explores efficient 3D scene construction methods suitable for complex workshop environments via drone-simulated inspection, laying the groundwork for semantic environmental understanding in future intelligent patrol robots. Experiments are conducted on a self-built real-world industrial dataset containing over 90,000 images across 8 defect categories from 20 inspection stations in a welding workshop. Results demonstrate that the proposed detection system achieves a mean Average Precision mAP@0.5) of 97.1% and a Robustness Retention Rate (RR) as high as 96.1% under strong interference, significantly outperforming mainstream methods. Meanwhile, the 3D reconstruction also shows advantages in efficiency and completeness over traditional solutions. This research provides key technological support and a feasible pathway for the intelligent manufacturing vision upgrade from “fixed-point high-precision detection” to “mobile-area wide-range inspection”.

title: Res2Net-MMF: A Multi-scale Attention Network for Traffic Scene Classification
authors: Dongnan Zhang, Hailong Chen, Jinghan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Traffic scene classification is crucial for intelligent transportation systems but faces challenges due to scale variations and complex environments. This paper proposes Res2Net-MMF, a multi-scale attention network for traffic scene classification. The core innovation is a hierarchical progressive attention distribution strategy, which integrates three complementary lightweight modules—Dual Stream Attention (DSA), Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement Block (MSFEB), and Spatial-Frequency Domain Attention Module (SFDAM)—into different stages of a Res2Net backbone. This design forms a progressive feature enhancement pathway from local detail perception to global semantic understanding. Experiments on the public Traffic-Net dataset demonstrate that Res2Net-MMF achieves 96.63% accuracy with only 31.2M parameters, outperforming baseline Res2Net by 1.88% and other state-of-the-art models. Ablation studies validate the effectiveness of the proposed hierarchical strategy.

title: EOGRelax: A Dual Action Wearable Platform for Electrooculographic Stress Sensing and Instant Relief via Cooling
authors: Anum Rashid, Aliza Ranash, Aamnah Sultan, Rania Khalid, Zia Mohy-Ud Din
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: EOGRelax is an innovative biomedical feedback system for determining and classifying the degrees of the stress according to the Electrooculography (EOG) measurements recorded through the eye band. The system records horizontal and vertical eye tracking to monitor eye tracking patterns in terms of stress such as increased saccades or blinking. The processed EOGs are wirelessly transmitted to the Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) technology and displayed in a mobile application which displays real-time metrics and stress statuses. Once stress is detected, the system establishes a cooling effect through application of a neck band made of thermoelectric to the cervical region to instigate direct cooling to provide immediate relief and correct the body state. It is a combination of a non-invasive biosignal acquisition, mobile monitoring and therapeutic feedback that offers a wearable and compact stress management and can be implemented in the field of human- computer interaction, workplace health and in health care.

title: EMA-WIoU-YOLO11: Efficient Multi-Scale Attention and Geometry-Aware Regression for Public-Space Obstacle Detection
authors: Bocen Liu, Chunyang Fang, Yuang Zhang, Weiya Tong, Xiaoqin Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Reliable detection of diverse obstacles in unstructured public spaces is critical for assistive navigation systems serving visually impaired individuals and autonomous platforms. Existing approaches face three key limitations: (1) vanilla single-scale attention mechanisms fail to discriminate visually similar obstacle pairs under varying illumination and occlusion; (2) standard IoU-based regression losses assign equal gradient weight to geometrically inferior proposals, degrading localisation in crowded scenes; (3) prior benchmark models are validated on narrow-category datasets that do not reflect the fine-grained 22-class diversity encountered in real-world urban environments. To address these challenges, we propose EMA-WIoU-YOLO11, an enhanced detection framework built upon the YOLO11n backbone. Our key innovations are threefold: (1) we embed an Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA) module after the final C3k2 backbone stage to jointly capture global channel statistics and local spatial context with negligible parameter overhead; (2) we replace the standard box regression loss with the Wise-IoU (WIoU) formulation, which introduces a sample-wise focusing weight to suppress geometrically inferior anchors; (3) we provide the first systematic evaluation on the 22-class Dist-YOLO Public-Space Obstacle dataset comprising 3,350 annotated images. Experiments demonstrate that our model achieves mAP@0.5=0.849, precision=0.90, and recall=0.80, with peak F1=0.81 at a confidence threshold of 0.374, confirming the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed framework.

title: AI-Assisted Electromagnetic Radiation Reconfigurable Antennas for Energy-Efficient and Green 6G Wireless Networks: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Meryem Hamdi, Emna Jebabli, Mohamed Hayouni
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The evolution toward green and energy-efficient 6G wireless networks requires innovative antenna technologies capable of reducing power consumption while maintaining high communication performance. In this context, AI-assisted reconfigurable antennas have emerged as promising solutions for intelligent and sustainable wireless systems. By dynamically adapting their radiation patterns, these antennas can direct electromagnetic energy toward active users, thereby reducing unnecessary power dissipation and improving overall energy efficiency. Among these technologies, Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) have gained significant attention as a new paradigm for programmable wireless environments. Indeed, RIS structures enable dynamic control of electromagnetic wave propagation, allowing efficient beam steering toward intended users while minimizing energy loss and enhancing spectral efficiency. This paper presents a comprehensive review of RIS based techniques for 6G wireless networks, with a particular focus on AI-assisted beamforming and energy-efficient antenna architectures. Thus, recent machine learning and deep learning approaches including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), CNN-LSTM, BF-Net, Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), and Soft Actor–Critic (SAC) with state-space exploration are examined. These techniques demonstrate strong potential for real-time beam steering, adaptive resource allocation, and mitigation of RIS hardware imperfections, contributing to the development of intelligent and energy-efficient 6G communication systems.

title: A Three-Stage Resilient Communication and Broadcast Recovery Scheme for Post-Disaster Scenarios Based on 5G Adaptive Networking
authors: Jilong Li, Hailiang Feng, Lingyan Xu, Lin Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: This paper proposes a three-stage resilient communication and broadcast recovery scheme for post-disaster scenarios based on 5G adaptive networking. In the event of large-scale disasters where conventional infrastructure is severely damaged, the scheme employs rapidly deployable 5G Emergency Gateways to restore communication in a phased manner. Stage 1 focuses on immediate public warning dissemination via the Public Warning System (PWS). Stage 2 enables basic two-way emergency calls and rich-media broadcast content dissemination. Stage 3 achieves full-service restoration by integrating the local ad-hoc network with external mobile operator cores via multi-hop or satellite backhaul. The system leverages adaptive self-organizing networking, lightweight 5G core functions, and synergistic use of PWS and 5G broadcast technologies to ensure robustness, scalability, and rapid deployability. The system architecture and mechanisms for each stage are analyzed. The proposed 3S-ECRS offers a robust and scalable solution to bridge the critical communication gap in disaster-stricken areas.

title: Design and Experimental Assessment of a Speed Breaker-Integrated Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting System
authors: Koppisetti Giridhar, Chandu P, Manikandan R, S Bharath Reddy, R Divya Sree, C Bharath
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Road traffic continuously dissipates mechanical energy as vehicles interact with road features such as speed breakers. This paper presents a compact piezoelectric energy-harvesting prototype in which a piezoelectric sensor array is embedded beneath a speed breaker plate to convert transient compressive stress into electrical energy. The harvested alternating voltage is conditioned using a bridge rectifier and smoothing capacitor, then routed to an energy-storage stage suitable for low-power roadside loads. A laboratory-scale prototype using a 3×3 PZT disc array demonstrates repeatable voltage generation under different equivalent loading levels. Measured results show that peak open-circuit voltage increases with load, while the usable energy delivered to a resistive load remains in the millijoule range for disc-type sensors, highlighting the importance of impedance matching, mechanical force distribution, and scalable modular deployment. The suggested design and working model will serve as a low-cost design to power ultra-low power devices (e.g. beacons, sensor nodes, and intermittent indicators) as well as future lane-scale integration with higher energy piezoelectric stacks and robust encapsulation.

title: Electrocutaneous Apparent Motion for Telepresence Systems
authors: Tom Hueting, Jan van Erp, Robin Lieftink
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Telepresence systems aimed at social interaction between an operator and people in a remote environment benefit from mediating social cues including social touches like a gentle stroke on the lower arm. Electrocutaneous stimulation has several advantages over mechanical stimulation (e.g., vibration), but it is unclear if it can evoke a (illusory) percept of a stroke and if so, whether the temporal parameters are similar to those for mechanical displays. Thirteen participants rated the occurrence of motion along five electrodes (stimulus sites) on the lower arm for stimuli with a different Burst Duration (5 levels, range 16.5-165ms) and Stimulus Onset Asynchrony (9 levels, range 5-130ms). Overall, smooth motion was reported in 31.6% of the trials. This number was close to 80% under optimal timing parameters. Burst Duration, Stimulus Onset Asynchrony and their interaction all reached statistical significance. Reports of motion increased with increasing Burst Duration and were higher for Stimulus Onset Asynchrony values of 20-30ms than for values above or below this range. The interaction showed that the range of Stimulus Onset Asynchrony values for motion to occur increased for higher values of Burst Duration. We conclude that electrocutaneous stimuli can reliably elicit smooth motion and are relevant for telepresence systems supporting social interaction, and that the temporal parameters differ from those reported for vibrotactile stimuli.

title: A Multi-Layer Analytical Framework for Secure and Energy-Aware IoT:Integrating Resource Allocation with KSGO-Based Optimization
authors: S.Venkatasubramanian, Arunadevi Thirumalraj, S.Hariprasath
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: With the rapid expansion of the Internet of Things (IoT), Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs), along with Mobile Ad Hoc Networks, are playing an increasingly crucial role in smart transportation, healthcare, industrial automation, and real-time monitoring. These networks are full of promise, but they still face challenges, including insufficient energy, security vulnerabilities, and scaling. In response to these issues, this research introduces a multi-layer theoretical structure that optimizes energy-efficient resource allocation and security- aware routing using the novel Knowle Sugar Glider Optimization (KSGO) technique. Experiments in a variety of settings showed significant improvements, including static urban, mobility, industrial interference, rural long-range, and energy-harvesting. Compared to the top baseline methods, the proposed KSGO-based architecture has a 19.8 percent longer network lifetime (LND) and a 23.5 percent reduction in energy consumption per transmitted bit. It maintained a Secure Packet Delivery Ratio (PDR) of 93% in security-oriented tests, even when 30% of the nodes were hostile. This outperformed conventional routing by as much as 12.1%. The framework reduced end-to-end delay by 24-31%, according to latency studies. Compared with PSO, GWO, and ACO, KSGO achieved higher fitness values with fewer iterations. These findings confirm the suggested framework as a durable and robust solution for IoT-driven WSNs in addition to MANETs. The new thing about it is that it uses cross-layer coupling and the adaptive exploration-exploitation of KSGO. This makes it good for use in mission-critical IoT applications that need to last, be reliable, and be safe.

title: Research on the Speed Suppression of Inter-Turn Short Circuit in Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor based on a New Approach Law
authors: Tao Zhou, Guang Tong
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To address the pronounced speed fluctuations that occur in permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSM) under inter-turn short-circuit(ITSC) fault conditions, this study proposes a suppression method based on an improved sliding mode control (SMC) strategy. Building on the conventional power reaching law, the approach introduces a hyperbolic tangent function to replace the sign function, thereby mitigating the intrinsic chattering issue of SMC. A current residual extraction mechanism is developed using a Luenberger observer, where the Euclidean norm of the residuals in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{d}-\mathbf{q}$</tex> axis currents is calculated to construct an exponential-type fault severity index. This index feeds into an adaptive regulator that dynamically tunes the reaching law parameters in real time. Comparative simulation results demonstrate that, relative to traditional PI control and conventional SMC, the proposed method achieves faster start-up response, enhanced operational stability under fault conditions, and greater robustness during speed variations, effectively suppressing speed oscillations in PMSM experiencing inter-turn short-circuit faults.

title: Video-Based Federated Traffic Anomaly Detection for Rule-Aware Urban Intersection Surveillance
authors: Shilpa Shashikant Chaudhari, V. Subbulakshmi, Keerthi M. S.
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Urban intersection surveillance increasingly requires automated traffic-violation analysis, but centralized video processing raises privacy and bandwidth concerns because raw CCTV footage must be continuously transferred to a central server. This work studies a federated traffic anomaly detection framework that combines Faster R-CNN object detection with region-of-interest (ROI)-guided spatiotemporal reasoning for rule-aware monitoring. To emulate distributed surveillance conditions, logical federated clients are formed from temporally partitioned segments of a continuous recording of a Bengaluru intersection, and only model parameters are exchanged during training. Experiments on real CCTV data show that federated training improves robustness across daytime and nighttime traffic conditions. For Faster R-CNN, the day–night performance drop decreases from 9.7% in centralized training to 2.3% in federated training. The strongest daytime accuracy is obtained by centralized Faster R-CNN at 0.93 mAP@50, while the federated Faster R-CNN model reaches 0.87 mAP@50 during daytime and 0.85 mAP@50 during nighttime. Across communication rounds, the global federated model shows stable convergence under non-IID temporal traffic partitions. These results support the use of federated learning with ROI-based rule reasoning for privacy-aware traffic anomaly detection in urban intersection surveillance.

title: Comparative Study of Single and Multi-Secondary Winding Dry-Type Distribution Transformers
authors: Kamran Dawood, Furkan Geze, Güven Kömürgöz, Semih Tursun
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Selecting the appropriate transformer type and winding connections is key to maximizing the performance and reliability of power distribution systems, especially when accommodating various load types. Among common configurations, the Dy transformer (a delta-connected primary winding and a star-connected secondary winding) is widely used for three-phase power systems. The Dd0y11 transformer configuration, on the other hand, is favored due to its two secondary windings (one connected to the delta and one to the star). This dual arrangement allows the transformer to supply both delta-connected and star-connected loads simultaneously, a feature particularly beneficial in industrial environments and factories where diverse load types coexist. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative study of 2200 kVA dry-type distribution transformers rated for 33 kV on the primary side and 0.690 kV on the secondary side, focusing specifically on how the winding configuration (single secondary winding (Dy) and dual secondary windings (Dd0y11)) influences key parameters such as transformer weight, losses, and initial capital cost. Through detailed analysis, the research highlights the operational flexibility offered by dual-winding transformers but also examines the trade-offs in terms of increased material usage and higher costs. The findings provide a broad overview of how transformer winding choices impact both the economic and technical aspects of power distribution design, which is helpful for researchers, engineers, and decision-makers tasked with specifying transformers for complex distribution networks.

title: The Reform of Aesthetic Education Courses in Higher Education Institutions Based on AI Technology
authors: Dongling Chen, Yue Zhao, Weihua Lan, Yu Chang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: With the continuous advancement and iteration of educational technology, aesthetic education has become a key focus in curriculum reform. Grounded in the OBE (Outcomes-Based Education) theoretical framework, this study explores practical pathways and outcomes for AI-enabled aesthetic education curriculum reform in higher education. A questionnaire survey was conducted, and the data were analyzed using SPSS 27.0, with reliability and validity tests performed to ensure data quality. The reliability analysis yielded a Cronbach's α coefficient of 0.847, and the validity analysis indicated a KMO value of 0.930, with Bartlett’s test of sphericity being significant (p < 0.001), confirming the suitability of the data for factor analysis. Findings reveal that AI-supported teaching content and formats exert a significant positive influence on students' learning engagement and aesthetic education achievement, with learning engagement partially mediating this relationship. The study further identifies existing challenges in current reforms, including disconnects in pathway implementation, insufficient depth in pedagogical transformation, and imperfect evaluation systems. Consequently, it proposes constructing a tiered aesthetic education curriculum system grounded in traditional culture and supported by AI technology, establishing a multi-dimensional "human-machine collaborative" evaluation mechanism, and cultivating an innovative aesthetic education faculty capable of guiding students toward outcome attainment. This research provides theoretical foundations and practical references for the deep integration and systematic reform of aesthetic education in higher education institutions within the AI era.

title: The Hidden Rise of Toxicity: How Retweets Obscure Increasing Hostility in Brazilian Politics
authors: Soroush Karimi, Marcos Oliveira, Diogo Pacheco
publish: Proceedings of the 18th ACM Web Science Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Characterization and Interference Mitigation of a 170-200 GHz 2 × 2 MIMO System
authors: Tao Wu, Xiao-Sheng Ni, Changjie Hu, Zebin Huang, Weichao Li, Xiongbin Yu, Xiaofeng Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: This letter presents an experimental evaluation of a 2 × 2 non-polarization-multiplexed multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) communication system operating within the 170–200 GHz band. Based on a heterodyne transceiver architecture with compact horn antenna arrays, we characterize the distance-dependent evolution of cross-antenna interference in sub-millimeter wave links. To mitigate the severe crosstalk observed at extended ranges, a measurement-driven, distance-adaptive equalization strategy is implemented, integrating a Turbo Successive Interference Cancellation (SIC) algorithm. The proposed system is validated using IEEE 802.11ay-compliant LDPC coding and high-order modulation. Experimental results demonstrate an LDPC-coded 2 × 2 spatial-MIMO transmission with a net aggregate data rate of 151.5 Gbps (2 × 75.75 Gbps) at a transmission distance of 100 cm, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed interference mitigation scheme for robust coded THz MIMO communications.

title: A Hybrid Multi-agent Fault Location Strategy in Active Distribution Network Using Synchrophasors
authors: Sandhya Chandran, Ramakrishna Gokaraju, Nadeem Jamali, Md Sajid Alam
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Locating faults and isolating the faulted sections of the network are integral to the smooth operation of any self healing distribution system. A novel multi-agent system based event location and isolation approach is proposed for a modern power distribution system using synchronized current and voltage from a limited number of PMUs. The developed multi-agent framework uses a decentralized hierarchical agent scheme and consists of heterogeneous agent types, with the PMUs defined as the main computational agents. The agents receive their local synchrophasor measurements and perform the algorithm by communicating with their neighboring agents to locate the faulted bus with minimal communication messages. The new fault location algorithm is a two-step process: first, the agents identify the faulted section using impedance comparison and isolate the faulted zone; second, the corresponding agents within the zone identify the faulted bus using voltage comparison and send the bus information to the control center. Studies are conducted by interconnecting the IEEE 34-bus network with distributed energy resources developed in PSCAD/EMTDC and a multi-agent system implemented on the JADE platform. Simulations of various test cases verify the efficacy of the proposed multi-agent scheme in accurately locating various faults occurring in the distribution network.

title: Enhancing Lesion Segmentation via Medical Image-Mask Pair Synthesis using Phenotype-Conditioned Diffusion Models
authors: Fei Lyu, Jingwen Xu, Ye Zhu, Grace Lai-Hung Wong, Pong C. Yuen
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Accurate lesion segmentation in medical images is critical for early diagnosis and treatment planning, yet it remains challenging because training robust lesion segmentation models suffers from the data scarcity and sample imbalance issues. Synthetic data augmentation is a promising approach to address these challenges, but existing methods may struggle to produce high-quality lesion-containing samples. In this paper, we present Le sionLab, a novel framework for medical image-mask pair synthesis which augments the existing training dataset to a more balanced and diverse one. Specifically, we first design a phenotype-guided text prompt that captures complex lesion characteristics by clustering radiomic features into distinct phenotypes, improving the controllability of synthetic data generation to achieve more precise trans formation from lesion-free to lesion-containing samples. Additionally, we introduce a dual-check quality control mechanism that integrates priors of foundation models to compute quality and hardness scores, effectively filtering low-quality samples while prioritizing challenging cases for model training. Extensive experiments on three public datasets demonstrate that our proposed Lesion Lab is superior to existing synthetic data augmentation methods for lesion segmentation. Code is available at: https://github.com/FeiLyu/LesionLab.

title: Physics-driven Sonification for Improving Multisensory Needle Guidance in Percutaneous Epicardial Access
authors: Veronica Ruozzi, Sasan Matinfar, Pasquale Vergara, Alessandro Albanesi, Serena Dell’Aversana, Stefano Carugo, Gianluigi Buccoliero, Nassir Navab, Alberto Redaelli, Emiliano Votta
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Percutaneous epicardial access (PEA), performed on a beating heart under fluoroscopy, enables arrhythmia treatment. However, advancing a needle toward the thin, moving pericardium remains highly challenging and risky. To address this challenge, we present a novel physics-driven sonification method for Extended Reality (XR)-based multisensory navigation to enhance user perception regarding critically sensitive needle landing phase in PEA. Dynamic cardiac anatomy from 4DCTA was reconstructed and registered to a real-world coordinate system. Real-time needle tracking provided data of the needle tip relative to moving cardiac structures, driving the audio-visual feedback module. The visual display presents navigational cues and dynamic cardiac structures, whereas the auditory display encodes physiological cardiac states into a rigorous mapping through a multilayered physical membrane. To prove this novel concept, a phantom study was conducted with twelve cardiologists performing needle insertions under visual-only and multisensory feedback. Results show that the proposed multisensory method improved the navigation safety (χ2 = 11.30, p < 0.01), reducing myocardial contact (3.64% vs. 7.27%) and increasing correct access (90.91% vs. 52.73%). Needle placement accuracy improved, with closer membrane proximity (Cliff’s δ = 0.19) and reduced variability (Fligner p < 0.05). Execution time remained comparable, but time–accuracy correlations differed significantly between modalities (Fisher’s p < 0.01). NASA-TLX showed lower cognitive load with multisensory guidance (t = −4.02, p < 0.01). In conclusion, the multisensory system is associated with improved spatiotemporal awareness and suggests potential benefits for surgical navigation, while indicating reduced cognitive load. These findings demonstrate the feasibility of physics-driven sonification for supporting user-centered interaction and motivate further validation in realistic and clinical environments.

title: Security-checking opening planning in transportation hubs with reinforcement learning and deep learning considering stochastic passenger arrival
authors: Wen Zhang, Haodong Chen, Yingxu Rui, Peng Liao
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Security checks are common in crowed places, and administrator should decide how many lanes be assigned in advance to ensure operation efficiency and cost reduction. However, the decision is complex due to two factors: (1) the stochastic nature of the crowed flow, and (2) the nonlinear behaviors of the passenger queues in security check lanes. To address this challenge, this study proposes an integrated approach combining simulation, reinforcement learning, deep learning and mathematical optimization. We use discrete event simulation to describe the queue dynamics; with the simulation as the environment, we build a reinforcement learning model to obtain a real-time adjustment of the lane configuration; then, we evaluate the influence of the stochastic passenger arrival and the initial checkpoint lane assignment on the real-time adjustment; and finally, we propose an integrated optimization model for solving the security-checking opening problem. In the hybrid method, the simulation can accurately reproduce the nonlinear and stochastic queue system. The reinforcement learning method can provide the real-time decision to enhance the system efficiency. And the deep learning method can reduce the complexity of the security checking planning, which was always modelled as a two-stage stochastic program. Numerical tests indicate the hybrid method is efficient in solving the model and effective in practice. Understanding the randomness can improve the service level while reduce the staff cost and the staff number, as well as the staff utilization. Our planning strategy can induce a win-win-win outcome: administrator can reduce the operation cost.
