title: An AI-Based Supervisory Measurement Integrity Validation Layer for Cyber-Resilient AC/DC Protection in Inverter-Based Microgrids
authors: Ahmad Mohammad Saber, Ahmed Saber Refae, Davor Svetinovic, Hatem Zeineldin, Amr Youssef, Deepa Kundur, Ehab F. El-Saadany
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Line current differential relays (LCDRs) are measurement-driven relays that rely on time-synchronized multi-phase current waveforms to infer internal faults in AC and DC power networks. In inverter-based microgrids, however, the increasing reliance on digitally communicated measurements exposes LCDRs to false-data injection attacks (FDIAs), in which adversaries manipulate remote measurement streams to create protection-triggering yet physically inconsistent current trajectories. This paper addresses this emerging measurement integrity problem by introducing a measurement integrity validation scheme that operates as a supervisory instrumentation layer for modern LCDRs. The proposed scheme interprets short windows of synchronized instantaneous current measurements recorded during relay operation and assesses their physical consistency to distinguish genuine fault-induced trajectories from cyber-manipulated measurement streams. A recurrent neural network is trained offline using only relay-available current measurements and exploits the temporal structure of differential current waveforms, which remains informative in inverter-dominated systems where current magnitude is no longer a reliable observable. The method requires no additional sensors, auxiliary protection elements, or prior knowledge of network topology, and is applicable to both AC and DC LCDRs without structural modification. The proposed measurement validation scheme is evaluated on an islanded inverter-based microgrid under a comprehensive set of fault and FDIA scenarios, demonstrating high detection accuracy while preserving relay dependability. Hardware-in-the-loop validation using an OPAL-RT real-time simulator confirms that the scheme satisfies protection timing constraints and can operate in real time under realistic operating conditions.

title: A Comparison Between Nanoparticle-doped Fibers and Standard Single Mode Fibers for Distributed Temperature Sensing_supp1-3693399.docx
authors: Arnaldo Leal-Junior
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of the thermal properties and distributed temperature-sensing responses of nanoparticles (NP)-doped optical fiber using optical frequency domain reflectometry. The thermomechanical analysis for the NP-doped fiber is performed to evaluate the thermal expansion coefficient of such fibers and their differences compared with standard telecom optical fibers (SMF). In addition, the thermo-optical coefficient of such fibers is analyzed as a function of their steady-state temperature responses, whereas the thermal conductivity is assessed from the transient thermal analysis of the NP-doped fiber. The results in distributed temperature measurement indicate the better sensing performance of NP-doped fiber, which has 2.6 times higher temperature sensitivity, a smaller response time, and high accuracy estimated from a determination coefficient (R²) of 0.993 and root mean squared error of 1.28 °C. Therefore, the main findings of this work indicate the influence of the nanoparticles composition on the thermal properties of the optical fibers, where the addition of oxide particles (in conjunction with the changes in the glass matrix) presented a positive effect on the distributed temperature sensing capabilities with a thermal expansion coefficient increase of 30% and thermal conductivity increase of 25% when compared with SMF. Thus, this paper provides not only the comparison of the temperature sensing performance of an NP-doped fiber but also a straightforward method for the assessment of distributed physical parameters of the fiber that can be used for any specialty optical fiber, which can also be used as a non-destructive method for the assessment of the optical fiber composition.

title: Inferring ECG Waveforms from Chest Wall Using Wearable Non-contact Electromagnetic Sensor and Machine Learning Models
authors: Shun Hinatsu, Saki Wada, Hidetoshi Makimura
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In this article, we propose and comparatively evaluate a system for inferring electrocardiogram (ECG) signal waveforms from chest wall displacement recorded by a wearable non-contact electromagnetic near-field sensor (NFS) module using machine learning models for convenient physiological monitoring rather than clinical diagnosis. The NFS module, equipped with an antenna records changes in the input impedance as a time-series orthogonal NFS In-phase (I) and Quadrature-phase (Q) signals, reflecting variations in the near-field between the wall and the antenna, with minimal restrictions on the body. Subsequently, the system infers ECG waveforms from the NFS signals using machine learning models developed based on the relationship between the signals. To evaluate the feasibility of the proposed system, we simultaneously recorded NFS and reference single-lead ECG signals from 12 participants in both resting and desk-working states during an experiment. We developed multiple linear regression in the time and frequency domains and deep learning-based models, leveraging state-of-the-art techniques, to infer ECG waveforms from the recorded NFS signals by varying the combinations of I and Q signals in resting and desk-working states for training and testing. Using a linear regression model in the time domain with NFS I and Q signals, the best performance achieved a root mean square error 0.022±0.011 (normalized amplitude) in a resting state and 0.023±0.011 in a desk-working state, measured between the reference single-lead ECG and the waveforms inferred from the NFS. These results indicate the potential of NFS measurements to produce waveforms correlated with single-lead ECG for convenient monitoring applications, while acknowledging that the inferred waveforms are not direct electrical recordings.

title: Bus Voltage Regulation Based on Dynamic Virtual Damping Consensus Control by Electrolyzer
authors: Yetong Han, Xiuchong Liu, Dazhong Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: To enable hydrogen electrolyzers to adapt to frequent voltage fluctuations in low inertia DC microgrid, this paper proposes a dynamic virtual damping consensus control scheme. The core of this scheme lies in constructing dynamic virtual damping to regulate the operating power of electrolyzers such that the operating state of the electrolyzers is adjusted to accommodate the frequently fluctuating DC bus voltage. Firstly, a thorough analysis of the load characteristics of electrolyzers is conducted. A temperature-current equation is derived, laying the foundation for the construction of dynamic virtual damping. Secondly, a dynamic virtual damping consensus control strategy is designed, with specific design conditions for damping parameters specified. Finally, a dynamic adjustment method for damping parameters is presented based on the established temperature-current equation. The proposed control scheme achieves precise matching between the operating power of electrolyzers and microgrid voltage fluctuations through accurate damping regulation. Its effectiveness is validated by experiments and hardware-in-the-loop simulations.

title: Deep learning framework on physics-model data for accelerated design of nanoarray photocathode
authors: Sihao Xia, Xinlong Chen, Xian Wu, Yu Diao, Yijun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Photoemission models are widely used for the structural design and physical analysis of photocathodes. However, the computational cost becomes prohibitive due to the complex equation-solving process when applied to nanostructured photocathodes, particularly for large-scale nanoarrays. In this work, we introduce a hybrid deep learning framework that employs a convolutional neural network (CNN) trained on data generated by rigorous three-dimensional photoemission simulations. The network is first pretrained on photocurrent data from small-scale nanoarrays (dimensions≤100 nm), demonstrating its capability to capture the underlying photoemission physics embedded in the simulation data. A lightweight transfer learning strategy using only hundreds of larger-scale samples then generalizes the Photoemission CNN (PECNN) to broader geometries. This approach reduces the average prediction error from 36.82% to 2.91% for emitted photocurrent and from 54.80% to 3.34% for collected photocurrent. Concurrently, the framework achieves computational speedups of approximately 1.6×103 for small nanoarrays and 1.5×104 for larger systems compared to traditional numerical solvers. Post-hoc interpretability analyses, including SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), Permutation Importance (PI), and Partial Dependence Plots (PDP), identify array spacing, nanorod diameter, and substrate thickness as the dominant parameters governing emission performance, and the learned relationships align consistently with established photoemission theory. By delivering cross-scale, interpretable, and ultra-fast predictions, the PECNN framework provides a powerful tool for the rational design and optimization of advanced photoemission devices.

title: ColorPCR++: Unleash the Power of Color for Point Cloud Registration Using Hypergraph Computation
authors: Juncheng Mu, Siqi Li, Yining Zhao, Yue Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Point cloud registration (PCR) has been an important research subject for many years but remains an open problem, presenting numerous challenges. The stability of existing registration methods is often inadequate, particularly in scenarios with low overlap. This issue primarily arises from the insufficient distinctiveness of extracted point cloud features, leading to ambiguous matches and the proliferation of outliers. To address these bottlenecks in point cloud registration, it is crucial to fully leverage the color information of the point clouds to discern point correspondences effectively. However, excessive reliance on color information during the registration process may impede the recognition of point cloud geometric structures, making it essential to find a balance between the aggressiveness and stability of color integration. To tackle these challenges, we propose ColorPCR++, which unlocks the potential of color information while maintaining stability. Specifically, we design a Curvature-Color Fusion Module (CCF) to initially introduce color, which leverages curvature to enhance geometric features and balance color stability. Additionally, to further incorporate color into scene structure representation, we model the point-wise hue gradient field (HGF) for positional embedding, which proves especially effective in lowoverlap scenarios. CCF and HGF fully harness the power of color, enabling the identification of most corresponding point pairs with prominent geometric and color features. However, the reliance on color can also introduce false correspondences, reducing the inlier ratio within the correspondences obtained through feature matching. Therefore, we assess the higher-order compatibility of correspondences using Feature-based Compatibility Hypergraph Convolution (FCH), effectively filtering out outliers and accurately estimating the transformation. Evaluation across multiple datasets has demonstrated the state-of-the-art performance of ColorPCR++.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Unsupervised Domain Adaptation in Spatio-Temporal Echocardiography Segmentation_supp1-3693615.mp4
authors: Arnaud Judge
publish: N/A
abstract: Domain adaptation methods aim to bridge the gap between datasets by enabling knowledge transfer across domains, reducing the need for additional expert annotations. However, many approaches struggle with reliability in the target domain, an issue particularly critical in medical image segmentation, where accuracy and anatomical validity are essential. This challenge is further exacerbated in spatio-temporal data, where the lack of temporal consistency can significantly degrade segmentation quality, and particularly in echocardiography, where the presence of artifacts and noise can further hinder segmentation performance. To address these issues, we present RL4Seg3D, an unsupervised domain adaptation framework for 2D + time echocardiography segmentation. RL4Seg3D integrates novel reward functions and a fusion scheme to enhance key landmark precision in its segmentations while processing full-sized input videos. By leveraging reinforcement learning for image segmentation, our approach improves accuracy, anatomical validity, and temporal consistency while also providing, as a beneficial side effect, a robust uncertainty estimator, which can be used at test time to further enhance segmentation performance. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on over 30,000 echocardiographic videos, showing that it outperforms standard domain adaptation techniques without the need for any labels on the target domain. Code is available at https://github.com/arnaudjudge/RL4Seg3D.

title: Improving Fairness in Airline Integrated Crew Scheduling - Colleague Familiarity and Team Experience Distribution Considerations_supp1-3693257.docx
authors: Xin WEN
publish: N/A
abstract: Psychological health of flight crew is critical to flight safety. Due to job nature (e.g., irregular work schedules, frequent jet lag), colleagues can sometimes outweigh the importance of family for flight crew. Thus, building colleague familiarity and workplace friendship is crucial. However, under the existing air crew scheduling (ACS) approach, crew members face frequent colleague changes and have to work with strangers, causing poor work environments. Besides, reasonable experience level distributions in crew teams are also crucial for crew satisfaction. However, the existing ACS approach frequently produces crew teams with unbalanced distributions of experience levels (e.g., all members from various ranks have lower-end experience levels within the qualified range). To overcome these drawbacks, this study develops a novel multi-objective ACS approach that can improve colleague familiarity (i.e., increase the chance for crew members to work together) and form crew teams with more reasonable and balanced experience level distributions. An evolutionary algorithm that incorporates the hyperplane separation and heuristic search strategy is proposed to derive Pareto solutions. The proposed ACS approach is examined on long-haul flight schedules which impose more complicated crew work/rest regulations. Extensive computational experiments with subsequent analysis validate the proposed method's effectiveness, showing an impressive improvement in colleague familiarity (1989.45%,

title: EEG-EIT Synchronous Acquisition System: Development and Preliminary Application in Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Wenjing Zhu, Xinyu Wu, Weichen Li, Cheng Chen, Xiao Yu, Xuetao Shi, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address the need for precise localization of refractory epileptic foci, this study developed a synchronous EEG-EIT detection system and validated the feasibility and accuracy of using electrical impedance tomography (EIT) for epileptogenic zone localizing. The system utilizes a single set of electrodes to achieve co-located, simultaneous, and same-channel acquisition of impedance and EEG signals. Cross-interference between devices was eliminated through a dual-domain cooperative strategy combining "hardware low-pass" and "software comb" filtering, ensuring the precision of signal acquisition. System performance was systematically evaluated using a kainic acid (KA)-induced epilepsy model in ten adult male Sprague-Dawley rats. Signals were acquired under three conditions: independent acquisition (reference baseline), synchronous acquisition without filtering (interference state), and synchronous acquisition with filtering (post-interference suppression). After filtering, the impedance difference between synchronous and independent EIT recordings was only 0.15% under normal conditions and 0.71% during seizures. The reconstructed EIT images achieved a structural similarity index (SSIM) of 0.97 and a Dice coefficient of 0.80. The filtered EEG signals exhibited high similarity to independently recorded signals, with cosine similarity and correlation coefficients both exceeding 0.80 under both normal and epileptic conditions. Spatial consistency analysis revealed that the average deviation between the centroid of the epileptogenic zone localized by EIT and that derived from EEG topography was only 0.13 mm, with a mean relative error of 6.37%. Histological examination confirmed that the regions identified by EIT corresponded to areas exhibiting characteristic epileptic neuronal damage. In 80% of seizures, a sustained impedance decrease (7.48% ± 5.339%) was observed in the focal region, and the area of decreased impedance gradually expanded during seizure progression. These results demonstrate that the system can reliably acquire multimodal signals, accurately localize epileptic foci, and monitor dynamic impedance changes during seizure activity. This study provides a foundational platform for future research on multimodal fusion-based epilepsy diagnosis and the development of portable neural monitoring devices.

title: Maximizing Resource Usage in 5G/6G CIoT Systems with Diverse Uplink/Downlink Traffic
authors: Eduard Sopin, Anastasia Daraseliya, Vyacheslav Begishev, Konstantin Samoylov, Yevgeni Koucheryavy
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Future 5G/6G cellular Internet of Things (CIoT) technologies need to be able to multiplex a number of traffic types having different arrival patterns, resource requirements, and directions. To this aim, the random access (RA) and data transmission (DT) phases in such technologies should be appropriately optimized. In this paper, we develop a computationally efficient and accurate model accounting for both RA and DT phases and the different nature of traffic types in uplink and downlink directions. We establish the stability criterion for the considered system and then utilize this model to optimize resources allocated to RA and DT phases under different traffic loads in uplink and downlink directions. We show that the proposed model provides accurate assessment of the delay at both RA and DT phases with an error of less than 1%, while its fully analytical structure makes it computationally efficient. Our results also indicate that modern CIoT system are not optimized for different load conditions and even a 5 % decrease in the amount of resources allocated to RA or DT phase changes the supported packet arrival rate by up to 80 %. The model developed in this paper allows optimal tuning RA and DT phases for any offered load conditions in the uplink and downlink directions.

title: GarmentRec: towards Individual Garment Reconstruction from a Monocular Human Image
authors: Zongyi Xu, Zhen Gao, Shiyang Cheng, Wang Fei, Qianni Zhang, Weisheng Li, Xinbo Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: Reconstructing high-quality garment models from monocular images is important as it provides a practical and effective solution for human digitization and virtual try-on etc. Recent implicit function-based garment reconstruction methods recover free-form geometry but struggle to reconstruct individual garment meshes from human images and tend to produce disembodied limbs or degenerate shapes for novel views. In contrast, explicit parametric garment template models can be utilised to construct separate meshes and constrain the shape reconstruction robustly. However, this limits the reconstruction of garment details and shape variations, such as the wrinkles and pockets etc. To address this problem, in this paper, we introduce a novel explicit garment template that is designed for both closed and open garment topology. Powered by our new garment template, we further propose a detailed garment reconstruction method based on a monocular view that can process both the closed and open types for shape recovery. To capture those challenging parts with unknown geometry and topology, we predict displacement maps on the parameterization domain for the target garment from the monocular image and elaborate it to the 3D garment surface via the UV coordinates, achieving realistic details on the 3D garment shape. Extensive experiments demonstrate the accuracy and robustness of our method and show that realistic details like garment wrinkles and pockets can be faithfully recovered in an explicit way. The code and dataset are available at https://github.com/worryDes/GarmentRec.

title: Dual-Mode Surface Sensing Based on Piezoelectric Micromachined Ultrasonic Transducer Array
authors: Junhao Wang, Kai Yang, Chenyuan Zhang, Jiao Xia, Yufeng Gao, Xinyue Zhang, Wei Wang, Bowen Sheng, Yipeng Lu
publish: Journal of Microelectromechanical Systems
abstract: Surface sensing is widely used to detect liquids and solids on surfaces and to characterize their properties. Guided-wave and pulse-echo methods are commonly used for these tasks. Guided-waves enable large-area inspection but lack accurate local thickness resolution and multi-frequency sensing capabilities. In contrast, pulse-echo techniques offer precise thickness measurements but are limited in spatial coverage. Consequently, neither method alone can provide both large-area coverage and high-precision local characterization. In this paper, we proposed a linear piezoelectric micromachined ultrasonic transducer (PMUT) array with a hexagonal close-packed layout, achieving a 70% fill factor and generating acoustic pressures up to 190 kPa at a distance of 5 mm from the array. By exciting higher-order vibrational modes, the PMUT array produces a broad response from 100 kHz to 10 MHz. Using a PMUT linear array for low-frequency guided-wave sensing enables the inspection of large areas on thin media. By employing phased-array excitation and receive beamforming, the guided-wave amplitude is enhanced to 283% and 208% of its original value, respectively, while eliminating the bulky wedge structures required by conventional transducers. Moreover, the broadband response supports multi-frequency pulse-echo operation, thereby improving thickness measurement accuracy and providing multi-frequency characterization of the medium. Therefore, the proposed dual-mode surface sensing approach based on broadband, high-acoustic-pressure PMUT arrays offers significant potential for compact, high-precision sensing in industrial and consumer electronics applications.[2026-0021]

title: Distributed Coordination of EV Charging and Discharging in Low-Voltage Networks
authors: Sajad Koushkbaghi, M. Imran Azim, Alireza Barzegar, Hui Song, Nameer Al Khafaf, Mahdi Jalili
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a fully distributed electric vehicle coordination framework that achieves optimality, accounts for user mobility requirements, and respects distribution network constraints without relying on a central coordinator. The method leverages peer-to-peer communication among households to achieve coordination and uses dual decomposition to enforce coupling constraints across households while integrating a surplus-based averaging mechanism to relax restrictive assumptions on the communication topology. Furthermore, a distributed voltage estimation strategy is developed that combines local smart meter measurements with the linearized distribution flow model, allowing voltage constraints to be enforced using only local information. The proposed approach ensures privacy, improves grid stability, and enables scalable coordination. Numerical simulations, carried out on a low-voltage Australian radial distribution network, demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in mitigating voltage violations under high solar and electric vehicle penetration while meeting user mobility needs.

title: Design of Balanced Double-Ridge Waveguide Coupler With Inherent Wideband Common-Mode Suppression
authors: Ying Xue, Xin-Yue Fang, Ya-Hui Zhu, Lei Wang, Wei Qin, Jian-Xin Chen
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: In this letter, a novel balanced double-ridge waveguide (WG) coupler based on the metallic patch ridge with a central slot is proposed. It is found that the differential-mode (DM) cutoff frequency (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{c}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) is much lower than that of the common mode (CM). By making full use of the unique characteristic, the proposed coupler is designed to be centered in the frequency range between them, achieving good DM 3-dB power allocation and wideband CM suppression simultaneously. For demonstration, a prototype of a balanced coupler is fabricated and measured, showing good agreement between the simulated and measured results. The coupler centers at 5.25 GHz and has a fractional bandwidth (FBW) of about 21.3% with measured DM return loss (RL) and isolation >15 dB. Furthermore, the CM suppression is over 45 dB from 0 to 6 GHz.

title: Mixed-Domain Predistortion of RF Power Amplifiers Based on a Wiener Model
authors: Tommaso Cappello, Md. Samiul Islam Borno, Qing Wan
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a mixed-domain predistortion (MPD) architecture for RF power amplifiers (PAs) based on a Wiener model targeting memory effects reduction. The linear part of the MPD is realized in the digital baseband with a transversal filter, while the nonlinear part is implemented with an analog predistortion (APD) circuit. With this architecture, the digital signal processors, digital-to-analog converters, and upconverters can operate at the original signal bandwidth. The MPD performance is evaluated using normalized root mean square error (NRMSE) and error vector magnitude (EVM) metrics. Compared with a fully digital Wiener digital predistortion (DPD), the MPD achieves comparable NRMSE for 50–400-MHz signal bandwidths while providing up to a 10% point improvement in NRMSE relative to a standalone APD. The MPD is validated using a 5G 100-MHz 256-QAM signal, demonstrating an EVM improvement of up to 16 dB for the same output power.

title: A SAT-Guard-V-Satellite-First Edge-AI Framework for Real-Time Vehicle Theft Detection & Resilient Alerting
authors: Manzar Ahmed, Muhammad Ajmal Naz, Muhammad Ibrar-Ul-Haq, Mishaal Ahmed, Syed Mohuddin Bukhari, Sheikh Junaid Yawar
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: Vehicle theft cases are biggest issues in worldwide and these cases are severe in ruler areas, where the mobile coverage areas are limited. SATGuard-V provide end-to-end vehicular security system that combines on-board edge AI, satellite IoT communication, and tamper-evident logging. The vehicle-mounted unit fuses CAN-bus telemetry, inertial (IMU) data, door/ignition status and GNSS signals. A lightweight anomaly detector (cascaded LSTM plus one-class auto-encoder) runs on this edge unit to identify theft patterns (unauthorized ignition, towing motion, flatbed transport, or GPS spoofing). Detected events trigger a prioritized cryptographically-signed alert payload sent via satellite (targeting Inmarsat’s ELERA/ISAT Data Pro L-band IoT network) to distributed servers. Those servers perform corroborative analysis and record incidents in a permissioned blockchain for auditing. The design explicitly copes with satellite link constraints and GNSS spoofing via multi-constellation cross-checks. The MATLAB-based simulation methodology used for real driving data (e.g. recorded GPS/telemetry logs), easy-to-use ML tools, and a software-modeled Inmarsat link. A Simulink model for analyzing and confirming the performance by measuring detection precision/recall/F1 and communication overhead will be used. Finally from results we can conclude that method significantly minimize the risk of false alarms and provide send alert of delivery received, even when terrestrial networks fail.

title: AI-Enabled Heritage Structure Monitoring System
authors: B Dhanam, B Mohan, K Ramalakshmi, B Vairavasamy, J Naveen Khanna, S Dharun
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: The preservation of ancient heritage structures, such as temples, is challenged by environmental stresses including heat, humidity, and moisture accumulation, which lead to micro-cracks, water seepage, and biological growths. Traditional manual inspections are time-consuming, inconsistent, and reactive, necessitating the development of real-time monitoring solutions. This paper proposes an AI-enabled Wireless Sensor Network (WSN) for heritage structure monitoring that integrates environmental sensors and edge-level analytics to detect early-stage deterioration. Sensor nodes equipped with temperature, humidity, moisture, and vibration sensors communicate with a microcontroller-based base station (e.g., ESP32) for local data processing, edge AI computation, threshold-based alerting, and offline data storage. The system employs simple AI models to predict potential damage patterns and generates visual and audible alerts for preventive maintenance. The design prioritizes non-invasive monitoring, low-power operation, and offline functionality, making it suitable for remote and rural heritage sites. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system can detect environmental and structural anomalies in real-time. The monitoring system achieved an accuracy of approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> for temperature sensing, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2 \%$</tex> for humidity measurement, and vibration sensitivity capable of detecting micro-structural disturbances, while generating alerts within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-3$</tex> seconds of abnormal readings. These results confirm the practicality and effectiveness of the system for real-time heritage structure monitoring.

title: Mapping the Knowledge Structure and Research Frontiers of Pedagogical Agents in Education: A CiteSpace Analysis
authors: Sichen Jiang, Heng Luo
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Pedagogical agents, including animated, embodied, and teachable agents, have been widely integrated into digital learning environments and continue to draw increasing scholarly attention. Given the rapid expansion of this literature across learning sciences, educational technology, and human–computer interaction, a systematic mapping of its knowledge structure and emerging directions is needed. Using CiteSpace, this study conducts a visual bibliometric analysis of 6,766 Web of Science Core Collection publications on pedagogical agents in educational contexts, retrieved from 2016 to 2025. The analysis examines annual publication trends, collaboration patterns across countries/regions and institutions, the intellectual base revealed by reference co-citation networks, major thematic clusters, and research hotspots and frontiers identified through keyword co-occurrence and burst detection. The results provide an integrated knowledge map of pedagogical agent research and inform future theory development, design optimization, and empirical inquiry in educational applications.

title: GraphST: Imbalanced Node Classification Using Graph Structure and Transformer
authors: Zihan Guo, Zhiwei Ma, Xiangbin Zhu, Jianmin Han, Yizhen Huang, Zhaolong Hu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Node classification is a cornerstone of graph data mining, yet real-world graphs often suffer from severe category imbalance. Existing oversampling techniques primarily focus on feature interpolation within the same minority class, which frequently results in poor node diversity and structural distortions. To overcome these limitations, we propose GraphST, a novel framework that leverages Graph structures and Transformers for imbalanced learning. GraphST introduces four key innovations: (1) A Transformer-based feature extractor that models global topological dependencies to prevent the local-bias and over-smoothing issues inherent in traditional GNNs. (2) A path-diversity-aware node sampler that incorporates cross-category interactions, moving beyond simple shortest-path heuristics. (3) A gated fusion mechanism that dynamically adjusts weights between majority and minority class features, suppressing noise during node synthesis. (4) A similarity-based neighbor generator to ensure structural integrity. Extensive experiments on five benchmark datasets demonstrate that GraphST significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods across multiple metrics, including ACC, F1-score, and Balanced Accuracy.

title: Frequency Characteristics Analysis of the Receiving-End Power Grid with Short-Circuit Faults
authors: Jiachen Liu, Jianhua Li, Lu Cao, Haibao Zhai, Yanqiang Shi, Jinwen Sun, Chuan Qin, Teng Zhu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: In receiving-end grids with high penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs), short-circuit disturbances trigger severe voltage sags and induce transient power surges via the coupled dynamics of converters, loads, and HVDC lines. This paper proposes a characterization method based on unbalanced power and develops an energy-based metric to quantify the severity of such impacts in a unified, comparable framework. Through a CSEE case study, a systematic investigation is conducted covering disturbance types, fault locations, durations, and grounding impedances. Results demonstrate that the proposed metric effectively captures both the magnitude and duration of transient power imbalances, enabling consistent ranking across diverse fault scenarios. Furthermore, continuous converter faults induce more pronounced and prolonged unbalance than single-point faults. This framework provides actionable insights for operational assessment, reactive power configuration, and control strategies to mitigate short-circuitinduced frequency risks.

title: Physics-Informed RetNet for Joint Battery RUL, SoC, and SoH Prediction
authors: Fahad M. Almasoudi
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: The predictive maintenance of battery systems, particularly in industries like electric vehicles (EVs) and energy storage systems (ESS), is crucial for optimizing performance, safety, and longevity. Accurate estimations of Remaining Useful Life (RUL), State of Charge (SoC), and State of Health (SoH) are essential for minimizing downtime and preventing failures. Current machine learning models face challenges with insufficient data processing, noisy measurements, and complex multi-task learning. This paper proposes a novel approach, Physics-Informed RetNet (PIRetNet) with multi-objectives which integrates deep learning with physical system knowledge, allowing for improved prediction accuracy while accounting for these challenges. The model is trained using publicly available datasets, addressing the need for a robust solution that enhances the efficiency and safety of battery-powered systems. Performance evaluation demonstrates that Physics-Informed RetNet outperforms physics informed transformer baseline models across RUL, SoC, and SoH tasks, achieving lower error rates and stronger multi-task learning performance.

title: Reinforcement Learning Based Adaptive Traffic Signal Control Using Deep Q-Network Variants
authors: Rahul Nalla, Vedhakshari Tanmayi Madhavareddy, Bhargava Ashish Sajjala, Naresh Sammeta
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Traffic congestion in urban areas leads to significant economic losses in terms of travel delays, air pollution, and unplanned urban growth, while conventional fixed-time or sensor-based traffic signal systems lack the flexibility required for real-time adaptation. In this paper, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is investigated for adaptive traffic signal control by formulating the intersection management problem as a Markov decision process, in which an agent learns optimal signal phase decisions through interaction with a simulated environment. A realistic simulation framework is developed using the Simulation of Urban Mobility (SUMO) platform and the Traffic Control Interface (TraCI). Three DRL models are evaluated, namely Deep Q-Network (DQN), Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN), and Dueling Double Deep Q-Network (D3QN). The experimental results indicate that DQN achieves the lowest vehicle waiting time, DDQN exhibits unstable training behavior under the considered settings, and D3QN improves throughput at the cost of increased delay.

title: Impact of a 2D Animation as a Didactic Resource on Mathematics Learning in Fifth-Grade Elementary Students
authors: Glendamira Serrano Franco, Víctor Manuel Zamudio García, Mario Alberto Gea Pérez
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study analyzes the use of 2D animation as a didactic resource in mathematics learning among fifth-grade elementary school students. A mixed-method methodological approach was adopted, using a quasi-experimental pretest– posttest design implemented in five primary schools. The sample consisted of fifth-grade students who were assessed through a questionnaire administered before and after the intervention. Quantitative data were analyzed using comparative statistical tests, which revealed a statistically significant increase in academic achievement levels following the implementation of the animated resource (p < 0.05), with José Vasconcelos School standing out with an improvement of approximately 50%. From a qualitative perspective, students reported greater understanding of mathematical content, as well as more dynamic and clearer learning experiences, facilitated by the use of visual and interactive material. These results are consistent with previous research indicating that audiovisual resources contribute to improving attention, motivation, and the assimilation of abstract concepts. Overall, the findings suggest that 2D animation constitutes an effective pedagogical resource for strengthening mathematics learning and promoting more meaningful educational experiences in basic education.

title: Future of Intelligence: Emerging Roles of Organoid and Quantum Intelligence
authors: A. Megalingam, Mas Ayu Hassan, Nurizzatul Atikha Rahmat, Azizuddin Abd Azi, Mohd Hazwan Yusof, K. Sudhakar
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The rapidly evolving Artificial Intelligence (AI) technology has revolutionized society. However, traditional AI systems possess limitations in terms of energy efficiency, adaptability, and scalability. This review delves into the developing paradigms of Organoid Intelligence (OI) and Quantum Intelligence (QI), which present revolutionary possibilities beyond traditional AI. Organoid Intelligence utilizes human neurons grown in the lab to develop adaptive and self-organizing bio-computational systems with ultra-low power consumption, high malleability, and potential uses in drug screening, neurological modeling, and bio-digital computing. Quantum Intelligence combines AI and quantum computing theories to employ superposition, entanglement, and quantum parallelism to tackle complex optimization problems, machine learning, and simulation at unprecedented speeds. This review discusses the current experiments, benefits, and challenges faced by these emerging paradigms to redefine computational systems and usher in the next generation of Intelligence.

title: Corrections to “Performance Enhancement in Rewound Industrial Retrofit Solutions for Five- and Six-Phase Permanent Magnet Assisted Synchronous Reluctance Machines”
authors: Kotb B. Tawfiq, Ayman M. EL-Refaie, Peter Sergeant, Hatem Zeineldin, Ahmed Al-Durra, Ehab F. El-Sadaany
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This correction provides clarification of the following in et al. 2026: (i) attribution of the rotor geometry associated with Figure 3 and (ii) correction of typographical errors in the rotor parameters reported in Table II. The updates are provided below with the exact locations, the original and corrected information, and a statement regarding their impact on the interpretation and conclusions of the paper.

title: Rescue Target Recognition Method Based on Feature Similarity Matching
authors: Rui Tan, Kunlong Hong, Jianzhong Tang, Bowen Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: With the rapid growth of global marine economic activities, the frequency and severity of maritime accidents have significantly increased, highlighting the urgent need for efficient and intelligent maritime rescue systems. Conventional search-and-rescue operations based on ship-borne equipment are often constrained by slow response times, limited spatial coverage, and high operational costs. Recent advances in deep learning have enabled substantial progress in visual object detection, offering novel technical solutions for maritime target identification. However, deep learning models face certain challenges in detecting small targets under complex marine conditions. This paper proposes a feature similarity matching-based rescue target recognition method that enhances the robustness and recall rate of maritime object detection. The proposed method integrates feature template matching with a CatBoost-based decision-tree discrimination mechanism to intelligently confirm low-confidence detections. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with the baseline YOLOv8n-p2 model, the proposed approach achieves notable improvements in recall and overall detection robustness while maintaining comparable accuracy. The integration of a Low-Confidence Non-Maximum Suppression (LC-NMS) strategy further refines the detection outputs, yielding a well-balanced trade-off between precision and recall.

title: Transformer Based Prediction Framework For Perinatal Health Multi Conditional Fetal Health And Delivery Risk Assessment From CTG Signals
authors: G M Ezhilarasan, A Chandrasekar, Mr B Blessed Sam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Cardiotocography (CTG) is commonly used in a clinical context to monitor and assess fetal health during a pregnancy. However, because the majority of CTG recordings are still on paper strips, automated analysis and clinical interpretation may be challenging to perform. This paper’s goal is to provide an advanced framework using deep learning approaches to interpret the images of CTG paper strips and provide clinically relevant predictions. A self-supervised transformer-based encoder will then be used to learn the complex temporal patterns from the CTG signals, followed by a multi-task learning architecture that will be used to provide multi-conditional clinical predictions. In contrast to traditional systems that provide classification of normal and abnormal for fetal health, this framework’s model will go further than this and provide further analysis of abnormal patterns to provide classification of individual clinical conditions such as hypoxia, bradycardia, arrhythmia, acidosis, head and/or cord compression, etc. In addition, the framework will provide estimates for perinatal distress indicators including likelihood of NICU admission, intubation requirements, and seizure risk. The framework will also provide users with predictions for most likely mode of delivery in order to facilitate clinical planning. An explainable dashboard is created to show predictions and risk indicators clearly. This supports better clinical decision-making in perinatal care.

title: Occlusion-Safe Shared Micromanipulation in Vision-Constrained Environments
authors: Leon Raphalen, Tom Goalard, Marco Ferro, Paolo Robuffo Giordano, Claudio Pacchierotti
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: Vision-constrained teleoperation systems are vulnerable to occlusions, which can affect closed-loop control and compromise both performance and safety. We present an occlusion-safe shared-control framework that prevents robots from entering unobserved regions under fixed-camera monitoring. Our method combines Control Lyapunov Functions (CLFs) and High-Order Control Barrier Functions (HOCBFs) within a Quadratic Programming (QP) solver to enforce task goals and occlusion-avoidance constraints in real time. A human operator provides high-level commands through a 6-DoF haptic interface, while an autonomous safety layer ensures continuous visibility. To help the operator understand the system behavior, we deliver haptic feedback that signals proximity to occlusions and provide visual overlays supported by a virtual reality camera for improved scene understanding.

title: An Intelligent System for Differentiating Real and AI-Generated Images Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: M. Saravanan, V. Venkata Sai Varun Kumar, G. Sai Jawahar Reddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Most recently, generative models such as GANs and diffusion architectures have developed rapidly enough to appear insane, and indeed so realistic that it is becoming difficult to distinguish between synthetic images and those of real photos. This has struck many subjects we are interested in including the digital forensics, to holding social media accountable and even identity authentication and cyber security. In this task, I am developing a smart system, which will automatically detect whether a picture is a real one or made by an AI using deep learning. I am combining convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with models based on transformers to ensure that we extract both spatial and frequency information that reveals the fact that something is generated. The tool allows uploading of image by file, or taking it by a camera or even URL, thus can be used in vivo. The model performed highly on a dataset consisting of real images and artificial ones generated by StyleGAN, Midjourney and Stable Diffusion, with a high degree of accuracy as well as good robustness. These findings set emphasis on the effectiveness of deep learning in the process of identifying AI-generated texts and why this aspect is important to authenticate digital media and ensure the safety of online environments.

title: Hybrid Rule-Based and AI-Driven Anomaly Detection for Secure IoT Gas Monitoring in Smart Buildings
authors: Olfa Gaddour, Salem Ben Moallem, Mouna Baklouti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Complex Systems and Intelligent Computing (ICCSIC)
abstract: The increasing deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) technologies in smart buildings has introduced new cybersecurity challenges, particularly for safety-critical applications such as gas leak detection systems. These systems are exposed not only to physical faults but also to cyber threats including data injection, sensor spoofing, and communication tampering, which may lead to hazardous situations if not promptly detected.This paper presents an intelligent and secure IoT-based gas monitoring framework that combines rule-based decision mechanisms with AI-driven anomaly detection to enhance cyberphysical resilience. The proposed architecture integrates a lightweight Rules Engine for real-time reaction, secure communication mechanisms to ensure data integrity and authenticity, and an unsupervised machine learning model for detecting abnormal system behavior at the edge.A threat model is defined to characterize realistic attack scenarios targeting sensor data and communication channels. Experimental evaluations are conducted on an ESP32-based prototype using real sensor data and simulated attack conditions. Results demonstrate that the AI-based anomaly detection significantly improves detection accuracy compared to static rule-based methods, while secure communication mechanisms effectively mitigate data manipulation attacks with acceptable computational overhead.The proposed approach highlights the benefits of combining intelligent detection and adaptive security mechanisms in resource-constrained IoT environments. It provides a practical and reproducible solution for enhancing the security and reliability of safety-critical smart building applications.

title: AEGIS: An Automated Evidence Collection Framework for E-commerce Infringement Based on Multi-Agent Collaboration
authors: Xiaoye Ouyang, Jing Zhang, Shijie Liu, Jing Wang, Yuan Wang, Liu Yuan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the vigorous development of the platform economy, merchant infringement activities exhibit characteristics of high concealment, diversified methods, and cross-modal features. This poses severe challenges for traditional supervision models based on keyword matching and manual evidence collection, leading to issues of incomplete detection, inaccurate identification, and low evidence collection efficiency. To address these problems, this paper proposes an automated evidence collection framework for e-commerce infringement based on multi-agent collaboration—AEGIS. This framework constructs a five-layer intelligent agent collaboration architecture comprising global command, data parallel perception, intelligent discovery of infringement leads, dynamic support from legal knowledge, and evidence chain synthesis. By leveraging domain-fine-tuned large language models to endow each agent with deep semantic understanding capabilities, the system can agilely capture potential regulatory risk leads from multi-source heterogeneous data. Furthermore, the framework introduces a causal reasoning algorithm based on weighted directed graphs, which automatically associates, deduplicates, and ranks fragmented leads to find the optimal logical path from the starting point of the behavior to the resulting harm. Experimental results demonstrate that AEGIS can effectively mine and integrate multi-dimensional infringement evidence, automatically generate logically rigorous, compliant, and legally valid structured evidence chain reports, significantly enhancing the intelligence level and enforcement effectiveness of online market supervision.

title: Prompt-to-Plan: A Multi-Agent AI Architecture for Autonomous 2D Construction Floor plan Generation
authors: Aravind Chandran, Gummadi Sarveswar Raj, Bhargav Balaram Ramanujakutam, Chapalamadugu Vasavi, Ganta Naga Venkata Mounika
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The increasing demand for rapid architectural planning has exposed inefficiencies in traditional floor plan drafting. Delays and misinterpretations frequently occur between user requirements and designer outputs. This work proposes an Agentic AI-driven system that automatically generates 2D architectural floor plans from natural language inputs, aiming to reduce the gap between client expectations and design outputs. The system contains a multi-agent pipeline coordinated through an AI orchestrator with an agentic AI framework, beginning with a NLP preprocessing agent to understand the user's prompt and extract room specifications, dimensions, constraints and to structures the data into proper JSON format for better understanding for the planning agent. A planning agent functions to structure the input to produce a logical layout plan with adjacency and spatial rules, and then it is converted into coordinate-based drafts by the layout generation agent. A validation agent will check the dimensional accuracy and constraints for the user-given prompt, creating a feedback loop to correct and refine the plan. Finally, the rendering agent gives output as the visual floor plan layout image, and the annotation agent labels the room names and dimensions. The resulting output is a high-quality and clean, annotated 2D floor plan construction layout image with minimal human interactions. Experimental results show that the proposed system outperforms baseline methods, achieving scores for layout validity as 0.92, constraint satisfaction as 0.93, and adjacency satisfaction as 0.89.

title: An Optimized Convolutional Block Attention Module-Based Method for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification
authors: Niqian Du, Zining Dang, Jinwang Feng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR), a serious ocular complication of diabetes, represents a major challenge to vision health worldwide, making its timely and precise detection essential. While deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have achieved impressive results in analyzing medical images, they typically depend on extensive datasets and may fail to emphasize fine-grained pathological cues in retinal fundus images when data is scarce. Consequently, there is considerable clinical interest in creating a reliable and efficient computer-aided diagnosis system that maintains strong performance with limited samples. In this work, we introduce a CBAM-Enhanced Deep Convolutional Neural Network (CEDCNet) designed for fast and accurate DR screening based on fundus images. The model incorporates an improved Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM), using a reduced 7×7 to 5×5 convolutional kernel to better localize subtle lesions, and an additional Batch Normalization layer is included to stabilize attention feature learning. Extensive evaluation on a targeted diabetic retinopathy dataset, including controlled experiments with fixed splits and random seeds under 30%, 40%, and 50% training data ratios, demonstrates that CEDCNet consistently achieves stable improvements in classification performance. This suggests its potential utility in supporting clinicians toward practical and reliable diagnostic assistance. Seven independent runs further confirm the model’s stable performance and the effectiveness of our optimized CBAM design.

title: Adaptive Bayesian PLV-Guided Data Augmentation for Few-Trial SSVEP-BCI Decoding
authors: Ruihan Zhang, Shuzhen Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the degradation of SSVEP decoding performance under few-trial conditions, this paper proposes an adaptive Bayesian-inspired data augmentation framework based on population PLV priors. Specifically, stable population PLV priors are first computed from cross-subject data. An adaptive weighted fusion mechanism then dynamically adjusts the contribution ratio between the population prior and individual observations, weighted by the number of calibration trials. Finally, augmented samples are incorporated with a decoupled training strategy to optimize the spatial filter and reference template. Experiments on the Benchmark dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves statistically significant improvement at a 3.5-second time window, while performance gains diminish at shorter windows where signal duration is insufficient for reliable frequency-domain augmentation. These results clarify the conditions under which population PLV priors are most beneficial for few-trial SSVEP-BCI scenarios.

title: The Role of Digital Twin Technologies in the Transition to Industry 5.0
authors: Anza Arain, Muhammad Ahsan Ansari, Sammer Zai YousufZai, Bhawani Shankar Chowdhry
publish: 2025 8th International MultiTopic ICT Conference (IMTIC)
abstract: The transition from Industry 4.0 to Industry 5.0 reflects a shift from technology-driven automation to a more human-centered and resilient industrial paradigm. Amidst this transition, Digital Twin (DT) technology has emerged as an essential component. It provides data-intensive virtual models of real-world counterparts with real-time tracking, simulation, and predictive analysis of machines, processes, and human behavior. Though the effectiveness of DTs in enhancing efficiency is widely studied, the recent literature tends to pay more attention to the fact that they contribute to the enhancement of ergonomics, personalization, sustainability, and human well-being. Integration of complementary technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Internet of Things (IoT), cloud computing, and immersive interfaces such as augmented and virtual reality (AR/VR) is cited as a significant factor in DT adaptability and innovation again and again. Comparisons across research areas also reflect DT use in intelligent human-machine interaction, circular economy optimization, and human digital representation. However, a critical review of the existing literature determines that there is still a challenge, especially concerning interoperability, ethical considerations, cybersecurity threats along with the lack of universal standards. In response, researchers have suggested various frameworks form five-layer architectures to Human Digital Twins (HDTs) and circular economyoriented models. This article summarizes and contrasts these strategies, with the aim of offering a clear picture on how DTs could not only achieve performance improvement, but deliver the Industrial 5.0 promise of a sustainable, inclusive, and human-centered industrial ecosystem.

title: Affective assessment of haptic stimuli on a large pneumatic haptic display
authors: Irene A. Kuling, Femke E. van Beek
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: The affective (or emotional) assessment of haptic stimuli is important to better understand how to use actuators and apply signals in haptic interaction design. In this study, we evaluated which affective percepts were elicited by different perceptual stimuli presented on a large soft pneumatic display. The results show that pressure, frequency, and actuation area all have a significant effect on arousal, but not on valence. These results suggest that soft pneumatic displays can convey different emotional responses. Further research is needed to translate the findings from the stimulus level to the experience level, e.g. in multisensory experiences, VR environments or teleoperated interactions.

title: BlinkSpeak: An Advanced Context-Aware Deep Learning Paradigm for Eye-Blink-Based Assistive Communication on Edge Computing Devices
authors: P Meenadharshini, R Raja Guru, B Mohan, B Premkumar, M Lavanya, R Prem Ananth, A Madhumathi
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Eye-blink-based assistive communication provides a non-invasive interface for individuals with severe motor impairments. However, existing systems typically rely on simple blink commands, lack contextual language understanding, and exhibit limited suitability for deployment on low-resource devices. This paper presents BlinkSpeak, a deep learning framework that translates structured eye-blink patterns into real-time, context-aware textual communication. The proposed system employs Vision Transformers for robust frame-level blink detection, a hybrid LSTM–Transformer architecture for temporal sequence modeling, and a transformer-based language model for coherent text generation. Experimental evaluation on the BlinkConverse dataset demonstrates a frame-level accuracy of 94.8%, precision of 93.2%, recall of 92.5%, and an ROC–AUC of 0.97. The end-to-end pipeline achieves low-latency inference suitable for real-time deployment on resource-constrained edge devices.

title: Recommendation System Framework with Integration of Learning Analytics
authors: Iva Orozova, Asya Stoyanova
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The report proposes a conceptual model for integrating AI and Learning Analytics (LA) tools into a recommendation system. Based on accumulated data from the system's work with different users, applying tools from the field of Data science, user profiles are identified. Thus, the system generates recommendations, appropriate materials and services tailored to the needs and interests of the user. A specific application is presented to support the work of medical personnel. The proposed model is the result of summaries of the processes of observation and analysis of activities in a work environment related to Learning Analytics, Data Mining, web metrics, machine learning.

title: Role-Structured Reinforcement Learning for Heterogeneous Airship-Guided UAV Pursuit Tracking
authors: Kejie Yang, Yuting Zhou, Ming Zhu, Yifei Zhang
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: This paper investigates heterogeneous aerial pursuit tracking with one high-endurance airship, multiple UAV pursuers, and evasive targets. The task is formulated as a partially observed mixed cooperative-adversarial game under centralized training and decentralized execution. A role-difference-potential multi-agent reinforcement learning framework is proposed, in which each agent maintains an individual actor and critics are shared at the role level with identity conditioning. To improve cooperative credit assignment, pursuer optimization combines potential-based shaping with counterfactual one-step difference rewards. The potential function integrates coverage guidance, approach pressure, capture-neighborhood entry, encirclement quality, and safety regularization; actions are parameterized by bounded speed and yaw rate for kinematic consistency. Simulation results show stable coordinated closure and reliable capture, indicating that role-structured entropy-regularized learning with difference-potential rewards is a practical baseline for heterogeneous pursuit tasks.

title: Cybersecurity for the AI-Driven Internet of Things: Streamlining Risk Assessment and Response in Complex and Constantly Changing Smart Technology Ecosystems
authors: Suresh Kottur, Lokesh Ramanathan Suresh, Sathiyandrakumar Srinivasan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Fast adoption of AI and the Internet of Things has changed healthcare, smart cities, transportation, and industrial automation. This enables adaptive decision-making, predictive analytics, and intelligent automation. AI-enabled IoT systems analyze enormous amounts of heterogeneous data from networked sensors, actuators, and edge devices to optimize operations. Due to system evolution, dynamic behaviors, and autonomous learning models, the attack surface is rising, making cybersecurity harder. Traditional perimeter-based protections and static risk models aren't working as IoT cybersecurity evolves. These ecosystems have decentralized architectures, various device capabilities, and dynamic threat scenarios. IoT AI models are prone to model inversion, data poisoning, and adversarial manipulation. Internet of Things devices that have been infected can result in the theft of data, the installation of malicious software, or disturbances in the decision-making process. There are concerns regarding the security of the integration of artificial intelligence with the Internet of Things. These concerns are a result of the rapid spread of vulnerabilities across several layers of systems. The purpose of this study is to respond to the cybersecurity threats that are present in AI-driven Internet of Things networks and to examine such risks.

title: Ultra-Low Power 7T SRAM Design with Power Gating for Energy-Constrained Edge Systems
authors: T. Rahul, M. Selva Sankar, M. Sukumar, B. Veerasamy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: In today's world, where devices are becoming more powerful and efficient, designing memory systems that consume less power and are tolerant to leaks has become a major challenge for IoT devices and handhelds. Since Static Random Access Memory (SRAM) is the main element of the majority of System-on-Chip (SoC) architectures, it considerably affects active and standby power usage. Due to increased leakage currents, reduced noise margins, and instability during low-voltage operation, transistor (6T) SRAM cells have really suffered from these issues. 6T SRAM cells, which are the conventional ones, continue to have a high standby leakage problem and also have limited read/write stability when the supply voltage is low. In this paper, the development, modelling, and efficiency checking of power, gated 7T SRAM cells are described in detail. These cells have been designed, manufactured, and tested using Tanner EDA tools. The newly suggested <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7 T$</tex> circuit has an extra access transistor and a gated power/ground arrangement by means of which the leakage current in standby mode is kept at a minimum level while the read and write windows have been preserved to be significantly large. The simulation results have confirmed the fact that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7 T$</tex> power gated SRAM cell proposed here is superior to an ordinary <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 T$</tex> cell by a wide margin. Key performance parameters that were evaluated at a supply voltage.

title: Multi-Sensory Digital Twin Teleoperation System with Haptic Glove-Based Tactile Feedback for Robotic Grasping
authors: Meipeng Huang, Runhui Feng, Kai Hu, Xiongbin Yu, Longhao Zou, Jin Xie, Zexin Deng, Zhenhui Yuan
publish: 2025 IEEE Conference on Telepresence
abstract: With the widespread application of remote robotic operations in fields such as healthcare and industry, achieving safe and precise multimodal haptic feedback has become a critical challenge. This study presents a robotic grasping multi-sensory teleoperation system based on a haptic glove and film-based piezoelectric sensors, which enhances remote operation immersion and control accuracy by integrating visual, force, and vibration feedback. The system hardware includes a wearable haptic glove, a robotic arm gripper (integrated with film-based piezoelectric sensors), and a digital-twin platform based on ROS-Unity. Edge computing is employed to achieve lowlatency physical-virtual state synchronization. The experiment selects two types of objects, a soft ball and a wooden block, for grasping tests, comparing three modes: no feedback, single pressure feedback, and multi-sensory feedback. The results show that the multi-sensory feedback group reduced the task completion time by 37%, decreased the grasping error rate to below 5%, and significantly improved contact force stability. NASA-TLX results confirm that integrated visual-haptic feedback significantly reduced mental workload. This study bridges the sensory-motor gap in remote manipulation through collaborative DT modeling, physics-based force simulation, and adaptive haptic encoding.

title: Vision-Based Dynamic Detection of Light Characteristics of Aids to Navigation at Night
authors: Zhengliang Wang, Mingyang Pan, Yu Liang, Yang Zhang, Ruolan Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To achieve intelligent detection of navigation lights during the night and enhance detection accuracy, this study develops a computer-vision-based navigation light characteristic detection network. The proposed method first extracts the color information of navigation lights using the improved YOLOv8-based detection module (SCW-YOLOv8), then analyzes the flickering time-series of target lights via a classification module, and finally integrates color and temporal rhythm information to generate comprehensive detection results. In the network design, the detection module builds upon YOLOv8, incorporating a small-object detection module (SPD-Conv), embedding a coordinate attention mechanism within the neck, and refining the loss function, thereby enabling precise recognition of navigation light colors from video sequences. The classification module employs a dual-layer LSTM architecture to efficiently capture the flickering frequency of navigation lights. Model training and evaluation were performed on approximately 200 minutes of navigation light video data. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves a detection accuracy of up to 91.6%, while maintaining real-time processing capability suitable for practical maritime applications.

title: Road Damage Detection and Classification using Drone Images
authors: Chinnathambi Kamatchi, Abhiram Seemakurthi, Jaswanth Reddy Madireddy
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Road infrastructure is the basis on which urban development, public safety, and transit operate. However, traditional manual inspection techniques are slow, require a lot of human work, and are susceptible to human error. This study introduces an automated approach using highresolution drone images to locate and classify damage. The proposed system can, in principle, accurately differentiate among potholes, longitudinal cracks, transverse cracks, and alligator cracks by using a specially designed CNN stands for convolutional neural network. To enhance model generalization, the dataset of 3800 hand-annotated drone images is subjected to preprocessing operations such as scaling, normalisation, and data augmentation. The lightweight CNN architecture, which makes a compromise between computational efficiency and depth thereby enabling real-time implementation, is the reason for this. In comparison to standard models like VGG16 and ResNet50, the experimental evaluation yields an overall classification accuracy of 91 percent with strong recall and precision being reported for all the classes of the damage. This automated technique, which also facilitates predictive maintenance, smart city infrastructure planning, and improves public safety, is a solution for rapid, reliable, and cost- effective road condition monitoring. The proposed system is a scalable solution for large rural and urban road networks.

title: A Fitting Method for Discharge Acoustic Waves Based on a Five-Atom Gabor Model
authors: Yuling Liu, Yong Li, Guanghua Wang, Yafei Qin, Jin Yang, Li Zhang, Xue Li, Xiaohan Ma
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: High-voltage discharge acoustic signals possess significant non-stationary and broadband characteristics. Addressing the difficulty in accurately describing the continuous physical evolution process using discrete acoustic sampling signals, this paper proposes a sparse decomposition and analytical reconstruction method based on a five-atom Gabor dictionary. First, an air-gap discharge experimental platform excited by a Tesla coil using a needle-to-ring electrode configuration was established. Subsequently, a five-atom Gabor linear superposition mathematical model was constructed, and the optimal parameter set was solved using Constrained Non-linear Least Squares (CNLS) combined with the Trust-Region Reflective algorithm. Experimental verification demonstrates that the five-atom model achieves the optimal balance between complexity and precision. This method successfully constructs a high-precision continuous mathematical analytical model for discharge acoustic signals, providing a reliable mathematical foundation for the quantitative analysis of discharge faults and mechanism research.

title: Research on Non-invasive Detection of Seedling Substrate Moisture Content Based on Capacitance Method
authors: Hongxu Tian, Jun Wei
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: To address the urgent demand for rapid and non-invasive detection of substrate moisture content in factory seedling cultivation, this paper designs and implements a high-stability capacitance detection system. Aiming at the limitation that traditional capacitance methods are susceptible to parasitic capacitance and coupling gap variations in dynamic detection environments, this paper proposes a non-contact sensing scheme based on a relative measurement strategy. To further enhance system stability, an auxiliary adaptive mechanical locking mechanism is introduced to suppress external environmental interference. By ensuring a constant geometric relative position through rigid constraints at the moment of measurement, the system effectively eliminates errors caused by positioning deviations. Experimental results demonstrate that in the full range of 0-170% (gravimetric water content), the cubic polynomial inversion model established by this system achieves excellent linearity and accuracy, with a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) reaching 0.998. The system features non-invasiveness, fast response, and strong robustness, providing a reliable online detection solution for precise moisture regulation of seedling substrates.

title: Anti-Misalignment Parameter Design for LCC-S Systems Based on Impedance Reshaping
authors: Weidong Lang, Fei Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In dynamic wireless power transfer (WPT) applications, random coil misalignment causes significant mutual inductance fluctuations, leading to power instability and efficiency drops. To address the high sensitivity of conventional full-resonance LCC-S topologies, this paper proposes an impedance reconfiguration-based parameter detuning strategy. A "zero-sensitivity" tuning method is derived to reshape the power-mutual inductance curve into a "saddle-shaped" profile. Zero-voltage switching (ZVS) boundary conditions are also integrated to ensure high efficiency. Simulink results (100W) demonstrate a maximum power fluctuation of 5.8% and peak efficiency of 91.2% under a 0-80mm misalignment range, validating the proposed method's robustness.

authors: Vijay Bala Mahalingam, Harish Parthasarathy, Aakash Baskaran, Bharath Chandrasekaran, Muthukrishnan Rajeshkumar
