title: A High-Accuracy, Hybrid Scalable Model for MIM Capacitors over Wide Temperature for MMIC Applications
authors: Zeng Li, Ziyue Zhao, Wei Zhao, Yang Lu, Chupeng Yi, Xin Liu, Ting Feng, Yilin Chen, Jiejie Zhu, Xiaohua Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Conventional Process Design Kits for Monolithic Microwave Integrated Circuits (MMICs) often lack capacitor models that are robust across wide temperature and frequency ranges, posing a significant challenge to the design reliability and first-pass success. To address this, a novel π-type equivalent circuit with grounding inductors is developed, yielding S-parameter fitting errors under 2% while also explaining the device’s thermal resonant drift. Building on this, a hybrid scalable model synergizing a physics-guided core with neural network compensation is proposed. This methodology demonstrates exceptional accuracy, achieving parameter-fitting R-squared values above 0.999. More importantly, the final model’s S-parameter predictions maintain an R-squared greater than 0.98 against measured data across the entire temperature and geometry space. This work provides a highly accurate, scalable MIM capacitor model essential for robust circuit design under harsh operating conditions and offers a generalizable hybrid methodology applicable to other passive components, enhancing the predictive power of modern PDKs.

title: An Empathetic Response Generation Framework Based on the Cognition-Emotion Chain-of-Thought
authors: Yujie Wan, Zaiquan Dong, Yao Li, Hanwen Zhou, Baoxing Jiang, Shenggen Ju
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: The goal of empathetic response generation is to enable dialogue systems to accurately understand and generate responses that reflect the seeker's experiences and emotions. Although recent research has made significant progress, most existing methods primarily rely on surface-level semantic cues to analyze emotions, thereby neglecting the seeker's cognitive state and the causal relationship between cognition and emotion. Furthermore, these methods lack the ability to model and control emotional intensity, often resulting in generated responses that fail to meet the seeker's needs. To this end, we propose an Empathetic Response Generation Framework based on the Cognition-Emotion Chain-of-Thought (ERF-CECoT). This framework achieves end-to-end coordination by integrating cognitive distortion detection, adaptive emotion routing, dynamic intensity constraints, and empathetic response generation: First, the cognitive distortion detector and adaptive emotion router dynamically capture the seeker's cognitive state and emotions. Second, the response constraint mechanism based on emotional intensity dynamically adjusts the model's generation boundaries in accordance with the seeker's emotional intensity. Finally, it constructs a prompt template based on a cognition-emotion chain-of-thought, guiding the model to integrate information such as the seeker's emotion and cognitive state to perform step-by-step reasoning and generate responses. Experimental results from multiple datasets (including both automated and human evaluations) demonstrate that our method effectively and jointly detects the seeker's cognitive state and emotion while significantly improving the quality of empathetic responses.

title: PMDCFNet: A Progressive Multi-Domain Collaborative Fusion Network for Optical Image Cloud and Shadow Removal
authors: Zhongchen He, Tianman Sun, Mengxi Zhu, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Significant progress has been made in the reconstruction of cloud-free images using SAR images as auxiliary information over the past few years. However, existing methods typically face the challenges of modal heterogeneity and insufficient available features due to cloud coverage and speckle noise. These methods only consider the spatial domain, which is not enough to achieve satisfactory results. To address the above issues, we propose a progressive multi-domain feature fusion network that consists of four stages, named as PMDCFNet. To our knowledge, PMDCFNet combines the advantages of the gradient, spatial, and frequency domain to improve the performance. Specifically, we first design a multi-scale gradient feature enhancement module in the feature extraction stage, which introduces the central difference convolution apart from traditional vanilla convolution to extract magnitude and gradient features simultaneously. Subsequently, the One-stage transformer is proposed to capture intermodal complementary and intramodal contextual relationships in the feature interaction stage, which integrates a sparse cross-attention and spatial-channel self-attention mechanisms into a unified network to reconstruct the features of cloud-contaminated regions. Then the frequency feature fusion module is established in the feature fusion stage, which combines the global characteristics of the Fourier transform with the local characteristics of the wavelet transform to fully fuse optical and SAR features in the frequency domain. Extensive experimental results confirm that PMDCFNet is much more competitive with eight state-of-the-art approaches in respect to the visual and objective evaluation results on the SEN12MS-CR and SMILE-CR datasets.

title: Causality Based data-Augmentation for Offline Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wei Zhang, Azlaan Mustafa Samad, Yuan Xue, Daniel Kudenko
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Offline reinforcement learning (RL) seeks to derive an optimal decision-making policy from a static dataset, addressing scenarios in which interaction with the environment is impossible or data collection is costly or risky. The inherent limitation of the static dataset, incomplete state-action space coverage, leads to extrapolation errors when out-of-distribution data points are encountered during policy training. One way to mitigate this problem is to augment the static dataset by generating new data points, thereby broadening the state-action space available for policy training. These new data points should follow the same distribution as the original static dataset. The static dataset is collected by a behavioral policy whose decision-making logic determines the distribution. In this work, we propose Causality Based data-Augmentation (CBA), a novel framework that models the Markov Decision Process (MDP) in the form of a Structural Causal Model (SCM) and augments the static dataset while explicitly accounting for the decision-making logic of the behavior policy. Experimental results demonstrate that CBA enhances policy performance by augmenting static datasets with higher reward transitions, highlighting its potential for advancing research in offline RL.

title: BELLTP: Bandwidth-Efficient and Low-Latency Transaction Propagation in Ethereum Network
authors: Chonghe Zhao, Deen Ma, Shengli Zhang, Taotao Wang, Lisheng Fan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Ethereum is a leading blockchain system whose security and performance depend critically on efficient transaction propagation. Currently, to curb bandwidth, Ethereum employs a hybrid protocol wherein nodes eager-push full transactions to a random peer subset and announce hashes to the remainder for on-demand lazy-pull. Yet, our MainNet measurements reveal that this strategy suffers from “blind” forwarding—indiscriminately transmitting full transactions without evaluating receiver acceptance. This causes peers to receive multiple redundant copies of non-viable transactions that are ultimately discarded by local pools. Also, randomized peer selection results in substantial redundant transmission, excessive hops, and a high lazy-pull rate (the proportion of transactions acquired via the slower request-response mechanism). These two inefficiencies collectively prolong propagation latency and limit further bandwidth savings. To address these limitations, we propose BELLTP, a Bandwidth-Efficient and Low-Latency Transaction Propagation protocol that incorporates two key awareness modules. Transaction Awareness classifies transactions into “urgent” and “leisure” using a lightweight predictor trained on Mainnet data to forecast receiver acceptance. Based on this, leisure transactions (with low predicted acceptance) are propagated via full lazy-pull to ensure that each node receives the full payload once, while urgent transactions utilize strategically targeted eager-push guided by node awareness to rectify random selection inefficiencies. Node Awareness identifies hub and common nodes for urgent transaction propagation by combining a theoretical network model, enabling hub nodes to eagerly push to all peers (shortening hop count while maintaining low redundant transmission) and common nodes to leverage a peer need score to eagerly push only to high-need peers (lowering lazy-pull rate). Extensive experiments on Ethereum MainNet and in a controlled local testbed demonstrate that BELLTP significantly outperforms the current protocol in both bandwidth consumption and propagation latency.

title: Uplink Short-Packet Communications in Symbiotic IoT Networks: Theoretical Analysis and Resource Allocation
authors: Tan N. Nguyen, Thai-Hoc Vu, Anh-Tu Le, Thuong Le-Tien, Miroslav Voznak
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The advent of next-generation Internet of Things (IoT) networks necessitates communication capabilities characterized by ultra-reliability and low latency (URLLC) and energy efficiency requirements that traditional wireless designs frequently struggle to satisfy under practical constraints. In this context, realizing symbiotic IoT networks using mutualism backscattering technologies with short-packet transmissions has emerged as a viable solution to achieve the goals of net zero for sustainable IoT network deployment while satisfying URLLC conditions. Following that, this paper first develops theoretical frameworks for evaluating the performance of short packet transmissions in uplink symbiotic systems by deriving formulas for the block error rate (BLER) and total symbiotic ergodic rate under two diversity techniques, namely selection combining with maximal-ratio combining (SC-MRC) and full maximal-ratio combining (Full-MRC). Then, we scale up this considered system for large-scale IoT networks, where we propose an energy-aware resource allocation method using successive convex approximation (SCA) techniques for multi-user scenarios while adhering to URLLC conditions. Finally, simulation results are provided to validate the developed theoretical analyses as well as demonstrate how our proposed optimization solution achieves substantial improvements in energy efficiency performance. These findings lay a robust groundwork for developing scalable and dependable communication systems in future energy-efficient IoT eras.

title: A Comparative Techno-Economic Analysis of Traditional and AI-Driven Predictive Solar Photovoltaic Pumping Systems for Banana Plantations
authors: Krismadinata, Leo Raju, M. Pandikumar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article provides a detailed comparison of the economics and technology of two different types of Solar Photovoltaic Water Pump Systems (SPVWPS); one type is a traditional SPVWPS and the other is an AI based Predictive Solar Pump System for banana production in the semi-arid regions of Theni district of Tamil Nadu, India. In this study the new AI system is made up of a high-fidelity sensor array (weather, soil moisture, evapotranspiration), a Cloud-Edge Computing Architecture made possible by a Texas Instruments TMS320F28379D DSP for real time motor control and an STM32H743 Microprocessor for coordinating all of the components of the system. An LSTM Network Based Predictive Algorithm uses historical data on the banana crop to predict when the crop needs water, allowing for optimal scheduling of irrigation, providing water at the precise time it is needed. For the eight-month trial period of the 5 HP Prototype, results indicated the AI system provided 33.7 percent savings in water use, and a 19.2 percent increase in banana yield (tons/hectare) over the traditional timer based SPVWPS used as a reference point for this research. Economic evaluations indicated the AI system would provide a 0.93 year payback (11.2 months) for the additional costs associated with implementing the AI system; these savings are derived from reduced water usage and increased yields. These results validate the ability of intelligent resource utilization in precision agriculture to produce positive environmental impacts.

title: Resilient Sector Scheduling Scheme for Wireless Rechargeable Sensor Networks
authors: Chun-Hsien Lin, Yen-Ting Lin, Yingrong Coral Sung, Meng-Hsun Tsai
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Wireless Rechargeable Sensor Networks (WRSNs) extend the operational lifetime of battery-powered sensor deployments through Wireless Power Transfer (WPT). Traditional approaches often address node clustering, charging strategies, and charger routing independently, frequently failing to adapt to real-time demand variations and network survivability. This paper proposes a two-phase Sector-based Charging with Resilient Scheduling (SCRS) scheme designed for WRSNs utilizing fixed directional chargers. In Phase I, the SCRS scheme provides initial energy to mitigate the risk of early sensor outages. Phase II employs a quantile-based strategy to dynamically allocate charging time, thereby tightly constraining energy overflow. By optimizing charging schedules based on real-time demands, SCRS enhances energy utilization and improves network survivability. Simulation results demonstrate that SCRS reduces charging time by up to 62.96%, decreases energy overflow by up to 88.89%, and achieves near-zero sensor outage durations on average across broad configurations. Furthermore, the proposed scheme completely eliminates outages under specific high-agility configurations (e.g., utilizing the Q1 setting). Crucially, the validation context of this study incorporates rich stochastic radio models, including Nakagami-m fading and log-normal shadowing, to closely emulate realistic multipath and obstructed environments. Consequently, while the analytical bounds and comparative advantages of the SCRS scheme are robust within these environments, transitioning these findings to highly mobile real-world IoT deployments will necessitate further empirical validation in physical testbeds.

title: Analysis of CS Curriculum using Automatic Syllabus Crawling Methodology
authors: Takayuki Sekiya, Yoshitatsu Matsuda, Kazunori Yamaguchi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Contribution: We developed a method for collecting computer science (CS)-related syllabi from university websites with comparatively little effort. Using a large number of crawled web pages, we quantitatively and statistically analyzed the CS curricula of universities. Background: A syllabus defines the prerequisites, content, and outcomes of courses offered by an educational institution, as well as other information such as resources and assignments. Therefore, syllabus analysis was also used to understand the trends in educational content. We need a large number of syllabi to be generated automatically and regularly with minimal effort, so that the results of the statistical analysis are more convincing. However, collecting syllabus web pages is extremely time-consuming. Research Questions: Can we obtain syllabus information from many universities by utilizing crawling techniques? Can we demonstrate that using crawling techniques is comparable to manually collecting information? Can we demonstrate the findings obtained by collecting syllabus information from universities? Methodology: To crawl web pages, we utilized the Google Custom Search API and Scrapy, a Python-based general-purpose crawler. We extracted text from crawled webpages using Trafilatula. We also use Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) to detect a syllabus, and analyze syllabi with a simplified version of the supervised LDA (ssLDA) using the Computer Science Curricula 2013 (CS2013). Findings: We examined approximately 7,700 semi-automatically collected web pages and found that approximately 66% were syllabus pages. When we divided the group of universities that obtained syllabi into “Baccalaureate,” “Master,” and “Doctor” according to the Carnegie Classification, and analyzed curricula using the Body of Knowledge (BOK) of CS2013, we discovered that the syllabi of universities classified as Doctor-level universities were statistically significantly more varied than those of universities classified as Master or Baccalaureate.

title: MotionGPT-2: A General-Purpose Motion-Language Model for Motion Generation and Understanding
authors: Yuan Wang, Di Huang, Yaqi Zhang, Wanli Ouyang, Jile Jiao, Xuetao Feng, Dan Xu, Shixiang Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Generating lifelike human motions from descriptive texts has experienced remarkable research focus in recent years, propelled by the emerging requirements of digital humans. Despite impressive advances, existing approaches are often constrained by limited control modalities, task specificity, and focus solely on body motion representations. In this paper, we present MotionGPT-2, a unified Large Motion-Language Model (LMLM) that addresses these limitations. MotionGPT-2 accommodates multiple motion-relevant tasks and supports multimodal control conditions through pre-trained Large Language Models (LLMs). It quantizes multimodal inputs—such as text and single-frame poses—into discrete, LLM-interpretable tokens, seamlessly integrating them into the LLM’s vocabulary. These tokens are then organized into unified prompts, guiding the LLM to generate motion outputs through a pretraining-then-finetuning paradigm. We also show that the proposed MotionGPT-2 is highly adaptable to the challenging 3D holistic motion generation task, enabled by the innovative motion discretization framework, Part-Aware VQVAE, which facilitates fine-grained representations of body and hand movements. Extensive experiments and visualizations validate the effectiveness of our method, demonstrating the adaptability of MotionGPT-2 across motion generation, motion captioning, and generalized motion completion tasks.

title: LSCM-GAN: A GAN for Thermal Infrared Image Colorization using Vmamba with Large Kernel and Spatial-Channel Attention
authors: Yang Gao, Qian Jiang, Hongyue Huang, Shiwei Wang, Wei Zhou, Xin Jin
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Thermal infrared (TIR) imaging is effective in harsh environments such as low-light, nighttime, and smoky conditions, as it captures temperature distributions that are invisible in the visible spectrum. However, the lack of color cues and limited spatial detail significantly restrict its interpretability. To address this challenge, this work proposes a TIR image colorization method termed LSCM-GAN, which integrates Vmamba with a generative adversarial framework. Specifically, a newly designed Vmamba-based generator with skip connection is employed to enhance multi-level feature representation. LKNet is embedded within the VSSBlock to model both local and global dependencies, while a spatial-channel selective scanning module (SCSS2D) is introduced to strengthen spatial and channel-wise feature discrimination. In addition, a composite loss function is adopted to improve structural consistency and perceptual quality. Extensive experiments are conducted on seven public datasets, including KAIST, IRVI, LLVIP, OSU Color-Thermal, Pedestrian Infrared/Visible Stereo, DIAST, and TNO. The results demonstrate that LSCM-GAN achieves superior or highly competitive performance compared with selected state-of-the-art methods on both quantitative metrics and visual quality, indicating its robust effectiveness across diverse thermal-visible scenarios.

title: Hard-Label Black-Box Attacks on 3D Point Clouds
authors: Daizong Liu, Yunbo Tao, Junhao Dong, Keke Tang, Pan Zhou, Wei Hu, Yew-Soon Ong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: With the maturity of depth sensors in various 3D safety-critical applications, 3D point cloud models have been shown to be vulnerable to adversarial attacks. Almost all existing 3D attackers simply follow the white-box or black-box setting to iteratively update coordinate perturbations based on back-propagated or estimated gradients. However, these methods are hard to deploy in real-world scenarios (no model details are provided) as they severely rely on parameters or output logits of victim models. To this end, we propose point cloud attacks from a more practical setting, i.e., hard-label black-box attack, in which attackers can only access the prediction label of 3D input. We introduce a novel 3D attack method based on a new spectrum-aware decision boundary algorithm to generate high-quality adversarial samples. In particular, we first construct a class-aware model decision boundary, by developing a learnable spectrum-fusion strategy to adaptively fuse point clouds of different classes in the spectral domain, aiming to craft their intermediate samples without distorting the original geometry. Then, we devise an iterative coordinate-spectrum optimization method with curvature-aware boundary search to move the intermediate sample along the decision boundary for generating adversarial point clouds with trivial perturbations. Experiments demonstrate that our attack competitively outperforms existing white/black-box attackers in terms of attack performance and adversary quality.

title: Particle-Subspace Joint Filtering for Multi-Source DOA Tracking With Single-Snapshot Observations
authors: Jingchao You, Zhikun Chen, Zhibin Chen
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: In this letter, the problem of direction of arrival (DOA) tracking for time-varying signal sources with single-snapshot observations is studied. A novel particle-subspace joint filtering (PST) algorithm is proposed, which simultaneously tracks multiple DOAs and the signal subspace without relying on covariance matrix computation. To capture the temporal dynamics, the low-rank subspace is incorporated into the particle state and updated recursively via the recursive least squares (RLS) method. Furthermore, an enhanced weighted likelihood function is employed by integrating the reconstruction residual likelihood with an orthogonality constraint, effectively balancing observation consistency and subspace structure. Numerical simulations show that the proposed method achieves robust tracking under single-snapshot conditions with crossing sources, achieving a PROC greater than 92.4% when the SNR exceeds 15 dB, outperforming the other algorithms.

title: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Distinguishing Point-Requirement Acceptance From High-Confidence Reliability Assurance in Engineering Practice
authors: Huei-Yaw Ke
publish: IEEE Reliability Magazine
abstract: Qualification testing programs in engineering practice are often interpreted as evidence that a reliability requirement has been satisfied with high confidence. However, such interpretations depend critically on the statistical objective embedded in the test design. This article clarifies the distinction between two inferential frameworks that are frequently conflated in practice: decision risk-based acceptance, which controls producers’ and consumers’ risks in hypothesis testing, and high-confidence reliability assurance, which supports parameter-level claims through confidence-based inference. Although both frameworks may employ similar statistical tools, they address fundamentally different questions and control different forms of uncertainty. As a result, a “pass” outcome under an acceptance test does not, in general, imply that a reliability requirement has been demonstrated with a specified confidence level. Failure to recognize this distinction can lead to mischaracterization of acceptance outcomes as assurance statements. This work does not introduce new statistical methods; rather, it provides a structured clarification of an often-overlooked distinction in reliability engineering practice. Through analytical comparison and illustrative examples, the article shows how identical reliability targets, when embedded in different statistical frameworks, can lead to fundamentally different interpretations. The results highlight the importance of aligning test design, statistical inference, and engineering interpretation in reliability qualification and assurance.

title: Development and Validation of a Freehand 3D Ultrasound System for Real-Time Intraoral Image Reconstruction_supp1-3690813.pdf
authors: Lawrence H. Le
publish: N/A
abstract: Two-dimensional (2D) ultrasonography has shown great potential for evaluating periodontal structures due to its portability, flexibility, and non-ionizing nature. However, for clinical applications, three-dimensional (3D) ultrasound imaging of oral anatomy is essential. This study presents the development of a freehand 3D intraoral imaging system using high-frequency ultrasound for reconstructing the 3D anatomical structures of teeth. The proposed hand-held system integrates an in-house designed ultrasound transducer with positioning markers and four optical cameras to acquire 2D ultrasound images along with corresponding 3D tracking poses. A forward-mapping algorithm was employed for 3D data fusion to enable real-time imaging. Both dimensional measurements and 3D visualized difference colormaps were generated for a total of 24 teeth from maxillary and mandibular phantoms to compare the proposed system with an optical scanner and cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT). The mean distance differences ranged from -0.13 to 0.18 mm versus the optical scanner and from -0.18 to 0.15 mm versus CBCT. The overall mean absolute difference across all dimensional measurements of two raters were 0.17 mm for optical scanner and 0.16 mm for CBCT, respectively. The correlation coefficient with both reference modalities exceeded 0.98. The average model-to-model distance between the 3D volumes acquired from the proposed system and the corresponding reference volumes was approximately 0.20 mm for the optical scanner and 0.28 mm for CBCT. The proposed 3D intraoral imaging system demonstrated the feasibility of achieving high-quality 3D intraoral imaging in real time, showing the potential to significantly accelerate the clinical adoption of ultrasound technology in dentistry.

title: Multi-Level EEG Signal Analysis for Mental Workload Classification using Hybrid CNN-Transformer-BiLSTM Model
authors: Sai Meghana Penumaka, Hema Kumar Goru, Nanda Kishore Reddy Gadi, Gnaneswari Sirigudi
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: For adaptive human-machine interaction systems and real-time cognitive monitoring, accurate mental workload (MWL) classification is necessary. Reliable workload discrimination is significantly complicated by the fact that electroencephalogram (EEG) data are inherently non-stationary and susceptible to different types of noise and artifacts, despite being highly informative for underlying cognitive processes. In order to overcome these difficulties, this paper suggests a Multi-Level EEG-based MWL classification framework that uses a hybrid deep learning architecture that combines Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), a Transformer Encoder, and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks to differentiate between easy, medium, and high workload states. At various stages of the framework, Multi-Level workload-related features were extracted after EEG preprocessing steps such as bandpass filtering, windowing, and normalization. CNN layers were used to learn spatial characteristics, the Transformer Encoder network was used to capture global contextual interactions, and the BiLSTM network was used to model temporal dependencies. Class separation and overall classification performance were considerably enhanced by integrating these complimentary feature representations. The proposed model was implemented in MATLAB R2025b and the suggested model outperformed current baseline methods with an accuracy of 99.27%, specificity 99.64%, precision 99.27%, Matthew's correlation coefficient (MCC) 98.71%, Cohen's kappa 98.71%, and F1-score 99.27%.

title: Data Center Cost Reduction and Efficiency Enhancement-Storage Structure Optimization Based on MSVR Model
authors: Yiqun Liu, Xin Miao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In the era of artificial intelligence, data has become a new factor of production that has a profound impact on all levels of society. As a critical infrastructure in the data era, data centers are facing major challenges in terms of how to effectively control costs, improve operational efficiency, and ensure that performance meets the needs of technology iteration. In this paper, we propose a multi-scale supported vector regression (MSVR) model to predict the cost and performance of data storage centers at different storage media ratios, revealing a high correlation between storage structure and data center cost. According to the research results, the model proposed in this paper achieves a data center cost reduction of up to 12.7% under the premise of the same performance. At the same time, the model passed the generalization test, and when applied to data centers in the Asia-Pacific region, the MSVR model was significantly better than the traditional polynomial regression model in terms of generalization ability. These findings provide actionable insights for data center managers and investors to reduce costs and increase efficiency in data centers, ensuring that data centers provide a solid foundation for the digital economy.

title: Leveraging AI Workflows to Boost Fragmented Work Information Absorption in the Information Explosion Era
authors: Duyou Guo, Yu Yang, Jinyu Li, Yilin Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In the digital age, instant messaging tools have become the main channel for workplace communication. However, the resulting information fragmentation and overload consume substantial cognitive resources and reduce collaboration efficiency. Existing automation solutions struggle to effectively process multi-source heterogeneous unstructured information flows. To solve these problems, this study proposes a method that integrates artificial intelligence workflows and large language models, and designs and implements an intelligent information management system named "ComFlow". The system uses the open-source low-code platform n8n as its core workflow engine, supporting non-intrusive collection and standardized processing of multi-platform information. It realizes semantic understanding and intent recognition of messages through large language models, and introduces a message value quantification model based on group decision-making theory to achieve intelligent information sorting and summarization. Experimental results show that the system can convert unstructured chat records into structured tasks and summaries, significantly reducing manual processing time. This study verifies the feasibility of the proposed technical approach and provides a low-cost and highly flexible solution for organizations and individuals to address information overload.

title: Electrochemically driven dynamic switching between transparent and opaque radiative cooling
authors: Jingjing Mao, Shenghao Jin, Lin Zhang, Boxiang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: We report an electrochemically driven colorful smart window based on reversible silver electrodeposition and a coupled planar nanocavity, enabling switching between a colorful transparent radiative cooling state and a colorful opaque reflective state for efficient solar thermal management.

title: Design of a Simultaneous Wireless Power and Data Transfer System for Electric Vehicles Based on Series Capacitor Compensation
authors: Jingli Zhang, Jiayi Ji, Lin Hu, Chaoyue Zhao, Zhizhen Liu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: With the rapid development of new energy vehicles, wireless power transfer (WPT) technology has been widely adopted due to its safety and convenience. However, in high-power scenarios, interference from energy transmission to signal communication has become a critical challenge. This paper addresses high-power wireless charging systems for electric vehicles by proposing a capacitor compensation-based scheme for simultaneous energy and signal transmission. The system employs a dual-side LCC high-order topology to avoid creating additional loops for the signal and integrates a LC wave-blocking network to achieve full-duplex communication. By appropriately matching parameters and adding resonant capacitors to the energy transmission coils, interference from the energy loop to the signal channel is significantly suppressed. Experimental results show that the system achieves an output power of 7.86 kW at an energy frequency of 85 kHz with an efficiency of 92.8%, while supporting a bidirectional signal transmission rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$200 \text{kbit} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The signal gain remains stable, and the peak interference voltage from the energy channel is reduced by over 70%, meeting the requirements of high-power applications. This study provides a reliable solution for simultaneous wireless power and data transfer (SWPDT) in high-power wireless charging of electric vehicles.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition System Using Brain & Physiological Signals
authors: Saravanan S, Sachin M A, Radha N, L Jenitha Mary, Swathika R
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Emotion recognition plays a crucial role in the development of intelligent and adaptive systems for applications such as healthcare monitoring, human–computer interaction, and affective computing. Unimodal emotion recognition approaches often suffer from limited accuracy and poor generalization due to physiological noise and inter-subject variability. To address these limitations, this paper presents an ensemble-based multimodal emotion recognition framework that integrates electroencephalography (EEG), galvanic skin response (GSR), and blood volume pulse (BVP) signals. Each modality undergoes independent preprocessing and feature extraction, capturing neural oscillatory patterns, electrodermal activity, and cardiovascular dynamics. The extracted features are fused at the feature level and classified using ensemble machine learning models, including Random Forest and XGBoost. Experiments conducted on the DEAP dataset demonstrate that the proposed multimodal framework consistently outperforms unimodal approaches across multiple emotional dimensions, achieving up to 95.56% accuracy for valence classification. Unlike prior end-to-end deep learning methods, the proposed approach emphasizes interpretable feature-level fusion combined with ensemble learning, providing a robust and reliable solution for real-world physiological emotion recognition systems.

title: Tri-Plane-Based Style Transfermation for 3DGS
authors: Hongxi Li, Bin Liu, Yujun Zhang, Zhifen He, Bo Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: We propose a light-weight method for style transfer in 3D Gaussian scenes. This method realises style information encoding in a 3D feature space correlated spatially by using tri-plane feature representations, and then features are decoded through a learnable multi-layer perceptron (MLP) to generate new RGB values that correspond to center positions of the Gaussian sphere. We have achieved image rendering with target-style characteristics while maintaining the scene’s geometric structure. We compare our method with two state-of-the-art zero-shot 3D-Gaussian stylisation baselines. Based on the experiment, this method can ensure high-rendering quality and have strong style-representation capability and good computation efficiency.

title: Hybrid Market Trend Predictor With Event Alerts, Sentiment Visualization And Portfolio Recommendation Using AI
authors: M Nandhitha, Y Rithika Sri, R Revathi, S. Malathi, S Vimala
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Predicting stock market behavior is challenging due to its highly dynamic nature influenced by economic conditions, investor psychology, and real-world events. This project introduces a Hybrid Market Trend Prediction System that combines technical analysis, sentiment evaluation, and event-based insights to improve forecasting accuracy. The system allows analysis of multiple stocks simultaneously and provides real-time alerts based on significant financial or global events. It also visualizes public sentiment derived from news and social platforms to help investors better understand market trends. Additionally, a portfolio recommendation module suggests optimal investment strategies based on predicted outcomes and user risk preferences. By integrating both numerical and textual data sources, the proposed system enhances decision-making and supports smarter investment planning.

title: Exploration and Practice of a Training Model for Top Innovative Talents in Compiler Systems in the Context of Artificial Intelligence
authors: Min Dong, Sheng Bi
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence technology is driving profound transformations in the field of compiler systems. Based on years of teaching practice, this paper proposes and implements a novel training model for top innovative talents in compiler systems, characterized by "AI Empowerment + Multi-dimensional Integration". The model’s implmentation involves building an intelligent teaching system with a dynamic knowledge graph at its core, implementing a multi-dimensional integration strategy, a reformed curriculum, innovative teaching methods, and strengthened practical modules. The model has effectively boosted students' innovative capacities and engineering practice skills. Practice has shown its significant effectiveness in fostering student development and elevating teaching quality, providing a valuable reference for talent cultivation in China's foundational software industry.

title: Design and Validation of an IoT-Based Real-Time Soil and Water Quality Monitoring Kit for Sustainable Aquaculture
authors: Siva Asapu, Kalyan Sagar Kadali, Sreenivasula Reddy Pilli, Buddha Hema Rishitha, Bhargavi Gandi, Raju Lakkabattula
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Aquaculture can significantly contribute to a country's economic growth. Before introducing a new group of prawns or fish to aquaculture ponds, it's essential to test the soil and water. Traditional agricultural methods are inefficient because collecting soil and water samples by hand and then transporting them to a lab for analysis requires a lot of time and effort. Farmers in rural areas sometimes face delays of more than two days before receiving their soil test results; due to this delay, farmers may suffer reduced crop productivity and financial losses. Aquaculture farmers could benefit from a quick solution using a real time monitoring kit. The main goal of this work is to develop a IoT (Internet of Things) based real – time soil and water quality monitoring kit for aquaculture farmers. The main goal of the proposed kit is to measure soil nutrients: nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K), using the JXBS-3001-NPK-RS Soil Sensor. In addition, the kit will measure water quality parameters, including temperature, oxygen levels, and pH, using dissolved oxygen (DO) and pH sensors. The proposed kit will quickly show the results on an OLED (organic light-emitting diode) display, making it easy for farmers to see the information. Furthermore, the RGB-LEDs (Red-green-blue -light Emitting diode) are provided for each soil parameter, and the depending upon LEDs status light indication to help the farmer quickly see if the soil parameters results are acceptable. In addition, the farmer can use IoT (Internet of Things) technology to check the same soil and water parameter test results on their mobile app. The research work compares the sensor-based proposed kit results with conventional laboratory method results and other existing systems.

title: Rapid Prediction of Aerosol Dispersion Based on CNN-Transformer Hybrid Model
authors: Yigeng Zhao, Chunyong Li, Jingwei Yue, Peng Zan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Aerosols, as a significant component of atmospheric pollutants, primarily refer to stable suspension systems formed by liquid or solid particles uniformly dispersed in gas. These include particulate matter such as dust, smoke, and haze, and may also carry biological particles like bacteria and viruses, forming bioaerosols that pose serious threats to public health. Therefore, leveraging neural network models to achieve efficient and rapid prediction of aerosol dispersion is crucial for emergency response to sudden events and safeguarding urban public health and safety. While existing computational fluid dynamics (CFD) methods offer high accuracy, their slow inference speeds fail to meet real-time demands in emergency scenarios. The model proposed in this study dynamically integrates local spatial features with global temporal dependencies through a gated fusion unit (GFU), coupled with a dynamic weight adjustment mechanism. This enhances prediction accuracy for high-concentration and active diffusion zones while achieving inference times under one second. Experimental validation on datasets demonstrates that the model achieves high accuracy of 0.86 and 0.91 on FAC2 and FAC5 metrics, respectively. Crucially, it delivers predictions highly consistent with real-world scenarios in critical areas while significantly outperforming traditional numerical simulation methods in inference speed, substantially enhancing the timeliness and practical value of predictions.

title: Physics-Guided Few-Shot Anomaly Image Generation for Industrial Inspection
authors: Yang Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: In industrial manufacturing, defect detection is an essential stage that cannot be compromised. However, there is an immediate barrier in training data-driven models for this task: real anomalous examples are extremely infrequent. In order to close this data gap, academics have suggested a number of synthetic generation techniques; nevertheless, the generated images usually exhibit irrational visual aberrations and flagrantly disregard physical rules. We utilize Physics-Guided Diffusion (PGD) to fill this essential gap. PGD, specifically designed for industrial inspection’s few-shot anomaly generation, compels the diffusion process to adhere to real physical limits. The main method by which it accomplishes this is Decoupled Spatial-Semantic Conditioning (DSSC), which combines learnable embeddings with physical priors, including surface topology gradients, to separate a defect’s spatial footprint from its visual appearance. We also present Denoising-Aware Attention Calibration (DAAC) to maintain the structural soundness of the created anomalies. When DAAC finds structural incompatibilities between the synthetic defect and the background, it dynamically shifts attention instead of utilizing static weights. PGD, which has been thoroughly tested on the MVTec AD dataset, not only produces extremely plausible faults but also significantly enhances downstream detection and localization tasks.

title: Multi-Objective System Optimization of an R134a/DMF Absorption Refrigerator Based on an Artificial Neural Network Algorithm and Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Zhao Chen, Zaidi Mohd Ripin, Bingxu Shi
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: The objective of this study is to perform a multi-objective analysis of the coefficient of performance (COP) and exergy efficiency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\eta}_{\mathbf{E}}$</tex>) of a single-effect R134a/DMF absorption chiller. The artificial neural network (ANN) method is a modeling tool used to determine the best parameter values (or unknown parameter values) for a given ideal condition in a process. In this paper, the process modeling software Aspen Plus is used to model and simulate a single-effect R134a/DMF absorption refrigeration system. The model considers the generation and evaporation temperatures as well as the heat loads of four main components, with the COP and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta_{\mathrm{E}}$</tex> as outputs. A configuration of 6 e 11 e 2 (6 input, 11 hidden, and 2 output neurons) showed excellent agreement between the predictions and data results (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}>0.99$</tex>). The relative importance of the input variables to the COP and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta_{\mathrm{E}}$</tex> was calculated through a sensitivity analysis to explain the behavior of the system. The generation and evaporation temperatures were shown to have the greatest effect on the COP and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta_{E}$</tex>. The best input was obtained using the particle swarm optimization algorithm with the maximum COP and exergy efficiency of 0.523 and 10.7%, respectively.

title: Modeling Teaching Readiness Among Pre-Service Science and Mathematics Teachers: A Structural Equation Modeling Approach
authors: Rolly N. Apdo
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study explored the relationships between subject mastery, technology utilization, teaching pedagogy, teaching self-efficacy, and reflective practice with teaching readiness, among a sample of 113 Bachelor of Secondary Education pre-service teachers majoring in Mathematics and Sciences. Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) was used in the study. It examined the direct and indirect influences professional competencies have on teaching readiness, with teaching self-efficacy and reflective practice as mediators. The results revealed that teaching self-efficacy was the strongest direct predictor of teaching readiness and significantly mediated the influences of subject mastery and technology utilization. Subject mastery had a direct positive influence on teaching readiness. While technology utilization and teaching pedagogy had a significant influence on teaching self-efficacy and reflective practice, their influence on teaching readiness was not significant. Reflective practice was also an insignificant mediator of teaching readiness. The findings emphasize the importance of teaching self-efficacy to transform professional competencies into teaching readiness and highlight the roles of both technical competence and the psychological aspect in teacher education programs.

title: Prediction method of wind turbine blade clearance value based on WOA-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention
authors: Changjie Sun, Zuoxia Xing, Shanshan Guo
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: As megawatt wind turbines witness the continuous growth of blade dimensions and structural flexibility, the hazard of blade-tower collision has become a critical operational challenge. Consequently, monitoring and predicting blade clearance are essential to safeguard structural integrity. This study presents a hybrid prediction framework integrated with the whale optimization algorithm (WOA) and the CNN-BiLSTM-Attention architecture. To identify the most impactful input variables, Pearson and Spearman correlation analyses were initially carried out. Subsequently, WOA is utilized to automatically fine-tune the hyperparameters of the CNN and BiLSTM modules so as to guarantee an optimal configuration. The model captures spatial and temporal features via CNN and BiLSTM layers, respectively, and incorporates an attention mechanism to prioritize prominent information in the data stream. Validation with the Bladed simulation dataset demonstrates that the proposed WOA-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model outperforms the standalone CNN, LSTM, and CNN-BiLSTM-Attention benchmark models in terms of accuracy and stability. These results suggest that the proposed method serves as a reliable tool for enhancing the safety and operation management of wind farms.

title: Fan-In Aware Graph-Based Optimization for MAC-Based in-Memory Computing
authors: Fatemeh Shirinzadeh, Abhoy Kole, Kamalika Datta, Saeideh Shirinzadeh, Rolf Drechsler
publish: 2026 IEEE 29th International Symposium on Design and Diagnostics of Electronic Circuits and Systems (DDECS)
abstract: Resistive RAM (RRAM) has emerged as a promising technology for in-memory computing, allowing both storage and computation within the same physical substrate. Although its ability to perform analog computations, especially multiplyaccumulate (MAC) operations, has been effectively utilized in neuromorphic systems, there has been limited research on its applicability to Boolean logic synthesis. Existing approaches typically rely on graph-based representations of Boolean functions that are mapped to column-wise MAC operations on standard RRAM crossbars. However, these representations largely inherit binary fan-in constraints from conventional logic synthesis flows, resulting in limited exploitation of MAC-level parallelism and underutilization of available crossbar resources. In this work, we address this limitation by introducing the concept of multi-input OR-Inverter Graphs (m-OIGs), which allow OR nodes with fanin greater than two to better match the accumulation semantics of MAC operations. Experimental results on standard benchmark suites demonstrate that increasing OR fan-in consistently reduces both crossbar area and total evaluation cycles, leading to improved performance and more efficient use of RRAM crossbar resources, highlighting the importance of fan-in-aware logic representations.

title: Design of an Isolated DC-DC Converter Using On-Chip Transformer for Miniaturized Electronic Systems
authors: Xinjie Tang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Raditional isolated DC-DC converters for microelectronic systems have large volume, high parasitics, low integration and limited efficiency due to external transformers. This paper proposes an on-chip transformer-based isolated DC-DC converter with a four-layer on-chip architecture to break the low magnetic coupling coefficient bottleneck of CMOS processes. A magnetically coupled on-chip transformer with dual-layer interleaved spiral windings and slot-core structure is designed, whose leakage inductance is reused in the resonant network. Adopting phase-shifted full-bridge LLC resonant topology, full-load soft-switching control and comprehensive loss model optimization, the 8.96mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> chip (0.18μm CMOS process) achieves 12V/3.3V/2A rated output, 92.5% conversion efficiency and 850W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> power density. Compared with external transformer solutions, it realizes 78% volume reduction, 65% parasitic leakage inductance reduction and 12mW no-load loss, maintaining high stability under 9-15V input and 20%-100% load, providing a highly integrated power solution for miniaturized systems.

title: Logitrust: Enhancing Supplychain Security With Blockchain Technology
authors: Sumathi M, Prabu S, Deepa K, Jacquelin Mary, Suresh T
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The global transportation of commodities is a tough sector, especially in maritime transport. There is sufficient documentation, a huge amount of gathered data, and a complicated process that lacks trust. This is an indicator of the problems with existing unimodal and multimodal logistics solutions regarding security, trust, reliability, traceability, transparency, and audit. All these issues lead to additional costs and delays in handling freight. By employing blockchain (BC) technology, all of the abovementioned problems are addressed systematically; one of the common problems exhibited by the transportation and logistics sector yet to be addressed using BC technology is related to security, transparency of transactions, traceability, involvement of stakeholders, preventive measures against counterfeits, and the complexities of the transportation sector. It primarily focuses on SC development and describes methods widely deployed, tested, and validated across unimodal and multimodal logistics scenarios. It also underscores how decentralized storage helps solve the problem, as it operates without data vaults or single points of failure. The proposed work presents a financial and security evaluation to further support the stability and economics of the suggested BC-based solution. In this work, leveraging BC and an in-depth analysis of numerous logistics applications, an effort is made to improve SCM techniques while strengthening transaction security.

title: Dual-channel Multi-Scale Attention Transformer Network with Multi-Domain Feature Fusion for Bearing Remaining Useful Life Prediction
authors: Jiasen Chen, Li Jiang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Ensuring the operational safety and reliability of industrial machinery relies heavily on the precise estimation of the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) for rolling element bearings. Conventional approaches frequently encounter difficulties in concurrently modeling intricate temporal dependencies and multi-scale frequency features. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning framework named Dual-channel Multi-Scale Attention Transformer (DMSAT). To address this, we introduce the DMSAT. This framework utilizes a two-stream architecture to handle multi-dimensional features derived from both Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) and Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT), facilitating the fusion of information from frequency and time-frequency domains. A Local-Global Feature Extraction block is designed to capture multi-scale degradation patterns, while a 1D Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM1D) is integrated to adaptively emphasize informative features and suppress noise. Evaluations performed on the PHM 2012 Challenge dataset indicate that the proposed DMSAT achieves superior performance compared to existing state-of-the-art techniques (e.g., CNN-BGRU and TCN-RSCB), exhibiting enhanced accuracy and robustness in RUL estimation across metrics such as MAE, RMSE, and a specialized scoring function.

title: Real-Time Tiny Defect Detection in Oil and Gas Pipelines via YOLOv8 with Multi-Scale Feature Enhancement
authors: Guojian Cheng, Jiakang Li, Jianpeng Che, Wang Feng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Real-time detection of micro-defects (e.g., cracks, leak points) in oil and gas pipelines remains challenging due to extreme pixel scarcity (<5% area ratio) and complex background interference (oil stains, rust, vegetation). To address these issues, we propose STF-YOLO, an enhanced YOLOv8 framework with three innovations: (1) a Small-Target Feature (STF) branch integrated into the C2f module for fine-grained information preservation; (2) a CBAM attention mechanism embedded in the Neck to suppress background noise; (3) Soft-NMS post-processing to mitigate missed detection of densely distributed defects. Evaluated on a dataset of 1,812 field-collected images (73% tiny defects), our method achieves 47.3% mAP@0.5 (+2.2% over baseline), 77.2% Precision, and 74.3% Recall, while maintaining 230 FPS inference speed with only 2.9M Params. The proposed method balances accuracy and efficiency, offering practical value for industrial pipeline inspection. Future work will explore its generalization on more diverse datasets and extreme conditions.

title: Influence of Teachers’ Digital Literacy and ICT Skills on the Performance of Students in Public Secondary Schools
authors: Hansel Jay L. Poliga, Leo L. Codilla
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: This study investigates how teachers’ digital literacy and ICT skills, along with their access to ICT resources, attitudes toward technology integration, and professional development in ICT, influence the academic performance of public secondary school students. Data were collected from 140 teachers in selected public secondary schools under the DepEd Butuan City Division, using the adapted questionnaires and regression analysis. The results show that among all the predictors, teachers’ attitudes toward technology integration in teaching are statistically significant in influencing students’ academic performance. Students benefit the most when teachers are confident, motivated, and willing to integrate technology in enhancing the students’ learning. The study recommends nurturing the teachers’ positive attitudes toward technology integration in delivering lessons in the classroom.

title: A Multilingual Visual Reasoning Assistant for the Visually Impaired with Context-Aware Conversational Feedback
authors: Poovarasan G, Sujeth S, Justindhas Y
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The paper describes an Visual Reasoning Assistant that helps visually impaired students with gaining real-time knowledge about the environments, as well as, finding auditory guidance. Traditional helping technologies like canes and guide dogs have little depth of context and this encourages the development of smart perception systems. The suggested system combines the identification of the public transport route, multistage staircase security guidance, chest-mounted vision recording, multilingual voice communication, and vision-based estimation of distance. An object detector and a risk assessor are run on a deep learning model, which uses a camera to capture the environment around, and the deep learning model uses analyzing exclusively the main object to determine its riskiness. Distance estimation improves awareness of space, which makes it safe to navigate in both indoor and outdoor space. The technology of speech-to-text processing makes voice queries whereby users can speak commands that get interpreted based on the visual context and are answered through natural languages speech synthesis. The system is configured to two languages, English and Tamil, to enhance ease of use to various users. The low latency and high contextual relevance have been shown through the results of the experiment and a generalized accuracy of 94 %, which dramatically assists in independence, safety, and situational awareness.

title: Review of Electromagnetic Crimping Technology for Cable Joints
authors: Zhonglin Xu, Yuqin Ding, Zhengxi Zhang, Wushuang Wang, Yang Feng, Xianjie Rao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Currently, traditional crimping methods suffer from numerous shortcomings. As a form of electromagnetic forming technology, electromagnetic pulse crimping offers a novel technical solution to address these limitations. This paper systematically introduces the concept and development process of electromagnetic crimping for cable joints, highlighting its significant application potential. First, the systematic design of electromagnetic crimping devices for cable joints is discussed. Subsequently, the key technologies of existing electromagnetic crimping for cable joints are elaborated from three dimensions: coil and magnetizer design, multi-objective optimization of crimping process parameters, and metal interface bonding mechanisms along with adaptation control techniques. Following this, focusing on quality inspection methods, the current research status in temperature monitoring and defect identification, visual inspection and dimensional measurement, as well as non-destructive evaluation and performance testing is summarized and analyzed. Finally, this paper explores the urgent technical challenges in electromagnetic crimping for cable joints and outlines its future development trends. This review uniquely synthesizes design, process, and inspection methodologies, thereby providing a consolidated framework to guide future research toward intelligent and reliable cable joint systems.

title: Solaris Protocol: A Solana-Based Decentralized Micro-Lending Protocol for Financial Inclusion
authors: Yash Goel, Sadiq A K
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) lending protocols have demonstrated the viability of peer-to-pool lending without traditional intermediaries. However, the dominant Ethereum-based platforms such as Aave and Compound impose transaction costs of $5–$50 per operation, rendering micro-loans below $1,000 economically unviable for the 1.4 billion unbanked individuals globally. This paper presents Solaris Protocol, a decentralized micro-lending protocol built natively on the Solana blockchain, designed specifically for financial inclusion through micro-lending. Solaris Protocol leverages Solana’s sub-second finality, parallel transaction processing via Sealevel, and transaction costs averaging $0.00025 to achieve a 99.98% cost reduction compared to Ethereum-based alternatives. We present a complete system architecture utilizing Program Derived Addresses (PDAs) for deterministic state management, Cross-Program Invocations (CPIs) for atomic fund transfers, and Rust’s ownership model for memory-safe smart contract execution. Our implementation comprises five core instructions (initialize, deposit, borrow, repay, withdraw) validated through 13 comprehensive test cases covering both success paths and adversarial scenarios. Comparative analysis demonstrates that a $10 micro-loan lifecycle on Solaris Protocol costs $0.00125 in total fees versus $25–$150 on Ethereum-based protocols, establishing the technical feasibility of blockchain-based micro-lending for underserved populations.

title: Enhanced Cardiovascular Disease Prediction Using XGBoost with Optimized Threshold and Extended Feature Set
authors: Xingzhu Lv, Xiaoling Shang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease (CVD) remains a leading cause of mortality globally, necessitating early and accurate prediction tools. This study proposes an enhanced machine learning framework based on the XGBoost algorithm, utilizing a publicly available dataset rigorously cleaned and enriched with domain-specific composite features. Unlike standard approaches, we implemented a strict data partitioning protocol comprising training, validation, and independent test sets. A key contribution is a threshold optimization strategy determined on the validation set to maximize the F1-score, shifting the decision boundary from the default 0.5 to 0.445. Experimental results demonstrate that the optimized XGBoost framework achieves a validation accuracy of 74.4% and an AUC of 0.7939 on the independent test set, significantly outperforming baseline models configured with default thresholds. Furthermore, SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) analysis reveals Systolic Blood Pressure as the primary predictor, while identifying elevated Body Mass Index (BMI) as a critical marker defining the highest-risk patient subgroup. This framework offers a robust, interpretable, and clinically actionable tool for CVD screening and risk stratification.

title: Metasurface Inspired E Shaped Patch Antenna for 6G Applications
authors: S. Murugan, K.S.M. Ragadharshan, P Bharathkumar, S Pranav Ram
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: This work introduces a new design of a metasurfaceinspired E-shaped patch antenna optimized for next-generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 G$</tex> applications. The antenna combines a metasurface structure, namely four-unit cells of a split-ring resonator (SRR) with an interdigital capacitor, with a conventional E-shaped patch antenna to attain improved performance features. The suggested design works on various frequency ranges, so it can easily fulfill the variable needs of future communication systems. The antenna was simulated and designed in ANSYS HFSS. Simulation results exhibit an extraordinary improvement in the meaningful parameters of antenna. The plot of return loss contains deep resonances at different frequencies with return loss of −16.56 dB at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.30 \text{GHz}(\text{ml}),-20.96 ~\text{dB}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.24 \text{GHz}(\text{ml})$</tex>, −26.52 dB at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11.83 \text{GHz}(\mathrm{m} 3)$</tex>. and −19.29 dB at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$12.60 \text{GHz}(\mathrm{m} 4)$</tex>. This multi-band capability is imperative to provide an extensive array of services anticipated in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 G$</tex> networks. Additionally, the antenna has a peak gain of 9.98 dB, a very high figure for a patch antenna of such dimensions. This outstanding gain demonstrates good signal and big range, both of which are required for effective communication in urban agglomerations and rural regions. Metasurface's utilization allows miniaturization of a high-gain multi-band antenna, which resolves critical issues in compact device. The improved figures such as multiband behavior and high gain make the proposed design viable for use in future GG wireless communication systems. The research offers a contribution to the development of compact and efficient antennas for the future wireless technology scenario.

title: Adaptive Iterated Local Search for Path Planning with Job Priority Constraints
authors: Zhiyang Liao, Zhuokun Shi, Pengfei He
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: In laser sheet metal cutting processes, the rationality and efficiency of tool path planning determine the machining efficiency and cost. For parts with complex topological structures, the machining process is subject to process sequences such as inner-before-outer. This study focuses on such path planning problems with strict job precedence orders. We model it as the precedence constrained generalized traveling salesman problem and propose an effective adaptive iterated local search algorithm to solve it with large instances. The algorithm combines the variable neighborhood descent to find high-quality local optima and a simulated annealing-based adaptive acceptance criterion to escape local optima and generate diversified solutions. We present the computational results of 39 benchmark instances. The experimental results indicate that the algorithm is significantly superior to an iterated local search algorithm and the Gurobi solver in terms of solution quality, validating the effectiveness of the AILS algorithm in solving large sheet metal cutting path planning problems.

title: Deep Learning–Based Predictive Anomaly Detection in Textile Manufacturing Using LSTM and Isolation Forest
authors: T. Manoj Prasath, Jeya Sree C, Jinu Mistika R, Pricilla Ruby S
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Early detection of anomalies is important in the modern manufacturing setting to avoid sudden failures in equipment and minimizing the operational downtimes. Textile spinning machines are under continuously changing conditions and produce mass scale multi-sensor time-series data which render conventional threshold-based and statistical fault detection techniques ineffective. The current paper suggests a multi-sensor data fusion and unsupervised learning-based hybrid anomaly detector framework of the textile manufacturing system. The presented method is based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) encoder that learns the relationship between times and extracts the latent feature representations, with temperature, pressure, and vibration sensor data. These acquired characteristics are then studied with an Isolation Forest algorithm to detect suspicious operating patterns without having to be provided with labeled fault data. The model is tested on publicly available industrial data of NASA and Kaggle to confirm its usefulness. The experimental evidence shows that the hybrid model LSTM-Isolation Forest network achieves better performance in the detecting anomalies than the corresponding models because it is able to detect both temporal and feature-level anomalies. The explicitly defined performance evaluation based on the metrics of confusion matrix and ROC analysis results also point to the strengths of the system and the areas that need additional optimization. The suggested framework helps to sustain predictive maintenance by improving the health monitoring of machines, decreasing the maintenance cost, and enhancing stability of textile spinning processes.
