title: Polyimide-Based Neural Interfaces: From Implantation to Microscopy: Stability Across Fixation and Storage Conditions
authors: Ioana-Georgiana Vasilaș, Anagha Navale, Paul Čvančara, Ali Usama, Bastian Rapp, Thomas Stieglitz
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Engineering
abstract: Objective: Decades of studies have enabled a constant optimization of thin-film-based neural implants, with technologies designed to adapt to the biological surroundings through biocompatible and miniaturized materials. Careful assessment of these materials is essential prior to photolithographic fabrication of multilayered devices, as delamination from adhesion mismatches between neighboring layers can compromise recording or stimulation and lead to channel crosstalk or device failure. Multi-layer interactions are therefore closely examined to tailor devices for long-term stability, yet post-implantation examinations still reveal failure modes that are not fully addressed and understood. Here, we propose an in vitro investigation of polyimide (PI), a widely used substrate material, in a simplified single layer configuration, to isolate substrate-related effects and evaluate long-term stability under combined conditions that cover not only the implantation period, but also post-implantation steps. Methods: Accelerated ageing was used to mimic processes occurring in the brain during implantation while aged samples were also exposed to aldehyde-based fixation and storage conditions commonly encountered during post-mortem processing. Surface morphology was assessed using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM). Chemical microstructure was analyzed in a dual approach using Time-of-Flight-Secondary-Ion Mass-Spectroscopy (ToF-SIMS) and Fourier Transform Infrared Spectroscopy (FTIR). Conclusion: PI remains morphologically and chemically stable under the harshest conditions, in both implantation scenarios and subsequent post-implantation steps. Significance: These findings provide critical validation for the use of PI as a durable substrate in long-term neural interfaces. They further indicate that damage observed after implantation may arise from factors beyond intrinsic PI degradation, helping prevent misinterpretation during post-implantation analysis.

title: A model-independent reverse Hartmann test for wavefront aberration measurement
authors: Hongshun Zhang, Lei Liu, Suhui Zhang, Zhuotong Li, Xianlei Liu, Xiaodong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The traditional wavefront aberration (WFA) measurement methods based on the reverse Hartmann test requires prior knowledge of design models of tested optical systems and high-precision calibration equipment for optical simulation to obtain the WFA. To address these issues, this paper combines the phase-shifting method with the moving screen method to propose a model-independent reverse Hartmann test for WFA measurement. It directly measures the light aberration and calculates the WFA of the optical system under test based on the mapping relationship between the light aberration and the WFA. A measurement simulation model was established to analyze the main error sources affecting measurement accuracy. Strategies for accuracy improvement were proposed for the primary errors. The measurement accuracy is validated through experiments on lenses and a three-mirror optical system, showing a relative error of 1.02% compared with the results from a Zygo interferometer.

title: Multivariate Feature-Guided Infrared Image Synthesis with High-Frequency Fidelity
authors: Xuhang Chen, Aoduo Li, Yihang Dong, Yunshou Mao, Zexiao Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Visible and infrared images, captured by two different sensors as multimodal data, offer complementary information that is increasingly valuable in consumer electronics. Applications range from enhanced night vision in smartphones and security cameras to advanced driver-assistance systems in consumer vehicles. However, the high cost of dedicated infrared imaging hardware has limited its widespread adoption in the consumer market. To address this, generating infrared images from readily available visible-light sensors using deep learning presents a cost-effective, software-based solution. While Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) have shown promise in image translation, they often suffer from training instability and can produce artifacts. Furthermore, existing methods often neglect the frequency domain, which is critical for rendering fine details, leading to images with poor structural fidelity. To overcome these limitations, we introduce the Spectral Transformation Enhancement Network (STE-Net), an end-to-end Transformer-based architecture that synthesizes high-fidelity infrared images by leveraging multivariate features from both frequency and spatial domains. STE-Net integrates features from Fourier and wavelet transformations with boundary and spatial information, preserving sharp details that are essential for consumer applications. Our approach also employs neural implicit representations to learn the complex mapping between visible and infrared modalities in a higher-dimensional space. Experimental results show that STE-Net generates superior quality infrared images, paving the way for advanced imaging features in a new generation of consumer electronics.

title: DeTracker: Motion-decoupled Vehicle Detection and Tracking in Unstabilized Satellite Videos
authors: Jiajun Chen, Jing Xiao, Shaohan Cao, Yuming Zhu, Liang Liao, Jun Pan, Mi Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Satellite videos provide continuous observations of surface dynamics but pose significant challenges for multi-object tracking (MOT), especially under unstabilized conditions where platform jitter and the weak appearance of tiny objects jointly degrade tracking performance. To address this problem, we propose DeTracker, a joint-detection-and-tracking framework tailored for unstabilized satellite videos. DeTracker introduces a task-driven Global–Local Motion Decoupling (GLMD) module to address the motion imbalance between dominant platform motion and weak target motion. It suppresses background-dominated motion via global semantic alignment at the feature level and captures target-specific motion through local refinement, improving trajectory stability and identity consistency. In addition, a Temporal Dependency Feature Pyramid (TDFP) module is developed to perform cross-frame temporal feature fusion, enhancing the continuity and discriminability of tiny-object representations. We further construct a new benchmark dataset, SDM-Car-SU, which simulates multi-directional and multi-speed platform motions to enable systematic evaluation of tracking robustness under varying motion perturbations. Extensive experiments on both simulated and real unstabilized satellite videos demonstrate that DeTracker significantly outperforms existing methods, achieving 61.1% MOTA on SDM-Car-SU and 45.3% MOTA on real satellite video data. The code and dataset will be publicly available at https://github.com/alex-chenjiajun/DeTracker.

title: An Air-Jet Continuum Robot for Industrial Integrated Fluidic Circuit Inspection_supp6-3682403.mp4
authors: Yongchang Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Industrial fluidic circuits operating under long-term extreme conditions are prone to defects such as cavitation, corrosion, and cracks, raising a critical demand for in-situ robotic inspection. Traditional manual disassembly-based methods are inefficient, while existing robots lack the ability to simultaneously adapt to complex geometries and maintain stable adsorption on surfaces of varying curvature. This study proposes an air-jet continuum robot (AJCR) that integrates three core modules: air-jet modules, a unit flexible continuum skeleton (UFCS), and stiffness-adjustable flexible suction cups (FSCs), enabling effective crawling and inspection inside integrated industrial fluid circuits. The UFCS provides omnidirectional bending capability of up to 196° enabling navigation through complex channels including abrupt turns of 120° inside stop valves. Under an input pressure of 0.8 MPa, the AJCR generates a maximum air-jet counter-thrust of 4.72 N for adsorption and 4.16 N for driving, ensuring reliable locomotion on unstructured, slippery, and discontinuous surfaces. Based on the sliding suction strategy, the FSC offers a maximum static adsorption force of 35.1 N, reduces sliding friction to 2.25 N on stainless steel, and adapts to curved surfaces with radii as small as 125 mm, facilitating stable sliding adsorption even across welded seams. Experimental results demonstrate that the AJCR smoothly navigates pipeline systems, enters stop valves, mixed-flow pumps and pressure vessels, and operates in liquid-filled environments, significantly improving inspection coverage and operational safety in fluidic delivery systems.

title: Image-Guided Navigation Control for Automated Nanopositioning Based on Dense Photometric
authors: Nuo Xu, Xianmin Zhang, Jianhao Lai, Benliang Zhu, Hai Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Image-guided automated positioning of microobjects in dynamic environments is highly needed in robot micro–nano manipulation. In this article, rendering the problem of collision-free microobject nanopositioning problem as a visual servoing problem, an image-guided navigation control scheme based on dense photometric is proposed. Specifically, the finite convergence domain existed in classical dense photometric-based visual servoing is treated as a constraint and uniformly modeled together with obstacle constraints in the navigation process. Subsequently, an online constraint handling unit based on artificial potential fields is developed to generate collision-free poses while consistently constraining the image evolution to remain within the local convergence region. Additionally, an inverse compositional algorithm is adopted to accelerate control computation, thereby enabling real-time performance. Finally, a series of experiments are conducted to verify the performance of the proposed method. Experimental results demonstrate that the method achieves reliable automatic nano-positioning and effective collision avoidance in both static and dynamic obstacle environments, confirming its feasibility and robustness in practical applications.

title: Physics-driven Conservative Contour Evolution for Building Extraction
authors: Shuaizhen Yao, Bosi Guo, Chunyan Xu, Zhen Cui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Active Contour Models (ACMs) offer a powerful approach for extracting object boundaries from remote sensing imagery. However, most existing ACMs focus solely on minimizing contour energy, while overlooking the vast contour evolution space and the inherent challenges in determining whether and when convergence is achieved. In this work, we propose a Physics-driven Conservative Contour Evolution (PCCE) method to progressively enhance contour learning. By thoroughly reformulating the general optimization objective from a physical energy perspective, we construct an energy-conservative contour model comprising potential energy, kinetic energy, and damping energy components—each governed by the law of energy conservation. In this formulation, potential energy encodes the geometric shape of building contours, kinetic energy accelerates the evolution, and damping energy converges the model. To optimize this contour model, we derive a partial differential equation that captures the evolution dynamics, and further discretize it to obtain an explicit recursive inference equation over the image domain. This evolution process is seamlessly embedded into an end-to-end deep neural network, where both the model parameters and contour evolution dynamics are learnable through backpropagation. Moreover, to address the high memory demands of ultra-high-resolution remote sensing images, we design a memory-efficient evolution paradigm: evolution parameters are learned from image slices, then aggregated and applied to produce full-image predictions through the contour evolution mechanism.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications

title: A Self-Powered Wireless Deformation Sensor System for Monitoring the Expansion Joints of Gas Insulated Switchgear
authors: Xinyi Wang, Hui Wang, Hang Gao, Zhipeng Luo, Shudong Wang, Chengbo Hu, Zhen Wang, Yunjia Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: In this work, a self-powered wireless deformation sensor system is designed, fabricated, and characterized. The self-powered monitoring is realized by the combination of the low-power operation of a sensor system and energy harvesting from a weak stray magnetic field. The magnetic energy harvester (MEH) is a resonating electromagnetic device based on a cantilever beam structure, capable of generating 113.6 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex-math></inline-formula>W power under a stray magnetic field with a very low amplitude of 50 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex-math></inline-formula>T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub> at 50 Hz. The harvested power is used to charge a battery and then power a deformation sensor system with an average operation current of 18.0 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex-math></inline-formula>A and voltage of 3.3 V. Finally, experimental results confirm that the proposed self-powered sensor system can be used to monitor the deformation of an expansion joint on a gas-insulated switchgear (GIS).

title: Lightweight Deep Learning for Real-Time River Surface Velocity Estimation from Low-Altitude Drone Remote Sensing
authors: Minghu Zhang, Jianpeng Guo, Yingying Chen, Xiaowei Nie, Xin Li
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Drone-based low-altitude remote sensing has become an important means for hydrological monitoring, where real-time on-device perception of both waterbody extent and surface flow velocity is urgently required. However, existing algorithms struggle to simultaneously achieve high monitoring accuracy and low inference latency, due to motion-feature interference induced by complex field environments and the computational bottleneck on embedded devices. To address these challenges, we propose AquaEdge, a lightweight deep learning framework for remote sensing inversion of river surface flow velocity. AquaEdge integrates a segmentation branch built upon a Frequency-augmented Star (FAStar) backbone and a lightweight feature extraction module, Cross Stage Partial with Partial Multi-Dilated Depthwise Convolution and Channel Attention (CSPPC-MDCA). The segmentation branch generates a water-mask prior that suppresses non-water spurious motion and specular-reflection noise propagation. This water-mask prior is then used as a constraint in a lightweight optical flow model, NeuFlow-lite, enabling water-surface motion estimation and velocity inversion under constrained computational resources. Experimental results show that AquaEdge achieves a mean Average Precision (mAP@50–95) of 97% for waterbody segmentation. In field tests across multiple cross-sections in the Heihe River Basin, AquaEdge achieves a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 13.6%–14.2% relative to a propeller current meter and 12.0%–13.9% relative to a handheld radar velocimeter for surface flow velocity. Furthermore, AquaEdge delivers an end-to-end inference latency of approximately 200 ms on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX, validating its feasibility for real-time integrated hydrological sensing in real-world natural river scenarios. This study establishes an on device deployable pathway for low altitude remote sensing based surface velocity retrieval and provides ground based validation and algorithmic references for hydrodynamic inversion using remote sensing platforms with high temporal resolution.

title: Distributed Security and Safety-Critical Formation Control for Multirobot Systems Subject to Distributed Denial-of-Service Attacks
authors: Lang Zou, Xiangbin Liu, Dechao Gao, Hongye Su, Wei Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: This article addresses the problems of cybersecurity and physical safety in formation control of multirobot systems (MRSs) subject to distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. A novel distributed formation control method based on a directed acyclic graph (DAG) is proposed to enhance the system resilience against DDoS attacks. The proposed approach ensures the achievement of the formation objective, provided that no edge in the communication topology is completely disrupted by the attacks. Furthermore, to guarantee collision avoidance among the robots, the centralized pairwise safety barrier certificates, derived from high-order control barrier functions (HOCBFs), are decomposed into the distributed components for the respective robots. Each robot integrates these distributed safety barrier certificates into a unified safety constraint as the inequality constraint in a quadratic programming (QP) problem, which is solved to derive the safety-critical control input. Finally, the effectiveness and practical applicability of the proposed control method are verified through both numerical simulation and physical experiment.

title: ECPNet: A Multi-Modal Sea-Land Segmentation Network with Edge and Confidence Priors
authors: Jianming Cui, Jing Zhang, Yan Lu, Shengyun Liu, Binge Cui
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Sea-land segmentation in remote sensing imagery is of great significance for dynamic coastline monitoring. However, single-modality remote sensing images often struggle to capture the complex structure of coastlines effectively. To address this challenge, we propose ECPNet, a multi-modal sea-land segmentation network that integrates optical and SAR images. The complex and variable nature of coastlines, coupled with dynamic factor disturbances such as waves and tides, further exacerbate the inherent feature misalignment between optical and SAR modalities due to their fundamentally different imaging mechanisms. Moreover, optical images are easily affected by weather conditions, while SAR images are affected by speckle noise,both of which severely affect the confidence of modal features and limit segmentation accuracy. To overcome these challenges, we propose the Edge-Guided Deformable Alignment Module (EDAM), which uses edge information as a structural prior to guide fine-grained, pixel-level adaptive alignment and boundary enhancement of shallow optical and SAR features. In addition, we introduce the Triple Confidence-guided Fusion Module (TCFM), which dynamically evaluates the reliability of features from each source and adaptively adjusts their fusion weights. This mechanism effectively suppresses the adverse effects of clouds, haze, and speckle noise. Experimental results on dataset based on Sentinel data demonstrate that our method achieves more accurate segmentation and sharper boundary delineation compared to existing approaches, under both clear and cloud-covered conditions. The dataset is publicly available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/cuibinge/Multimodal-Sea-Land.

title: Evaluation of Ice Crystal Scattering Models Using Polarimetric Observations_supp1-3694098.docx
authors: Lei Bi
publish: N/A
abstract: Ice clouds are an essential component of the Earth’s atmosphere, and their radiative effects strongly depend on the morphology of ice crystals. To identify ice crystal scattering models that are representative and robust for remote sensing and atmospheric modeling applications, we systematically evaluate the performance of nine established ice habit models (i.e., plate, 5-plate aggregate, 10-plate aggregate, droxtal, 8-column aggregate, hollow bullet rosette, solid bullet rosette, hollow column and solid column) along with latitude-dependent super-ellipsoidal schemes. Model performance is assessed by comparing simulated polarized radiance against measurements from the third-generation POLarization and Directionality of the Earth’s Reflectances instrument (POLDER-3). Results show that traditional ice habit models exhibit pronounced latitude dependence. Notably, the models with higher structural complexity (e.g., hollow bullet rosettes and roughened plates) show reduced latitude sensitivity and improved agreement with observations. Increasing surface roughness also improves model performance, particularly in low-latitude zones. In contrast, the mixed super-ellipsoidal scheme consistently outperforms all habit-based models, showing lower RMSE values, weaker dependence on latitude and season, and more uniform agreement across scattering angles. This likely stems from the fact that a limited set of idealized ice habits cannot fully capture the natural variability and complexity of ice crystals. These results highlight the applicability of the super-ellipsoid representation as a robust surrogate scheme for ice cloud remote sensing and its potential value for improving climate-related applications.

title: High-Power Wide-Dynamic-Range GaN Rectifier Using Diode-Topology Impedance Compression
authors: Xianghao Min, Ming Xiao, Xiufeng Song, Zhengxing Zhang, Menghan Zheng, Mengjiao Xiang, Hong Zhou, Kui Dang, Jincheng Zhang, Yue Hao
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a systematic analysis of the input impedance characteristics of GaN Schottky barrier diodes (SBDs) under different topologies, and proposes a streamlined design methodology for wide-dynamic-range rectifiers targeting high-power applications. The rectifier adopts a parallel configuration of two subrectifiers, each providing a fundamental input impedance through deliberate diode topology selection. Wide-range impedance matching is then realized using only a single series microstrip line, thereby minimizing circuit complexity and parasitic loss. Two rectifier prototypes, operating at 2.45 and 5.8 GHz, are implemented and measured to validate the concept. At 2.45 GHz, the rectifier maintains a power conversion efficiency (PCE) above 50% over a 27-dB input power dynamic range (18–45 dBm), with a peak PCE of 81.7% at 37 dBm. At 5.8 GHz, over 50% PCE is sustained across a 24.2-dB range (20.7–44.9 dBm), with a peak efficiency of 85.1% at 34 dBm. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, this work achieves the highest peak efficiency and the highest power handling capability among all reported wide-dynamic-range rectifiers.

title: Design of Double Pulse Test Platform for High Voltage SiC Devices
authors: Yang Zhang, Jingling Zhang, Yumeng Cai, Peng Sun, Xuebao Li, Zhibin Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Accurate evaluation of switching characteristics through the double pulse test (DPT) is crucial for the development and application of high voltage (HV) silicon carbide (SiC) devices. This paper presents a 20 kV/250 A DPT platform, which achieves the highest voltage level reported to date. The design methods for HV laminated busbars with minimized stray inductance and HV load inductors with minimized parasitic capacitance are proposed. Furthermore, the common-mode ringing current caused by ground loops in the HV DPT circuit is analyzed, and an improved grounding configuration is introduced. Several HV measurement setups are also recommended for precise characterization. Finally, a 14 kV DPT is conducted on an 18 kV/12.5 A SiC IGBT using the proposed platform. The results show that the DPT platform developed based on the proposed method can accurately evaluate the dynamic characteristics of HV SiC devices with a rated voltage of 10 kVand above.

title: A Seven-Degree-of-Freedom Modulation Scheme for Dual-Bridge Series Resonant Converter
authors: Yinan Li, Xiaodong Li, Chuan Sun, Song Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In this article, a seven-degree-of-freedom (7 DOF) modulation scheme is introduced to mitigate the switching loss and conduction loss over the whole power range for dual-bridge series resonant converter (DBSRC). According to the analysis with fundamental harmonic approximation (FHA) approach, the steady-state characteristics models of 7-DOF modulation scheme are established. By adopting the vector diagram method (VDM), the root mean-square (rms) current is minimized graphically, and the backflow power is almost eliminated. Besides, the proposed modulation scheme enables the converter to achieve zero voltage switching (ZVS) under wide-range variations in the voltage gain by taking full consideration of the relationship between the soft-switching condition and each modulation parameter. Compared with the existing typical modulation schemes, the proposed 7-DOF modulation scheme exhibits better merits in terms of wider ZVS operation ranges, reduced conduction loss, and higher conversion efficiency for both buck mode and boost mode. Eventually, the effectiveness of the proposed modulation scheme and optimization methods is validated on a 400W experimental prototype.

title: Aligned Information Bottleneck for Multimodal Social Sentiment Analysis
authors: Runqi Zou, Jian Zhang, Anyu Ren, Wenqing Wang, Zhan Gao, Jialun Wu, Kai He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) infers affect from synchronized text, speech, and visual signals, but it still faces cross-modal misalignment and noisy fusion, especially in naturalistic social communication. Differences in temporal resolution, semantic granularity, and modality-specific noise mean that simple concatenation or coarse interaction can amplify redundant cues from one modality while weakening informative signals from others. Recent approaches based on the Information Bottleneck (IB) principle help reduce redundancy. However, matrix-based Rényi-entropy estimators typically rely on a fixed low-rank truncation, making the resulting bottleneck sensitive to spectrum variation across batches, modalities, and difficulty levels. We propose a text-anchored IB framework for MSA that jointly addresses alignment and fusion. First, we construct a language-centered semantic space using a pretrained Qwen-3 encoder and map acoustic and visual features into this space, yielding an aligned information bottleneck representation that reduces cross-modal semantic gaps before fusion. Second, we introduce an energy-adaptive low-rank Rényi-entropy based information bottleneck. It builds on matrix-based Rényi-entropy estimators and selects the effective rank via an energy criterion. This suppresses redundant directions while preserving label-relevant structure. We evaluate the proposed framework on standard utterance-level MSA benchmarks with text, audio, and video. Extensive experiments and ablation studies show that our method improves over strong baselines across standard evaluation metrics. We also observe better robustness under modality noise and imbalance, supporting the value of text-anchored alignment and energy-adaptive low-rank IB for multimodal affect modeling.

title: Tasks Distribution and Machine Learning Enabled In-Device Task Scheduling in IoT
authors: Mikhail Tishin, Constandinos X. Mavromoustakis, Jordi Mongay Batalla, George Mastorakis, Evangelos K. Markakis
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Traditional centralized scheduling mechanisms often have performance issues related to scalability, delayed system-wide state awareness and inefficient resource utilization. This paper proposes a software framework for distributed task execution within IoT ecosystems, which consists of: 1) a task offloading mechanism centered around enabling devices to pull tasks instead of being assigned by a server, 2) a machine learning-enabled, in-device offloaded task scheduling system considerate of devices’ internal state, 3) a lightweight fault tolerance mechanism. Devices dynamically report to a central distributor, receive tasks according to their processing capabilities and autonomously schedule, and execute them using real-time system state data. The initial experimental results indicate that the proposed framework is capable of processing workloads while being considerate of a device’s inner state, scales well with number of devices, increases data privacy and reduces reliance on the external resources.

title: Multi Person Localization and Breathing Rate Estimation using Translational and Rotational Synthetic Aperture Radar_supp1-3694141.pdf
authors: Ashutosh Deshwal
publish: N/A
abstract: Reliable detection and localization of humans through structural obstructions is critical for time-sensitive applications such as disaster response. This paper presents a novel ultra-wideband (UWB) radar-based framework for multi-person localization and breathing rate estimation in obstructed environments. To overcome the challenges posed by signal attenuation, multipath interference, and limited spatial resolution, we propose a synthetic aperture radar (SAR) system that combines linear translation and on-axis rotation of a single radar. By fusing intensity maps from multiple radar viewpoints, the system achieves enhanced localization of human targets. A signal quality assessment pipeline, including SNR-based filtering, dominance ratio analysis, and periodicity checks, is used to extract clean respiratory signals from selected range bins. The estimates are further integrated into a spatial breathing-rate matrix, which is fused with radar motion maps to suppress false detections and improve reliability. The proposed algorithm is validated experimentally under line-of-sight, through-wall, and emulated rubble conditions. Results demonstrate significant improvements in localization accuracy and robustness of respiration-based human presence detection. The proposed post-processing filtering algorithms reduce the 80<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> percentile Chamfer distance by 1.53×, while the maximum Chamfer distance is reduced by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\sim 3\times$</tex-math></inline-formula>. The estimated breathing rate correlates strongly with ground truth while achieving a Pearson correlation of 0.79.

title: ATSDAS: A Vision-Based System for Automated Traffic Sign Management and Condition Assessment
authors: Shucheng Zhang, Chenxi Liu, Nutvara Jantarathaneewat, Yinhai Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The maintenance and management of traffic signal assets are crucial for ensuring the safety and smooth operation of road transportation. However, timely repairs and updates of damaged traffic signs remain a significant challenge due to the inefficiency of manual measurements. This study proposes an innovative system that automates traffic sign collection and assessment using deep learning techniques. The system performs three key tasks, including traffic sign detection and tracking, condition assessment, and inventory building, by processing dashboard camera videos with GPS data or open-source street imagery. The system achieves a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\text {mAP}_{50}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> of 93.50% for detection and recognition and an accuracy of 93.56% for damage assessment by utilizing a retroreflectivity-related image feature extraction pipeline. The outputs are formalized into a visual inventory, enabling transportation agencies to manage traffic assets more efficiently. Field tests conducted in collaboration with the Washington State Department of Transportation validate the practical effectiveness of the framework. To further address the lack of publicly available condition datasets, this study also introduces the Damaged Traffic Sign Detection and Assessment (DTSDA) dataset, which combines real and synthetic images of damaged signs. Overall, the proposed approach offers a cost-effective and scalable alternative to the traditional labor-intensive method, thereby enhancing traffic asset management efficiency and contributing to safer transportation systems.

title: State-Space Modeling of Real-Time Visual Biofeedback and Late-Stance Belt-Speed Modulation for Quantifying Error- and Use-Dependent Learning of Gait Propulsion in Individuals Post-Stroke
authors: Seoung Hoon Park, Jooeun Ahn, Beom-Chan Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: This study developed an exploratory, model-constrained state-space model to characterize how real-time visual biofeedback (VB) and late-stance, phase-specific belt modulation (propulsion-facilitating, PF) were associated with improvements in affected-limb propulsion following stroke. Twelve individuals with chronic stroke participated in a single-session experiment conducted on the Adaptive Propulsion Enhancement eXperience (APEX) system, which utilizes an instrumented split-belt treadmill. The system continuously displays the affected-limb anterior ground reaction force (AGRF) while modulating belt speed during late stance. The experimental session consisted of three sequential phases: baseline, combined VB and PF training, and post-training assessment. Step-to-step AGRFs were modeled as the sum of an error-based learning (EBL) state with separate VB-related and PF-related model input channels, a use-dependent learning (UDL) state, and a direct same-step PF feed-through component. Model parameters and latent states were estimated using maximum likelihood with an extended Kalman filter and Rauch-Tung-Striebel smoother. The model reconstructed propulsion trajectories with high in-sample fidelity within the fitted session (R² = 0.976 ± 0.004), whereas temporal hold-out and post-training prediction analyses indicated limited extrapolative performance. Therefore, the decomposition should be interpreted as a model-constrained, within-session descriptive analysis rather than as evidence of robust predictive generalizability. In this model-constrained decomposition, propulsion gains were approximately distributed across EBL-related processes (~56%), UDL-related processes (~43%), and direct same-step PF feed-through (~1%). Within EBL, the VB-related and PF-related model channels contributed comparably (25% and 31%), and both adaptive states were consistent with very slow decay within the imposed identifiable range, rather than providing precise estimates of long-duration retention. These results suggest that, within the model-constrained decomposition and despite limited extrapolative performance on unseen data, propulsion enhancement in the fitted session was more closely aligned with adaptive components than with direct same-step PF feed-through. This exploratory, proof-of-concept framework provides interpretable patient-level parameters as hypothesis-generating descriptors of within-session propulsion learning and may serve as a foundation for future mechanism-informed, personalized post-stroke gait rehabilitation strategies that require prospective validation.

title: Meet One of the Pioneers of Integrated Circuits and Systems: An Interview With Prof. David J. Allstot
authors: Terri S. Fiez
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine

title: Towards rigorous protection against unauthorized image synthesis on diffusion models
authors: Zhangdong Wang, Tongqing Zhou, Tengfei Zheng, Jiaohua Qin, Zhiping Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: The misuse of image-conditioned generation tools comes along with their ubiquitous deployment, making user images face amplified risk of unauthorized synthesis. However, existing methods for handling such manipulation attacks still release the harmful image, with watermarking providing post-hoc detection and adversarial perturbation causing partial leakage. This work notably identifies the safety checker, which automatically filters out generations with harmful content, as a homogeneous component across commercial generation APIs and repurposes it for rigorous authentication against unauthorized image synthesis. A novel framework PBAuth is then proposed to inject an imperceptible perturbation into users’ online images, forcing any subsequent generation to be flagged and denied by the safety checker. For this, a diffusion surrogate is designed to mimic the low-pass filtering characteristics of the diffusion process and overcome the inaccessibility of diffusion models. Meanwhile, a multi-modal simulator is used to verify the robustness of safety-sensitive perturbation towards triggering safety moderation. A deliberate perturbation is obtained by optimizing both triggering success rate and human visual quality. Experiments on five generation tasks with ten models show that PBAuth can effectively prevent unauthorized synthesis by returning an error response while maintaining competitive visual quality. Code is available at https://github.com/one-zd/PBAuth2026.

title: The Use of Energy Self-balancing VSC Converters for AC Fault Ride-through in Ultra Long-distance HVDC Transmission Systems
authors: Wanyu Cao, Yuebin Zhou, Liu Yang, Yiliang Xu
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: A novel energy self-balancing voltage source converter(VSC) topology with integrated distributed energy consumption function and its VSC-HVDC transmission scheme are proposed for cross-regional transmission scenarios of large-scale renewable energy island bases in desert, gobi, and wasteland areas. By using such VSC toplogy is able to achieve full AC grid fault ride-through at the receiving end without the need of traditional DC energy consumption devices to solve the technical problem with less costs and losses where surplus power of the ultra long-distance VSC-HVDC transmission system cannot be dissipated in a timely manner under a fault condition. In this paper, the topology, working mechanism and control method of the energy self-balancing VSC converter are expounded. The fault ride-through strategies and configuration principles of the energy self-balancing VSC-HVDC transmission system under AC faults in the receiving end were analyzed. A 5000 MW photovoltaic island-base VSC-HVDC transmission system was built on the PSCAD/EMTDC simulation platform. The fault ride-through characteristics of the energy self-balancing VSC-HVDC system were verified under various AC faults.

title: AI-Driven RIS-Assisted Interference Management and Energy Optimization for WBAN-Integrated 6G Healthcare Networks
authors: S. Janani, N. Kumaratharan
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: Wireless Body Area Networks (WBANs) play an important role in healthcare monitoring systems by enabling continuous tracking of patient physiological activities through wearable sensors. With the integration of WBANs into 6G communication systems, energy efficiency and interference management become major challenges, since wearable sensors operate with limited battery capacity and often coexist with multiple WBANs within the same access point coverage. Interference becomes significant when multiple WBANs share the same communication channel, resulting in degraded communication quality. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface (RIS)-assisted communication framework combined with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) for adaptive interference management and energy-efficient transmission. The RIS phase shifts are dynamically configured to enhance the desired signal path while suppressing interference from other WBAN users. The joint optimization of beamforming, transmit power, and RIS phase configuration results in a non-convex problem. To solve this, a DRL-based optimization framework is developed to adaptively configure RIS parameters under dynamic channel conditions and user mobility. Simulation analysis shows that the proposed approach improves SINR, reduces energy consumption, and prolongs the operational lifetime of wearable sensors compared with conventional communication schemes. The proposed architecture offers a scalable and sustainable solution for next-generation healthcare networks, ensuring reliable and energy-efficient transmission of critical medical data in complex 6G environments.

title: Exploring and Cultivating Competency Structures for Cold Chain Logistics Personnel Using the LDA Topic Model under the OBE Framework
authors: Fanfan Jia, Huichuan Dai
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Cold chain logistics constitutes a critical industry safeguarding public welfare, with its high-quality development requiring support from highly skilled, multidisciplinary professionals. Addressing current issues such as the disconnect between talent cultivation and industry demands in cold chain logistics, this paper first employs the LDA topic model to mine textual data from online recruitment platforms concerning cold chain job descriptions. Through text prepossessing, tokenisation and vectorisation, model training, and topic consistency assessment, core job themes are extracted. Subsequently, these themes are interpreted and synthesized into a competency matrix for positions. Finally, guided by the Outcome-Based Education (OBE) philosophy, the competency matrix serves as the output target to reverse-engineer a talent development pathway for cold chain logistics. This research validates the LDA model's efficacy in educational needs analysis, while the proposed pathway offers a solution for overcoming industry-education integration challenges and transitioning talent cultivation from disciplinary logic to competency-based logic.

title: Variable-Speed Pumped Storage Power Control Strategy Based on BP Algorithm
authors: Yuting Lu, Sipeng Hao
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To address issues such as nonlinearity and time-variation in the power control of variable-speed pumped storage units, an adaptive power control method based on BP neural networks is proposed. By establishing a simplified mathematical model of the variable-speed pumped storage system, a 3-5-1 feedforward neural network controller with online learning capability is designed. This controller uses the error backpropagation algorithm to adjust network weights in real time. Matlab/Simulink simulation results show that, compared to the traditional PI controller, the BP neural network controller exhibits near-zero overshoot, small steady-state error, and smooth, oscillation-free response during step response. Furthermore, intermediate process visualization confirms the stability and convergence of the proposed algorithm.

title: Enabling Adaptive Chinese Idiom Teaching with Prompt-Guided Text-to-Cypher Knowledge Graph Queries
authors: Yi Liang
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Idiom teaching presents a significant challenge in international Chinese education. The core of idiom teaching lies in decoding the complex relationships among characters, semantics, and structures within idioms. A knowledge graph is an effective model for representing these relationships. However, traditional methods of manually constructing and querying such graphs are inefficient and unable to support dynamic, personalized teaching needs. This study proposes a novel approach that integrates Prompt Engineering with Text-to-Cypher technology to intelligently mine idiom knowledge graphs and facilitate the design of adaptive instructional paths. We first constructed an idiom knowledge graph based on the Chinese Proficiency Grading Standards for International Chinese Language Education (hereafter referred to as the Grading Standards). We then designed a set of prompt templates and processes to translate teachers’ natural language questions into efficient Cypher query sentences. This facilitates the retrieval of multi-dimensional relationships—such as character co-occurrence, semantic associations, and grammatical structures— directly from the Neo4j graph database. This study demonstrates how this approach can rapidly generate complex queries for teaching scenarios and make the underlying associative networks among idioms explicit. These insights enable educators to design targeted learning paths and content based on individual learner profiles. This study provides a practical, data-driven framework for adaptive instructional design in Chinese idiom teaching. It presents a concrete application of AI-assisted curriculum development through the integration of prompt engineering and knowledge graphs.

title: Tool Path Smoothing Algorithm for Five-Axis CNC Machining Based on SQP Optimization
authors: Jinyong Li, Cong Geng, Jiayi Fu
publish: 2026 2nd International Conference on Smart Energy and Control Engineering (SECE)
abstract: To address the poor corner continuity of polyline toolpaths, which often induces feedrate fluctuations and dynamic impacts, a local smoothing method based on Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP) is proposed. Centered on corner-wise local replacement, finite-length segments are truncated on both sides of each corner, and the maximum curvature of the NURBS transition is taken as the optimization objective. A constrained optimization model is formulated by incorporating the smoothing error bound, isolation-length constraint, and endpoint curvature-continuity conditions. The weight factors are then solved iteratively using SQP, and the overall algorithmic procedure is provided.

title: Dual-Path Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Few-Shot Pest Recognition using Vision-Language Models
authors: Yanming Zhang, Ji Jin, Chuanwen Lin, Qi Gu, Shaojie Geng, Zhaoting Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Few-shot crop pest recognition aims to identify and localize pest species under extreme annotation scarcity. Although recent works leverage large-scale Vision-Language Models (VLMs), such as CLIP, for Few-Shot Learning (FSL) via strong cross-modal priors, these methods struggle in crop pest recognition due to semantic misalignment with domain-specific taxonomies. To this end, we propose a Dual-Path Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (DP-PEFT) framework that enhances both semantic alignment and instance-level discrimination without updating backbone encoders. Specifically, we learn agriculture-specific continuous prompts to adapt the textual embedding space and construct a lightweight instance-aware visual feature cache to preserve discriminative morphological patterns via nonparametric affinity modeling. The dual-path design effectively bridges semantic and structural gaps under extreme data scarcity. On the IP102 benchmark, our method achieves 16.81%, 21.69%, 34.08%, 42.93%, and 48.62% accuracy under 1-, 4-, 8-, 16-, and 32-shot settings, respectively, consistently outperforming uni-path adaptation baselines with negligible computational overhead. These results demonstrate a scalable and practical solution for real-world agricultural deployment.

title: Lightweight Infrared Small Target Detection with Wavelet-Based Frequency Decomposition and Spatial-Aware Attention
authors: Zhan Jin, Haonan Chen
publish: 2026 International Conference on Robot Perception and Intelligent Control (RPIC)
abstract: In military, surveillance applications, infrared small-target detection (IRSTD) is highly challenging due to the extremely small size of targets, strong background noise, and sparse features. Based on this, this paper proposes IR-WASNet, a lightweight network improved from YOLOv10. IR-WASNet introduces Discrete Wavelet Transform Convolution (DWaveletConv) into the backbone network to enhance highfrequency details and low-frequency semantics while suppressing background noise. Additionally, a set of POS-SHSA modules is designed, at the same time, we will also study the integration of POSConvEmbedding with the SHSA module to enhance the feature fusion capability of IR-WASNet. Experiments validate the effectiveness of IR-WASNet on the SIDD and HIT-UAV datasets, achieving mAP scores of 82.6% and 82.8%, respectively, representing improvements of 2.4% and 13.8% over the baseline YOLOv10 model, maintained the model's lightweight nature while balancing computational costs. These experimental results demonstrate that IR-WASNet effectively enhances infrared smalltarget detection in complex scenes by integration of wavelet transform and attention channels.

title: Compression Feedback Algorithm for Terahertz Channel State Information Based on Swin Transformer and Quantization-Aware Training
authors: Jiawei Zeng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: With the rapid development of high-speed data services, terahertz communication systems have imposed higher requirements on the efficiency of channel state information (CSI) compression and feedback. This paper proposes a novel CSI compression and feedback algorithm that integrates Swin Transformer with quantization-aware training (QAT) to address the challenges of high-dimensional CSI feedback in terahertz large-scale MIMO systems. The method innovates based on existing CsiNet and its Transformer-improved models by introducing a hierarchical Swin Transformer encoder-decoder structure, which effectively extracts both local and global features from the CSI matrix through a sliding window mechanism. Meanwhile, quantization-aware training is introduced during the training phase to simulate low-bit quantization inference, improving the model's deployment efficiency on edge devices. On the decoding side, we design a dual-branch reconstruction module that integrates channel attention mechanisms with frequency-domain residual learning to further enhance CSI reconstruction accuracy under high compression ratios. Experimental results demonstrate that this model outperforms existing methods across multiple compression scenarios and quantization precisions.

title: Adaptive Feature Selection with Recursive K-Fold Validation for IoT Security
authors: P.B.Arun Prasad, P.B. Aravind Prasad, V.Mohan, Bharathi Kannan B
publish: 2026 Third International Conference on Networking and Communications (ICNWC)
abstract: The primary threat to every network communication channel is network intrusion, necessitating the development of improved Intrusion Detection Systems (IDSs) specifically designed for IoT applications. Machine learning methodologies for network intrusion detection systems (NIDS) have facilitated the avoidance of future intrusions using supervised machine learning approaches, which analyse historical data to identify patterns indicative of potential threats. To overcome these limitations, this research aims to identify NIDS. To determine the link among the attributes, feature selection employs unweighted univariate feature selection and covariance coefficients, using a recursive K-fold cross-validation procedure. The UNSWNB-15 network dataset, including 257,673 records with 47 distinct attributes and 4 categories, together with the TON_IoT dataset, which includes telemetry data from IoT or IIoT service domains, was used to evaluate the performance of the approach. Machine learning models, including naïve Bayes (NB), support vector machines (SVM), decision trees (DT), and K-nearest neighbors (K-NN), are used to evaluate accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity. In comparison to other correlation algorithms, the Recursive Feature K-Fold Elimination methodology selected the fewest features using a decision tree model and achieved optimal accuracy and sensitivity in detection.

title: AI-Based Modeling and Simulation for Enhancing Systems Thinking in High School Ecosystem Education: An Empirical Study
authors: Chang Junhong
publish: 2026 15th International Conference on Educational and Information Technology (ICEIT)
abstract: Systems thinking is a higher-order cognitive ability essential for analyzing ecosystem structures and understanding organism–environment interactions. Traditional biology instruction often limits students’ perception of dynamic ecosystem processes and provides insufficient support for developing systems thinking. To address these challenges, this study constructed an interdisciplinary instructional model integrating AI-supported modeling and simulation with biological knowledge, data science, and computational thinking. A quasi-experimental design was implemented with 168 Grade 11 students from four parallel classes in two public senior high schools, with an experimental group receiving AI-based modeling and simulation instruction and a control group receiving traditional lecture-based teaching supplemented by basic simulation software. Data were collected through a self-developed systems thinking scale, an ecosystem concept test, student artifacts, and semi-structured interviews. Results indicated that students in the experimental group significantly outperformed those in the control group in overall systems thinking and across four dimensions—element identification, structural analysis, dynamic prediction, and feedback regulation. Moreover, the experimental group demonstrated deeper conceptual understanding, stronger interdisciplinary learning interest, and improved problem-solving abilities. These findings suggest that AI-supported modeling and simulation provides an effective approach for cultivating systems thinking in high school biology education and offers empirical evidence supporting the integration of intelligent technologies into science teaching.

title: Maize Leaf Disease Detection using Vision Transformer with Attention Mechanism
authors: K Deepak Kumar, S Ajay, Karthik Raja, M Dhanush, R Selvam, M Sadhasivam
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Computing (ICONIC)
abstract: Maize grain is among the most commonly consumed cereal crops globally, serving as both a food source and an industrial feedstock. The development of maize is affected by many foliar diseases (i.e., diseases of the leaf of the maize plant), including Common Rust, Leaf Blight, and Grey Leaf Spot, which if not identified at an early stage can reduce maize yields by 30 to 70%. Traditionally, experts would physically inspect maize plant leaves to find foliar diseases, which takes a considerable amount of time, expense, and can only be performed once or twice per growing season on a large farm. For a cost-effective and scalable solution to this problem, the authors are developing an automated foliar disease detection system (Vision Transformer network-based machine learning algorithm) for maize plants using an attention mechanism. The proposed architecture for this network consists of multiple layers: a patch embedding layer, a positional encoding layer that retains spatial relationships among patches embedded in the network; a multilayer self-attention layer that captures many complex features in the data image sample; and a feed-forward layer. The results of these layers are merge to produce a last classification layer that is used for predicting (i.e., determining types and/or presence) of disease in the photographic image of the maize leaf sample. The authors trained and tested the entire model using a dataset of thousands of images of maize leaves located in four categories (healthy, common rust, leaf blight, and grey leaf spot), achieving overall model predictions with accuracy of 97.3%; precision of 96.8%; a recall of 97.1%; and an F1 score of 96.9%. Additionally, the authors used an attention mechanism to demonstrate where the proposed methodology was able to identify the locations of the images that were impacted by disease. Thus, the proposed model provides a cost-effective and scalable solution for early detection of foliar diseases in maize used in precision agriculture.

title: Hazardous Prevention and Safety Management in Crackers Industry
authors: C. Arunachalaperumal, P. Anantha Narayanan, P. Harihara Sudhan, M. Karthikeyan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Future and Advanced Computing Technologies (ICFACT)
abstract: Along with continuous exposure of workers and materials to fire prone chemicals, volatile gases and sources of ignitions, the cracker manufacturing industry functions within a setting where the materials and workers are constantly exposed to such substances. These requirements call on an active and technology-based safety system. This paper introduces a Hazard Prevention and Safety Management System of STM32 type, which is aimed at the ability to ensure constant monitoring, fast detection of an incident, and automated response to an emergency. This system will include gas sensors of MQ-2 and MQ-135 to detect combustible and toxic gases and flame sensors to detect fire in real-time. The received sensor data goes through the STM32 microcontroller which performs a multi- level safety protocol which incorporates automatic turning off of electric circuits, activation of water-based fire suppression and sending immediate alerts using GSM and Ethernet connectors. Simultaneously, the framework focuses on organized emergency preparedness, staff training in safety, and operational business practices that are not harmful to the environment. The offered system would promote the situation awareness, reduce the number of human interventions during emergency situations, and facilitate the safer and more environmentally friendly cracker production.

title: A Novel Distributed Energy Collaborative Optimization Scheduling Method for Power Systems Based on Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Lei Jia, Yujie Shi, Ning Wang, Dejian Gao
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: Under the “dual carbon” goal and new power system, large-scale distributed energy integration creates a decentralized and complex system, challenging traditional scheduling methods. This paper proposes a distributed energy collaborative optimization scheduling method based on Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). It constructs a scenario with photovoltaics, energy storage, adjustable loads, and electric vehicles, considering equipment constraints, safety requirements, and uncertainty. Using the CTDE framework, each agent makes autonomous decisions with local observations and limited global information. A collaborative reward mechanism integrates economy, energy consumption, and safety, while constraint perception and feasible action projection ensure scheme safety and feasibility. Simulations show the method outperforms rule-based and independent RL methods in costs, abandonment rates, and voltage violations, approaching centralized optimization with good real-time performance and scalability, offering an effective solution for distributed scheduling.

title: Research on the Application of Structured Process Systems Based on 3D Models
authors: Jian Li
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: This paper addresses the application requirements of structured process systems driven by 3D models, and establishes an integrated approach: “Model Semantic Enhancement—Process Meta-model—Hybrid Mapping—Multi-Constraint Scheduling—Circular Governance.” The research uses traceable primary keys and versioning mechanisms as core elements, transforming model elements into processes, steps, checkpoints, and resource units. Filtering is performed using rule constraints, and process routes are generated through learning and sorting. Dependency networks and resource conflict metrics are employed during the scheduling phase to achieve a balance between time, cost, and risk. Evaluation of offline process generation and online change triggering is conducted using data from real-world engineering scenarios. Results show that the system demonstrates stable gains in improving attribute completeness, reducing conflict rates, and ensuring traceability chain integrity. It also significantly reduces the time required for manual verification and achieves an auditable ROI. This research provides an engineering path for the standardized implementation and continuous governance of enterprise-level 3D models into process data. Also, it makes clear what the present validation boundaries are, and it gives a production-focused verification procedure for multi-sector transferability and business deployment scalability (high concurrency, data growth, and cross-platform integration).

title: Research on Modeling and Time-Domain Solution Methods for Transformer Windings Based on Unequal-Length Transmission Lines
authors: Ziyi Yao, Xiaojun Zhao, Yaxin Li, Kefan Jiao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Advances in Electrical, Electronics and Computing Technology (EECT)
abstract: The calculation of voltage distribution in transformer windings under high-frequency transients is of vital importance for the structural design of transformers. To improve the accuracy of traditional multi-conductor transmission line (MTL) models caused by assuming geometric uniformity, a improved model considering both unequal-length geometric characteristics and frequency-dependent effects is proposed in this paper. First, an unequal-length description is introduced to establish a distributed-parameter matrix including curvature effects in polar coordinates. Second, frequency-varying internal impedances, accounting for skin and proximity effects, are transformed into pole-residue form via vector fitting and integrated into the finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) framework for time-domain solution. Experimental results demonstrate that this method accurately characterizes highfrequency transient distributions, providing a reference for insulation design and analysis.

title: Multi-Dimensional Performance Evaluation Method for Object Detection Models in PCB Electronic Component Detection
authors: Xuhan Shen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Advanced Electronic Technology, Computers and Software Engineering (AETCSE)
abstract: Existing PCB component detection research relies on single-metric evaluation and lacks a systematic multi-dimensional assessment framework. This paper proposes a comprehensive evaluation system with 12 indicators across three dimensions—usability, efficiency, and robustness—and a game theory-based AHP-entropy weight method to adaptively balance subjective and objective weights via Nash equilibrium. Five models are bench-marked on the FICS-PCB dataset. Results show that YOLOv10n achieves the best overall performance, providing a standardized framework for algorithm selection in PCB inspection and AOI systems.

title: Research on MPC Trajectory Tracking Algorithm Based on Fuzzy Adaptive Weight Adjustment
authors: Zhikai Jing, Chengjun Ding, Tan Zhang, Ke Wang, Ji Zhang, Ping Cao, Wenli Xu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Intelligent Manufacturing and Automation (IMA)
abstract: To address the issue of reduced tracking accuracy in traditional MPC caused by fixed weight parameters struggling to adapt to complex environments, this paper analyzes the impact of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Q R$</tex> weight coefficients on tracking precision and proposes a fuzzy adaptive weight adjustment-based MPC trajectory tracking algorithm. This method first uses the optimal fixed parameters of the test vehicle as a baseline, then introduces a fuzzy control mechanism to achieve online adaptive adjustment of MPC weights, thereby enhancing the controller's adaptability to changing operating conditions. Simulation results demonstrate that compared to the original fixed-parameter MPC, the proposed fuzzy MPC achieves significant improvements in tracking accuracy, control smoothness, and system stability, effectively enhancing the trajectory tracking performance of autonomous vehicles.
